observations.reason: The reconciliation of Due from Other Reciprocal accounts is currently in progress. Reiterated in Observation No. 1, page 45 of the report

observations.recommendation: We reiterate the previous year’s audit recommendation that Management: a. Instruct the end-users to prepare the RIS for every request in the issuance of supplies to facilitate the preparation of SSMI by the Acting Municipal Treasurer; b. Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to: a. Maintain stock cards, prepare the SSMI and complete the physical inventory of supplies for the preparation of the RPCI and submit the said inventory report to the Audit Team not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively; and b. Prepare and maintain the required forms, registries and reports for semi-expendable properties and assign a separate ICS control number for low-valued items and high-valued items to strengthen the controls and safeguards over the semi-expendable properties.
observations.managementAction: SSMI were attached to the JEV in recording expense. RIS were not prepared for every issuance of supplies. No action has been taken by Management. Required reports and records were not prepared and submitted. No action has been taken by Management. The necessary records were not prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: 1. Require the Municipal Agriculturist and Acting Municipal Treasurer to submit a report on the physical count of breeding stocks with documents substantiating the amounts indicated in the report; 2. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a) Trace in the accounting records all purchased/donated breeding stocks and record the same in the WOABSLC; and b) Update the columns in the WOABSLC pertaining to births of offspring and stock dispositions, and draw the necessary accounting journal entries to adjust the balance of Breeding Stocks in the books of accounts. c. Require the Municipal Agriculturist to:: • Prepare a comprehensive analysis of all issuances and movements of breeding stocks including the death or loss of animals, receipts of agricultural produce and changes in the fair market value resulting from price and physical changes; and • Submit the reports and supporting documents to the Acting Municipal Treasurer upon determination of all the price and quantity changes or movements in breeding stocks. d. Instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to: • Validate the Inventory Report submitted by the Municipal Agriculturist by conducting a physical count of the breeding stocks; and • Prepare the WOABSPC using the information provided by the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Agriculturist.
observations.managementAction: No action has been taken by Management. Comprehensive analysis of all issuances and movements of breeding stocks is ongoing. No action has been taken by Management. Management has yet to comply with the audit recommendation. No action has been taken by Management. WOABSPC was not prepared. No action has been taken by Management. Inventory Report was not submitted to the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to determine the discrepancies noted and effect the necessary adjustments in both records to arrive at reconciled balances of the cashbook and subsidiary ledger to ensure the reliability and correctness of the Cash in Bank balance. observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer was not able to submit an updated cashbook as of December 31, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to require the former officials and employees of the LGU to immediately settle their unliquidated cash advances by issuing demand letters and to withhold the salaries of the employees with unliquidated cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Require the BAC to a.) consider the SRP issued by the DOH in the procurement of medical equipment and medical supplies to ensure that prices obtained for the procured goods are not excessive and disadvantageous to the government, b.) require the supplier to submit their FDA authorization to sell COVID-19 testing kits and the Special Certification for the COVID-19 test kits delivered and c.) refrain from transacting with middleman to ensure that most advantageous prices are obtained; and b. Require the Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the BAC, to submit the aforementioned lacking documents for further evaluation of the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The DOH Suggested Retail Price was considered in the procurement of medical supplies for CY 2022. The Municipality submitted the needed supporting documents. The submitted documents were not appropriate to address the audit observation.
observations.reason: FDA Authorization to sell COVID-19 testing kits remained unsubmitted; some supporting documents still lacking.

Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the aforementioned lacking documents for further evaluation of the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year recommendations and made additional recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
(a) instruct the Acting-Municipal Accountant to (i) take appropriate corrections on the correct preparation of BRS; (ii) continue the maintenance of SLs and provide SL for the remaining bank account on the General Fund to easily track balances for a particular period; (iii) undertake the determination of the source of the unreconciled balance between the SL and GL of the trust fund accounts; (iv) look into the unreconciled balance/s of the two identified accounts for fair presentation of the CIB balance as mentioned in item 1.20.2 of this report; and
(b) direct the Municipal Treasurer (i) in coordination with the Acting-Municipal Accountant to effect proper disposition of stale checks in accordance with the procedures set in Section 59, Volume I of the New Government Accounting System Manual as cited above; (ii) to continue the regular monitoring of checks that are becoming stale and send appropriate notices to payees with unclaimed checks at least one month before they become stale, if warranted; and (iii) to facilitate cancellation of stale checks in his custody and transmit the cancelled checks and supporting documents to the Acting-Municipal Accountant for proper recording of appropriate payable account in the accounting records as called for by the aforecited regulation.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations and the Local Chief Executive in coordination with the LDC and MPDO agreed to:
(a) continue the checking/regular monitoring of the fund’s utilization in harmony with the approved AIP and introduce necessary interventions, if circumstances warrant;
(b) present in detail the PPAs charged under the 20% DF in the Annual Investment Program (AIP) to dispense the need to secure such SB approval for the timely implementation of the development projects, otherwise, seek approval from the SB of projects charged to the appropriation described in generic terms; and
(c) continuously refer to the JMC for appropriate guidance in crafting the Annual Investment Program and inclusion of PPAs on the 20% DF that will aim to fully attain the desired socio-economic development targets of the Municipality, with emphasis on the recent issuance on the utilization of the fund and inclusion of budget intended for the implementation of CBMS to aid in planning PPAs in the Municipality.
We also recommended and the LCE agreed to require the BAC and BAC Secretariat, in coordination with other concerned departments to:
(a) improve the quality and extent of their compliance with the requirements of RA 9184, its IRR and relevant rules and regulations in the Municipality’s procurement process by (i) paying attention on the procurement lapses noted in Annex B of this Memorandum, to avoid the recurrence thereof in future procurements; (ii) preparation of the Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) as basis for the preparation of the Annual Procurement Plan (APP); (iii) furnishing COA with copies of all purchase orders/contracts and all papers relative thereto within five days after the perfection of the contract and sending of notification of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 and 95-006; (iv) in coordination with the former BAC Chairman, cause the submission of the noted deficient documents of the projects as appearing in Annex A of this Report, for further evaluation as basis for the Audit Team’s subsequent audit action; (v) preparation of an explanation/justification on other deficiencies noted also mentioned in Annex B; and (vi) look into the noted deviations on the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects, to avert the recurrence thereof in future similar undertakings; and
(b) direct the Municipal Accountant to (i) check the completeness of the required supporting documents for every transaction before processing payment/s; and (ii) facilitate the deduction of retention money that will serve as warranty from defects of procured goods and infrastructure projects.

We reiterated and supplemented our previous year’s recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
(a) direct the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) to (i) ensure full provision of at least five per cent of the total budget appropriation as a separate GAD Fund to support GAD focused programs, projects and activities or as fund to support integrating gender-perspectives in regular/flagship programs and projects; and (ii) refrain from charging ineligible charges to the fund and provide for charges attuned with the PPAs embodied in the GPB;
(b) instruct the GAD Focal Point Person and MPDO to continue with the coordination and ensure collaborative efforts are exhausted to make sure that the implementation of CBMS will be effective in the LGU, in order to promote a reliable baseline data for the establishment of PPAs for a more gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; and
(c) direct the GFPP to (i) strictly/closely monitor the implementation of the programs and projects embodied in the approved GPB by the DILG to ensure full attainment of the GAD targets/objectives, intervene immediately, if delays in the implementation were observed, amend the GPB, if warranted; and (ii) prepare a GPB based on prior years data on budget and actual expenses, plan for PPAs that are feasible to implement and with workable budget, adequate to support and accomplish the activities to its full intent and objectives; and
d) remind all implementing offices to make sure that expenditures for GAD activities are within the allocated budgets so as not to compromise the implementation of other planned/budgeted activities.

We reiterated to recommend and Management agreed to:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain an updated property cards and for each class of PPE;

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to establish the PPE Ledger Cards for each class of PPE and reconcile with the records maintained by Property Unit; and

Enjoin the Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count on the agency assets in order to establish a verifiable PPE account balances and render a report necessary for the one-time cleansing of all PPE accounts in accordance with the prescribed procedures embodied in COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended and Management agreed to strictly comply with the rules and regulations of COA Circular 77-61 by setting controls and monitoring systems, to wit:

Prepare the required Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption complete with their attachments and submit to the Office of the Auditor for verification purposes and for determining the reasonableness of the fuel consumed for the period; and

Ensure the withdrawal slip for fuel, oil and lubricants are issued to government owned vehicles and their usage are authorized and appropriate, in order to avoid excessive and unnecessary expenditures.

We recommended the following:

Management take charge and hasten the operation of the CFLC.

Coordinate with BFAR-6 and lot owner to clarify the roles of each of the stakeholders on the operation of the CFLC.

Immediately resolve issues on turn-over and provision of right-of-way so that the project can operate according to its purpose.

We recommended that the Management consider the creation of a separate Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Office and the appointment of a full-time MENRO who will implement the SWM program of the Municipality. For the meantime, only permanent personnel of the Municipality be designated to supervise the SWM program.

We recommended that if found to have manufacturing defects, avail of the warranty as provided in RA No. 9184. On the other hand, in case of abuse/misuse by the end-users, hold the users concerned liable.

We recommended the following:

The Acting Municipal Treasurer exert more effort to collect delinquent real property taxes. Avail of all collection remedies applicable in the current circumstance of the Municipality.

The Acting Municipal Treasurer issue warrants of levy and conduct public sale or auction of delinquent real properties. These are indispensable actions to avoid the criminal and administrative penalties provided in RA No. 7160. Coordinate with other local treasurers who had recently conducted public sale or auction in their own local government units to adopt their procedures and best practices.

Management provide support to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer in the conduct of public sale or auction. Include in the Municipality’s appropriations the budgetary requirements of the activity and provide augmentation of personnel when needed.

We reiterated our recommendation that carried-over amounts of inter-agency payables be reconciled. Secure billing statements and confirm with BIR, GSIS, HDMF and PhilHealth if they have outstanding receivables from the Municipality, results of which will then be the basis of adjusting the long outstanding payable balances.

We recommended that Management prioritize processing of transfer of land titles in the name of the Municipality. To secure ownership on succeeding purchase of lots, payment be made only after the title has been transferred in the name of the Municipality.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management create a separate Municipal Tourism Office with its own appropriations. Due to the magnitude of work and responsibility, consider upgrade of position and/or designation of the Tourism Operations Officer I and provide the Office with at least another permanent personnel. In support of the Resort as a feasible economic enterprise, collections derived therefrom be utilized only for tourism investments, projects and activities.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Inventory Committee carry out a comprehensive physical count, reconcile PPE accounts per property and accounting records, and submit to COA a Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant, and Equipment reflecting the actual status of the assets in compliance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
observations.reason: Inventory Committee was not able to re organize again since some members were already resigned and retired and there is a lack of manpower to conduct a physical count of PPE’s.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant incorporates in the depreciation of each PPE item a residual value of at least five percent (5%) of the cost and reflect the correct carrying amount of PPEs. We further recommended to prepare adjusting entries to rectify the understatement of the LGU’s asset and overstatement of contra-asset and expense accounts.

The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer make a written follow-up with the bank management concerning the unresolved issues so that proper actions will be taken by the bank on the reconciling items noted.

Management requires both the Accounting and Treasury Departments to conduct at least quarterly reconciliation of the balances in the cashbook and ledgers to ensure that the reported funds are accurate and reliable.

The Municipal Mayor instructs the Inventory Committee to place the markings “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY” under which is the name of the municipality on each side of the vehicles owned by the LGU in adherence to COA Circular No. 75-6.

Management with the assistance of the MPDC and other key players in the decision making of the LGU closely coordinate on the immediate implementation of the priority development projects and programs funded under the 20% Development Fund during the year the funds are appropriated in order to ensure that the intended beneficiaries could avail of the maximum benefits and the desired outcome from the said programs or projects.

Management submits a written justification in utilizing the 20% Development Fund for expenditures that are not in accordance with the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020, for evaluation of the audit team.

Management requires the Head of the Inventory Committee to place property stickers on all identified properties of the LGU which will contain vital information suggested on Sections 5.6 and 5.7 of COA Circular 2020-006.

Management justify the charging of expenses that are not allowed against the 20% Development Fund as listed in DBM, DOF and DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020. 

Moreover, management strictly adheres to the Joint Memorandum Circulars and updates of DBM, DILG and DOF regarding the utilization of 20% Development Fund.

Management requires the Inventory Committee to prioritize and complete the physical inventory within a reasonable period in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006. Also, management is enjoined to ensure the full implementation of “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” as provided for in the same Circular.

The Local Chief Executive assigns appropriate committee to undertake proper disposal of unserviceable assets in accordance with COA Circular No. 89-296 and 2020-006 so that these will be derecognized in the books of the agency.

Management submits the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) between the LGU and the barangays, the certification from the Accountant that funds previously transferred to the barangays were liquidated as well as other documents necessary to support the validity and propriety of the transfer of funds made from the LGU to the barangays.

Management submits an explanation and the SB Resolution authorizing the transfer of funds with specific purpose from the previous 13 barangays to the new lead barangay, Sta. Cruz, for a newly identified project.

Management justify the charging of expenses against the development fund that are not allowed as listed in DBM, DOF and DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020. 

Moreover, Management strictly adheres to the Joint Memorandum Circulars and updates of DBM, DILG and DOF regarding the utilization of 20% Development Fund.

Management prepares the annual LDRRMFIP to include projects, programs, and activities chargeable from prior year’s unexpended balance of LDRRMF to show judicious and meticulous planning in the procurement activities of the LGU.  

Moreover, these should be incorporated in the revised AIP and be approved by the Local Sanggunian and LCE in accordance with NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1.

Management requires the inventory committee to provide the necessary details in the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to support the validity and reliability of the PPE accounts reported in the financial statements.

Management, in coordination with the Local Sanggunian and the Local Budget Officer, create the other three positions and appoint all these personnel to conduct Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (DRRM) activities in compliance with Section 2, Rule 6 of the IRR of RA10121.

The Municipal Accountant, guided by COA Circular 2016-005, initiates the verification, validation, review and analysis of the abovementioned non-moving accounts to effect necessary adjusting/ correcting journal entries for the eventual disposition thereof. 

Further, the said accounts can be requested for write-off as these affect the fair presentation of the financial statements.

Management instructs the inventory committee to conduct physical inventory on the remaining Breeding stocks and require the maintenance of Ledger Cards and submission of monthly inventory report to the Municipal Accountant so as to validate its existence.

Management requires the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to closely coordinate on the immediate implementation of the priority development projects, programs and activities funded under the 20% Development Fund during the year the funds are actually appropriated in order to ensure that the intended beneficiaries could avail of the maximum benefits and the desired outcomes from the said programs or projects.
Required the immediate refunds from all concerned officials and employees who receive the Extra Cash Gift of ₱18, 000.00.

observations.recommendation:
1.5 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, to prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list of taxpayers showing the names and the amount due and collectible based on RPTAR/Taxpayer’s index card to provide an accurate and reliable basis in the setting-up of receivables for RPT and SET at the beginning of the year.

1.6 We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to analyze and reconcile their records and reports to determine the cause of discrepancy amounting to ₱2,528,619.08 to reflect a reliable and accurate RPT and SET Receivable accounts.

2.9 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:

2.9.1 Record the proposed adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct balance of RPT/SET Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET accounts and to reclassify the fines and penalties for property taxes under SEF to the appropriate income account by following the proposed adjusting journal entries; and
2.9.2 Use the account Tax Revenue - Fines and Penalties – Property Taxes in recording penalties from delinquent payment of property taxes for proper presentation of accounts in the Financial Statements.

4.5 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a JEV to reverse the recorded deferred income accounts for the remitted share of the Province from advance payment of RPT and SET by following the proposed adjusting journal entries.

5.4 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to analyze the transactions affecting the balance of Continuing Appropriation and make the necessary adjustments on the discrepancy between the ending balance in CY 2023 and forwarded balance in CY 2024 in the net amount of ₱509,814.70.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the proposed journal entry to effect the reclassification of the issued semi-expendable PPE into its corresponding expense account/s and/or Government Equity account.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the unaccounted beginning balances and maintain and update PPELC for each class of PPE.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to reconcile the affected accounts. The proposed correcting entries are as follows:

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the review, verification, and reconciliation of all PPE accounts.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Set-up and/or record financial liabilities only when valid and complete supporting documents are available and attach said original supporting documents to the JEV for submission to the Audit Team to establish validity, proprietary and existence; and
Further, analyze the accounts which had been outstanding for more than two years and look into causes of non-settlement or dormancy. If found invalid or no original supporting documents could be found, the Municipality may revert said payables to the unappropriated surplus for the fair presentation of the payable account in the financial statement.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to comply with the disclosure requirements of relevant IPSAS to ensure that relevant information is available to the intended users of the financial statements.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to correct the prior period errors by restating the comparative amounts for prior periods presented in which the error occurred or by restating the opening balances of assets, liabilities and net assets/equity for the earliest prior period presented for fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to stop drawing cash advance for the payment of expenses other than those which purposes are allowed by existing rules and regulations;
Require the Municipal Treasurer to adhere strictly that all payments be made by check directly to suppliers/creditors in a per transaction basis and not through single check scheme for various expenses; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to existing laws, rules and regulations on the proper application of cash advances and ensure that payments shall be made by check directly payable to the creditor so that applicable withholding taxes can be deducted and the same be remitted to the national government.

We recommended that Management stop allocating and disbursing funds for advertisements which do not promote the programs and activities of the Municipality but are merely for publicity or propaganda purposes.

We further recommended that the LCE refrain from allowing the incurrence of advertising expenses for purposes other than the authorized items specified by relevant laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management instruct the BAC, through its Secretariat¸ to post all NOAs and Contracts/POs in the PhilGEPS website and attach print out copies to the disbursement vouchers as proof of posting.

We recommended that the BAC, through its Secretariat, indicate the ABC in every RFQ sent to suppliers.

We recommended that Management instruct the BAC to:

1. Consider adopting a Framework Agreement for the procurement of meals and snacks to avail of its benefits and advantages; and

2. Conduct an evaluation to determine the practicality, economy, and overall advantage of entering into such an agreement.

We recommended that Management stop directly hiring individual technical consultants and undertake the appropriate procurement process as required under RA No.  9184 by instructing:
1. The end-users or the implementing units/departments to prepare the “Terms of Reference” and all other documents necessary, based on their specific needs, for proper planning and implementation of the procurement activity for the technical consultants requested; and

2. The BAC to properly administer the procurement activities for the technical consultants.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that Management limit the hiring of experts to its actual need of consultancy services taking into consideration the specific work requiring highly specialized or technical expertise in a field of special knowledge or training required for the government project or related activities that are not available in the agency and are not redundant to the functions of the existing offices or regular employees of the Municipal Government to avoid incurrence of unnecessary expenditures.

We recommended that Management require the Local School Board to appropriate and utilize the SEF in accordance with the guidelines set forth in the aforementioned laws, rules and regulations and discontinue the incurrence of expenditures which are not related thereto.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that a budget be allocated for the titling of lands.

We further recommended that Management require the Municipal Assessor, in coordination with the Acting Municipal Treasurer, to exhaust all possible means for the speedy titling of lands and secure Certificate of Title to protect the interest of the Municipality from adverse claims by third parties.

We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that Management stop the practice of serving snacks during SB sessions, meetings and other municipal activities which are part of the regular functions, duties and responsibilities of the officials and employees.
We recommended that Management stop granting rice subsidy to the employees of national government agencies as they are already receiving such allowance from their respective agencies.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that Management direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to ensure collection of the delinquent accounts on real property taxes and stall rentals by adopting the procedures and remedies provided by law to provide additional funds to support the operations and programs of the Municipality geared towards the welfare of its constituents.

We also reiterated another previous audit recommendation that Management prepare contract agreement with the existing market stall holders to properly define measures and remedies in case of late payment of rental fees and to adopt measures in the policies and procedures of the Municipality before approving the renewal of Business Permits and Licenses of stall owners to intensify the collection efforts on delinquent stall rentals.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that Management employ the use of ATM Payroll System for the payment of wages and incentives of JO workers and COS personnel, as well as incentives and benefits of officials and employees and Sangguniang Bayan members to deter possible risks of loss of government funds through theft, robbery, and/or misappropriation.

We further recommended that Management, through the Human Resource Management Office, coordinate with the authorized government depository bank to facilitate the enrolment of all JO workers and COS personnel to the ATM Payroll accounts.

We recommended that, for proper and timely validation and monitoring of the GPB and AR, Management instruct the Gender and Development Focal Point System (GFPS) to:
1. Strictly observe the timelines in the preparation and submission of GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report accompanied by the supporting documents; and
2. Prepare annual status reports on the institutional mechanics for gender mainstreaming and submit these to the DILG Regional Office for consolidation.

1.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to (i) investigate and resolve the
discrepancy in the year-end balances of the Cash in Local Treasury
account as recorded in their respective books; and (ii) ensure that cash
book and ledger balances are reconciled at the end of every quarter
moving forward.

2.28 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a)
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to conduct regular
reconciliation of the Cash-in-Bank records at least quarterly to avoid
accumulation of unreconciled items in the books; (b) Municipal
Accountant to (i) prepare Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to adjust the
noted book reconciling items and errors based on competent documents;
(ii) cancel the stale checks and restore the balances of the corresponding
Cash-in Bank and liability accounts; and (iii) maintain Subsidiary
Ledgers for each PPAs under Trust Fund to monitor its cash balances at
year-end; and (c) Municipal Treasurer to (i) send written notices to payees
of checks a month before said checks become stale; and (ii) strictly adhere
to existing rules and regulations regarding the disposition of stale checks.

3.16 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to (i) timely prepare an updated Certified List of Taxpayers
including those delinquents, bearing the names, particulars of the
property, location, amount due and collectible for the applicable year and
other pertinent information and submit the same to the Accounting Unit
as the basis in setting up of RPT/SET Receivables in the books of accounts
of the Municipality at the beginning of the year; (ii) introduce collection
strategy that would significantly help increase the revenue from RPT/SET
Receivables of the Municipality; and (iii) institute applicable remedies,
administrative and/ or judicial action, for the collection of over-due taxes
as provided in Section 256 of the Local Government Code of 1991.

4.22 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a)
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to refrain from granting
additional cash advances to the Special Disbursing Officer unless the
previous cash advance given to her is first settled or properly accounted
for; (b) Municipal Treasurer to consider increasing the amount of Fidelity
Bond of the Special Disbursing Officer whose cash advances exceeded the
maximum cash accountability covered by her existing bond, as required
in Annex C of the Treasury Circular No. 002-2019 or to designate
additional disbursing officer; and (c) Municipal Accountant to record
advances on appropriate account based on the intended purpose to
accurately represent the nature of advances in the Financial Statements.

5.18 We reiterated that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to (i) exert best effort to trace from old accounting records the
details and ownership of Cash Advances/Due from Officers and
Employees totaling ₱1,189,693.26 which lack supporting records; (ii)
initiate the procedures for the application of request for write-off of
dormant accounts under COA Circular No. 2023-008; and (iii) and
intensify collection measures or institute appropriate remedies that would
require the identified Accountable Officers to settle or liquidate their past
due accounts.

6.25 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the (a) Municipal
Accountant to properly follow the steps of recording incoming and out-
going inventories in a perpetual method of inventory keeping; (b) OIC-
General Services Officer (GSO) to (i) maintain complete stock cards of
Inventories; and (ii) consolidate weekly the RIS in the Summary of
Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and submit the same to the Office
of the Accountant; and (c) Municipal Accountant and OIC-GSO to do the
reconciliation of all inventory accounts to determine the correct balances;
and (d) Inventory Committee to (i) undertake complete physical count of
all inventories of the Municipality; and (ii) prepare the corresponding
Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and submit the same
to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

7.38 We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief
Executive instruct the (a) Inventory Committee to (i) undertake complete
physical inventory of all property, plant and equipment of the
municipality; and (ii) prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical
Count of PPE and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the
prescribed period; (b) Municipal Accountant and OIC-General Services
Officer (GSO) to (i) maintain complete property records; (ii) do
reconciliation of all the properties to establish correctness of balances of
PPE accounts; and (iii) consider the guidelines on one-time cleansing of
PPE accounts to render a reliable PPE information; (c) Municipal
Accountant to (i) allocate depreciation expenses yearly to all PPEs, except
land, until fully depreciated; and (ii) make necessary reconciliation/
adjustment for the abnormal balances of accumulated depreciation; and
(d) OIC-GSO to facilitate the disposal of unserviceable PPE in accordance
with the prescribed guidelines and procedures. Furthermore, Municipal
Engineer to submit the necessary documents related to the demolished old
public market including the list of scrap materials, and the Municipal
Accountant to effect the necessary dropping from the books of the cost of
the said structure.

8.23 We reiterated our previous year recommendation that the Local Chief
Executive instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant to (i) record the balance
of the unpaid billing of the completed projects as Accounts Payable to
complete the recorded cost of the Construction in Progress (CIP) and
reclassify the cost of the said completed projects from CIP account to the
appropriate Property Plant and Equipment account; (ii) cause thorough
review of accounting and related records to determine the composition of
the forwarded balances of the CIP accounts to establish the particular
completed projects which remained recognized under the CIP accounts
and effect the necessary corrections/adjustments, if warranted; (iii)
record the depreciation expenses of the completed projects from the time
of completion; and (iv) prepare Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to adjust
the identified erroneous entry; and (b) Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Engineer to make proper coordination on timely determination
of the completion of infrastructure projects to subsequently facilitate the
necessary recording/transfer to the appropriate asset accounts for fair
presentation of balances in the financial statements.

9.8 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to (i) conduct a thorough reconciliation of the "Due to BIR"
account for CYs 2022-2023 and the current year-end balance to identify
discrepancies and confirm the correct balances; (ii) coordinate with the
BIR Office to determine whether a refund or credit is applicable, and take
appropriate steps to recover any excess remittance; and (iii) if
overpayment cannot be recovered, require responsible officials to refund
any overpayment resulting from their errors.

10.26 We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief
Executive instruct the (a) implementing offices to continue adhering with
the guidelines and requirements on the efficient utilization of funds
received from the National Government Agencies for the implementation
of various programs, projects, and activities; and (b) Municipal
Accountant to (i) conduct reconciliation of Due to NGAs account against
relevant records and effect proper adjustments, if necessary; (ii) maintain
subsidiary records for all funds received on a per project basis; and (iii)
coordinate with the implementing offices on the completion of the
required liquidation/terminal report and to return to the source agencies
or remit to the National Treasury the balance of funds transferred to
settle the long outstanding and non-moving accounts that can be used by
the government for other developmental purposes.

11.9 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a)
Municipal Human Resource Management Officer to continue updating
and reconciling leave credits of employees of the Municipality; and (b)
Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget
Officer to take coordinated actions to increase revenue collections and
allocate sufficient budgetary provisions to facilitate the recognition of
expense and liability accounts related to employee benefits.

12.19 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a)
Municipal Accountant to (i) observe full compliance with the disclosure
requirements provided under International Public Sector Accounting
Standards (IPSAS) 3 regarding Prior Period Errors; and (ii) ensure that
only lawful expenditures and obligations incurred during a fiscal year
shall be recognized or taken up in the accounts of that year; and (b)
Municipal Budget Officer to (i) refrain from charging prior year’s
expenditures against the current year appropriations; (ii) review the
available funding sources to facilitate the payment of prior year’s
expenses; and (iii) seek approval of the Sangguniang Bayan before
allocating funds for prior year’s expenses.

13.22 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) OIC-
Business Permit and Licensing Officer (BPLO) and Municipal Treasurer
to establish proper coordination and communication, particularly to
concerning matters related to business tax administration and collection;
(b) Municipal Treasurer to (i) regularly conduct examination of the books
of accounts of business establishments by assigning staff/personnel who
are equipped with the necessary knowledge and training in the
examination of the books of accounts to determine the truthfulness of the
declared gross sales/receipts used in the computation of business taxes
paid and to collect the correct taxes as provided under Section 171 of the
RA 7160; and (ii) cause recovery of the under collection of ₱17,391.94
arising from undeclared gross receipts/sales of the sampled business
establishments; and (c) BPLO to (i) fully enforce the submission of Sworn
Statement of Gross Receipts/Sales or copy of Certified Income Tax Return
from taxpayers to verify or assess the correct tax base to be used, as
provided by Section 2E.04 (d) and (e) of the Revenue Code; and (ii)
perform an on-site assessment of businesses claiming non-operation to
verify their actual operational status.

(a) require close coordination between the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and
Municipal Accounting Office to (i) continue to remit promptly and intact loan
amortization and mandatory contributions to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth within
the prescribed period; (ii) make sure that the same amount withheld is remitted
within the prescribed period; (iii) exert extra efforts in tracing the identified variances
starting off with the most recent data to cause the identification and cause corrective
actions such as remittance of unremitted employee premiums and loans; and (iv)
coordinate with PhilHealth on the noted unsettled balance of the Municipality in the
SPA;
(b) instruct the Acting Municipal Accountant to maintain a complete Index of
Payments for proper monitoring of claims and for prompt and accurate recording
and remittances to GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG; and
(c) provide the Municipal Accounting Office with additional personnel, if
possible, to aid in the preparation of the Index of Payment and immediate detection
of errors in the remittances.

17.16. We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations and the Local Chief
Executive agreed to direct the Acting Municipal Accountant to (i) continue with the
efforts made on withholding and remittances of taxes in accordance with the
aforequoted regulations; (ii) make the necessary adjustments/amendments to BIR
returns, if necessary, on noted discrepancies in the prior years and in CY 2024, to
reflect the actual deductions based on specific reference months; and (iii) henceforth,
ascertain that all taxes withheld are remitted intact to the BIR.

18.12. We restated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive:
(a) in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer and Acting Municipal
Accountant, continue to exert efforts to collect receivables originating from the said
disallowance, by seeking legal action against the rest of the accountable persons still
alive, they being solidarily liable for the disallowance or institute the remedy on the
collection afforded under Section 35 of PD 1445 as mentioned above; and
(b) direct the Acting-Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary
ledgers/records and properly update each official/employee for any payments made
in accordance with the requirement set forth in Section 7.2 of the COA Circular No.
2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the LCE:
a. Require the MA and OIC-MA to exert extra effort to locate the missing
unrecorded disbursement vouchers and supporting documents contained in
the BRS as reconciling items and immediately record and submit the same
to the Audit Team for post-audit; and
b. Instruct the MA and OIC-MA to produce documents for unrecorded deposit
reflected in the Bank Statement in CY 2020.
Further, we recommended that the LCE:
c. Continue to actively oversee the timely preparation and submission of the
BRSs as well as the monthly submission of complete accounts to the Audit
Team; and
d. Instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer (AMT) to conduct a thorough
inspection of all cabinets and storage areas within the MTO to ensure that no
disbursement vouchers, collections, or other financial documents have been
improperly concealed or misplaced.

We commended the efforts of the management and the OIC-MA for the gradual
improvement in the granting and liquidation of CAs, including no multiple CAs,
designation of additional disbursing officers, and compliance of most officials with
the prescribed settlement period. However, we still recommended the following
actions:
a. The Municipal Mayor require all Accountable Officers/concerned LGU
employees and officials with outstanding balance to immediately settle their
respective CAs;
b. The Human Resource Management Officer enforce the withholding of
salaries to those who were unable to settle/liquidate their CAs within the
prescribed period after the demand pursuant to Section 37 of PD No. 1445; and
c. The MA issue demand letters to former LGU Officials, with copies furnished
to this Commission, and to the LGU’s Legal Officer for appropriate action.
We also reiterated our previous recommendation that the LCE instruct the
Legal Officer to assess the issues on unliquidated cash advances, and if warranted,
initiate appropriate legal action against the former LGU officials, estates of the
decedents, and non-adhering officials.

We recommended that the previous AMT and his staff submit the Report of
Collections and Remittances to the Accounting Department for proper recording in the
books and deposit the remittances made by the COs to the LGUs corresponding bank
accounts.
We also recommended that the newly appointed AMT and the Internal Audit
Services Officer properly monitor the completeness of the reported collections and
issuances of ORs by the COs. Proper control must be observed in the issuance of the
AFs, and ensure that the stub was fully consumed/used by the COs before issuance of
another stub.

We recommended that the previous AMT and his trusted staff submit the Report of
Collections and Remittances together with the duplicate copy to the Accounting Department
for proper recording. Also, immediate deposit of all the amount on hand is required to
prevent misuse and loss of government funds.

We recommended that the previous AMT submit the duplicate copy of OR and
the Accounting Office record the same to correct the previously recorded understated
amount of collection.
Finally, the LCE direct the LGU Legal Officer to evaluate the act committed by the erring
employee and instigate appropriate sanctions against the former AMT for acts he committed
in violation of Section 24 of Revised Cash Examination Manual and Section 63 of PD No.
1445.

We recommended that the:
a. LCE require the previous AMT to reconcile his records with the Accounting
and submit duplicate copies of the official receipts issued by him to taxpayers for
proper recording of the Accounting Department.
b. New AMT deposit all collections at least on a weekly basis, or when the total
collections reach ₱10,000.00, in consideration of the physical distance from
LGU-Pandan to the AGDBs.
We also recommended to submit copies of the cashbooks of the Municipal
Treasurer to COA and Accounting Office on a monthly basis for monitoring and
reconciliation purposes.

We recommended that the new AMT implement the following measures:
a. Designate a Liquidating Officer to verify and acknowledge collections,
particularly when the AMT is on official travel;
b. Prepare a daily RCD to ensure that all collections at the end of the day are
recorded accordingly;
c. Verify and acknowledge daily collections, including inspection of on-hand
accountable forms, particularly the first and last issued official receipt during the
day;
d. Transmit the previous day’s RCDs to the Municipal Accounting Office
(MAO) and Internal Audit Service on a daily basis; and
e. Prohibit the transfer of Accountable Forms from one collector to another.

We recommended that the new AMT implement the following measures to
improve the internal controls in the MTO:
a. Procure cash receptacles, such as but not limited to cash box and cash trays,
for each Accountable Officers; and
b. Allocate funds for the repair of the locks on the table drawers and prepare
Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) for the procurement of
CCTV cameras.

We recommended that the:
1. Municipal Mayor require all departments and offices to account for and report
to the GSO all unserviceable PPEs and semi-expendable property in their
custody;
2. GSO conduct complete inspection of the reported unserviceable properties and
update the IIRUP and IIRUSP accordingly; and
3. Disposal Committee inspect and verify the IIRUP and IIRUSP, and immediately
undertake the disposal of the unserviceable PPEs and items.

We commend Management’s substantial compliance with laws, rules, and
regulations on the proper deduction and remittance of contributions and loan
repayments to GSIS and PHIC, as mandated under RA No. 8291 and RA No. 7875.
However, to ensure full compliance, we recommend that the LCE instruct the MA
and Human Resource Management Officer to reconcile the balance of the Due to
GSIS and PHIC accounts from prior years and CY 2024, and coordinate with the
concerned agencies if necessary. Furthermore, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to
promptly remit all outstanding premium contributions upon completion of the
reconciliation.

We recommended that management and the Municipal Development Council:
a. Submit the CY 2025 1st quarter status report for the unimplemented projects
of CY 2024 and re-evaluate the feasibility of those that remain for
implementation as of the same period, if any;
b. Provide justification for the frequent realignments made, particularly the
year-end realignments made to new priority projects which were eventually
discontinued or remained unimplemented by the end of CY 2024;
c. Prepare and submit an action plan detailing the LGU’s strategies to achieve
optimal physical execution of priority projects and effective utilization of funds
for CY 2025 and CY 2026; and
d. Entrust the preparation of Purchase Requests and the market studies or
research to the end-users, with assistance from the Technical Working Group of
the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and other experts, to expedite the
implementation and procurement process.
We also recommended that the BAC provide an explanation for the non-
implementation of the previously recommended Early Procurement Activity,
discuss the challenges encountered and corrective measures that may be applied.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a. The Municipal Engineering Office (MEO) and BAC to fast-track the
procurement of the required electrical wiring as specified by the local electric
cooperate, and to provide the Audit Team with a projected timeline for its
procurement and commencement of operations of the water desalination facility
for monitoring purposes;
b. The Municipal Engineer to assess whether the SWPF requires improvement,
such as the construction of protective walls, as mentioned by the MENRO-designate
and EMS;
c. The MENRO-Designate and EMS to transfer the solid waste processing
equipment (SWPE) to the SWPF if the site is deemed ready to house the
equipment;
d. The LDRRM Office to relocate the DRRM vehicles from the solid waste
processing facility prior to the transfer of SWPE; and
e. The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to identify a prospective
vacant lot that could be used for the construction of parking area for the LGU’s
motor vehicles.
We also recommended that end-users and concerned department ensure that the
designs and specifications of infrastructure projects are thoroughly discussed during
the planning stage to avoid complications during utilization and operation of
infrastructure assets.

We recommended that Management refrain from:
a. Reimbursing travel expenses incurred by JO/COS personnel for travels not
considered as official; and
b. Authorizing JO/COS personnel to accompany permanent or elected officials
when their presence is not necessary/critical in the activity or event.
We also recommended that:
c. The Municipal Mayor include and discuss the relevant provisions of EO No. 77
during one of their meetings with all Department Heads and Elected Officials; and
d. The Supervising Administrative Officer conduct an orientation for newly
hired and currently employed JOs/COS regarding the official travels and
activities that they are allowed to undertake.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal DRRM Council prioritize the procurement of DRRM equipment,
and other equipage and only include PPAs in the LDRRMP that will directly
contribute to Disaster Prevention and Mitigation, Disaster Preparedness,
Disaster Response, and Disaster Rehabilitation and Recovery.
b. Municipal Budget Officer refrain from charging expenditures to the
LDRRM Fund that are administrative in nature; and
c. MA exclude ineligible DRRM expenditures from the monthly LDRRM
utilization reports.
Additionally, we recommended revising the LDRRMP for CY 2025, if it is found to
be non-compliant with the above provisions.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Person and the GFPS Committee
prepare and submit the attachments to duly support the GAD accomplishment report
as required under Section 4.1.C.8.5 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JC No. 2013-1 and
provide sufficient details of the activities undertaken to facilitate validation of GAD
programs and projects reported.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Person submit the GAD AR within the
period prescribed by Section 5 of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014,
and continue close-monitoring of the submission of GAD data and reports by various
departments to ensure timely consolidation.
We also recommended that the LCE direct all concerned department heads and
concerned personnel to coordinate and cooperate with the GAD Focal Person in the
timely preparation and submission of the required GAD data and reports.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Person utilize the prescribed template
and guide for completing the GAD GPB and AR as provided in PCW-DILG-DBM-
NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 or its latest related revisions or issuances, if any.

We recommended that the:
a. LSB submit a written explanation/justification on its inability to prepare
and submit LSB resolution approving the SEF Budget;
b. Municipal Mayor direct the members of the LSB to revisit the provisions of
DEPED-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017; and
c. The OIC-MA, AMT, and the LSB comply with the procedures for the
preparation and approval of the SEF Budget as provided in Item 5.5 of the
same JC.

We recommended the following:
1. The BAC, TWG, End-User, GSO, and Inspectorate Committee submit
written justifications for the lapses and observations mentioned above;
2. The LCE, the PESO Manager and other concerned personnel conduct a
meeting with the PETGA to determine and reevaluate the status and
viability of the project, considering that the delivered boats did not conform
to the requested specifications. A copy of the minutes of meeting, along with
the proposed actions for the implementation of the project, be furnished to
the COA and DOLE for their reference.
3. The Management enforce the conditions set forth in the MOA, particularly
the creation of Project Management Team, implementation of profit-sharing
scheme and reporting to DOLE if the project has ceased operations;
4. The BAC instruct the TWG to visit the place of business of bidders during
post-qualification stage, verify their capacity and capability to deliver and
ensure the availability and compliance of goods with the required technical
specifications especially for technical and complex procurements;
5. The BAC require the participation of end-users and beneficiaries in the pre-
procurement conference for highly technical and complex procurements to
ensure that specifications to be advertised align with their needs; and
6. The GSO and Inspectorate Committee reject deliveries that do not conform
to the agreed technical specifications, and the OIC-MA impose liquidated
damages in accordance with RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the concerned municipal
officials:
a. Discontinue the payment of registration fees to various leagues and
associations for the conduct of regular and routinary meetings; and
b. Consider proposing to their respective leagues that the conduct of 1-day
meetings may be done through teleconferencing to eliminate the incurrence of
traveling expenses and registration fees, or holding them at government-
operated function halls, session halls and event centers to minimize costs.

We recommended that:
1. The LCE strictly enforce compliance with the requirements set forth in the
provisions of COA Circular No. 2007-001 by discussing these with the
NGOs/POs before implementing the proposed program/project; and
2. The MA record the grant of financial assistance to NGOs/POs under the
Due from NGOs/POs Account and require the liquidation thereof.

We recommended that:
1. The LCE, together with the MSWD Officer and other concerned officials,
revisit and revise the policies for AICS and PAF, and furnish a copy to COA for
reference;
2. The Office of the Mayor refer beneficiaries to the MSWD Office for proper
assessment and subsequent issuance of Certificate of Eligibility;
3. The OIC-MA strictly require the submission of CoE in accordance with the
approved internal guidelines; and
4. The MSWD Officer prepare and issue the CoE in accordance with the
Manual of Operation.

We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct periodic physical count of all supplies and materials on hand including the semi-expendable properties, submit the required RPCI and RPCSP and coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the same with the accounting records;
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to properly record the purchase of inventories in the appropriate inventory accounts and the issuance, consumption and distribution thereof as expense upon receipt of RIS and SSMI of a given period and make the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balance of the affected inventory and government equity accounts; and
c. Require the GSO-Designate and the Municipal Accountant to properly maintain, update and reconcile the SCs and SLCs, respectively. Also, strengthen controls over the semi-expendable properties by establishing and maintaining accurate records, reports, and registries as required by COA Circular No. 2022-004.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to give preferential attention to the timely preparation and submission of the required BRS for all bank accounts and maintain an updated SL for each account;
b. Require the Municipal Treasurer to: i) consistently update all cashbooks to ensure that the data reflected therein is accurate, complete and can be reliably used in the preparation of a BRS and ii) coordinate with the concerned Source Agencies to verify the status of fund transfers to the Municipality prior to initiating the closure of dormant accounts and the transfer of balances to the General Fund (GF); and
c. Instruct both the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to prioritize the reconciliation of the Cash in Bank accounts to establish an accurate and reliable balances to be reflected in the FS.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to conduct a detailed review of the accounts and make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the affected accounts. Henceforth, exercise care in recording and classifying the Municipality’s financial transactions to ensure that the balances of reciprocal accounts are at all times the same.

We recommended that Management:
a. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to: i) immediately issue checks to each LGU at an amount equivalent to their respective RPT shares and ii) henceforth, ensure the prompt and regular remittance of the shares of concerned LGUs within five days after the end of each quarter; and
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to: i) consistently notify the LGUs of their respective RPT share amounts each quarter to enable them to claim the same from the Treasury Office and ii) trace and verify the prior years’ balances, prepare the necessary adjusting entries and provide complete and appropriate supporting schedules to ensure fair and accurate presentation of account balances in the FS.

We recommended that Management:
a. Allocate adequate budget for the titling of its land properties in the name of the Municipality to establish absolute ownership and reduce the risk of third-party claims;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary records and RPLC for the Land account; and
c. Submit the lacking documentary requirements cited in the foregoing discussion and ensure strict compliance on the completeness of the supporting documentary requirements on subsequent transactions before every DVs be approved for payment in order to ascertain the propriety and validity of all claims against the agency funds.

We recommended that Management require the LSB to:
a. Ensure that prescribed planning and budgeting tools are prepared, submitted and effectively utilized and that the timelines in the planning and budgeting process are strictly observed as prescribed under the aforementioned Circulars. Further, include the previous years’ unexpended balances and continuing appropriations, in determining the net amount available for appropriation and incorporate in the SEF budget PPAs intended for ECCD;
b. Stop the practice of lump sum appropriation and ensure that specific PPAs are identified with corresponding amounts and designated beneficiary schools during budget preparation; and
c. Refrain from charging and payment of expenses that are not among the allowable expenditures chargeable against the SEF and ensure that all projects funded by SEF are properly procured and implemented by the LGU.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly ensure that all supporting documents are proper and complete before processing the claims or paying the same.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) and the Local Sanggunian, with the assistance of the Local Finance Committee, facilitate the revision/updating and enactment of the Municipal Revenue Code, in compliance with RA No. 7160, to improve the revenue generation of the Municipality and provide more funds for its operations. Also, incorporate all relevant ordinances enacted or amended from CYs 2009 to 2024 in the revised Revenue Code.

We recommended that Management immediately submit the lacking DVs with complete supporting documents. Henceforth, strict adherence with the reglementary periods in the rendition of accounts with complete supporting documents and necessary financial reports, schedules and financial statements is also enjoined.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
a. In coordination with the HRMO, prepare a staff pattern plan for non-permanent employees and review and evaluate the existing manpower resources to ensure adequate staffing pattern that suit the present needs of the offices and create clear-cut policies and guidelines to address the issue on the necessity and propriety of hiring JOs/holders of COS; and
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the lacking supporting documents and henceforth, ensure that all payments to JOs and casual employees are properly supported with complete documentation.

We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit SLs for each barangay and projects to be implemented in order to properly monitor the utilization of the funds intended for the implementation of these projects;
b. Fast track the implementation of the downloaded projects and ensure that the eligible programs and projects are completed within the prescribed timelines; and
c. Prepare and submit the following:
i. Supplemental LDIP and AIP which include the projects funded by the LGSF and Unprogrammed Appropriations - SBDP NTF-ELCAC; and
ii. Quarterly Report on Fund Utilization and Status of Program/Project Implementation using the prescribed format.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant:
Prepare Journal Entry Voucher to reclassify the completed projects to its appropriate asset accounts and provide appropriate depreciation expense; and
Compute and prepare necessary adjusting entries to record the related accumulated depreciation from the period the building was occupied.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct:
Concerned offices to evaluate the presence or status of projects included in the establishment of Trust Liabilities – LGU Equity and make appropriate recommendation for the reversion of funds no longer needed in connection with the activities funded by continuing appropriations for capital outlay; and
Municipal Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Voucher for reversion of unexpended balances of appropriations to the surplus of the general fund. Below is the proposed adjusting journal entry:
Trust Liabilities – LGU Equity	xxx
Prior Period Adjustment	        xxx
To revert the unexpended balance to surplus of the general fund

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Compel the Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count of the agency assets and render a Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment complete with all the required data; and
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain an updated property cards and for each class of PPE and the Municipal Accountant to establish the PPE Ledger Cards for each class of PPE and to reconcile their records.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant make a review of the accounts to determine the cause of the unreconciled amounts. Furthermore, prepare a reconciliation of the two reciprocal accounts and effect the necessary adjusting entries to bring the balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts into agreement in order to establish the accuracy, reliability and fair presentation of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Notes to Financial Statements complete with appropriate disclosure in accordance with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-004 in order for the financial statements to be presented fairly.

1.7 We recommended and the Management agreed that the Inventory Committee immediately undertake a comprehensive physical count of all PPE. This should be followed by a meticulous reconciliation of PPE accounts between property and accounting records. A complete and accurate RPCPPE reflecting the status of all assets, must then be submitted to COA to ensure compliance with existing guidelines.

2.6 We recommended and the Inventory Committee agreed to perform a comprehensive physical count of all existing assets to establishing an accurate record, verify asset records against existing documentation, and determine if retroactive depreciation is necessary which should be recorded as “Prior Years’ Adjustment” in the subsequent year.
2.7 Moreover, we recommended and the OIC – Municipal Accountant conformed to periodically review and update depreciation policies and procedures to ensure they remain accurate and relevant and create a schedule for recording depreciation on a regular basis.

3.7 We recommended and the OIC – Municipal Accountant agreed conduct a comprehensive review and verification of the Trust Fund accounts to identify any other unrecorded PPE; and
3.8 To record the necessary adjusting entries to recognize the assets in the General Fund and retroactively calculate and record the accumulated depreciation expense from the date the assets were fully paid and completed once proper documentation has been established. Otherwise, these assets shall be included in the One-Time Cleansing of PPE due to unavailability of records.

4.8 We recommended and the OIC – Municipal Accountant conformed to prepare and submit monthly BRS in strict adherence to COA Circular No. 96-011 to rectify the current backlog and ensure timely reconciliation going forward; and
4.9 Continue to actively engage with Landbank to resolve the weAccess issues for the missing bank statements from May to December 2023 and expedite the enrollment of all Trust Fund bank accounts into the system to facilitate efficient reconciliation.

5.5 We recommended and the OIC – Municipal Accountant committed to immediately designate a dedicated focal person responsible for the timely submission of all quarterly and annual SEF utilization reports to the mandated recipients within the prescribed deadlines to ensure strict compliance with DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017 and to prevent future lapses in transparency and accountability hindering effective oversight of SEF funds.

7.8 We recommended and the OIC – Municipal Accountant agreed to verify the adequacy of the supporting documents accompanying the disbursement vouchers, as outlined in COA Circular No. 2012-001 so that the propriety and validity of these transactions can be ensured.

8.7 We recommended and the OIC – Municipal Accountant conformed to conduct a thorough reconciliation of all intra-agency receivables and payables, specifically the "Due from Other Funds" and "Due to Other Funds" accounts, identify and correct any discrepancies, ensuring that the balances reflect accurate and synchronized transactions; and
8.8 Enforce a policy requiring the synchronous recording of all intra-agency reciprocal transactions such as implementing internal controls and regular reconciliation procedures to detect and prevent asynchronous recording.

We recommended that Management, through the City Accountant, verify the status of the projects recorded in the CIP and reconcile them with the list of projects in the Report on Government Projects.
We also recommended that the City Accountant correct the erroneous entries and identified misclassifications through the following proposed adjusting journal entries:
The Management agreed with the recommendation and committed to record the completed projects.
We recommended that Management, through the City Engineering and the General Services Office, conduct the inspection and assessment of the current condition of the CICC and the Old Market, and identify their respective recoverable amounts.
We also recommended that the City Accountant immediately take up the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts, once including the provision for impairment loss.
We recommended that Management, through the City Accountant, prepare the lapsing schedule and take up the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the depreciation and amortization of the recorded PPEs and Intangible assets.
We recommended that Management, through the coordination of the City Accountant and the DGS, ensure that all City-owned land and buildings which are  held primarily to earn rentals or for capital appreciation be accounted for as Investment Property.

We recommended that Management, through the City Accountant:
analyze and conduct a thorough review of the outstanding accounts payable, retrieve pertinent supporting documents and check/validate the existence of actual claimants by sending confirmations prior to the reversion of these outstanding payables; and if the transactions cannot be substantiated with necessary documents and the payable accounts are found to be non-existing, the funds intended for the said obligations shall be adjusted and reverted to the unappropriated surplus in accordance with the pertinent provisions of PD No. 1445; and
effect the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify other payables erroneously recorded in the Accounts Payable following the proposed adjusting journal entry:
Accounts Payable			₱1,350,000.00
Due to LGUs 			    ₱ 1, 350,000.00
To reclassify payables due to LGU erroneously charged to accounts payable account.

We recommended that Management direct the City Accountant to:
coordinate with the City Treasurer to trace the discrepancy between the reciprocal accounts;
correct the erroneous entries and the identified misclassifications of SET and RPT advance payments through the following proposed adjusting journal entries:
coordinate with the City Treasurer, with the assistance of the systems-provider, to resolve the noted issues in the recording of the RPT and SET in the e-system.

We recommended that Management:
require the City Treasurer to cancel the unclaimed stale checks and to regularly monitor all outstanding checks and issue written notices to the payees a month before these become stale; and
direct City Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry debiting the Cash in Bank and crediting Accounts Payable for stale checks as follows:
Cash in Bank                                              ₱3,953,963.39
Accounts Payable			₱3,953,963.39
We also recommended that Management, through the City Accountant, review and verify the items listed in the BRS; and make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to reflect the correct balances of the Cash in Bank and other affected accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed that, through the City Accountant, effect the necessary adjusting entries to correct the erroneously recorded transactions, following the proposed adjusting journal entry:
Account Title					Debit		  Credit
Prior Years Adjustment				  ₱6,180,225.00
Cash in Bank- Local Currency, Current Account	  ₱6,180,225.00

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Management, through the City Accountant:
exert effort in locating the accountable officers with long overdue cash advances and issue demand letters for its immediate liquidation especially to those who are still connected with the City and/or consider the possible legal remedies especially with regard to the substantial unliquidated balances;
consider the option of writing-off the dormant account under COA Circular No. 2016-005 for the unsettled balances pertaining to the deceased accountable officers; and
identify the cause of variance between the balance per books and per schedule and effect the required adjustments to accurately reflect the unliquidated cash advance balance.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the concerned department responsible for the procurement to submit a justification on the procurement of securitized letterhead without meticulous and judicious planning that resulted in overstocking;
Direct the CTO, DGS and Accounting Office to collaborate by installing a system of control to properly monitor the utilization of the securitized letterhead and submit periodic inventory report pursuant to the procedures prescribed in Chapter 7 of the Manual on NGAS for LGU Volume I; and
Direct the City Accountant to refrain from recording the procurement of supplies as outright expense and prepare the necessary adjusting entry to recognize in the books the unissued securitized letterheads through the following proposed entry:
To record reclassification of inventory on hand erroneously charged to other expense account.

In view of the above, we recommended that Management submit justification/ report outlining the specific role or task that each delegate has performed or accomplished during the travel and how this undertaking brings improvement to the City.
Likewise, we recommended that Management submit the lacking Narrative Report on trip undertaken/Report on Participation by each delegate or participant for the Audit Team’s further evaluation.

We recommended that the Management direct the City Engineer to submit the PAG-ASA weather reports or equivalent documents supporting the Suspension Orders issued due to adverse weather conditions.  It is further recommended that Management submit a written justification for the delay in finalizing the Variation Order for Phase I of the project.
Also, we recommended that Management, in coordination with the CPDO and Engineering Office, ensure that the budget preparation includes specific details for the Program/Plans/Activities appropriated for the year. This will help streamline the project implementation process and minimize delays.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management, through the concerned offices, review and reevaluate the identified program, projects and activities funded by the 20 per cent DF in CY 2024 to conform with the guidelines set forth in DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 and 2020-1. Further, we recommended that Management prioritize the timely implementation of projects and ensure the optimal utilization of funds to advance the City's development goals and objectives.

We recommended that the Management:
formally request an extension of the validity of the allocated funds from the DBM to prevent them from lapsing and ensure they can still be utilized for their intended purpose within the prescribed timelines or targets; and
take immediate action to comply with the mandated posting and reporting requirements for the LGSF-SBDP as outlined in Item 5.0 of LBC No. 151.

We recommended that Management:
Recover the total amount of ₱55,952.50 for the cost of repairs or participation fees from the individuals accountable for these expenses; and
Conduct a Pre-Inspection of the damaged properties and a Post-Inspection of the completed work. Additionally, Management should prepare and submit the Pre- and Post-Repair Inspection Reports, a Report of Waste Materials, an Invoice, and a Property Index Card, all of which should summarize the repairs and maintenance performed on each service vehicle.

We recommended that, moving forward, Management ensure that all projects and activities funded by both previous years' unexpended balances and the current year’s LDRRMF allocation are incorporated into the LDRRMFIP or Revised AIP before submitting the investment plan to the LDRRMC for approval.
We likewise recommend that Management verify that all PPAs are included in the LDRRMFIP or supported by an approved AIP before  expenditures are charged against the LDRRMF, in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1.

We recommended that Management, through the City Accountant, properly monitor the utilization of DRRMF to reflect the correct balances of the fund in the Notes to Financial Statements pursuant to 5.1.16 of COA Circular 2012-002.
We recommended and Management agreed to submit all perfected contracts and supporting documents to COA within five days after perfection or execution to facilitate prompt review and evaluation.
