We recommended that the Management, through the Sangguniang Panlungsod, prioritize the timely updating and amendment of the City’s Local Revenue Code, pursuant to sections 129 and 191 of RA No. 7160 to increase local revenues and other fees that would support the funding of the City’s programs and projects that benefit the people.

We recommended that the Management:
Direct the City Accountant to reconcile the recorded taxes withheld against the actual remittances to identify the breakdown of the unremitted balance and ensure that remittances are made to the BIR once the reconciliation is complete;
Instruct the City Accountant to conduct monthly reconciliation of the Due to BIR account to prevent undue accumulation of unremitted balances; and
Consider hiring of additional personnel to oversee the reconciliation process, given the large volume of transactions and the significant impact of non-remittance on government.

We recommended that Management, through the City Accountant, to:
Reconcile the balance between the amount reported in the schedule of contributions against the amount recorded in the SL;
Make the necessary adjusting entries and remit the same to the SSS or refund to the personnel whichever is deemed fit as a result of reconciliation; and
Conduct monthly reconciliation of the Due to GOCC- SSS account to prevent undue accumulation of unremitted balances.
Moreover, we recommended that Management consider the hiring of additional accounting personnel to handle the reconciliation of the mandatory contributions withheld against the actual remittance for each personnel.

We recommended that Management, through the City Accountant:
Reconcile the recorded mandatory contributions withheld and loan amortization payments against the actual remittances to identify the breakdown of the unremitted balance for each employee or contractual personnel and ensure immediate remittance to the HDMF once reconciliation is complete; and
Implement monthly reconciliation of the Due to Pag-IBIG account to prevent unnecessary accumulation of unremitted balances.
We further recommended that Management, through the HRMO:
Submit the missing or updated information as required by HDMF to prevent the accumulation of unremitted contributions due to missing or erroneous data; and
Ensure that all newly hired personnel submit the necessary requirements and information, while maintaining an updated database of employees and contractual personnel records.
We likewise recommended that Management consider hiring additional accounting personnel to handle the reconciliation of mandatory contributions withheld against the actual remittances for each employee and contractual personnel.

We recommended for  Management, through the City Accountant to:
Reconcile the recorded PhilHealth premium contributions withheld against the actual remittances to identify the breakdown of the unremitted balance for each employee and ensure immediate remittance to the PhilHealth once reconciliation is complete; and,
Implement monthly reconciliation of the account to prevent undue accumulation of unremitted balances.

We recommended that Management, through the GAD Focal Chairperson, ensure the timely preparation of the GAD Accomplishment Report and submit it to the Auditor within five (5) working days from the end of January of the preceding year in accordance with Item V of COA Circular 2014-001, for audit review.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the depreciation for each PPE item to accurately reflect the correct value of PPEs. We further recommended to prepare adjusting entries to rectify the overstatement of the LGU’s asset and understatement of contra-asset and expense accounts.
Further, a journal entry is proposed to be taken up in the books:
Adjusting Entry No. 1
To record adjustment for depreciation expense (CY 2024).

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant incorporate in the depreciation of each PPE item a residual value of at least five percent (5%) of the cost and reflect the correct carrying amount of PPEs. We further recommended to prepare adjusting entries to rectify the understatement of the LGU’s asset and overstatement of contra-asset and expense accounts.
Further, the following journal entries are proposed to be taken up in the books:
Adjusting Entry No. 1
To record adjustment for recognition of residual value.
Adjusting Entry No. 2
To record adjustment for negative carrying amount of PPEs.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the composition of the PPE items and reclassify tangible items that fall below the ₱50,000.00 capitalization threshold and implement the following proposed adjusting journal entry (PAJE):
To record adjustment for semi-expendable properties.

We recommended that the inventory committee include in the “Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment” (RPCPPE) all properties owned by the municipality and classify them according to their specific PPE account. We further recommended that the concerned officials facilitate the reconciliation of the results of the physical inventory with the Property and Equipment Ledger Cards maintained by the Office of the Municipal Accountant and effect one-time cleansing in compliance with COA Circular 2020-006.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to transfer only the unexpended/unobligated balances of the QRF and DRRMF-Mitigation Fund (MOOE) to the Trust Fund Books while the Capital Outlay (CO) shall be made continuing in the General Fund books until the project completion, in accordance with Section 5.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
Further, the following journal entries are proposed to be taken up in the books:
Adjusting Entry No. 1 – General Fund Books
To record the adjustment on previously transferred unexpended QRF and DRRMF-Mitigation Fund (MOOE) for CY 2024.
Adjusting Entry No. 2 – Trust Fund Books

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant take immediate action to reconcile the GL and SL balances, update the records, and prepare necessary adjusting entries to correct the erroneous tax recordings. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant perform regular and thorough reconciliations between the taxes withheld on supplier payments and the amounts remitted, ensuring that all withheld taxes are properly accounted for and accurately remitted to the BIR.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to trace the transactions recorded in the Due From Other Funds and Due To Other Funds accounts and make the necessary corrections/adjustments.  Moreover, require the Municipal Treasurer to settle these accounts to allow the creditor-fund to utilize the amounts for their intended purposes, if any.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to adjust/reclassify the above transactions to its appropriate accounts in conformity with the New Chart of Accounts under COA Circular 2015-009:
Other Professional Services		    xxx
Other General Services 			xxx

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make immediate action to review and rectify the erroneous taxes withheld and facilitate refund for the excess of the taxes withheld over the tax due. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the entries made related to withholding tax and remittances for compensation, and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the erroneous tax recordings.

10.8	We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to perform further reconciliation between the subsidiary ledger balances and schedules related to withholding tax and remittances and ensure remittance of all withheld taxes accurately accounted for to the BIR in accordance with National Internal Revenue Code of 1997.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Engineer to ensure complete, detailed, and updated reporting on all projects, including contractors and specific timelines to improve transparency and accountability on the PPAs undertaken by the agency.

We recommended that management consistently implement proper waste segregation as prescribed in RA 9003 and maintain order around the vicinity of its MRF to promote public health and care for the environment.

We recommended that the HRMO prepare and issue only one contract per hired personnel based on their established policy guidelines on the hiring of JO/COS employees to prevent any inconsistencies between the two contracts.

We recommended that Management, in coordination with the Sangguniang Bayan, direct the HRMO to incorporate clear parameters regarding the compensation of JO personnel into their comprehensive policy guidelines based on qualifications, responsibilities, and job complexity, subject to the municipality’s financial capacity.

We recommended and the management agreed to direct the GAD Focal Person to ensure the timely and effective implementation of GAD programs, projects and activities, as planned, to achieve the GAD objectives.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Budget Officer submit written justification on the incurrence of cash deficit/overdraft. We further recommended that management establish ways to improve the cash position of the municipality, such as, but not limited to, efficient revenue collection and adoption of effective austerity measures, in order to eliminate the cash deficit in the succeeding year.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the amounts withheld, as recorded in the financial statements, with the amounts actually remitted. Additionally, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that any outstanding balances are promptly remitted to the appropriate agencies and any penalties incurred due to late remittances are not to be charged against government funds.
We recommended and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries.
We recommended and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries.
We recommended and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries.
We recommended and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries.
We recommended and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries.
We recommended and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor stop the charging of ineligible expenditures against the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor observe the aforementioned policies and guidelines before conducting a study tour or “lakbay aral” outside the territorial jurisdiction of the LGU.
We also recommended and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned LGU officials/personnel to submit the aforementioned lacking requisite documents/information/justifications.
Furthermore, we also recommended and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor demand refund of the airfare expenses paid from the concerned LGU officials/personnel who backed-out from the said activity.

We recommended and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to:
incorporate the said assistance in the MSWDO’s Manual of Operations;
submit the updated manual to the Municipal Mayor for approval; and
submit the approved updated manual to the local sanggunian for adoption.

1.9. We recommended and Management agreed that the City Accountant:
1.9.1. Reverse the Accounts Payable by the amount it was credited for and submit a copy of the adjusting journal entry vouchers to the Office of the Auditor to substantiate compliance;
1.9.2. Ensure that the recognition of liability shall be made on the date of delivery/ rendition/ completion and acceptance of the goods/ services/ projects and such should be supported with valid documents; and
1.9.3. Record accounts payable only when the goods and services covered by the ORS have already been actually delivered/rendered.

2.7. We recommended and Management agreed that:
2.7.1. The City Accountant and Budget Officer work closely and prepare the lacking Notes to FS, which is the explanation/schedule of the material differences between the original budget and final budget in the SCBAA; and
2.7.2. Henceforth, strict compliance with IPSAS in preparing the Financial Statements is enjoined, particularly compliance with Par. 29 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards 24 on the Notes to FS for SCBAA.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to: a. Effect the adjustments to reflect the advance payments of RPT and SET, net of discounts; • Record the discounts in the books of accounts as Discount on RPT – Discount on SET; and • Use the account Tax Revenue - Fines and Penalties – Property Taxes in recording penalties from delinquent payment of property taxes for proper presentation of accounts in the Financial Statements. d. Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to record discounts granted for prompt payment of property taxes due for the year in the Abstract of Collections.
observations.managementAction: Advance payments are recognized in the books. Advance payments are reflected, net of discount. Discounts are recorded using the Discount on RPT – Discount on SET accounts. Discounts granted were recorded in the Abstract of Collection.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate the previous year’s audit recommendation that Management require the Municipal Accountant to review and analyze the transactions in prior years to account the discrepancy of ₱3,362,538.20 and take-up the necessary adjustments to reconcile the Due from and Due to Other Funds accounts in order to present the accurate balances of the reciprocal accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments were made to reconcile the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to adopt the accrual basis of accounting by recording any work accomplishments for on-going projects as at year-end based on the percentage of work accomplished indicated in the Report on GPPA duly supported by Inspection Reports from the Municipal Engineering Office as basis for recording of the CIP and Accounts Payable accounts in the books of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments were made to record the work accomplishment at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Instruct the members of the BAC and TWG to prepare Abstract of Bids as Calculated within the prescribed period; b. Require the BAC Secretariat to: • Ensure that invitations to the NGO in all stages of procurement are duly received. • Ensure that the Contract Agreement bears the signatures of witnesses; • Observe the prescribed period on the posting of NOA and NTP in the PhilGEPS website. c. Direct the Municipal Engineer to: • Ensure the accuracy of computation of the Detailed Estimates of Infrastructure Projects and to review thoroughly the computation of ABC; • Specify the work item and description in the prescribed format of the ABC; and • Submit the Certificate of Final Acceptance after the one-year defects liability period. d. Instruct the BAC to accept only the form of Performance Security as prescribed in Section 39.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, unless the situation permitted the other form of security; e. Instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the Delivery Receipt and Sales Invoice are duly dated; and f. Require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the BAC Secretariat, to submit the lacking documents to support the claims and for review and evaluation of the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Abstract of Bids were prepared within the prescribed period. Invitations to observers were duly received. Witnesses were required to sign the Contract Agreement. NOA and NTP were posted in the PhilGEPS website. OCM and profit mark-ups were applied appropriately. Computation of ABC did not include those items not subjected to OCM and profit mark-ups as validated by the Audit Team. Copies of Certificate of Final Acceptance after the one-year defects liability period were submitted to the Audit Team. The prescribed form has now been adopted. Only the forms of Performance Security prescribed in Section 39.1 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 were accepted by the BAC. Management ensures that Delivery Receipts and Sales Invoices are duly dated. Management submitted the lacking documents to support the claims.
observations.reason: Some NOAs were not posted within the prescribed period. Certain computations and documentation require further verification; some items remain to be fully verified or submitted as noted in auditor validation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to: • Cause the collection of the deficient business taxes totaling ₱235,637.61 from the suppliers concerned; • Be judicious in assessing the business taxes by considering the all data available to come up with the correct assessment. b. Require the taxpayers to present and submit a copy of audited financial statements and ITR to support the declared gross receipts for proper computation of business tax due.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer submitted copy of the Verification of computation of the business tax on the tax code. The Municipal Treasurer required the presentation of audited financial statements and ITR upon renewal of business permit.
observations.reason: No documentary evidence was submitted to the Audit Team to validate action taken by the Municipal Treasurer; presentation/submission of audited financial statements and ITR were not presented to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Formulate and issue a policy/guidelines for the implementation of the AICS program detailing who are entitled, the amount to be given and the needed documentary requirements; b. Instruct the MSWDO to: • Ensure that all financial assistance be supported with appropriate, valid and complete documentary requirements to avoid possible misuse or misapplication of funds; and • Conduct a thorough screening and assessment by validating the submitted documents and other necessary means to confirm the qualifications of individuals seeking financial assistance through preparation of Case Summary Reports.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the guidelines of DSWD on the grant of AICS. Grants of financial assistance are supported with appropriate, valid and complete documentary requirements. Thorough screening and assessment are conducted. Preparation of Case Summary Reports was observed.
observations.reason: Management has not yet formulated its own AICS policy; submitted DSWD guidelines only. Some procedural documentation strengthened but full local policy not instituted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Require the PSW to refund the excess claims for hazard pay in the amount of ₱7,264.89 equivalent to the duration of training/seminar attended; b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to: • Ensure the completeness of supporting documents in the claims for hazard pay; and • Verify and check the DTR, in coordination with the HRMO, before the preparation of claim for the accurate computation of hazard pay due to the PSW.
observations.managementAction: The responsible party/personnel agreed to make refund in October 2024. The Municipal Accountant diligently checks the attachments for the hazard pay claims. Preparation of claim has been coordinated with the HRMO. Proper coordination with the HRMO was observed for the preparation of hazard pay claims.
observations.reason: Refund not yet validated as implemented by Audit Team; coordination and controls strengthened.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from obligating and charging prior year’s expenditures against the current year’s appropriations. Proper coordination must be observed among municipal officials and employees in order that expenses are obligated and taken up as Accounts Payable in the current year but are to be paid in the succeeding year.
observations.managementAction: Management required the Municipal Budget Officer to observe the proper charging of transactions. No prior year’s expenditures were obligated or charged against the current appropriation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the documentary requirements; b. Instruct the BAC Chairman to review thoroughly the mode of procurement indicated in the RFQ, RFQ posted in the PhilGEPS and BAC Resolution; c. Instruct the BAC, in coordination with the BAC secretariat, to update/revise the RFQ form used in gathering the lowest calculated and responsive quotation for all transactions involving procurement undertaken through alternative methods to indicate the Approved Budget for the Contract, and other terms and conditions of the contract; d. Ensure that the RFQs, as a formal letter from the agency, are signed by the BAC Chairman; and e. Require the suppliers to indicate the brand of goods they are offering and other necessary information that may affect the final evaluation of the offers.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the documentary requirements. The BAC Chairman ensures that mode of procurement are consistent on all documents. The BAC, in coordination with the BAC Secretariat, updated the RFQ form. The BAC Chairman signs the RFQ. The Municipal Accountant submitted the noted documentary requirements.
observations.reason: Some RFQs lacked certain information (e.g., stage of documentary submission, signatures) and some purchases were delivered before PO date; full compliance pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the ICO-Municipal Budget Officer and the Local Finance Committee to avoid the reversion of continuing appropriations unless obligations therefor have been fully paid or otherwise settled and to prepare the Supplemental Budget only when supported by funds actually available.
observations.managementAction: Appropriations for CO were not realigned unless fully paid or settled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure immediate operation of the Anda RHU 2 by requiring the MHO to make a follow-up letter to DOH on the project proposal for the needed equipment of the RHU 2 under the HFEP and on the status of their request to be recipient of the Doctors to the Barrios so that the constituents of the LGU may already avail of the benefits offered by the HFEP of Department. We further recommended that Management include the provision of equipment and personnel in the RHU 2 to their priority programs to ensure its immediate operation.
observations.managementAction: Due to the demolition of RHU 1 to make way to the construction of Anda Super RHU, all operations and equipment of RHU 1 were transferred to RHU 2. Equipment of demolished RHU 1 was transferred to RHU 2. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant informed that the RHU has already a Work and Financial Plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Instruct the Procuring Officer to indicate the complete item name, complete detailed item specification, including accessories i.e., narrative description, detailed item size/dimension/volume, model, use of item, color, capacity, horsepower, voltage, watts, gauge, whether spare part is to be used whether replacement or original (for spare parts), part number of spare parts, type and classification of material, brand new or second hand and brand name of the items to be procured in the Purchase Order; b. Require the Inspection Officer to verify and inspect carefully all purchases made by the LGU to ensure its conformity with specifications provided in the Purchase Order; and c. Require the supplier to replace the delivered 10-Wheeler Dump Truck and Mini Dump Truck in conformity with the specifications indicated in the Purchase Order and bid proposal, respectively.
observations.managementAction: Management instructed the Procuring Officer to indicate required details in POs. The Procuring Officer was required to indicate complete name of the item, complete specification, accessories, part numbers and classification. Management required the Inspection Officer to verify and inspect carefully all purchases. Management coordinated with the supplier to replace the item delivered in order to conform with the PO.
observations.reason: Some POs still lacked full item details; replacement/rework with supplier not fully completed for all units; inspection reports need improvement.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Engineer to follow up the As-Built Plan with the DPWH and ask for the assistance of the Project Engineer of the agency to conduct an ocular inspection of the road network project to verify its existence.


We further recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the adjusting journal entries to take up the transfer of the asset from the DPWH once the project is inspected and accepted by the  Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
The As Built-Plan was received from the DPWH on June 26, 2024 and an inspection was already  conducted by the Municipal Engineer with the assistance of the Project Engineer on July 1, 2024.



Adjustments were already made under JEV No. 100-24-07-0030 to record the cost of the road network, and JEV No. 100-24-07-0031 for the amount of depreciation expense, both dated July 12, 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to submit a list of unreleased checks at year-end to the Accounting Office as basis for the preparation of JEV by the Municipal Accountant to take up the necessary adjustment.

We further recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to restore cash and recognize liability in the appropriate cash and liability accounts. Below are the proposed adjusting journal entries:
observations.managementAction:
The Acting Municipal Treasurer  submitted a list of unreleased checks at year-end to the Accounting Office.



The Municipal Accountant recognized the liability in the appropriate cash and liability accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Instruct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Acting Municipal Treasurer, to collect LD totaling ₱127,767.63 from the concerned contractor by deducting the same from any money due or which may become due to him; and 



Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that LD are strictly imposed on the contractors for delayed completion of projects.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipality’s computation of LD was based on the extended deadline which was February 9, 2022 until the actual completion on March 25, 2022.



The Municipal Accountant adheres to the recommendation that an LD should be strictly imposed on the contractors for delayed completion of projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management create a disposal committee to facilitate the disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance with the disposal procedures to avoid further deterioration and loss of value and generate additional funds from the sale thereof.
observations.managementAction: Management had reorganized the Inventory and Disposal Committee under EO No. 06 series or 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended  that the LCE implement the approved PPAs of the  Municipality while the SB members shall be limited to their legislative functions.
observations.managementAction: It was agreed among management that clients who seek financial assistance from Mayor's Office and SB will be endorsed to the MSWDO for proper assessment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, through the initiative of the MENRO and MPDC Officer and approval of the SB members, develop a concrete plan for the utilization and operation of the MRF machineries, taking into account the activities that will be most beneficial for the  Municipality and to prioritize the implementation of such activities to attain the intended benefits for which the machineries are acquired.
observations.managementAction: Machineries that needs to be retrofitted and checked by professionals were being used starting the 1st quarter of the CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management fill up the mandatory position for the three staff of the MDRRMO through local ordinance to handle the administration and training, research and planning, and operations and warning, to provide adequate support to DRRM activities.
observations.managementAction: As of CY 2024, all three permanent positions filled up under the LDRRMO.
All barangay has operational MRF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the
(a) Municipal Accountant to (i) initiate the conduct of regular reconciliation of the Cash- in- Bank records with the Treasurer’s cash records at least quarterly to avoid accumulation of unreconciled items in the books; (ii) prepare Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to adjust the noted book reconciling items and errors based on competent documents; (iii) effect the necessary journal entries on the restoration of cash and corresponding liability accounts for the 19 unreleased checks; and (iv) maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for each PPAs under Trust Fund to monitor its cash balances at year-end; (b) Municipal Treasurer to (i) send written notices to payees of checks a month before said checks become stale; (ii) strictly adhere to existing rules and regulations regarding the disposition of stale checks; and (c) the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all unreleased checks at the end of the year are reported and properly treated in accordance with the Manual on NGAS.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to (a) exert best effort to trace from old accounting records the details and ownership of Cash Advances to/Due from Officers and Employees amounting to ₱374,371.41 and intensify collection measures or institute appropriate remedy that would require the identified Accountable Officers to settle or liquidate their past due accounts and conduct reconciliation of the ₱788,448.42 accounts against relevant records and effect proper adjustment, if necessary; and (b) initiate the procedures for the application of request for authority to write-off dormant accounts under COA Circular No. 2016-005. We also recommended the Management to strictly enforce the settlement or payment of overdue accounts totaling ₱28,886.05 from the seven employees of the Municipality who are still in active service.

We reiterated that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Inventory Team to (i) undertake complete physical inventory of all property, plant and equipment of the municipality; and (ii) prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of PPE and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period; (b) Acting – General Services Officer to facilitate the disposal of unserviceable PPE in accordance with the prescribed guidelines and procedures; and (c) Municipal Accountant to (i) allocate depreciation expenses yearly to all PPE, except land, until fully depreciated; and (ii) make necessary reconciliation/adjustment for the abnormal balances of accumulated depreciation. Furthermore, Municipal Accountant and the Acting – GSO to maintain complete property records, do reconciliation of all the properties to establish correctness of balances of PPE accounts and consider the guidelines on one-time cleansing of PPE accounts to render a reliable PPE information.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant to (i) record the balance of the unpaid billing on the completed projects as Accounts Payable to complete the recorded cost of the Construction in Progress (CIP) and reclassify the cost of the said completed projects from CIP account to the appropriate Property Plant and Equipment account; (ii) cause thorough review of accounting and related records to determine the composition of the forwarded balances of the CIP accounts to establish the particular completed projects which remained recognized under the CIP accounts and effect the necessary corrections/adjustments, if warranted; (iii) record the depreciation expenses of the completed projects from the time of completion; and (iv) prepare Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to adjust the identified erroneous entry; and (b) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer to make proper coordination on timely determination of the completion of infrastructure projects to subsequently facilitate the necessary recording/transfer to the appropriate asset accounts for fair presentation of balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) implementing offices to continue adhering with the guidelines and requirements on the efficient utilization of funds received from the National Government Agencies for the implementation of various programs, projects, and activities; and (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) conduct reconciliation of Due to NGAs account against relevant records and effect proper adjustments, if necessary; (ii) maintain subsidiary records for all funds received on a per project basis; and (iii) coordinate with the implementing offices on the completion of the required liquidation/terminal report and to return to the source agencies or remit to the National Treasury the balance of funds transferred to settle the long outstanding and non-moving accounts that can be used by the government for other developmental purposes.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the (a) Bids and Awards Committee, BAC Secretariat, and Technical Working Group to (i) ensure compliance with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184 and its RIRR in all procurements through public bidding; (ii) prepare the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC), as prescribed under DPWH Department Order No. 197, series 2016; (b) Municipal Accountant to strengthen her review function on the requisite documentation of procurements to support the claims; and (c) Municipal Development Council to specifically describe the projects for inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan, and always secure approval or authorization of the Sanggunian before incurring expenditures and/or entering into contract in the implementation of projects described in generic terms.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant reclassify those tangible items that were already issued to end-user with the acquisition cost of below ₱50,000.00 to semi-expendable property by drawing an appropriate Journal Entry Voucher charging the carrying amount to Prior Period Adjustments and Government Equity, and close the accumulated depreciation and accumulated impairment loss of the asset.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to fully disclose in the Notes to FS the details of Prior Period Errors as required by Paragraph 54 of the IPSASs No. 3 in order to provide the users adequate information in understanding the financial status and capability of the agency through their FS.

We recommended and Management agreed to effect the required transfer of unutilized QRF and DRRMF – MOOE for Calendar Year 2023 and ensure in the succeeding years that transfer of actual cash pertaining to unexpended balance of current year DRRM be made immediately at year-end.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Chairman of Municipal Development Council, through the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator – designate, present their identified PPAs in the local budget with specific details and information, in order avoid getting additional authority from the Sangguniang Bayan before any charges can be made to the lump-sum or generic appropriation.

We recommended that the Management:

Require the Human Resource Officer to evaluate the personnel structure and staffing requirements of each office and propose the hiring of a qualified personnel on the budgeted vacant positions; and

To regulate the hiring of Contractual and Job Order personnel by evaluating the necessity and urgency of needs and assess their individual assignments to avoid redundancy and duplication of work, and incurrence of excessive expenditures.

We recommended the following:

a.	The Municipality provide a separate appropriation for MCPC programs, activities and projects (PAPs) equivalent to one percent of its share from the IRA. Only PAPs enumerated in DILG Memorandum No. 2012-120 be charged out of the separate appropriation.

PAPs on children be complete and inclusive, and to contain programs to assist children in need of special protection (CNSP) and intervention and diversion programs for children in conflict with the law (CICL) among others.
We recommended that the LGU open a separate bank depository account with any authorized government depository bank for the 20% Development Fund to ensure proper monitoring and control of the fund.

b. We further recommended and it was agreed upon that the OIC – Municipal Accountant consistently oversee the receipt and utilization of fund transfers from other LGUs and other NGAs. Any remaining funds after implementation should be returned to the Source Agency as specified in the MOA.
c. Henceforth, cash advances should be liquidated within the prescribed period and closed at year end, pursuant to Item 5.1 of COA Circular No. 97 002 dated February 10, 1997.

a. We recommended and the OIC – Municipal Accountant agreed to make reconciliation on the Other Payables Account and make adjustments accordingly so the balances reported in the financial statements be more reflective of the actual financial position of the Municipality as of the reporting period.

b. We further recommended that the OIC – Municipal Accountant conformed to investigate the accounts and if found out that these accounts are inexistent, request for write off following existing COA guidelines.
observations.reason: Pending write-off request.

observations.recommendation: a. We recommended and it was agreed by the LDRRMO to revisit the DRRMP and allot for activities and projects that will cater to the four thematic areas of disaster risk reduction and management and not solely focus on trainings and seminars.

b. Moreover, we also recommended and it was agreed by the Management to fully utilize the LDRRMF by implementing the programs and projects outlined in the DRRMP following specific guidelines. This will enhance the Municipality's capacity to address potential hazards and disasters, ultimately leading to the prevention or reduction of loss of lives and assets.

We recommended and the OIC – Municipal Accountant agreed to transfer the unexpended balance of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE totaling ₱1.949 million to the STF under the Trust Fund books and record the transfer under the account “Trust Liability DRRM” at the end of the year for proper reporting and monitoring of the funds in compliance with pertinent guidelines.

a. We recommended and it was agreed that the Municipal Mayor in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) ensure that the development projects to be funded out of the 20% DF are well planned, and procurement and implementation ready with specific development projects.

b. We also recommended and the Municipality conformed to discontinue the practice of appropriating funds in lump-sum amount and instead resort to line budgeting to ensure regularity and transparency of government transactions. In case lumpsum appropriations cannot be avoided, Management must secure prior authority from the SB before

We recommended and the OIC – Municipal Accountant agreed to verify the adequacy of the supporting documents accompanying the disbursement vouchers, as outlined in Sections 1.2 and 9.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 so that the propriety and validity of these transactions can be ensured.

Management prepares documents to transfer ownership and record of these PPE items to the barangays. Subsequently, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries to rectify the overstatement of the LGU’s asset, contra-asset, and expense accounts.

Stale checks still in the hands of the Municipal Treasurer be cancelled and replaced according to the guidelines set forth in Section 59 of NGAS Manual, Volume 1.  Stale checks that are already released to the payees shall only be cancelled and replaced upon presentation of the stale checks.  Meanwhile, the Treasurer should send a written notice to the payee to verify the existence of said checks.

The municipal accountant records only checks that are released to claimants pursuant to Section 43 of the NGAS Manual Volume 1. 

Thus, the Municipal Accountant draw the following proposed Journal Entry Voucher to adjust for the checks that are unreleased to claimants at year-end:

Dr. CIB-Local Currency, Current Account	₱252,588.31	
        Cr. Accounts Payable		₱158,131.31
             Due to Officers and Employees              1,242.00
             Other Payable	                            93,215.00

The inventory committee include in the “Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment” (RPCPPE) all properties owned by the municipality and classify them according to their specific PPE account. We further recommended that the concerned officials facilitate the reconciliation of the results of the physical inventory with the Property and Equipment Ledger Cards maintained by the Office of the Municipal Accountant.

Management updates their Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) and ensure to account all accountable forms with or without money value, including checks for their various bank accounts.

The LDRRMO submits the LDRRMFIP and Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of MDRRMF using the standard form prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-002 for the proper monitoring of the fund, thereby facilitating easier oversight.

Management submits a written explanation on the inconsistencies noted on the project. Further, management should substantiate for the two (2) defective and one (1) missing solar lights otherwise the same shall be disallowed in audit.

The BAC thoroughly checks the eligibility documents of all prospective suppliers/contractors and refrain from procuring goods and services from businesses whose owners are also officials of the municipality to avoid conflict of interest with the procuring entity.

The Municipal Treasurer directs all collectors of the municipality to remit their collections intact daily to the Liquidating Officer. Further, ensure that collectors are not issued additional accountable forms unless the collections pertaining to the accountable forms previously issued to them are remitted intact.

The procuring entity adheres to the provisions of Sections 10, 48 and 48.3 of RA 9184 which require recommendation of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and prior approval of the Head of the Procuring Entity or his duly authorized representative on the use of Alternative Method of Procurement whenever justified by the conditions provided for in the same Act.
Management submits the lacking documents indicated in Section 13.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-01 for further validation in order to establish the propriety and validity of the transaction.

Management expedite the hiring of personnel to fill-up these vacant positions and/or fast track the promotion of deserving and qualified workers among the present pool of manpower resources in the municipality.
The Local Chief Executive creates an Inventory Committee guided by the provisions of COA Circular 2020-06 and that the Internal Auditor shall only serve as a witness to the inventory taking.

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to require or demand for the issuance of an official receipt of the government by the recipient Leagues concerned in order to support the release of financial aid to the latter, pursuant to Section 69 of the GAAM, Volume I.

Required the Municipal Accountant to monitor the utilization of the funds released to these Leagues by requiring the recipient Leagues concerned to submit liquidation reports. In this manner, the propriety and validity of the expenditures could be properly evaluated.

We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor direct:

1.  The Acting Municipal Treasurer to: 

require its Collectors to prepare their RCDs; and 

turn-over their collections to the designated LO at end of each business day; and 

2. The Municipal Accountant to: 



make necessary adjusting entries.

We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:



make the necessary adjusting journal entries.

We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to: 

make the necessary adjusting entries.

We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor, as the designated SDO, to:



maintain a Cash Disbursement Record (CDR); and 

record therein daily the receipts and disbursements of the CF.

We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor:

stop the practice of granting additional cash advances to Accountable Officers without settling first their previous cash advances.

We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:

make the necessary adjusting entries upon settlement thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to:

submit an explanation for the delayed implementation of the said projects; and

submit a recommendation to him, endorsing the imposition of liquidated damages on the delayed implementation of the said projects, if warranted.

We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to

require the contractors concerned to post the subject warranty securities.

We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor direct:

the Municipal Accountant to deduct and withhold taxes upon accruals made at year-end; and


the Acting Municipal Treasurer to remit the same within the prescribed timeline.

We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor require the requisitioning officer to: 

completely and properly accomplish the accounting forms.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:



reconcile the discrepancies noted between the balances presented in the General Journal and Summary of Accounts Payable records;

make the necessary adjustments to arrive at the correct balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements; and

verify the validity of the unreconciled prior years’ Accounts Payable balance presented in the Summary of Accounts Payable.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to: 

clearly state on the face of the disbursement voucher the duration of the implementation of the projects; and

certify by stamping or printing on the disbursement voucher that previous cash advance for the same purpose, project or activity given to the SDO requesting cash advance has been liquidated and proper accounting thereof was made in accordance with aforesaid Circular.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require:

the MHO to:

record all donations-in-kind received by the RHU in the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances, duly supported with the (1) Acknowledgment Receipts, (2) proof of receipt by and distribution to the beneficiaries not necessarily in the required form, and (3) the inventory of remaining undistributed items, if any; and 

submit a copy of the same to the MGSO and the Municipal Accountant - Designate.

the MGSO to:

assist the MHO in the inspection and acceptance of all donations-in-kind, and in the conduct of inventory of the remaining undistributed items, if any; 

maintain copies of the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances and its supporting documents; and 

prepare and maintain a Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM.

the Municipal Accountant – Designate to, based on the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM, 

make a disclosure in the Notes to FS as to the quantity of received, issued and balance of all donation-in-kind received by the Municipality, with categorization as to food items, medicines and medical supplies, hygiene kits, and others.

We recommended that Management require the Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to determine the discrepancies noted and effect the necessary adjustments in both records to arrive at reconciled balances of the cashbook and subsidiary ledger to ensure the reliability and correctness of the Cash in Bank balance.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer regularly reconcile the SL balances of the Cash in Bank - LCCA with the cashbook balances every end of the month.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant furnished the Acting Municipal Treasurer copy of the monthly BRS to facilitate reconciliation of records.
observations.reason: Verification still showed unreconciled difference between the subsidiary ledger and cashbook.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that Management require the Municipal Accountant to exert more effort to obtain records of the transactions in prior years pertaining to the discrepancy of ₱3,362,538.20 and take-up the necessary adjustments to reconcile the Due from and Due to Other Funds accounts to present the accurate balances of the reciprocal accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management ensures that transactions affecting reciprocal accounts for the current year were taken-up in the books.
observations.reason: Verification of the accounts showed a net difference of ₱3,362,538.20 after consolidation of all funds pertaining to prior years.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Instruct the end-users to prepare the RIS for every request for issuance of supplies to facilitate the preparation of SSMI by the Acting Municipal Treasurer;
b. Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to maintain a stock card, prepare the SSMI and complete the physical inventory of supplies for the preparation of the RPCI and submit the inventory report to the Audit Team not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively; and
c. Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain the required forms, registries and reports for semi-expendable properties and assign a separate ICS control number for low-valued items and high-valued items to strengthen the controls and safeguards over the semi-expendable properties.
observations.managementAction:
The Accounting Office reviewed the procedures set forth in the MNGAS and has initially discussed it with Management. Management agreed for the immediate implementation of the procedures in the next calendar year.
The absence of GSO and stockroom hinders the LGU to observe the provisions in the MNGAS for LGUs and COA Circular No. 2022-004. Various meetings initiated by the LCE were conducted for the planning of how this concern will be addressed.
observations.reason:
The absence of GSO and stockroom hinders the LGU to observe the provisions in the MNGAS for LGUs and COA Circular No. 2022-004.
Reconciliation of the reports is on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Engineer to review thoroughly the computation of ABC taking into consideration those items not subjected to OCM and profit mark-ups to ensure that all infrastructure projects are acquired at a price advantageous to the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Management reminded the Municipal Engineer to review the computation of ABC taking into consideration those items not subjected to OCM and profit markup, specify the work item and description, and to submit the Certificate of Final Acceptance after the one-year defects liability period.
observations.reason:
Verification of projects for CY 2023 revealed items not subjected to OCM and profit mark-ups in the computation of ABC were yet to be imposed in one of the projects of the LGU.
Computation of ABC did not include those items not subjected to OCM and profit mark-ups as validated by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the ICO-Municipal Budget Officer and the Local Finance Committee to avoid the reversion of continuing capital outlay appropriations unless obligations therefor have been fully paid or otherwise settled and to prepare the Supplemental Budget only when supported by funds actually available.
observations.managementAction: Management informed that the appropriations for capital outlay were realigned for other capital outlay and development projects.
observations.reason: Verification of the Supplemental Budget for CY 2023 revealed that appropriations for continuing capital outlay were realigned for other capital outlay and development projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to include the list of projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF of previous years in the LDRRMFIP under a separate caption of the current year to ensure implementation of the projects and activities supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council within the next five years.
observations.managementAction: Management conferred to include list of projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF of previous years in the LDRRMFIP.
observations.reason: Verification showed that separate caption for the list of projects and activities was not enumerated yet in the LDRRMFIP for CY 2023.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all accountable forms are issued in numerical sequence and all copies of each receipt shall be exact copies or carbon reproduction in all respects of the original.
We further recommended that Management require the accountable officers concerned to submit written justification on the deficiencies observed for further evaluation of the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The Acting Municipal Treasurer required the accountable officers to issue accountable forms in chronological order. The Municipal Treasurer required the accountable officers to issue accountable forms in chronological order. The Acting Municipal Treasurer required the accountable officers to issue accountable forms in chronological order.
observations.reason:
Inconsistencies in the date of the ORs and date of remittance and alteration in the ORs were noted in CY 2023.
The required justifications for the deficiencies were not submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to continue sending notices to delinquent taxpayers and to make information drive campaigns such as posting of the notice of dates when the tax may be paid with discounts and without interest at a conspicuous and publicly accessible place at the Municipal Hall to encourage taxpayers to pay real property taxes in advance or on time in the ensuing year.
We also recommended that Management instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to send notice to market stall owners to settle their unpaid rental fees and electric bills to provide additional funds to support the operations and programs of the LGU geared towards the welfare of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: Management informed that the Acting Municipal Treasurer will intensify collection to enforce collection of delinquent accounts. The Acting Municipal Treasurer will send notices to market stall owners to settle their unpaid rentals and water bills.
observations.reason: No legal actions were undertaken by the LGU to enforce collection of delinquent accounts. Several stall owners were not able to settle their accounts.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Management require the GAD Focal Person to spearhead the preparation of adequate and systematic GAD Database which will aid in the gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation.
observations.managementAction: Management informed that updating of the AIMSD is already completed.
observations.reason: Gender issues of several PPAs in the 2023 GAD Plan and Budget were not well-defined to achieve a more effective GAD Plan and Budget.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the GFPS to identify the specific gender issues caused by the unequal situation between women and men in the locality in the GPB and to formulate PPAs that are responsive to these gender issues to attain the main intentions in promoting gender-responsive governance, protection and fulfillment of women’s human rights, and the promotion of women’s economic empowerment.
We further recommended that Management, through the GFPS, prioritize in the succeeding GPB the recurring/emerging gender issues in the LGU, results of gender assessment of the organization or its PPAs and other GAD PPAs identified in the GAD Code of the LGU that have not been considered in the previous year to achieve a more effective GPB.
observations.managementAction: Management informed that GFPS studied the recommendation and conducted meetings to address the issue.
observations.reason: Gender issues to be addressed were not well-defined in the GAD Plan of CY 2023.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated the previous year’s recommendation that Management require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to make a follow-up on the demand letters sent to debtors. In case the debtors fail to settle their accounts after due notices, apply legal sanctions. This may help to improve the collection efficiency of the LGU wherein the funds collected may be utilized for re-loaning to other beneficiaries.
Further, we recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to inquire in writing regarding the terms and conditions of the fund transfer received from the source agency, obtain a copy of the written agreement and submit a copy thereof to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were already made and some were delivered to the debtors. The Municipal Treasurer issued demand letters to all the recipients of this fund. The Municipal Accountant to inquire in writing regarding the terms and conditions of the fund transfer received from the source agency.
observations.reason:
Absence of records to determine where the fund came from and when was this downloaded to the LGU.
No legal action was undertaken by the LGU to enforce collection.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated the previous year’s recommendation that Management:
a. Insure the aforementioned properties amounting to ₱43,307,834.34 with the GIF of the GSIS; and
b. Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds needed for the payment of premiums.
observations.managementAction: The Acting Municipal Treasurer will submit the list of insurable properties of the LGU to the GSIS before the budget for the following year will be finalized to be able to get the total premium for budget purposes. The Budget Officer will then appropriate the amount in the 2025 annual budget.
observations.reason:
Only Legislative Building, Honda Motorcycle and Two Toyota Ambulance were insured.
Management will still have to comply with the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the former officials and employees of the LGU to immediately settle their unliquidated cash advances by issuing demand letters and to withhold the salaries of the employees with unliquidated cash advances. The Audit Team further recommended that Management should put the verbal agreement in writing and duly notarized the deduction of the unliquidated cash advances from the terminal pay of the Municipal Vice Mayor.
observations.managementAction: Management issued demand letters and caused the deduction of unliquidated cash advances to those still in service. The Vice Mayor liquidated his outstanding cash advance.
observations.reason: Long outstanding cash advances remained unliquidated amounting to ₱46,560.00.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Require the BAC to consider the SRP issued by the DOH in the procurement of medical equipment and medical supplies to ensure that prices obtained for the procured goods are not excessive and disadvantageous to the government, b.) require the supplier to submit their FDA authorization to sell COVID-19 testing kits and the Special Certification for the COVID-19 test kits delivered and c.) refrain from transacting with middleman to ensure that most advantageous prices are obtained; and
b. Require the Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the BAC, to submit the aforementioned documents for further evaluation of the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The DOH Suggested Retail Price was considered in the procurement of medical supplies for CY 2022. Likewise, the LGU submitted the needed supporting documents. The Audit Team could not validate the applicability of the FDA Certification of Panbio COVID-19 Ag Rapid Test Device (Nasal) since the attached Purchase Order, AIR and Delivery Receipt did not indicate the brand of the delivered rapid antigen test device to the LGU. Also, the FDA Authorization to sell COVID-19 testing kits of Marich Med Enterprises was not submitted.
observations.reason: The submitted documents were not appropriate to address the audit observations. The required documents were not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure the immediate operation of the Anda RHU 2 by requiring the MHO to make a follow-up letter to DOH on the project proposal for the needed equipment of the RHU 2 under the HFEP and on the status of their request to be recipient of the Doctors to the Barrios so that the constituents of the LGU may already avail of the benefits offered by the HFEP of the Department. We further recommended that Management include the provision of equipment and personnel in the RHU 2 to their priority programs to ensure its immediate operation.
observations.managementAction: Management made a follow-up of the request to the DOH on the project proposal for the needed equipment of the RHU 2 under the HFEP and their request to be recipient of the Doctors to the Barrios. The RHU 2 is already in operation since March 2023. The MHO and physician from the provincial government have scheduled regular visits. However, the RHU 2 did not meet the established criteria of the Sentrong Sigla Quality Standards List for Rural Health Units and Health Centers Level 1, thus, still unable to provide the health services needed by the constituents.
observations.reason: The RHU 2 still lacks the needed equipment and personnel to provide quality health services to the constituents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Instruct the BAC to make certain that all request for the procurement of drugs and medicines are supported with a certification signed by the requisitioning officer that the drug products being procured are enlisted and conform with PNDF Volume I, current edition to assure the quality, effectiveness and safety of products in catering the medical needs of its constituents; and
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to submit the aforementioned lacking documents for further evaluation of the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The BAC was advised to require the requisitioning officer that the drug products being procured are supported with certification signed by requisitioning officer.
observations.reason:
The certification signed by the requisitioning officer that the drug products being procured conform with PNDF Volume I, current edition was not attached to the disbursement voucher for the procurement of medicines in CY 2022.
Required documents were not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Require the municipal officials designated in the issuance of DTT to ensure that necessary data are duly provided, include a portion for the certification of the authorized passenger/s that the vehicle is used for official travel and prepare the Summary of Requisition and Issue Voucher, Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption; and
b. Direct the drivers to properly accomplish the portion of the DTT to be filled-up by the driver indicating all the necessary information and ensure that passengers sign the certification portion that the government vehicle will be used for official travel.
observations.managementAction: Management informed that the employee designated in the issuance of DTT is required to ensure that necessary data are duly provided. The management mandated all drivers to properly accomplish the portion of the DTT. Required reports were not yet submitted but will be prepared.
observations.reason: Some DTT were not properly accomplished and the required reports remained unsubmitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to conduct a detailed review and analysis of the Due to GSIS, Due to PhilHealth and Due to Pag-IBIG accounts in prior years and if warranted, make the corresponding adjustments; and to cause the immediate remittance of the premiums/contributions to Philhealth and HDMF.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office conducted a detailed review and analysis of the accounts.
observations.reason: The Accounting Office conducted a detailed review and analysis of the accounts. Transactions prior to CY 2015 were not reviewed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the MSWDO to submit copy of valid ID, Barangay Certificate with specimen signature of the beneficiary provided in the schedule and the certificate of eligibility of the 132 beneficiaries in Barangay Sablig for further evaluation of the Audit Team; and
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the MSWDO to issue demand letters to the five recipients of financial subsidy who were not listed in the Certificate of Eligibility to cause the refund of the financial subsidy amounting to ₱27,500.00 and return the amounts collected to the DSWD.
observations.managementAction: Management required the MSWDO to check documentary requirements and submit the same to the Accounting Office. Demand letters were issued to the five recipients.
observations.reason: Copy of valid ID, Barangay Certificate with specimen signature and certificate of eligibility for the 132 beneficiaries in Barangay Sablig were not yet submitted to the Audit Team. Proof of issuance of demand letters were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the BAC, with the assistance of the TWG and BAC Secretariat to strictly comply with the procurement provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and the fundamental principle on financial transactions prescribed in Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445; and to instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents in conformity with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 pursuant to Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445. Also, the expired Mayor’s Permit was supported only with business tax assessment and billing from One Stop Business Center of Dagupan City, instead of an OR as proof that the bidder has applied for the renewal of permit.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the required reports and justification on the audit observations. However, the following documents remained not submitted: a. Bid Securing Declaration dated Dec. 26, 2021; b. Manpower requirement dated Jan. 2020; c. Proof of posting of NOA, Contract and NTP in the PhilGEPS website; and d. Copy of registration documents of the vehicle under the name of the LGU.
observations.reason: Required documents remained not submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Instruct the Procuring Officer to indicate the complete item name, complete detailed item specification, including accessories i.e., narrative description, detailed item size/dimension/volume, model, use of item, including color, capacity, accessories, horsepower, voltage, watts, gauge, kind of equipment where spare part is to be used whether replacement or original (for spare parts), part number of spare parts, type and classification of material, brand new or second hand and brand name of the items to be procured in the Purchase Order;
b. Require the Inspection Officer to verify and inspect carefully all purchases made by the LGU to ensure its conformity with specifications provided in the Purchase Order; and
c. Require the supplier to replace the delivered 10-Wheeler Dump Truck and Mini Dump Truck in conformity with the specifications indicated in the Purchase Order and bid proposal, respectively.
observations.managementAction: Management instructed the Procuring Officer to indicate complete details in the PO. Management required the Inspection Officer to verify and inspect carefully all purchases made by the LGU. Management coordinated with the supplier to replace the item delivered in the PO.
observations.reason: Verification of CY 2023 accounts showed that required details in some POs were not indicated. The mini dump-truck was not replaced with item in conformity with the PO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE ensure strict compliance with the procurement provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and the fundamental principle on financial transactions prescribed in Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445; and to instruct the BAC, the BAC Secretariat, TWG, Municipal Engineer, Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to submit the required documents prescribed in conformity with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 pursuant to Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Management did not submit the required documents.
observations.reason: Required documents were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the responsible persons to submit the required documents for the donated assets and liquidation of the BUB Program to facilitate further verification of the project expenditures and avoid the issuance of Notice of Suspension in audit.
observations.managementAction: The MHO submitted the required documents for the donated assets and liquidation of BUB Program to facilitate further verification of the project expenditures.
observations.reason: The Approved Project Expenditures or Estimated Expenses indicating the project objective and expected output remained unsubmitted.

observations.managementAction:
No action was taken by Management. The creation of stockroom is not feasible due to limited budget.
Management created an inventory team. Management started an annual inventory tagging for CY 2023. Due to lack of manpower, Management is incapable of conducting physical count of inventories in all offices of the LGU.
The Acting Municipal Treasurer is exerting effort to issue RIS and maintain supplies. RIS were already issued, but due to manpower constraints, the inventory stock cards are not maintained and updated. Instead, an inventory worksheet is used to monitor and verify the remaining stocks by each department on a monthly basis.
The Acting Municipal Treasurer proposed to the Audit Team to prepare a consolidated RIS and RSMI on a monthly basis instead of the required weekly consolidation, due to budget and manpower constraints.
observations.reason: Creation of stockroom not feasible due to limited budget; lack of manpower prevented full physical counts; inventory stock cards not maintained; proposed monthly consolidation instead of weekly due to budget and manpower constraints.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the BAC members and BAC Secretariat to ensure that procurements of goods of the same specifications or qualities be consolidated into a single requisition to attain better terms in the procurement process and to avoid splitting of government contracts.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, through the initiative of the MENRO to and MPDC Officer and approval of the SB members, develop a concrete plan for the safe utilization and operation of the MRF machineries, taking into account the activities that will be most beneficial for the LGU and to prioritize the implementation of such activities to attain the intended benefits for which the machineries are acquired.
observations.managementAction: Some of the machineries need to be retrofitted and checked by professionals in order to secure operation.
observations.reason: Some machineries need retrofitting and professional checking to secure operation; not yet operational.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to locate the documents pertaining to the non-moving accounts in order to substantiate its balances and to enforce collections. In case of possibility of non-collection or could no longer be accounted or reconciled, the Management, through the Municipal Accountant, may request for write-off of the dormant accounts to clean the accounts.
observations.managementAction: Efforts are being exerted by Management to locate the documents for dormant accounts.
observations.reason: The amount of ₱613,635.81 remained dormant and are still recorded in the books of accounts as of December 31, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Agriculturist to exert effort to locate the pertinent documents relative to the 11 cows for verification before adjustments could be made in the books of the LGU. Management, after exerting best efforts, could not furnish the Audit Team the necessary documents pertaining to the 11 cows since the late Municipal Agriculturist of the LGU was not able to endorse the documents due to his sudden death and according to his family, all documents related to his work were already disposed of. Likewise, no data were left at his office; hence, Management could not drop the asset from the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Efforts are being exerted by Management to locate the needed documents for the breeding stocks.
observations.reason: Breeding stock account of ₱110,000.00 was still recognized in the books; necessary documents could not be located due to the death of the late Municipal Agriculturist and disposal of his documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant to (i) initiate the conduct of regular reconciliation of the Cash- in- Bank records with the Treasurer’s cash records at least quarterly to avoid accumulation of unreconciled items in the books; (ii) prepare Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to adjust the noted book reconciling items and errors based on competent documents; and (iii) at year-end, effect the necessary journal entries on the restoration of cash and corresponding liability accounts for stale checks; and (b) Municipal Treasurer to (i) send written notices to payees of checks a month before said checks become stale; and (ii) strictly adhere to existing rules and regulations regarding the disposition of stale checks.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2022 The Local Chief Observation No. 1 Reiterated in Observation No. 1 instructed the concerned officials to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: There are still reconciling items that remain unadjusted as of year-end between the records maintained by Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Inventory Team to (i) undertake complete physical inventory of all property, plant and equipment of the municipality; and (ii) prepare the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of PPE and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period; (b) Municipal Accountant and Acting- General Services Officer (GSO) to (i) maintain complete property record; and (ii) do reconciliation of all the properties to establish correctness of balances of PPE accounts; and (c) Municipal Accountant to (i) allocate depreciation expenses yearly to all Property, Plant and Equipment, except land, until fully depreciated; and (ii) make necessary reconciliation/adjustment for the abnormal balance of accumulated depreciation. Furthermore, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Acting- General Services Officer to consider the guidelines on one-time cleansing of PPE accounts to render a reliable PPE information.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in Observation No. 3 The Local Chief Executive instructed the concerned officials to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: There was no physical count conducted in CY 2023. Due to non-conduct of inventory of PPE, the preparation of report is not facilitated. The GSO maintains only the records from CYs 2019 to present. Due to non-preparation of RPCPPE, the reconciliation could not be made. Some PPEs has no depreciation due to lack of records. There are still PPE accounts that has abnormal balance of accumulated depreciation as at year-end. Lack of manpower to facilitate.
