observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management refund the amount of ₱1.20 million representing payment for cash assistance to BHERT members and other LGU personnel which was not in accordance with the purpose of BGCM Fund and thus considered as irregular expenditure
observations.managementAction: Submitted an appeal due to the reason given by management in the preceding column.
observations.reason:
No refund was made due to the Management appealed to reconsider the prevailing circumstances at the time the food allowance was granted. 

They appealed for the reason that this was discussed during the Municipal Inter-Agency Task Force (MIATF) that the food/meals served per shift were insufficient thus the barangay officials suggested to the former mayor to augment their needs thru cash assistance. They justified that it was necessary for them to receive cash incentives to cater their basic needs and to alleviate the difficulties prompted by the situation. 

The LGU practiced substance over form since there were restrictions imposed during pandemic. The intention of granting food allowance in the form of cash assistance is to help our frontliners buy the basic commodities they need such as food so they could execute properly their tasks to our people. This is the best option to take during that time due to restrictions such as difficulty in transportation and refusal of caterer to render services. Our frontliners play a vital role in implementing LGU’s COVID related programs so we should also give important to what they need to properly execute their tasks.

We recommended the following:

a.	If found to have manufacturing defects, avail of the warranty as provided in RA No. 9184. On the other hand, in case of abuse/misuse by the end-users, hold the users concerned liable.

We recommended the following:
The Acting Municipal Treasurer exert more effort to collect delinquent real property taxes. Avail of all collection remedies applicable in the current circumstance of the Municipality.

The Acting Municipal Treasurer issue warrants of levy and conduct public sale or auction of delinquent real properties. These are indispensable actions to avoid the criminal and administrative penalties provided in RA No. 7160. Coordinate with other local treasurers who had recently conducted public sale or auction in their own local government units to adopt their procedures and best practices.

Management provide support to the Office of the Municipal Treasurer in the conduct of public sale or auction. Include in the Municipality’s appropriations the budgetary requirements of the activity and provide augmentation of personnel when needed.

We reiterated our recommendation that carried-over amounts of inter-agency payables be reconciled. Secure billing statements and confirm with BIR, GSIS, HDMF and PhilHealth if they have outstanding receivables from the Municipality, results of which will then be the basis of adjusting the long outstanding payable balances.

We recommended that Management prioritize processing of transfer of land titles in the name of the Municipality. To secure ownership on succeeding purchase of lots, payment be made only after the title has been transferred in the name of the Municipality.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management create a separate Municipal Tourism Office with its own appropriations. Due to the magnitude of work and responsibility, consider upgrade of position and/or designation of the Tourism Operations Officer I and provide the Office with at least another permanent personnel. In support of the Resort as a feasible economic enterprise, collections derived therefrom be utilized only for tourism investments, projects and activities.

We recommended the following:

a.	The Municipality provide a separate appropriation for MCPC programs, activities and projects (PAPs) equivalent to one percent of its share from the IRA. Only PAPs enumerated in DILG Memorandum No. 2012-120 be charged out of the separate appropriation.

PAPs on children be complete and inclusive, and to contain programs to assist children in need of special protection (CNSP) and intervention and diversion programs for children in conflict with the law (CICL) among others.
We recommended that the LGU open a separate bank depository account with any authorized government depository bank for the 20% Development Fund to ensure proper monitoring and control of the fund.

We recommended and management agreed that the Inventory Committee conduct a complete physical count, finish all procedures in the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances, reconcile PPE accounts per property and accounting records and submit to COA a Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment containing the actual status of the assets in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements pursuant to COA Circular No. 96-011 so that accuracy and reliability of the Cash-in-Bank account could be ascertained and validated.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel under the Accounting Office to perform the reconciliation process.

observations.recommendation: 4.1 We recommended and management agreed that the OIC – Municipal Accountant to submit immediately their Liquidation Reports and supporting documents for the liquidation of their cash advances.
observations.reason: No agreements as to monthly deduction from salary with employees that are still in the service.

observations.recommendation: 4.2 Moreover, for Accountable Officers and employees who are not anymore in active service or those who are deceased, we recommended that the OIC – Municipal Accountant facilitate the request for write-off of their accounts in consonance with existing regulations.
observations.reason: No request for write-off submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed that the OIC – Municipal Accountant review all dormant accounts shown in the financial statements and if these accounts are found to be non-existent, initiate action by recommending to the Municipal Mayor the request for write off dormant receivable and payable accounts to COA following the procedures under COA Circular No. 2016-001 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.reason: No request for write-off submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the lacking documents indicated in Section 13.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-01 for further validation in order to establish the propriety and validity of the transaction.

We recommended that Management require the Inventory Committee to prioritize and complete the physical inventory within a reasonable period in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006. Also, management is enjoined to ensure the full implementation of “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” as provided for in the same Circular.

We recommended that Management expedite the hiring of personnel to fill-up these vacant positions and/or fast track the promotion of deserving and qualified workers among the present pool of manpower resources in the municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive assign appropriate committee to undertake proper disposal of unserviceable assets in accordance with COA Circular No. 89-296 and 2020-006 so that these will be derecognized in the books of the agency.

We recommended that Management submit the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) between the LGU and the barangays, the certification from the Accountant that funds previously transferred to the barangays were liquidated as well as other documents necessary to support the validity and propriety of the transfer of funds made from the LGU to the barangays.

We recommended that Management submit an explanation and the SB Resolution authorizing the transfer of funds with specific purpose from the previous 13 barangays to the new lead barangay, Sta. Cruz, for a newly identified project.

We recommended that Management prepare the annual LDRRMFIP to include projects, programs, and activities chargeable from prior year’s unexpended balance of LDRRMF to show judicious and meticulous planning in the procurement activities of the LGU.  

Moreover, we recommended that these be incorporated in the revised AIP and be approved by the Local Sanggunian and LCE in accordance with NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1.

We recommended that Management require the inventory committee to provide the necessary details in the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to support the validity and reliability of the PPE accounts reported in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory Committee guided by the provisions of COA Circular 2020-06 and that the Internal Auditor shall only serve as a witness to the inventory taking.

We recommended that Management, in coordination with the Local Sanggunian and the Local Budget Officer, create the other three positions and appoint all these personnel to conduct Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (DRRM) activities in compliance with Section 2, Rule 6 of the IRR of RA10121.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, guided by COA Circular 2016-005, initiate the verification, validation, review and analysis of the abovementioned non-moving accounts to effect necessary adjusting/ correcting journal entries for the eventual disposition thereof. 

Further, the said accounts can be requested for write-off as these affect the fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended that Management instruct the inventory committee to conduct physical inventory on the remaining Breeding stocks and require the maintenance of Ledger Cards and submission of monthly inventory report to the Municipal Accountant so as to validate its existence.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to closely coordinate on the immediate implementation of the priority development projects, programs and activities funded under the 20% Development Fund during the year the funds are actually appropriated in order to ensure that the intended beneficiaries could avail of the maximum benefits and the desired outcomes from the said programs or projects.
Required the immediate refunds from all concerned officials and employees who receive the Extra Cash Gift of P18, 000.00.

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to require or demand for the issuance of an official receipt of the government by the recipient Leagues concerned in order to support the release of financial aid to the latter, pursuant to Section 69 of the GAAM, Volume I.

Required the Municipal Accountant to monitor the utilization of the funds released to these Leagues by requiring the recipient Leagues concerned to submit liquidation reports. In this manner, the propriety and validity of the expenditures could be properly evaluated.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should: require the Municipal Accountant to
(a) reconcile the discrepancies noted between the balances presented in the General Journal and Summary of Accounts Payable records;
(b) Make the necessary adjustments to arrive at the correct balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements;                                                              
(c) Verify the validity of the unreconciled prior years' Accounts Payable balance presented in the Summary of Accounts Payable.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct/require the Municipal Accountant to:
(a) trace and reconcile the monthly variances between the deductions in the payroll including government share as against its corresponding remittances to the Fund in order to facilitate the identification of the persons liable/responsible to said new over remittance, after which, require the concerned employees to refund the amount remitted by the Municipality for the personal shares of their premium contributions and loan amortizations which were not deducted from their respective salaries and if deducted, were less than what was remitted while for other discrepancies noted,
(b) submit to the Audit Team the lacking official receipts issued by the Fund to acknowledge the MP2 monthly remittances together with the corresponding signed Pag-IBIG Data Entry System Forms as supporting documents to said monthly remittances, of which no deduction was withheld from the salaries of said employee/s, demand for its refund as such is considered as a members' savings for which the funds of the Municipality were used to pay;
(c) maintain subsidiary ledger for each type/nature of deductions withheld from officers and employees including government share for the account of the Pag-IBIG Fund and its corresponding remittances to said Fund in order to ascertain the validity and correctness of the entries posted therein; and
(d) facilitate its reconciliation to the controlling account in the General Ledger of any variance/s and/or adjustments at the end of every month.
We also recommended that management evaluate and strengthen the internal control system on the accounting and cashiering functions/duties and ensure that said functions/duties be segregated not only to prevent or reduce the occurrence of errors or fraud but also to pinpoint accountability and responsibility in safeguarding the LGU's assets.
observations.reason: Various entries not implemented due to lack of personnel in the Accounting Department and time constraints; some actions implemented as noted.

Henceforth, conduct an annual inventory of all properties of the Municipality and submit the corresponding report thereon to the Audit Team not later the January 31 of each year.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that
Management direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal
Accountant, to analyze and reconcile their records and reports to
determine the cause of discrepancy amounting to ₱1,589,238.74 to reflect a
reliable and accurate RPT and SET Receivable accounts.

We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to:
• Effect the adjustments to reflect the advance payments of RPT
and SET, net of discounts;
• Record the discounts in the books of accounts as Discount on RPT
– Basic or Discount on SET; and
• Use the account Tax Revenue - Fines and Penalties – Property
Taxes in recording penalties from delinquent payment of property
taxes for proper presentation of accounts in the Financial
Statements.
b. Require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to record discounts granted
for prompt payment of property taxes due for the year in the Abstract
of Collections.

We reiterate the previous year’s audit recommendation that Management
require the Municipal Accountant to review and analyze the transactions
in prior years to account the discrepancy of ₱3,362,538.20 and take-up the
necessary adjustments to reconcile the Due from and Due to Other Funds
accounts in order to present the accurate balances of the reciprocal
accounts in the financial statements.

We reiterate the previous year’s audit recommendation that Management:
a. Instruct the end-users to prepare the RIS for every request for
issuance of supplies to facilitate the preparation of SSMI by the
Acting Municipal Treasurer;
b. Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to:
• Maintain stock cards, prepare the SSMI and complete the physical
inventory of supplies for the preparation of the RPCI and submit
the said inventory report to the Audit Team not later than July 31
and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters,
respectively; and
• Prepare and maintain the required forms, registries and reports
for semi-expendable properties and assign a separate ICS control
number for low-valued items and high-valued items to strengthen
the controls and safeguards over the semi-expendable properties.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to
adopt the accrual basis of accounting by recording any work
accomplishments for on-going projects as at year-end based on the
percentage of work accomplished indicated in the Report on GPPA duly
supported by Inspection Reports from the Municipal Engineering Office as
basis for recording of the CIP and Accounts Payable accounts in the books
of the LGU.

We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Agriculturist and Acting Municipal Treasurer
to submit a report on the physical count of breeding stocks with
documents substantiating the amounts indicated in the report;
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
• Trace in the accounting records all purchased/donated breeding
stocks and record the same in the WOABSLC; and
• Update the columns in the WOABSLC pertaining to births of
offspring and stock dispositions, and draw the necessary
accounting journal entries to adjust the balance of Breeding
Stocks in the books of accounts.
c. Require the Municipal Agriculturist to:
• Prepare a comprehensive analysis of all issuances and movements
of breeding stocks including the death or loss of animals, receipts
of agricultural produce and changes in the fair market value
resulting from price and physical changes; and
• Submit the reports and supporting documents to the Acting
Municipal Treasurer upon determination of all the price and
quantity changes or movements in breeding stocks.
d. Instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to:
• Validate the Inventory Report submitted by the Municipal
Agriculturist by conducting a physical count of the breeding
stocks; and
• Prepare the WOABSPC using the information provided by the
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Agriculturist.

1.35 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a)
Municipal Accountant to (i) initiate the conduct of regular reconciliation
of the Cash- in- Bank records with the Treasurer’s cash records at least
quarterly to avoid accumulation of unreconciled items in the books; (ii)
prepare Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) to adjust the noted book
reconciling items and errors based on competent documents; (iii)
effect the necessary journal entries on the restoration of cash and
corresponding liability accounts for the 19 unreleased checks; and (iv)
maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for each PPAs under Trust Fund to monitor
its cash balances at year-end; (b) Municipal Treasurer to (i) send written
notices to payees of checks a month before said checks become stale;
(ii) strictly adhere to existing rules and regulations regarding the
disposition of stale checks; and (c) the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all unreleased checks at the end of the
year are reported and properly treated in accordance with the Manual on
NGAS.

2.15 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to (a) exert best effort to trace from old accounting records the
details and ownership of Cash Advances to/Due from Officers and
Employees amounting to ₱374,371.41 and intensify collection measures or
institute appropriate remedy that would require the identified
Accountable Officers to settle or liquidate their past due accounts and
conduct reconciliation of the ₱788,448.42 accounts against relevant
records and effect proper adjustment, if necessary; and (b) initiate the
procedures for the application of request for authority to write-off
dormant accounts under COA Circular No. 2016-005. We also
recommended the Management to strictly enforce the settlement or
payment of overdue accounts totaling ₱28,886.05 from the seven
employees of the Municipality who are still in active service.

3.27 We reiterated that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Inventory
Team to (i) undertake complete physical inventory of all property, plant
and equipment of the municipality; and (ii) prepare the corresponding
Report on the Physical Count of PPE and submit the same to the Office
of the Auditor within the prescribed period; (b) Acting – General Services
Officer to facilitate the disposal of unserviceable PPE in accordance with
the prescribed guidelines and procedures; and (c) Municipal Accountant
to (i) allocate depreciation expenses yearly to all PPE, except land, until
fully depreciated; and (ii) make necessary reconciliation/adjustment for
the abnormal balance of accumulated depreciation. Furthermore, we
recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Acting- General
Services Officer (i) maintain complete property record; (ii) do
reconciliation of all the properties to establish correctness of balances of
PPE accounts; and (iii) consider the guidelines on one-time cleansing of
PPE accounts to render a reliable PPE information.

4.13 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a)
Municipal Accountant to (i) record the balance of the unpaid billing on
the completed projects as Accounts Payable to complete the recorded cost
of the Construction in Progress (CIP) and reclassify the cost of the said
completed projects from CIP account to the appropriate PPE account; (ii)
cause thorough review of accounting and related records to determine the
composition of the forwarded balances of the CIP accounts to establish
the particular completed projects which remained recognized under the
CIP accounts and effect the necessary corrections/adjustments, if
warranted; and (iii) record the depreciation expenses of the completed
projects from the time of completion; and (b) the Municipal Accountant
and Municipal Engineer to make proper coordination on timely
determination of the completion of infrastructure projects to
subsequently facilitate the necessary recording/transfer to the
appropriate asset accounts for fair presentation of balances in the
financial statements.

5.21 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a)
implementing offices to continue adhering with the guidelines and
requirements on the efficient utilization of funds received from the
National Government Agencies for the implementation of various
programs, projects, and activities; and (b) Municipal Accountant to (i)
conduct reconciliation of Due to NGAs account against relevant records
and effect proper adjustments, if necessary; and (ii) maintain subsidiary
records for all funds received on a per project basis.

6.36 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the (a) Bids and
Awards Committee, BAC Secretariat, and Technical Working Group to
(i) ensure compliance with the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184 and its
RIRR in all procurements through public bidding; (ii) prepare the
Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC), as prescribed under DPWH
Department Order No. 197, series 2016; (b) Municipal Accountant to
strengthen her review function on the requisite documentation of
procurements to support the claims; and (c) Municipal Development
Council to specifically describe the projects for inclusion in the Annual
Investment Plan, and always secure approval or authorization of the
Sanggunian before incurring expenditures and/or entering into contract
in the implementation of projects described in generic terms.

7.27 We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management
Council (LDRRMC) headed by the Local Chief Executive (a) ensure the
optimal utilization of Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management
Fund (LDRRMF) through a more realistic project planning and
budgeting; and (b) require the Municipal Accountant to (i) effect the
adjustment of error made in recording the transfer of unspent LDRRMF;
and (ii) ensure that the rules and regulations in utilizing unexpended
previous years’ LDRRMF are properly and strictly followed.

8.22 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive through the MENRO (i)
review and update the Solid Waste Management Plan every two years
or as the need arises to ensure the sustainability, viability, effectiveness
and relevance in relation to local and international developments in the
field of solid waste management; (ii) pursue the functionality of inoperative
MRFs in two barangays to enhance solid waste management practices,
safeguarding the environment, and fostering the well-being of barangay
residents; (iii) intensify the information drive and awareness campaign on
the regulations on solid waste management in coordination with barangay
officials; (iv) rigorously implement the Municipal Ordinances in relation
to SWM and impose sanctions to violators, if warranted; and (v)
accelerate efforts in the enforcement of “no segregation, no collection”
and “no littering” policies.

9.18 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a)
Municipal Treasurer to (i) regularly conduct examination on the books of
accounts of business establishments by assigning staff/personnel who are
equipped with the necessary knowledge and training in the examination
of books of accounts to determine the truthfulness of the declared gross
sales/receipts used in the computation of business taxes paid and to
collect the correct taxes as provided under Section 171 of RA 7160; and
(ii) monitor if businesses are engaging on transactions outside their
registered line of business and impose different tax rates for such
transactions (if any); and (b) Business Permit and Licensing Officer
(BPLO) to fully enforce the submission of Sworn Statement of
Gross Receipts/Sales or copy of Certified Income Tax Return from
taxpayers to verify or assess the correct tax base to be used, as
provided by Section 2E.04 (d) and (e) of the Revenue Code.

10.24 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) GAD
Focal Point System (GFPS) and Municipal Budget Officer to coordinate
each other to ensure that GAD PPAs be at least five percent (5%) of the
LGU’s total budget appropriation; and (b) GFPS through the GAD Focal
Person to (i) inform the concerned DILG Office, in writing, of changes
introduced in the DILG-endorsed GPB and to prepare and submit
revised GAD Accomplishment Report whenever necessary; (ii) create a
multi-year GAD Agenda as basis of preparing the annual GAD Plan and
Budget; (iii) carefully plan the corresponding budget allocations of GAD
PPAs by preparing project breakdown of expenses before the approval
of the plan and budget; and (iv) conduct a review after three years and
an end-term evaluation to reflect on the status of accomplishment,
variances, and overall assessment of the implementation of the GAD
agenda based on the goals and desired results/outcomes.

11.8 We reiterated that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant to revisit the prior year’s transactions and conduct the
reconciliation of the balances of Due from Other Funds and Due to Other
Funds accounts and effect the necessary adjustments as warranted to
bring their balances into agreement. Henceforth, exercise due care in
recording and classifying the Municipality’s financial transactions to
ensure that the balances of reciprocal accounts are equal at all times.

12.6 We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief
Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee to submit regularly the
advance copies of Contracts and Purchase Orders together with its
supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed
period.

13.33 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to (a) prepare complete and accurate statements of accounts
for remittance of taxes/premiums/loans repayments deducted from
suppliers/contractors/employees to the BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and
PhilHealth, to facilitate the intact remittance thereof; (b) exert best effort
in identifying the nature of negative balances of Due to BIR and Due to
Pag-IBIG and make the necessary reconciliation to allow correction of
account balances.

14.6 We recommended and Management agreed to exercise due prudence in
the disbursement of government funds to avoid audit
suspensions/disallowances.

Henceforth, strictly observe the notification to the AT and public as required by Items 2.1 and 2.2 of COA Circular 2013-014.

We recommended that the management allocate funds for the procurement of the required 15 kVA single-phase distribution transformer to utilize the Solid waste management equipment donated by DENR-EMB V to LGU-Pandan.

We also recommended that the MENRO strictly comply with the provisions of the MOA with the DENR-EMB V to ensure attainment of the objectives of the SWM program and enhance compliance with RA No. 9003.

Submit to the GPPB the APP including the supplemental APP and revisions, if any, as required by Section 7.3.5 of the RA No. 9184.

The MBO submit the SAAOB and RAAOs to the MAO and COA to be used in preparing the SCBAA and verifying disbursements and specific charges, respectively;

The OIC-Municipal Accountant to facilitate the preparation and submission of SCBAA to COA; and 

Strict compliance with the reglementary period for submission of reports.

We recommended that the Local Elective Officials propose to their respective leagues the use of government-prescribed official receipts, specifically AF No. 51 for the registration fees paid by the participants in attending various government leagues, meetings, conventions, etc.
We also recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant require that all forms of payments to government organizations, entities, and institutions are duly supported by appropriate government official receipts.
Furthermore, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive enforce strict implementation of the applicable laws, rules, and regulations pertaining to payments to government leagues, ensuring they are duly supported by government official receipts.

We recommended that Management:
Create an Inventory Committee who will conduct a systematic and complete physical count of PPE in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 and ensure timely submission of RPCPPE, complete with appropriate information on their conditions, to the Office of the Auditor; and
Require the GSO-Designate and the Municipal Accountant to maintain an updated Property Cards and Property Ledger Cards, respectively, per category on all PPE as recorded in the books of accounts, and continuously coordinate with each other in order to reconcile the discrepancies in the respective PPE accounts.
For missing PPE items which can no longer be found/ located despite great efforts, we recommended that Management strictly follow the guidelines of COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the disposition of non-existing/missing PPE items of government agencies.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to give preferential attention to the preparation and submission of the required BRS, prepare subsidiary ledgers per bank accounts, review the records relevant to the Cash in Bank account and effect necessary adjustments to correct the amount presented in the FS and other documents.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant:
Properly analyze, classify and record every transaction in the books in accordance with the Manual on NGAS for LGUs and IPSAS;
Depreciate the constructed asset when it is available for use, not when progress billing is paid; and
Effect the necessary adjusting/correcting entries for the erroneous accounts used in the preceding transactions.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to compute tax withholdings based on the cost of this billing of the project. Likewise, recover the under withholding amount of ₱65,125.02 for the succeeding payments to be made to the contractors and make necessary adjusting entries for the affected accounts.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to strictly comply with the prescribed period of submission of their respective reports and documents to the Office of the Auditor;
Devise accounting procedures/methods to cope up with the submission of Accounting and Budget Reports to COA as well as other Financial Reports required by other agencies; and
Constantly remind/coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer and other concerned Officials or employees of the LGU to submit the pending reports/documents in order to avoid delay in the preparation and submission of financial reports to COA.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Engineer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the submission of reports on information and publicity of LGU Projects, Programs and Activities as provided in COA Circular 2013-004, within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive:
Require the concerned LGU Officials to submit immediately the aforesaid monthly DVs, collection accounts and reports to COA Auditor within the prescribed period; and
If practicable, assign or designate some accounting staff whose prime responsibility is to facilitate the recording and sorting of Barangay financial transactions so as not to interfere or hinder the accounting works of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management:
Strictly comply with the requirements on the submission of Purchase Order and their supporting documents to the Commission on Audit within five working days upon its issuance pursuant to COA Circular 2009-001; and
Require the concerned officials or employees to provide a written justification as to non-submission of POs and their supporting documents to COA within the prescribed period, for the whole year of 2023.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
Ensure that the POs were properly filled out with all the information/data required as prescribed in the guidelines;
Require the Municipal Accountant to conduct a thorough examination on the supporting documents and ensure that all documentary requirements relative to the procurement of goods, as stated in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 and the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, are present before making any payments; and
Submit the lacking supporting documents, duly approved by the proper officials and submit explanations on the noted deficiencies.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive:
Require the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with the documentary requirements provided in COA Circular No. 2012-001 prior to processing the claims;
Submit the lacking documents of the payments for terminal leave benefits with deficient documentation; and
Dissemination of the pertinent COA Circular to retiring officials and employees is advisable, in order for them to promptly prepare the necessary documents and expedite the processing of their claims.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2014-001 and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01, and require the concerned Officials and employees, particularly the GAD Focal Point person, to diligently observe the submission of GPB and GAD Accomplishment Report to COA Audit Team, as well as in other government agencies such as the DILG, within the prescribed period.

We reiterated to recommend and Management agreed to:

Enjoin the Inventory Committee to conduct a physical count on the agency assets in order to establish a verifiable PPE account balances and render a report necessary for the one-time cleansing of all PPE accounts in accordance with the prescribed procedures embodied in COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant reclassify those tangible items that were already issued to end-user with the acquisition cost of below ₱50,000.00 to semi-expendable property by drawing an appropriate Journal Entry Voucher charging the carrying amount to Prior Period Adjustments and Government Equity, and close the accumulated depreciation and accumulated impairment loss of the asset.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to fully disclose in the Notes to FS the details of Prior Period Errors as required by Paragraph 54 of the IPSASs No. 3 in order to provide the users adequate information in understanding the financial status and capability of the agency through their FS.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the concerned department responsible for the procurement to submit a justification on the procurement of security seal stickers without meticulous and judicious planning, which resulted in overstocking.
Require the MISO, DGS and Accounting Office to collaborate by installing a system of control to properly monitor the utilization of the security seal stickers and submit periodic inventory report pursuant to the procedures prescribed in Chapter 7 of the Manual on NGAS for LGU Volume I
Further, we reiterated our recommendation that Management through the City Accountant refrain from recording the procurement of supplies as outright expense and prepare the necessary adjusting entry to recognize in the books the unissued security seals stickers using the proforma adjusting journal entry, as follows:
During the exit conference, Management informed the Audit Team that the City will not procure additional security seals until the existing supply will be consumed.

We recommended that Management through the City Accountant review and verify the reconciling items listed in the bank reconciliation statements and make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to reflect the correct balances of the Cash in Bank and other affected accounts.

We recommended that Management through the:
BAC and other concerned officials submit a written explanation on the deficiencies noted in the rental of the backhoe excavator through framework agreement modality of procurement.
City Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entry to recognize in the books of account the amount payable to the supplier under a framework agreement as follows:
Dr: Prior period adjustment		₱6,211,516.46
Cr: Accounts Payable 			₱6,211,516.46
BAC to conduct a study if framework agreement is more practical, economical and advantageous for the procuring entity and ensure that the conditions are met before recommending its use.
City Engineering Office to conduct a review of all the projects to be implemented which requires the use of backhoe excavator and determine if it is more advantageous to purchase the unit or rent out from private company.

We recommended that Management, through the BAC, submit an acceptable explanation why the bid evaluation was not conducted in conformity with the guidelines of GPPB Circular No. 02-2010 which resulted in the disqualification of the cooperative bidder.

We recommended that Management direct the General Services Officer and the Chief, Property Division to inspect and assess the current condition of the CICC building and conduct an investigation among the security personnel on the loss of its parts and fixtures.  Further, we recommended that pending the demolition of the building, provide additional security personnel to man the gates and to strengthen the safeguarding of government properties.

We recommended that Management
Direct the City Accountant and the Chief, Property Division to collaborate in order to facilitate the reconciliation and updating of the list of insurable properties of the City and submit the Property Inventory Form using the prescribed format as shown in the attached Appendix 12 not later than April 30 of each year; and
Require the Chief, Property Division to apply with the GSIS property insurance all the insurable properties of the City, consistent with R.A No. 656 and COA Circular No. 2018-002 dated May 31, 2018 within the required timeline to avoid any penalty to be imposed to officials and employees responsible for the payment of the prescribed premiums.

We recommended that Management, through the concerned implementing offices of the City, identify specific infrastructure projects to be undertaken and to be funded out of the financial assistance received with concurrence through a resolution from the SP of the City of Mandaue.  We also recommended that prior to the implementation of the identified projects, furnish the Province of Cebu with copy of the resolution together with the request for authority to implement the identified infrastructure projects.
Furthermore, we recommended that Management find effective ways and exhaust all possible means to address issues, if any, that could likely impede the implementation of the programs and projects intended for the benefit of its constituents, otherwise, request authority from the Province of Cebu to use the funds for other priority projects.

We recommended that Management instruct the City Accountant to regularly coordinate and inform the LDRRM Officer on the financial assistance received by the City so that the intended programs, projects and activities related to disaster /calamity response could be included in the plan, and to facilitate the timely utilization of the funds in accordance with the purpose for which it was given.

We recommended and Management, through the City DRRM Office, agreed to develop a proactive strategy such as initiating/preparing the procurement requests for the identified PPAs on disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation in the early part of the year to ensure the optimal utilization of the DRRM Fund during the fiscal year.

We recommended  and Management agreed, through the City Budget Officer and CDRRMO, to stop the practice of utilizing the fund for the payment of honorarium of Job Order personnel of the LDRRMO and allocate the fund for PPAs that are responsive to the four thematic areas such as disaster prevention and mitigation, disaster preparedness, disaster response and disaster rehabilitation and recovery.

We recommended that Management direct the DRRMO and the City Budget Office to prepare and maintain the CDUR and RCDU, respectively, and ensure that the charges and utilization of the donated funds are properly monitored.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that Management direct the concerned offices to review the identified programs, projects and activities funded out of the 20 per cent DF in CY 2023 and submit an explanation/justification for their non-implementation. Further, we recommended that Management ensure that priority projects are timely carried out and the fund is optimally utilized with the view of effecting efficiency, economy and effectiveness in the disposition of government resources in compliance with the provisions set forth in DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 and 2020-1.

We recommended and the Management agreed, through the City Assessor’s Office, to fast tract the preparation of the Schedule of Fair Market Values and initiate the general revision of properties in accordance with RA No. 7160 and the Manual on Real Property Appraisal and Assessment Operations.

We recommended that Management fill-up the vacant plantilla positions with due consideration on the existing job order personnel who are qualified for the position and fast track the hiring process.  We further recommended that the HRMO speed up the re-evaluation of the plantilla positions to address the current needs of the City for the efficient and effective discharge of its functions.

We recommended that Management through the City Accountant immediately remit the taxes withheld from compensation and payment of goods/services to the BIR to avoid penalties and surcharges.  Moreover, instruct the assigned accounting personnel to properly monitor the account and ensure that remittances are made within the due dates as prescribed under BIR Regulation 2-98.

We recommended that the Management prepare documents to transfer ownership and record of these PPE items to the barangays. Subsequently, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries to rectify the overstatement of the LGU’s asset, contra-asset, and expense accounts using the suggested journal entry below:
Adjusting Entry No. 1 – Books of LGU
To record transfer of PPEs to appropriate barangays.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to incorporate in the depreciation of each PPE item a residual value of at least five percent (5%) of the cost and reflect the correct carrying amount of PPEs. We further recommended to prepare adjusting entries to rectify the understatement of the LGU’s asset and overstatement of contra-asset and expense accounts.
Further, the following journal entries are proposed to be taken up in the books:
Adjusting Entry No. 1
To record adjustment for recognition of residual value.
Adjusting Entry No. 2
To record adjustment for negative carrying amount of PPEs.

We recommended that stale checks still in the hands of the Municipal Treasurer be cancelled and replaced according to the guidelines set forth in Section 59 of NGAS Manual, Volume 1.  Stale checks that are already released to the payees shall only be cancelled and replaced upon presentation of the stale checks.  Meanwhile, the Treasurer should send a written notice to the payee to verify the existence of said checks.
Further, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to draw the following proposed Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the stale and cancelled checks at year-end:
Dr. CIB Local Currency, Current Account ₱49,000.00
Cr. Accounts Payable ₱49,000.00

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record only checks that are released to claimants pursuant to Section 43 of the NGAS Manual Volume 1.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw the following proposed Journal Entry Voucher to adjust for the checks that are unreleased to claimants at year-end:
Dr. CIB-Local Currency, Current Account ₱252,588.31
Cr. Accounts Payable ₱158,131.31
Cr. Due to Officers and Employees ₱1,242.00
Cr. Other Payable ₱93,215.00

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer make a written follow-up with the bank management concerning the unresolved issues so that proper actions will be taken by the bank on the reconciling items noted.

We recommended that the inventory committee include in the "Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment" (RPCPPE) all properties owned by the municipality and classify them according to their specific PPE account. We further recommended that the concerned officials facilitate the reconciliation of the results of the physical inventory with the Property and Equipment Ledger Cards maintained by the Office of the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended and the Management agreed to update their Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) and ensure to account all accountable forms with or without money value, including checks for their various bank accounts.

We recommended and the LDRRMO agreed to submit the LDRRMFIP and Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of MDRRMF using the standard form prescribed by COA Circular No. 2012-002 for the proper monitoring of the fund, thereby facilitating easier oversight.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require both the Accounting and Treasury Departments to conduct at least quarterly reconciliation of the balances in the cashbook and ledgers to ensure that the reported funds are accurate and reliable.

We recommended that Management submit a written explanation on the inconsistencies noted on the project. We further recommended that management substantiate for the two (2) defective and one (1) missing solar lights otherwise the same shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the BAC thoroughly check the eligibility documents of all prospective suppliers/contractors and refrain from procuring goods and services from businesses whose owners are also officials of the municipality to avoid conflict of interest with the procuring entity.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to direct all collectors of the municipality to remit their collections intact daily to the Liquidating Officer. Further, ensure that collectors are not issued additional accountable forms unless the collections pertaining to the accountable forms previously issued to them are remitted intact.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the Inventory Committee to place the markings “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY” under which is the name of the municipality on each side of the vehicles owned by the LGU in adherence to COA Circular No. 75-6.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the procuring entity adhere to the provisions of Sections 10, 48 and 48.3 of RA 9184 which require recommendation of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and prior approval of the Head of the Procuring Entity or his duly authorized representative on the use of Alternative Method of Procurement whenever justified by the conditions provided for in the same Act.

We recommended that management, with the assistance of the MPDC and other key players in the decision making of the LGU, closely coordinate on the immediate implementation of the priority development projects and programs funded under the 20% Development Fund during the year the funds are appropriated in order to ensure that the intended beneficiaries could avail of the maximum benefits and the desired outcome from the said programs or projects.

We recommended that management submit a written justification in utilizing the 20% Development Fund for expenditures that are not in accordance with the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020, for evaluation of the audit team.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Head of the Inventory Committee to place property stickers on all identified properties of the LGU which will contain vital information suggested on Sections 5.6 and 5.7 of COA Circular 2020-006.

1.7 We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor
direct:
1. the Acting Municipal Treasurer to:
a. require its Collectors to prepare their RCDs; and
b. turn-over their collections to the designated LO at end of each business
day; and
2. the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries.

2.6 We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor
direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting journal
entries.

3.11 We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor
direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries.

4.7 We recommended that the Municipal Peace and Order Council be judicious
in the preparation and review of the Peace and Order Programs of the
Municipality.

5.5 We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor, as
the designated SDO, to:
1. maintain a Cash Disbursement Record (CDR); and
2. record therein daily the receipts and disbursements of the CF.

6.8 We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor
stop the practice of granting additional cash advances to Accountable Officers
without settling first their previous cash advances.

7.4 We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor
direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries upon
settlement thereof.

8.8 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineer to:
1. submit an explanation for the delayed implementation of the said projects;
and
2. submit a recommendation to him, endorsing the imposition of liquidated
damages on the delayed implementation of the said projects, if warranted.

9.8 We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor
stop the charging of prohibited expenditures against the 20% Development
Fund.

10.5 We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor
direct the Municipal Accountant to require the contractors concerned to post
the subject warranty securities.

11.11 We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor
stop the charging of ineligible expenditures against the 5% LDRRMF.

12.8 We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor
direct:
1. the Municipal Accountant to deduct and withhold taxes upon accruals
made at year-end; and
2. the Acting Municipal Treasurer to remit the same within the prescribed
timeline.

13.8 We recommended, and the Management agreed, that the Municipal Mayor
require the requisitioning officer to completely and properly accomplish the
accounting forms.

We recommended that the City Accountant review the charges to the Accounts Payable, listed above, and derecognize those that are not valid claims to ensure that only those valid financial liabilities are recognized as Accounts Payable.
We recommended that the City Accountant comply with the pro-forma journal entries relevant to the transfer of unutilized DRRM funds.

We recommended that the City Accountant immediately transfer the CY 2023 unutilized DRRM-MOOE funds to the Special Trust Fund and comply with the suggested accounting entries for the transfer of unspent funds.

We recommended that the City Accountant reconcile the accounting records with the City General Services Office as the basis for preparing the Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the Drugs and Medicines, Welfare Goods and Agricultural and Marine Supplies for Distribution accounts amounting to ₱11,043,100.83, ₱1,028,578.00, and ₱1,589,100.00, respectively.

We recommended that the City Accountant validate the debit memos reflected in bank statements as the basis for preparing appropriate journal entry vouchers to record the reconciling items to ensure a fair presentation of the Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account.

We recommended that the City Accountant:
Monitor the status of all ongoing projects recorded under the Construction in Progress accounts;
Prepare a Journal Entry Voucher reclassifying the completed projects under the Construction in Progress accounts to the appropriate Property, Plant, and Equipment Account; and
Compute the required depreciation of the completed projects and accordingly adjust the balances of the affected PPE accounts.
We recommended that the City Accountant prepare adjusting entries to rectify the misclassifications of financial liabilities.

We recommended that the City Accountant, in coordination with the City Engineer, submit the As-built plan of the completed infrastructure projects and ensure the complete documentation of future final payments for infrastructure projects.

We recommended that:
The SP Secretary inform in writing the CDRRM Council through the CDRRM Officer of the status of any submitted plan, whether the same was approved or not by the Sanggunian;
The CDRRM Officer secure a copy of the SP Resolution approving the Revised AIP before requesting any charging to the unexpended DRRM funds; and
The City Accountant ensure that disbursements charged to unspent DRRM funds are supported with an approved revised AIP.

We recommended that the City Engineer establish a system that would ensure that the appropriate project signboards are properly installed throughout the project implementation to ensure public awareness of projects implemented by the City Government.

We recommended that the City Treasurer:
Review the Memorandum of Agreement covering any future receipt of funds from other agencies to ensure that all obligations and commitments are complied with; and
Open a special bank account intended only for the receipt of PCSO’s semi-annul lotto and monthly STL charity fund shares in compliance with the MOA.

We recommended that the City Accountant and the City Treasurer:
Issue demand letters to notify the concerned Officials and Employees of their responsibility to settle their unliquidated cash advances. If the settlement was not received, considering that they have been notified, withhold from their salaries the whole amount of the cash advance; and
Strictly monitor the submission of liquidation reports to ensure timely accounting thereof and to avoid an undue accumulation of outstanding cash advances.

We recommended that the:
Inventory Committee be updated on the prescribed format of the RPCPPE to conform with the guidelines set; and
City General Services Office fast track the implementation of a uniform property identification system for all its PPE and ensure observance of tagging of property stickers.

We recommended that the City Accountant coordinate with the City Engineer to require the concerned contractors to provide warranty securities for structural defects as required in Section 62.2.3.3, effective one year from the end of the defects liability period, to ensure sufficient financial coverage for potential structural defects during the warranty period.

We recommended that
Management:
a. Direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer, with the assistance of the Municipal Accountant, to analyze and reconcile their records and reports to determine the cause of discrepancy amounting to ₱1,681,302.88 to reflect a reliable and accurate RPT Receivable and SET Receivable accounts; and
b. Require the Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the Acting Municipal Treasurer, to analyze the discrepancy of ₱369,128.90 between the RPT Receivable and SET Receivable accounts and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry to reconcile the accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to submit a list of unreleased checks at year-end to the Accounting Office for the preparation of the JEV by the Municipal Accountant to restore cash and recognize liability in the appropriate cash and liability accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management instructed the Acting Municipal Treasurer to submit list of unreleased checks at year-end to the Accounting Office. observations.reason: Unreleased checks were recognized. Cash equivalent and the appropriate liability had been properly adjusted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to adjust the balances of MOOE and CO in the Current Appropriation of the Final Budget by ₱20,684,991.70 consistent with the Supplemental Budgets to present fairly the balance of appropriations in the financial statements;
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to analyze the transactions affecting the balance of Continuing Appropriation and to make the necessary adjustments on the discrepancy between the ending balance in CY 2021 and forwarded balance in CY 2022 in the amount of ₱8,433,517.27; and
c. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to disclose the details of reconciling items between the actual amounts on budget basis and the amounts recognized in the Statement of Financial Performance totaling ₱43,749,746.63 in the NFS to provide reliable information to the users of the financial statements and enable the Municipality to identify the major source of difference and improve its performance in the discharge of its accountability obligations.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed with the audit observation and submitted a revised SCBAA for reference of the Audit Team. The Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjustments in the balances of MOOE and CO. The Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjustments on the discrepancy between the ending balance in CY 2021 and forwarded balance in CY 2022. Reconciling items were disclosed in the NTFS.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant made the necessary adjustments. Verification of SCBAA for the year ended December 31, 2023 disclosed no error in the presentation of supplemental appropriations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly impose the withholding of retention money or submission of irrevocable standby letters of credit from a commercial bank, bank guarantees or surety bonds callable on demand, of amounts equivalent to the retention money substituted for and acceptable to Government to cover for manufacturing defects of delivered goods and uncorrected discovered defects and third party liabilities that may arise before final acceptance of infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: Management imposed the withholding of retention money in every progress billing.
observations.reason: Verification of payments made in CY 2023 disclosed that retention money/Special Bank Guarantee for every progress payment for the procurement of goods was enforced.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with MHO and Human Resource Management Officer, to submit the duly signed OCA Report together with the complete required certifications, approved DTRs and ARs of JOWs to support the grant of OCA for further evaluation of the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The submitted duly signed OCA Report together with the required documents for support of the grant of OCA and the said documents were complete.
observations.reason: The Audit Team verified the mandatory certifications, support of the grant of OCA and the said documents were complete.

We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Treasurer, with the assistance of the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Accountant, to cause the immediate retrieval of all necessary documents of ownership of the unregistered parcels of land and to facilitate the transfer and registration of ownership with the LRA to establish absolute ownership and protect the interest of the LGU;
b. Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds which shall cover the expenses on the transfer and registration of parcels of land; and
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the cost of the two parcels of land duly supported with certificate of title in the books of accounts and to gather the necessary documents of the remaining unrecorded parcels of land as basis in recording its costs in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Direct the BAC to require the suppliers to submit the mandatory documents in the use of alternative method of SVP and to indicate in the RFQ at what stage of the procurement process the supplier will submit the documentary requirements prior to the issuance of NOA;
b. Require the BAC Secretariat to ensure that the required PHILGEPS posting of procurement opportunities in the agency, and the NOA, NTP and contracts be strictly observed; and
c. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents for further evaluation of the Audit Team and to see to it that all of the documentary requirements are complete to ensure transparency and propriety of the procurement transactions.
observations.managementAction: Management required the BAC to require the suppliers to submit the mandatory documents in the use of alternative mode of SVP and to indicate in the RFQ at what stage of the procurement process the supplier will submit the documentary requirements prior to the issuance of NOA. The BAC ensures that the activities are posted in conspicuous places, PhilGEPS website and agency website. Management submitted the lacking documents.
observations.reason: Proof of postings at PhilGEPS website were attached to the DV. The required documents were submitted for further evaluation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the Municipal Treasurer, to require the suppliers to provide the SI or ORs for the abovementioned goods procured by the LGU; and
b. Require the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that receipt issued by the suppliers/contractors upon payment is in accordance with BIR Revenue Memorandum Circular.
observations.managementAction: The Acting Municipal Treasurer was instructed to coordinate with suppliers to provide the SI or OR. Payments in CY 2022 were provided with SI or OR.
observations.reason: Verification showed that payments in CY 2022 were provided with SI or OR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Require the BAC, with the assistance of the BAC Secretariat, to post all NOA, contract, or Purchase Order in the website of the LGU or any conspicuous place in the premises of the PE and GPPB online portal for Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act for information purposes and to require the submission of the mandatory documentary requirements from the suppliers, distributors, manufacturers or contractors concerned;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the MSWDO and MHO, to submit the list of affected families and proof of receipt of distribution of relief goods, medicines and vitamins and use of testing kits and other supporting documents; and
c. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to require the supplier to provide the Sales Invoices for the payments made.
observations.managementAction: The BAC ensures that all procurement activities are posted in conspicuous places, PhilGEPS website and agency website. The MBO reviewed carefully and determined the conformity of the PPAs with the provisions of the DBM Circular before certifying the availability of allotment. The BAC ensures the inclusion of warranty clause in contracts. Management submitted the lacking documents.
observations.reason: The BAC ensures that all procurement activities are posted in conspicuous places, PhilGEPS website and agency website. The MBO reviewed and certified the availability of allotment. Some required documents such as distribution lists were not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.managementAction: The SB Resolution No. 103-2021 authorizing the LCE for the implementation of the PPAs was submitted to the Audit Team. The BAC ensures the inclusion of warranty clause in contracts. The BAC ensures that all procurement activities are posted in conspicuous places, PhilGEPS website and agency website. The MBO reviewed carefully and determined the conformity of the PPAs with the provisions of the DBM Circular. Management submitted some of the lacking documents but list of affected families and proof of receipt of distribution of rice, medicines and other relief goods remained unsubmitted.
observations.reason: The authorization for implementation of the PPAs was submitted. BAC included warranty clause and posted procurement activities. However, list of affected families and proof of receipt of distribution were not yet submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.managementAction: The BAC required the TWG to be diligent in checking the accuracy of the bidding documents. Management ensures the completeness of the offer of bidders.
