observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant no longer effected the proposed adjusting journal entries. The PPEs were paid under check DV Nos. 100-23-02-0149 dated February 10, 2023 and 100-23-02-0159 dated February 14, 2023 resulting in correct balances of affected PPE accounts.
The Municipal Engineer regularly coordinates with the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer agreed to properly record construction of ongoing and completed infrastructure assets.
observations.reason: The PPEs were paid under check DV Nos. 100-23-02-0149 dated February 10, 2023 and 100-23-02-0159 dated February 14, 2023 resulting in correct balances of affected PPE accounts. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer agreed to properly record construction of ongoing and completed infrastructure assets.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Effect the following adjusting journal entries to correct the error made in recording the intangible asset:
Account Debit Credit
Intangible Asset - 1,012,500.00
Computer Software
Accumulated 961,875.00
Depreciation – ICT
Equipment
ICT 1,012,500.00
Equipment
Prior Period 961,875.00
Adjustment
b. Review every accounting period for any events and circumstances affecting the useful life of the computer software, any changes thereof will be reflected in the financial statement prospectively.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant effected the proposed adjusting journal entries. The Municipal Accountant effected the JEV No. 100-23-05-0021 dated 5/29/2023 was drawn to correct the errors made.
The Municipal Accountant regularly reviews the useful life of intangible assets.
observations.reason: effected the JEV No. 100-23-05-0021 dated 5/29/2023 was drawn to correct the errors made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to remove from the PPE account the items with a unit cost of below ₱15,000.00 and record the same as debit to Prior Period Adjustment account under Special Education Fund and credit to PPE account. We, likewise, recommended that the Management create a disposal committee to facilitate the disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with the disposal procedures to avoid further deterioration and loss of value and generate additional funds from the sale thereof.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant effected the proposed adjusting entries. Verified JEV Nos. 100-23-12-0049 to 0055 under General Fund and JEV Nos. 200-23-12-0014 to 0017 under Special Education Fund and found in order.
Management is in the process of creating new disposal team. Because some of the members of the disposal committee had already retired, a new set of members is necessary for the disposal of unserviceable properties.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant effected the proposed adjusting entries. Verified JEV Nos. 100-23-12-0049 to 0055 under General Fund and JEV Nos. 200-23-12-0014 to 0017 under Special Education Fund and found in order. Management is in the process of creating new disposal team because some members retired.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the 15 percent mobilization fees to contractors as Advances to Contractors and credit the same for recoupments to properly recognize the assets in its books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant agreed to observe the proper recording of the 15 percent mobilization fees. Payment of the 15 percent mobilization fees was properly recorded in the books.
observations.reason: Payment of the 15 percent mobilization fees was properly recorded in the books.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the BAC Chairman and BAC Secretariat, to submit to the Audit Team all lacking documents relative to the payments of BAC honoraria to establish the validity of claims and ensure that all claims for honoraria are supported with the required documentary requirements; and
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to record the collections from the sale of bid documents as Trust Liabilities in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the lacking documents. The Municipal Accountant started to record the collections from the sale of bid documents. The lacking documents were submitted on August 17, 2023 and found in order. The Municipal Accountant started to record the collections from the sale of bidding documents as Trust Liabilities in the books of accounts in June 2023.
observations.reason: Lacking documents were submitted and found in order; collections from sale of bidding documents were recorded as Trust Liabilities starting June 2023.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to:
a. Stop the practice of paying expenses through reimbursements, instead draw checks payable directly to the suppliers; and
b. Pay petty operating expenses through the PCF or through cash advance of the disbursing officer.
observations.managementAction: The Acting Municipal Treasurer agreed to closely coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to ensure that payments will be made directly to the suppliers. The Acting Municipal Treasurer agreed that PCF will be used for petty operating expenses. There were still payments made through reimbursements, instead of drawing checks payable directly to suppliers. Regular expenses were still paid through PCF.
observations.reason: Acting Municipal Treasurer agreed to coordinate and use PCF for petty expenses; however, some transactions were still paid through reimbursements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, through the MSWD Officer to create a policy or guideline as basis of the procedure in granting the financial assistance. We, likewise, recommended that the LCE implement the approved PPAs of the LGU while the SB members shall be limited to their legislative functions.
observations.managementAction: An order providing for policies and procedure in granting the financial assistance was created under Executive Order (EO) No. 27, series of 2023 dated June 29, 2023. The Management agreed that the clients who seek financial assistance will no longer give transportation assistance and the MSWDO will assess the clients before granting assistance following EO No. 27, series of 2023 which is still subject for implementation.
observations.reason: EO No. 27, series of 2023 was created as basis of LGU procedure in granting financial assistance; MSWDO to assess clients before granting assistance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Require the designated drivers to properly and fully accomplish the DTT before and after using a government vehicle; and
b. Require the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Executive Assistant, to prepare Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels and submit the same to the Audit Team for verification to ensure that expenditures for fuel consumption are reasonable and effectively controlled.
observations.managementAction: Management instructed the drivers to properly and fully accomplish DTT before and after using government vehicles. Some DTT were still not properly accomplished as verified with the documents submitted to the Accounting Office in CY 2023. The Acting Treasurer, in coordination with the Executive Assistant, agreed to prepare Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Official Travels and created a monitoring file on fuel consumption of all vehicles and submitted to COA on December 21, 2023. However, Official Travels were not attached.
observations.reason: Drivers instructed to properly accomplish DTT; monitoring file on fuel consumption created and submitted to COA, but Official Travels were not attached.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the municipal officials and employees travelling without provision for government vehicle to claim transportation expenses equivalent to fare for ordinary public conveyance.
observations.managementAction: Management agreed to require transportation expenses equivalent to fare for ordinary public conveyance. Some employees and officials still claim transportation expenses in excess of fare for ordinary public conveyance in CY 2023.
observations.reason: Management consented to require claims equivalent to ordinary public conveyance, but some employees still claimed excess amounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management stop the practice of serving meals and snacks during SB sessions and other municipal activities which are part of the regular functions, duties and responsibilities of the officials and employees.
observations.managementAction: The Management consented to stop serving meals and snacks in the performance of the regular function, duties and responsibilities of the officials and employees. There were times when meals and snacks were served during regular and special sessions of SB.
observations.reason: Management consented to stop the practice, though there were instances when meals and snacks were still served.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain a record of repairs for all the properties of the LGU which shall form part of the supporting documents for disbursements on repair and maintenance; and
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the supporting documents before affixing her signature on the Disbursement Voucher and effecting payment.
observations.managementAction: The Acting Municipal Treasurer agreed to closely coordinate with the Office of the Mayor to properly maintain the record. The Municipal Treasurer started to create a monitoring file on repairs and maintenance of the agency’s vehicles and a copy will be furnished to the Audit Team. The Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure the completeness of the supporting documents before affixing her signature on the Disbursement Voucher and effecting payment.
observations.reason: Acting Municipal Treasurer to maintain repair records and Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness before payment; monitoring file started.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the HRMO, to submit the lacking documents listed above to support the accuracy and propriety of claims; and
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of supporting documents on claims for terminal leave benefits before affixing the signature in the DV and effecting payments thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the HRMO, submitted some of the lacking documents to the Audit Team. The Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the HRMO, consented to ensure the completeness of documents on claims for terminal leave benefits before signing.
observations.reason: Some lacking documents were submitted on July 3, 2023; Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure completeness before signing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the GAD Focal Person to formulate PPAs in the GAD Plan and Budget that will fully address the issues and concerns in order to achieve gender equality and women empowerment.
observations.managementAction: Management instructed the GAD Focal Person to allocate the GAD funds for PPAs and monitor the maximization of the GAD fund appropriated in the GAD Plan and Budget.
observations.reason: Management instructed GAD Focal Person to monitor implementation and maximize utilization of GAD funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to require the only accept sales invoice or official receipt as proof of payment.
observations.managementAction: The Acting Municipal Treasurer will only accept sales invoice or official receipt as proof of payment. There were still suppliers/contractors not issuing sales invoice and/or official receipt on various procurement of goods and services made by the LGU.
observations.reason: Acting Municipal Treasurer will only accept sales invoice/official receipt; however, some suppliers still did not issue them.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to charge to the appropriate budgetary account by considering the actual nature of the transaction. Likewise, we recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that transactions recorded are based on the actual nature of the transaction and not on the account shown in the obligation request to present the correct balances of accounts presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer agreed to charge appropriate expenses to appropriate accounts. Post audit of appropriate accounts showed there were transactions that were not charged according to budgetary accounts and recorded based on the nature of transactions. The Municipal Accountant agreed to record transactions based on the actual nature of the transaction.
observations.reason: Post audit showed some transactions were still not properly charged but Management agreed to charge based on actual nature.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Assessor and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to exhaust all possible means for the speedy titling of lands and acquire Certificate of Title in order to protect the interest of the municipality from the adverse claims by third parties.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor agreed to work continuously to secure Certificates of Title for lots acquired by the municipality. The Municipal Assessor was able to acquire two lot certificates of title in CY 2022. Due to budget limitations, no lot title was secured in CY 2023.
observations.reason: The Municipal Assessor was able to acquire two lot certificates of title in CY 2022; no additional titles in CY 2023 due to budget limitations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Person to monitor the implementation of PPAs and to maximize the utilization of GAD fund appropriated in the GAD Plan and Budget.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person acceded to monitor the implementation and maximization of the GAD fund appropriated in the GAD Plan and Budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management create an Inspectorate Team or the Project Monitoring Committee (PMC) who will undertake preliminary inspections on all infrastructure projects implemented by the LGU to ensure that the progress of the work is properly monitored and any deficiencies will be addressed in a timely manner.
observations.managementAction: A PMC was created under EO No. 38 dated August 22, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management fill up the mandatory position for the three staff of the MDRRMO through local ordinance to handle the administration and training, research and planning, and operations and warning, to provide adequate support to DRRM activities.
observations.managementAction: As of CY 2023, only two permanent positions were filled up under the LDRRMO.
observations.reason: Only two positions filled as of CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and Human Resource Officer to ensure that monthly net take home pay of municipal employees shall not be reduced to an amount lower than ₱5,000.00.
observations.managementAction: The Human Resource Officer and the Acting Municipal Treasurer exerted effort to monitor the net take home pay prior to approval of loans and no longer certify the loan application of employees if after deducting the loan amortization, the employee’s net take home pay will be lower than ₱5,000.00.
observations.reason: There were still employees receiving net take home pay lower than ₱5,000.00 in CY 2023 despite efforts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the BAC Secretariat to submit copies of perfected contracts and supporting documents to the Audit Team within the prescribed period of five days for a systematic and effective review process to come up with timely and relevant audit results.
observations.managementAction: Management makes effort to submit copies of perfected contract on time to adhere to this observation.
observations.reason: Reiterated under Observation No. 6, page 39 of this report; some contracts and supporting documents still not submitted within prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to continue and intensify their collection strategies on taxes due and collectible by sending Real Property Tax Order Payment (RPTOP) and notice of delinquency to taxpayers and forming committee to conduct an information dissemination regarding the importance of paying taxes promptly and the consequences of non-payment of taxes.
observations.managementAction: Management started to coordinate with barangay captains since 2022 to help in the distribution of the Notice of Delinquency.
observations.reason: Acting Municipal Treasurer coordinated with barangay captains to distribute Notice of Delinquency; delinquent accounts remain.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the remaining five barangays to maintain a MRF which will help the LGU to convert wastes into product that can be useful to the environment.
observations.managementAction: From the two barangays with no operational MRFs as of CY 2022, one was already provided product in CY 2023. Thus, only one barangay has no operational MRF because Management is still searching for a proper location.
observations.reason: One barangay still has no operational MRF as Management searches for proper location.

observations.recommendation: We likewise recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
observations.managementAction: A Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network was already provided to the Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjusting entries to report the account Cash Local Treasury in its normal balances. Henceforth, ensure that transactions are recognized in the books using the appropriate accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2022 The Local Chief Observation No. 2 made necessary adjusting entries on Cash Local Treasury Account on March 2023. Implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant to (i) reconcile the balances RPT and SET Receivables as at year-end to support the integrity of the reported accounts; and (ii) prepare the necessary adjusting entries, if warranted; and (b) Municipal Treasurer to (i) introduce collection strategy that would significantly help increase the RPT/SET collection of the Municipality and institute remedies, administrative and/or judicial action, for the collection of over-due taxes as provided in Section 256 of the Local Government Code of 1991; and (ii) timely prepare an updated Certified List of Taxpayers, bearing the names, particulars of the property, location, amount due and collectible for the applicable year and other pertinent information and submit the same to the Accounting Unit as the basis in setting up of RPT/SET tax receivable in the books of accounts of the Municipality at the beginning of the year.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2022 The Municipal Accountant already reconciled the RPT/SET Receivables as of December 31, 2023. While RPT collection or CY 2023 increased by 1.3 million.
observations.reason: The preparation of Certified List of Taxpayers is still not updated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC) headed by the Local Chief Executive to instruct the (a) Municipal Budget to observe strictly the rules on utilization of LDRRMF as stated in DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and ensure that only eligible programs/projects/activities are to be financed by the LDRRMF; (b) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the transfer of the unutilized amount of ₱14,894.36 from the General Fund account to Trust Fund account that is still under the five-year threshold period; and (c) Municipal Accountant to (i) effect the adjustment of error made in recording the transfer of unspent LDRRMF; and (ii) ensure that the rules and regulation in utilizing unspent previous years’ LDRRMF are properly and strictly followed.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2022 The PPAs identified were eligible charged against LDDRMF. Likewise adjustments were already effected in the books. The Municipal Accountant already made adjustment on error in recording the transfer of unspent LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Budget Officer to require all departments/offices of submitting their respective Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) for the budget year necessary in the implementation of different projects/programs/activities; and (b) Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to (i) stop the practice of acquiring goods on a staggered basis through shopping/small value procurement and adhere to the provisions of Section 10, Article IV of Republic Act No. 9184, which requires that all procurement shall be done thru competitive public bidding to ensure that procurement of goods and services are obtained at prices which are most advantageous to the government; and (ii) limit the use of alternative mode of procurement, or if adopted, the provisions of Section 52, Rule XVI of RA No. 9184 should be complied with.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2022 The Local Chief Executive instructed the concerned officials to comply with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason: Procurement through public bidding is still not observed in some of the transactions. Lack of adherence to limit use of alternative mode of procurement.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Local Chief Executive (i) reconstitute the membership of GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) including its ExeCOM and TWG relevant for gender mainstreaming; and (ii) instruct the newly reconstituted/created GFPS to (a) create a centralized GAD database that would consolidate the information gathered and compiled by all offices to include gender statistics and other relevant sex-disaggregated data, as basis in GAD planning, programming and policy making; (b) employ the Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) in the attribution of costs of regular program/project to GAD; and (c) comply on the timely submission to the Audit Team of the GAD Accomplishment Report.
observations.managementAction: AAR CY 2022 The updated GAD Focal Point System was already created per Executive Order No. 31 s. 2023.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower resources hindered the completion of GAD Database. Lack of awareness with the HGDG tool. Some items not yet complied.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Engineer to closely monitor the installation of signboards/tarpaulins detailing the complete and relevant information of Programs Projects Activities (PPAs) implemented by the Municipality and document the compliance of the contractor for the said work item.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee to submit regularly the advance copies of Contracts and Purchase Orders together with supporting documents to the Auditor within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Dev’t. Officer informed the Audit Team that the submission of copies of contracts and other supporting documents for procurement of goods and infrastructure projects through public bidding is now being observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to (i) strictly adhere with the posting of all required reports within the required period to designated places and platform; and (ii) coordinate with the Department Heads of concerned offices for the prompt and timely submission of the reports to avoid delays on posting disclosure of information.
observations.managementAction: Implemented

observations.recommendation: We commended the Management for its continuous efforts in complying with the COVID – 19 vaccination program. We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Health Office to continue spearheading the LGU’s vaccination activities until the local herd immunity is attained.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is committed to continue its efforts to comply with the COVID-19 vaccination guidelines.

observations.recommendation: We commended the management for its efforts in implementing various Programs/Projects/Activities as responses and counter-measures to mitigate the impact of COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is committed to continue its effort to provide services for those affected by COVID-19.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations and made additional recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:

require the Acting-Municipal Accountant to (i) ensure that all purchases of supplies and materials are recorded under the proper inventory account; and (ii) prepare supplies ledger cards for delivered items as called for in the above-quoted regulation;

 inform the BAC, Municipal Health Officer and Acting-Municipal Accountant to examine noted deviations in Item 6.19 and 6.30, for future similar transactions, adhere closely with the requirements of the law, preceding to payment thereof.

instruct the GSO to (i) conduct periodic inventory of supplies as prescribed by the above regulation; and (ii) make an effort to procure directly from the PS-DBM all anticipated common-use supplies and resort to local suppliers only upon unavailability of supplies needed; and 

direct the concerned officials to submit the lacking documents for further evaluation of the Audit Team.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Local Chief Executive:

submit the lacking documentations Memorandum for further evaluation of the Audit Team;
 
request the Sangguniang Bayan to prepare a clear-cut guideline or adopt the policies and procedures aligned with the DSWD Memorandum Circular; and 

request the Acting-Municipal Accountant to (a) require a certification, certifying “unavailability of funding” on concerned agencies for grants intended to Barangays, SK and schools prior to payment; and (b) continue checking the completeness of the required supporting documents for every transaction before any payment is made.

We reiterated previous years audit recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:

direct the LDRRMO (i) together with the LDRRMC, to prepare and submit annually the LDRRMFIP in the required format in reference to COA Circular 2012-002, inclusive of the plan relative to the unexpended balance of the fund for the current year and the trust fund for DRRM; and (ii) regularly monitor implementation thereof to avoid non-attainment of the target objectives of the fund; and

instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from charging disbursements which were inconsistent with allotment and/or do not have direct impact on disaster risk reduction and management and focus on the strict observance of correct charging of the allotments in congruence with the approved LDRRMFIP, AIP and the Appropriation Ordinance.

We reiterated and supplemented our previous year’s recommendations that the Local Chief Executive:

consider the immediate purchase of the CBMS to support a reliable baseline data for the establishment of PPAs for a more gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation;
 
direct the GFPP to strictly/closely monitor the implementation of the programs and projects embodied in the approved GPB by the DILG to ensure full attainment of the GAD targets/objectives, intervene immediately, if delays in the implementation were observed, amend the GPB, if warranted; and

instruct the Municipal Accountant to adhere with the requirements on the preparation of Subsidiary Ledgers in consonance with COA Circular 2021-008 dated September 6, 2021.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Local Chief Executive:

instruct the HRMO to:

continue looking for possible candidates from the array of job order employees for permanency based on available vacant positions for recommendation to the Personnel Selection Board (PSB) of the Municipality to reduce the number of job orders, taking into consideration the mandated PS limitation under the Local Government Code; 

persist with the evaluation as to the necessity of hired job orders in the LGU by objectively conducting work evaluation or devise a performance indicator system/mechanism which will identify the performing/non-performing personnel, along with immediate superiors of concerned JOs. In order to assess personnel who are value adding to the LGU and ultimately reduce the amount of expenses coupled therein; and

prepare and issue policy guidelines on hiring/recruitment.

direct the Municipal Budget Officer, to consider, if budget permits, the provision for an increase in pay of the existing JOs to be at par with comparable positions in the government; and

direct the Municipal Accountant to look into the noted deficiencies before allowing payments thereof.

We reiterated and supplemented our prior years’ audit recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: 

in close coordination with the Treasurers’ Office and Accounting Department, (i) ensure that the same amount withheld is remitted the following month; (ii) look into the breakdown of the variances from the withheld amount and remittances starting off with the current year and subsequently, the previous years and make necessary adjustments, if warranted; and (iii) coordinate with PhilHealth on the noted unsettled balance of the LGU and support proof of payments to eliminate the outstanding balances, if actually paid; 

as part of the internal control system, designate/appoint a person who will check all deductions and remittances made to ensure all are accounted for; and 

instruct the Acting Municipal Accountant to continue and ultimately complete and maintain the Index of Payments for proper monitoring of claims and for prompt and accurate recording and remittances to GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Acting-Municipal Accountant to:
continue with the efforts made on withholding and remittances of taxes in accordance with the aforequoted regulations;

the look into the cause of the variances between the withheld amounts and remittances made and make the adjustments/amendments to BIR returns, if necessary, to reflect the actual deductions based on specific reference months; and

henceforth, ascertain that taxes all withheld are remitted intact to the BIR.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to:

1. record in the books of accounts the unrecorded PPEs acquired through donation, construction and purchase; and

2. adjust the book value of assets with discrepancies, if warranted. 

We also recommended that the LCE create an Inventory Committee to conduct inspection of the 52 properties reported on quarterly completed projects from CY 2012 to 2022 to ascertain the existence & status of the properties.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to:

1. Exert extra effort to locate the documents supporting the reconciling items; if necessary, make representation with the bank for the furnishing of necessary documents to facilitate tracing of discrepancies. 

2. Coordinate with the previous Municipal Treasurers and validate the existence of the checks issued by them if it was actually released to the payees.

3. Submit supporting documents to justify the unsubstantiated previous years’ reconciling items and immediately record the book reconciling items previously determined.

4. Comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

observations.managementAction: Management committed to send another demand letter to the family of deceased persons liable and those persons who was no longer in service.
observations.reason:
1. For the Cash Advances amounting to P 540,000.00, this is for compliance of documentary requirements by previous AMT.

2. For the Cash Advances amounting to P85,274.52, two of them were deceased and the rest were no longer in service.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the OIC-Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the Municipal Accountant, to trace the errors from the previous years’ records and prepare the necessary JEV to correct the errors. 

We also recommended that the LCE instruct the OIC-MA to ensure that all accounts are in their normal balance to have a reliable and fair presentation of the agency’s financial statements

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary JEV to correct the errors made by debiting the account “Cash in Bank” and crediting “Due to NGAs in the amount of ₱ 1,083,600.00.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to adjust the above erroneous recordings.

We also recommended to review the recorded payable in CY 2021 and inform the creditors of their outstanding claim
observations.reason: The Due to BIR account is still subject for reconciliation

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management shall exert extra effort to collect the receivables, otherwise a request for Authority to write-off will be secured from COA when it becomes dormant for ten (10) years or more. Meantime, the LGU instruct the MA to restore the entry made until the request will be granted by the COA.
observations.managementAction: The original entry was restored as reflected in the 2023 Financial Statements. Please refer to GJ-June 2023# 1 and 2 adjustments.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the AMT to:

1. require the COs to remit to the AMT all collections daily; and

2. monitor strictly the remittances of collections made by COs and ensure that the full amount collected are remitted intact.

We recommended that AMT provide additional column headings to the permanent record book for the unissued AFs for proper monitoring and complete record keeping in consonance with section 86 (D.1) of the LTO Manual.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the new AMT to:

1. enforce strictly the preparation of monthly RAAFs of all Collecting Officers; and

prepare the Consolidated RAAF as prescribed in Section 86 of the LTO Manual Book II.

observations.reason:
An AOM was issued, which is discussed in Observation No. 9 in Part II.  















An AOM was issued, which is discussed in Observation No. 10 in Part II.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require:

A. the MHO to render a report to the Municipal Accountant on the receipt, issuance, and balances of vaccines directly received from outside sources; and 

B. the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the MHO, to determine the full cost of ending inventory of vaccines as at year end, if any and henceforth recognize and record future receipts and issuances of the same.

We recommended that Management comply strictly with the requirements under COA Circular No. 2012-001 and COA Circular No. 94-013 as the basis for the utilization of fund transfers received.

Further, furnish the AT with the MOA of the six PPAs for proper evaluation of the implementation and timely verification of the veracity and completeness of the disbursements made in relation to these trust accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the:
 
Municipal Treasurer (MT), in coordination with the Municipal Assessor, to prepare and furnish the Municipal Accountant (MA) at the beginning of the year a duly certified list of tax payers showing the amount due and collectible for the year to be used by the latter as the basis in setting up the RPT and SET Receivable in accordance with Sections 20 and 85 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1.

MA to coordinate with the MT for the timely submission of the certified list of taxpayers and eventual recognition in the books at the beginning of the calendar year of the Receivables and Deferred Income; and


3. MA to record the RPT and SET Receivable using the prescribed entry:
Real Property Tax Receivable	xxx	
Deferred Real Property Tax Income		xxx			
Special Education Tax Receivable	xxx	
Deferred Special Education Tax Income		xxx

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the following Department Heads:

A. The Municipal Accountant to:

Ensure the completeness of documentary requirements for cash advance and liquidation and DTRs/Accomplishment Reports were duly approved by concerned department heads as prescribed by COA Circulars 2012-001 and 2006-002; 

Prepare certifications that previous cash advances have been liquidated; and

Demand the cash return of any unclaimed/unsigned wages during liquidation.

B. Acting Municipal Treasurer to:

Refund any unclaimed wages or ensure that the payroll was duly signed/acknowledged by the payees; and

Submit a copy of the duly acknowledged payroll sheet.
 
C. Supervising Administrative Officer to:

observations.recommendation:
We commended Managements efforts for substantially reducing the outstanding balances of CAs at year end.  However, we reiterated our previous recommendation that Management continue to strengthen internal control in granting CAs, while concerned AOs liquidate their CAs strictly in accordance with Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002.   Specifically, we recommended that the MA:

Monitor strictly the settlement of unliquidated balance of ₱85,274; and

Send final demand letters to concerned personnel requiring immediate liquidation/ settlement of their unliquidated CAs.

We further recommended that the LCE request formally for authority to write-off dormant unliquidated CAs of already deceased personnel and submit the request to COA Audit Team Leader and/or Supervising Auditor.  In this connection, we recommended that the LCE direct the MA to prepare an aging of unliquidated CAs to support the request for write-off, indicating in the remarks column the existence of the applicable conditions as in indicated on Section 7.4 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

Also, we suggested that the LCE write formally the relatives of the deceased municipal personnel requesting for certified duplicate copy of the Death Certificate.

We recommend that the LCE direct the AMT to submit immediately to the MAO all paid disbursement vouchers, payrolls, and official receipts.  Subsequently, require the MA to submit on time the recorded accounts including interim FS together with the status of appropriation, allotment and obligations, journals and other reports in conformity to the above-mentioned provisions.

Prepare and submit regularly a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies to the Sanggunian for the body to assist in the enforcement of the remedies for collection including enforcing levy on real property as imposed by Sections 256 and 258 of RA 7160.

We recommended that the LCE should require the MT to deposit intact the full amount she received or collected to the authorized depository bank and refrain from retaining part of her collections in her custody.
We recommended that the MA exercise diligence in discharging his duties and functions and be more cautious in recording transactions to avoid misrepresentation of the accounts in the FS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary adjusting entries to transfer all completed projects to its appropriate asset accounts, and prospectively apply the Section 50, 4th paragraph of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I, and Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009; and

Oblige the Office of the Municipal Engineer and the Office of the Municipal Accountant to establish a reporting mechanism on the status of project implementation in order to have a basis in timely recognition of asset to their appropriate accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to update the real property tax account registers/taxpayer’s index cards and submit the certified list of taxpayers to the Municipal Accountant in compliance with Section 20, Volume I of the NGAS Manual; and

Direct the Municipal Accountant to take up and adjust in the books the RPT/SET receivables based on the certified list, with a corresponding subsidiary ledger that will monitor the collections and delinquencies in order to present a more reliable balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to effect the reversion of the payables which have been outstanding for more than two years and without existing valid claims in compliance with Section 98 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Direct all concerned department heads to submit the required financial reports and other supporting documents within the deadline set by the existing rules and regulations;

Determine the cause on delayed preparation of financial reports and the non-submission of financial statements and take necessary measures that will enable the agency to comply with the accountability reporting deadlines; and 

Subject to the availability of funds and the personal services limitations stipulated in Republic Act No. 7160, the Municipality hire additional personnel or designate an existing qualified employee as an additional workforce to the Accounting Office to expedite the processing of transaction documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Office spearhead the setting up and maintenance of the GAD database containing the sex-disaggregated data and gender statistics. Also, the GAD Focal Point System to provide technical assistance to the MPDO in ensuring the same to be fully operational, as basis in identifying the PPAs that properly address the gender issues and concerns pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer undertake the issuance and renewal of Property Acknowledgment Receipts every three years or whenever there is a change in custodianship in order to update the record of Property, Plant and Equipment and keep a copy on file.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive being the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council Chairman direct the LDRRMO to submit the LDRRMFIP based on the prescribed format, and ensure the timely submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended the following:

The OIC – Municipal Treasurer cancel the unclaimed stale checks and return the original Disbursement Vouchers to the Municipal Accountant in order to effect the necessary adjusting entries.  For the checks which became stale in the hand of payee, send written notice reminding them of the need to present their stale checks for cancellation before the replacement can be made.

The Municipal Accountant take up the necessary adjusting entries to record the stale checks and reflect the changes in the financial statements.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive:

Direct the Supply Officer and Municipal Accountant to maintain and update stock cards and inventory ledger cards, respectively, for each inventory item and corresponding issuances be covered by the RIS.  Likewise, the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) should be prepared as a basis in recognizing the expense portion of the inventory.

We recommended the following:

The Municipal Treasurer’s Office should inform the concerned suppliers and employees of the unclaimed payables, otherwise issue a certification on the status of long outstanding obligations to facilitate the reversion of funds.

The Municipal Accountant recognize only the duly approved payables with complete supporting documents and review the Accounts Payable and Due to Officers and Employees account for obligations that remain unclaimed for more than two years and revert to unappropriated surplus as required by Section 98 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that Management comply with the Show Cause Order of the PENRO and exert effort in seeing to it that the infrastructures already constructed will not be put to waste. Prospectively, all projects of the Municipality be implemented with consideration to existing environmental laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer and the OIC – Municipal Accountant agreed to reconcile their records at least every end of each quarter and any items for adjustment must be corrected and resolved promptly in order to present reliable Cash – Local Treasury and Cash in Bank balances.
4.3 Henceforth, cash advances should be liquidated within the prescribed period and closed at year-end, pursuant to Item 5.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.

6.1 We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to release the share of real property tax and other taxes due the barangays as mandated by the Local Government Code in order to augment the barangays’ meager income and to finance their developmental programs and projects for the benefit of their constituents.
6.2 We also recommended and the OIC – Municipal Accountant agreed to reconcile her records with the Municipal Treasurer on unremitted barangay RPT shares and draw appropriate adjusting entries to consider discounts and interests on RPT.

7.1 We recommended and the OIC-Municipal Accountant agreed to charge the honoraria to the proper sources of funds as provided in the DBM Circular and record the payment as against Trust Liabilities in the subsequent payments.
7.2 Moreover, we also recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that withholding taxes are deducted from additional compensation in the form of honorarium in accordance with BIR Revenue Regulation on Revised Withholding Taxes.
7.3 Finally, considering that the honoraria was paid in excess of what is allowed under DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-5A, we recommended the immediate refund of the amount of ₱110,571.50.

We recommended and the OIC – Municipal Accountant agreed to record the receipt of vaccines as donations in kind and recognize the amount of unused vaccines as Inventory at year-end at their fair value which may be ascertained by reference to an active market or by appraisal as provided in Paragraph 97 of the IPSAS No. 23.

We recommended and the OIC – Municipal Accountant agreed to transfer the unexpended balance of the QRF and DRRMF-MOOE for CY 2018 and prior years from the General Fund to the Special Trust Fund for proper monitoring and reporting of the fund and also there is an assurance that the fund will be utilized only for its intended purpose.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer monitor the effectivity and expiration dates of the fidelity bonds of the Accountable Officers and that the renewal thereof shall be made before their expiration.

We recommended that Management, through the MSWDO, submit the lacking distribution lists to account for the differences between the goods procured and distributed.

We further recommended that distribution lists show complete details necessary to establish propriety, validity and can easily facilitate proper accounting of relief goods actually distributed.

We recommended that Management evaluate if there is a need to increase the Accountable Officers amount of bond to cover higher amounts of cash in their possession or lessen their cash accountabilities at any given time to adhere with the provisions of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009.
We recommended that the LGU submit justification in writing as to the non-inclusion of BHWs to the One COVID-19 Allowance.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (MA) prepare adjusting entries to correct the noted deficiency as follows:

Dr. Land (1-07-01-010)	80,000.00
	Cr. Prior Period Adjustment (3-01-01-020)   80,000.00

We recommended that Management justify the charging of expenses that are not allowed against the 20% Development Fund as listed in DBM, DOF and DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020. 

Moreover, we recommended that management strictly adhere to the Joint Memorandum Circulars and updates of DBM, DILG and DOF regarding the utilization of 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the submission of the Request for Relief of Accountability supported with complete documents required for evaluation by the COA to facilitate recording of appropriate entries and present fair balances of properties in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management submit a written justification/explanation on the delays in the implementation of the project leading to the reversion of the funds back to the Bureau of the Treasury together with appropriate documents to support such justification.

We recommended that the Accounting Department prepare adjusting entries of the affected accounts by debiting Due from LGUs and crediting Prior Period Adjustment. 

Also, we recommended immediate liquidation of the fund transferred if the purpose for which it has been given has been served.

We recommended that the agency prepare the Monthly Report for Fuel Consumption (MFRC) as prescribed by COA Circular No. 77-61 to effectively manage and assess the reasonableness of fuel, gasoline and oil expenditures and submit such report to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that Management justify the charging of expenses against the development fund that are not allowed as listed in DBM, DOF and DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020. 

Moreover, we recommended that Management strictly adhere to the Joint Memorandum Circulars and updates of DBM, DILG and DOF regarding the utilization of 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Accountant to monitor and control disbursement of the funds by not exceeding fifty (50%) of the uncollected estimated revenue in addition to the actual collections so that no cash overdraft in any local fund shall be incurred at the end of the year.

We recommended that Management closely monitor and ensure that the LDRRM planned mitigation/disaster preparedness program /projects/activities are fully implemented so that intended purpose is attained/achieved.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be required to make representation to the Bureau of Internal Revenue for the computation of taxes due them from the exercise of corporate power in the LGU’s Quinale Beach Bar.

Required the Municipal Accountant to send confirmation and/or demand letters to individuals concerned.

For receivables from officials and employees arising from overpayment of claims, the Municipal Treasurer shall institute deductions on their subsequent claims and salaries in satisfaction of their indebtedness.

We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant to:
(1) Prepare the necessary correcting entry to adjust the erroneous booking of PPE accounts totaling ₱134,363.36, net of Accumulated Depreciation, into its corresponding expenses and equity account; and
(2) Take into consideration the capitalization threshold of ₱50,000.00 for recognition as PPE as provided in Section 4 of the Circular.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require:
a. the Acting Municipal Treasurer to:
(3) regularly update the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR)/Taxpayers Index Card (TIC); and
(4) furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year based on the updated RPTAR/TIC.
b. the Municipal Accountant to:
(5) establish RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the year based on the duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as prepared by the Acting Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
(10) make the necessary adjustment to rectify the improper presentation of the non-current portion of the Loans Payable - Domestic account of ₱23,379,969.27 from Current Liabilities to Non-Current Liabilities section in the Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor oblige:
(a) the Municipal HRMO to:
(11) prepare a Contract of Service with the complete required details incorporated thereto for each job order personnel for approval of the LCE;
(b) the Municipal Budget Officer to:
(12) strictly observe the proper charging of expenditures to the expense item provided in the Annual Budget; and
(c) the Municipal Accountant to:
(13) record the wages of job order personnel in the appropriate MOOE account titles in accordance with Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor direct:
(23) prohibit AOs, whose fidelity bonds have expired and have not been renewed, from performing collection functions; and
(24) require the AOs to renew their fidelity bonds before the expiration and ensure that their bonds be sufficient to cover their actual accountabilities.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor:
(25) utilize the 20% DF to finance only the LGU's priority development projects, as embodied in the duly approved local development plan.

We recommended that the Municipality's Solid Waste Management Board work out for the approval of its Solid Waste Management Plan as well as obtain the required Environmental Compliance Certificate (ECC) from the DENR in order to have an efficient and effective system of solid waste collection and disposal within its area of jurisdiction.  Moreover, the establishment of the Material Recovery Facilities in the barangays as well as the common waste treatment and disposal facilities be strictly implemented to safeguard the public health and environment.  In addition, formulate or strictly implement the ordinance in place and impose fines and penalties to erring individuals who does not take cognizance of the law.
observations.managementAction: The Management will comply

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management immediately stop the practice of entering into an agreement with private banks and lending institutions wherein the LGU is acting as their collecting agent, and individual loan borrowers should settle their obligations personally.  Henceforth, government funds shall be spent or used solely for public purpose.
observations.managementAction: The Management will comply
observations.reason: Salary deductions were still made for existing loan obligations from private lending institutions

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management  agreed to direct the City Accountant to evaluate and properly classify PPE items into proper account classification in accordance with the revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units.

We recommended that the Management instruct the City Accountant to:

a. Analyze the Loans Payable and the related interest to ensure proper recognition of the current and non-current portions; and

b. Ensure proper presentation of the current and non-current portion of the Loans Payable, thereby, ensuring that the Financial Statements are free from inaccurate or misleading information.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the City Accountant to ensure proper computation and recording of depreciation expenses of various PPE items in accordance with  IPSAS) 17 ‒Property, Plant and Equipment.

We recommended that the Management agreed to direct the City Accountant to ensure that the accrued interest expenses are recorded in accordance with accrual basis and International Public Sector Accounting Standards, moving forward.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the City Accountant to write off the Breeding Stocks amounting to  ₱278,000.00 for the disposal of the donated asset. Moreover, properly account for the subsequent purchase of Biological Assets in accordance with IPSAS 17 for its fair presentation in the Financial Statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
  
Ensure strict compliance with the provisions of LBC No. 141 for the utilization of funds received; and

Instruct the CDRRMO to coordinate with the DBM to determine the appropriate action to be undertaken regarding the unutilized financial assistance.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the submission of lacking documents and henceforth strengthen its management controls over the procurement of drugs and medicines by ensuring that all disbursement vouchers are supported with complete documents to establish the propriety, regularity and validity of the claims.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the CMO, CDRRMO and City Pharmacist III to ensure that only those drugs and medicines that are listed on PDNF Volume I or Essential Drug List are requested. But, if it is necessary to procure drugs and medicine, not on the list, then send a request with justification to the National Drug  Policy Office.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the City Accountant to:

Prepare adjusting entries to reclassify the misclassified accounts.

Ensure that proper account titles are used in recording the drugs and medicines as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-09.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

Require the concerned Requesting Officers, for drugs and medicines, not to specify brand names in their Purchase Requests, but instead, state the generic names; and

Direct the BAC Chairman and Secretariat to invite Personnel from the City Health Office who has the technical knowledge of medicines during BAC Meetings on the procurement of drugs and medicines.

We recommended and Management agreed to immediately create an Internal Audit Service/Unit that will undertake the following functions a.) Assist the Management on matters relating to management controls and operations audit; b.) Review and appraise systems and procedures ; and c.) Identify, analyze and evaluate management deficiencies and recommend courses of action.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the City Accountant to reclassify the non-current portion of the Loans Payable- Domestic and provide accurate information on the liquidity and solvency of the City.

We recommended that Management direct the City Accountant to ensure that the accrued expenses, such as accrued interest, are recorded in accordance with accrual basis and International Public Sector Accounting Standards, moving forward.

We recommended that the Management ensure transparency in government procurement and:

Require the BAC Secretariat to monitor the status of the Notices of Award and Notices to Proceed to ensure the on-time posting of the same to the PhilGEPS website, City’s official website and the three conspicuous places; and

Direct the BAC Secretariat, with coordination with the Office of the Mayor, to emplace mechanisms that would ensure on-time signing and posting of  Notices of Award and Notices to Proceed.
We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the City Accountant to maintain a special account in the General Fund for all loans availed by the City.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the City Accountant to provide adequate disclosures on Loans Payable account so that the liquidity and solvency of the City could be properly assessed.

We recommended that Management instruct the City Accountant to ensure on-time remittance of provincial and barangay shares from taxes on sand and gravel in the succeeding quarters as provided under Section 286 (a) of the Local Government Code.

We recommended that storage facilities be provided for supplies and material procured and procurements of such supplies and materials be accounted for using the perpetual inventory method recognizing inventory accounts in the books upon purchase and expenses, only upon consumption in accordance with the provisions of PPSAS 12.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Revenue Collectors to strictly adhere to the existing regulations pertaining to the frequency of remittance of collections and depositing them to the Agency’s authorized depository banks.

We recommended and Management agreed the use of ATM payroll system in the payment of wages for the Job Order workers. This will protect not only the payroll funds from losses but also the lives of the Accountable Officer/s who do withdrawals of huge amount of cash from the bank. Likewise use of such system will efficiently reduce the man-hours spent in manually paying the wages of the JOs.

We recommended that the BAC strictly comply with Section 4 of Administrative Order No. 17 dated July 28, 2011 in the procurement of common – use supplies to avail of the most advantageous prices of commodities for the government.

We recommended that Management instruct the City Accountant to adjust the RPT Receivable and SET receivable balances based on the Duly Certified List showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year. Ensure that the proper setting-up of the RPT receivable and SET receivable in the ensuing year

We recommended that Management provide a proper storage facility for the supplies and materials inventories and will only be treated as an expense upon release when the related revenue is recognized, or the related service is rendered in accordance with the provisions of IPSAS 12.

We recommended that the Management exert extra effort to fully implement its priority programs and projects that are envisioned to help in the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental outcomes; the City Engineer to complete the preparation of the program of works; the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to properly plan procurement activities to avoid delayed implementation of projects; the  City Development Council to judiciously plan the inclusion of priority projects in the 20% Development Fund to avoid revisions and realignment; and the Local Chief Executive to create the Project Monitoring Committee/Project Inspection Team that would assist, evaluate and monitor the extent of implementation of programs and projects in order to determine the causes of delay in the implementation.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management should strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Sections 37 and 24 of Republic Act 9003 - "An Act Providing for an Ecological Solid Waste Program, Creating the Necessary Institutional ZcvMechanisms and Incentives, Declaring Certain Acts Prohibited and Providing Penalties, Appropriating Funds Therefor, and Other Purposes", for the people of Tandag City to enjoy of the benefits that can be derived therefrom.

We recommended Management to strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Sections 37 and 24 of Republic Act 9003 - “An Act Providing for an Ecological Solid Waste Program, Creating the Necessary Institutional Mechanisms and Incentives, Declaring Certain Acts Prohibited and Providing Penalties, Appropriating Funds therefor, and Other Purposes”, for the people of Tandag City to enjoy the benefits of which this Act is created.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Deputy for Resource Management and Distribution who facilitated the receipt and distribution of donations in-kind to submit to the audit team the required supporting documents of the summary/list of donations received. The one-time separate reports for cash and in-kind donations shall also be prepared and submitted to the NDRRM Council through the Office of the Civil Defense.

remit Philhealth contributions regularly, on or before its due date to avoid payment of interest/penalties, preclude delay in the processing of members’/employees’ claims and present the account correctly in the financial statements; and

analyze and reconcile the amount withheld against the amount remitted to arrive at a corrected balance.

CY
2020 | No action/comment from Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory Committee guided by the provisions of COA Circular 2020-06 and that the Internal Auditor shall only serve as a witness to the inventory taking.
observations.reason: As per inquiry with management and review of RCPPE

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the inventory committee to conduct physical inventory on the remaining Breeding stocks and require the maintenance of Ledger Cards and submission of monthly inventory report to the Municipal Accountant so as to validate its existence.
observations.reason: Not implemented per review of FS. Breeding Stocks still had the same balance but no report on the physical count / inventory of the dispersed animals were submitted.

observations.recommendation: Require the Officer-in-Charge of the Waterworks Operation in coordination with the Municipal Accountant to identify and set up the receivables for the unpaid accounts. Likewise require the Waterworks Supervisor to regularly prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant’s Office summary reports on consumers’ meter readings, billings and payments in order that the corresponding income   and receivable accounts will be correctly/ accurately established and taken up in the books of accounts

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to further review/analyze the transactions in prior years pertaining to the discrepancy of ₱3,362,538.20 and take-up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to reconcile the balances of the Due from and Due to Other Funds accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office reviewed the entries in the GL for CYs 2014 to 2016 and taken-up in the books the necessary adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to transfer the cost of Office Building from TF to GF based on available records consistent with the accounting entries provided in Section 104, Volume 1 of MNGAS for LGUs and subsequently provide depreciation cost thereon to present fairly the balance of Office Building account in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation:

We also recommended that Management require the Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the RPCPPE with the accounting records and apply the guidelines and procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 for the noted discrepancies.
