observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the BAC, with the assistance of the BAC Secretariat, to post all NOA, contract, or Purchase Order in the website of the LGU or any conspicuous place in the premises of the PE and GPPB online portal for Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act for information purposes and to require the submission of the mandatory documentary requirements from the suppliers, distributors, manufacturers or contractors concerned; b. Require the Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the MSWDO and MHO, to submit the list of affected families and proof of receipt of distribution of relief goods, medicines and vitamins and use of testing kits and other supporting documents; and c. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to require the supplier to provide the Sales Invoices for the payments made.
observations.managementAction: Management require the responsible officer to comply on the audit recommendation.
observations.reason:
Proof of posting of NOA, contract, or Purchase Order in the GPPB online portal for Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act for information purposes was not submitted to the Audit Team.
Required documents not yet submitted.
Some PPAs charged to LDRRMF were not related to COVID-19 response as noted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Require the Municipal Budget Officer to submit the authorization issued by the Sangguniang Bayan for the implementation of the PPAs described in generic terms and to require the MPDC with the assistance of the Municipal Budget Officer to integrate all PPAs stated in the LDRRMFIP in the Annual Investment Plan in accordance with Section 22(c) of RA No. 7160 and JMC No. 2013-1; b. Require the BAC to include the warranty clause in the contract and Purchase Order for goods to be procured using the LDRRMF under the Emergency Procurement and to require the submission of the mandatory requirements from the suppliers before payment to protect the financial interest of the LGU; c. Require the BAC, with the assistance of the BAC Secretariat, to post all NOA, Contract, or PO in the website of the LGU, if any, or any conspicuous place in the premises of the PE and GPPB online portal for Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act for transparency purposes; d. Require the Municipal Budget Officer to review carefully and determine the conformity of the PPAs with the provisions of the DBM Circular before certifying the availability of allotment; and e. Require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the MSWDO and MHO, to submit the list of affected families and proof of receipt of distribution of rice, families and proof of receipt of distribution of medicines and other relief goods and other supporting documents.
observations.managementAction:
Management require the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) to submit the authorization issued by the Sangguniang Bayan for the implementation of the PPAs described in generic terms and required the MPDC with the assistance of the MBO to integrate all PPAs stated in the LDRRMFIP in the Annual Investment Plan.
Management require the BAC to include the warranty clause in the contract and PO for goods to be procured and to require submitting the mandatory requirements.
Management require the BAC to post all NOA, Contract, or NTP in the LGU website.
Management require the MBO to determine the conformity of PPAs and the Municipal Accountant to submit the list of affected families and proof or receipt of distribution of rice, medicines and other supporting documents.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Budget Officer Authorization issued by the Sangguniang Bayan for the implementation of PPAs described in generic terms was not yet submitted to the Audit Team.
No proof of posting in the GPPB online portal for Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act was submitted to the Audit Team.
Some PPAs charged to LDRRMF were not disaster related.
The list of affected families and proof of receipt of distribution of rice, families and proof of receipt of distribution of medicines and other supporting documents were not yet submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Require the Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the MSWDO and Municipal Treasurer, to submit the copy of valid ID, Barangay Certificate with specimen signature of the beneficiary provided in the schedule and the certificate of eligibility of the 132 beneficiaries in Barangay Sablig for further evaluation of the Audit Team; and b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the MSWDO, to issue demand letters to the five recipients of financial subsidy who were not listed in the Certificate of Eligibility to cause the refund of the financial subsidy amounting to ₱27,500.00 and return the amounts collected to the DSWD.
observations.managementAction: The MHO submitted the required documents for the donated assets and the liquidation of the BUB Program.
observations.reason:
Copy of valid ID, Barangay Certificate with specimen signature and the certificate of eligibility of the 132 beneficiaries in Barangay Sablig were not yet submitted to the Audit Team.
Proof of issuance of Demand Letters were not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the BAC, with the assistance of the TWG and BAC Secretariat, to strictly comply with the procurement provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and the fundamental principle on financial transactions prescribed in Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445; and to instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents in conformity with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 pursuant to Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the required reports and justification on the audit observations.
observations.reason: The following documents remain not submitted: a. Bid Securing Declaration dated Dec. 26, 2021; b. Manpower requirement dated Jan. 2020; c. Proof of posting of NOA. d. Copy of registration documents of the vehicle under the name of the LGU. Additionally, expired Mayor's Permit was supported only with business tax assessment and billing instead of an OR as proof that the bidder has applied for renewal of permit.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the BAC, with the assistance of the TWG and BAC Secretariat to: a. Post the Invitation to Bid, NOA, Contract and NTP in the LGU website, require the bidders to use the prescribed forms in the bidding documents and emphasize the possibility of non-acceptance of bid for non-compliance and indicate their initial in every page of the original copies of the bid received and opened; b. Ensure that equipment to be pledged by a bidder on a particular project are not the same equipment listed on another project where the bidder is already declared the bidder with lowest calculated responsive bid especially when the construction period will coincide and that the winning bidder post the performance security prior to the signing of the contract; and c. Require the Municipal Engineer to supervise the construction of a project to ensure that the construction will only commence after the issuance and receipt of the NTP by the winning bidder.
We also recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents.
observations.managementAction: Management required the concerned employees to ensure compliance with the audit recommendations.
observations.reason:
Required documents were not submitted. Verification of CY 2021 accounts showed that some contract agreement forms used were not the prescribed PBD. Also, some bidding documents did not bear the initials of the present BAC members.
Reiterated in Observation No. 11, Pages 57-62 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Instruct the Procuring Officer to indicate the complete item name, complete detailed item specification, including accessories, i.e., narrative description, accessories, size/dimension/volume, model, use of replacement or original (for spare parts), part number of spare parts, type and classification of material, brand new or second hand and brand name of the items to be procured in the Purchase Order; b. Require the Inspection Officer to verify and inspect carefully all purchases made by the LGU to ensure its conformity with the specifications provided in the Purchase Order; and c. Require the supplier to replace the delivered 10 Wheeler Dump Truck and Mini Dump Truck in conformity with the specifications in the Purchase Order and bid proposal, respectively.
observations.managementAction: Management instruct the Procuring Officer to indicate the required details in the PO. Management instruct the Inspection Officer to verify and inspect carefully purchases. Management coordinated with the supplier to conform the delivered items with the PO.
observations.reason: Verification showed that some required details in some POs were not indicated. The mini dump-truck was not replaced with the item in conformity with the PO and bid proposal.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer, with the assistance of the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Accountant, to cause the immediate retrieval of all necessary documents of ownership of the unregistered parcels of land and to facilitate the transfer and registration of ownership with the Land Registration Authority in order to establish absolute ownership and protect the interest of the local government unit.
We further recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds which shall cover the expenses on the transfer and registration of parcels of land.
We also recommended Management direct the Municipal Accountant to gather the necessary documents and record in the books of accounts the four parcels of land with total land area of 10,212 square meters.
observations.managementAction: Management is in process of retrieving the necessary documents to process the registration of the said land. Budget was allocated to cover the expenses on the transfer and registration of parcels of land.
observations.reason:
Parcels of Land with total area of 32,835 sq.m and assessed value of ₱1,098,290.00 were not supported with documents of ownership.
The budget was not utilized in registering the parcel of land. There are still land owned by the LGU that remains unregistered.
Reiterated in Observation No. 9, Pages 53-55 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to the former officials and employees of the LGU to immediately settle their unliquidated cash advances; b. Require the participants of the First General Assembly for the term 2019-2020 on March 8 to 11, 2020 at Marriott Hotel, Pasay City to refund the amount of ₱8,800.00; c. Oblige the Municipal Accountant to issue a Certification that the previous cash advance has been liquidated and accounted for in the books for the grant of cash advances before granting additional cash advance; and d. Require the administrative officer to submit his Certificate of Appearance during the 11th PLLENRO National Convention on March 3-6, 2020 at La Carmela de Boracay Hotel and Convention Center.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were sent to the accountable officers. The Municipal Accountant issued Certification that previous cash advance has been liquidated and accounted for in the books. The participants of the General Assembly were required to refund the amount of ₱8,800.00.
observations.reason:
Long outstanding unliquidated cash advances remain. Some actions implemented: the amount of ₱8,800.00 was refunded on October 20, 2021 (Implemented for that sub-item). Other cash advances remain unliquidated and not fully recovered.
Reiterated in Observation No. 10, Pages 55-57 of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to determine the discrepancies noted and effect necessary adjustments in both records of the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile the cashbooks with the subsidiary ledgers to ensure reliability and correctness of balances of said accounts.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer regularly reconcile the subsidiary ledger balances of the Cash in Bank- LCCA accounts with the cashbook balances every end of the month.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer has already adjusted the reconciling items identified in prior years in respective books. On-going reconciliation is being done.
observations.reason:
Verification still showed an unreconciled difference between Subsidiary Ledger with the Cashbook in the amount of ₱3,206,075.96. There was an increase in the unreconciled difference in CY 2021.
Reiterated in Observation No. 8, Pages 51-53 of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Acting Municipal Treasurer to make a follow-up letter to Land Bank of the Philippines to provide the details of the Debit Memo dated September 14, 2020 and Credit Memo dated August 8, 2019 and January 24, 2020 and request all depository banks to furnish the Municipal Accountant of copies of Debit Memo and Credit Memo every month.
Further, we recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to immediately record the amount of Credit Memo and Debit Memo in the books of accounts once the details of the Credit Memo and Debit Memo are obtained to present the correct balances of the Cash in Bank account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Acting Municipal Treasurer already adjusted the reconciling items identified in prior years in respective books. Reconciliation is being done on the remaining discrepancies.
observations.reason: Credit Memo totaling ₱15,683.60 remains unrecorded in the books of accounts. The Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant have not yet obtained and recorded all necessary bank memos; thus, the reconciling items remain unadjusted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the BAC Chairman, with the assistance of the BAC Secretariat, to observe the prescribed period of submission of five working days from the execution of a contract in order to gain the benefits of facilitating the review and evaluation process and obtaining suggested corrective measures on the defects and deficiencies noted in the contract and its supporting documents which are relevant in decision making.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Secretariat was advised to start sending copies of the needed documents to the Audit Team through electronic mail starting July 2022 transactions.
observations.reason: Copies of perfected contracts other than those procured through public bidding were only submitted to the Audit Team during the monthly transmittal of accounts when such procurements were paid. Reiterated in Observation No. 28, Pages 100-101 of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the remittance of collections of all collecting officers by verifying the RCD, checking the statement of accountable forms as to initial balances on hand, receipts, issuance and the ending balances on hand, making physical count of the accountable forms remaining in the custody of the collectors and comparing the same against the ending balance on hand before affixing her signature at the back of the triplicate copy of the last official receipt issued.
We also recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to count the money turned over to her before signing the certification and receipt portion in the RCD to provide adequate safeguard of the government funds against possible loss or misuse.
observations.managementAction: Management requires the Acting Municipal Treasurer to monitor the remittance of collections of all collecting officers.
observations.reason:
Post-audit of collections and deposits disclosed remittance delays of two to 30 days for the collecting officers. Delays in remitting collections were still observed.
Reiterated in Observation No. 18, Pages 78-79 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure the immediate operation of the Anda RHU 2 by requiring the MHO to secure the Letter of Acceptance from the DOH and to facilitate the immediate preparation of project proposal for the needed equipment of the RHU under the HFEP of the DOH so that the constituents of the LGU may already avail of the basic, essential as well as the specialized health services offered by the Health Facility Enhancement Program.
observations.managementAction: The MHO secured the Letter of Acceptance from the DOH and requested assistance from DOH for the needed equipment and doctor for the RHU 2.
observations.reason:
The RHU 2 remains non-operational due to lack of equipment and doctor. Management is following up with DOH for equipment and Doctors to the Barrios deployment.
Reiterated in Observation No. 21, Pages 83-86 of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to ensure collection of the delinquent accounts on real property tax and special education tax by requiring the Municipal Treasurer to adopt effective collection system by sending notice to delinquent taxpayers and request the assistance of barangay officials.
We also recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to send notice to market stall owners to settle their unpaid rental fees and electric bills and to the water concessionaires for the unpaid water bills in order to provide additional funds to support the operations and programs of the LGU geared towards the welfare of the constituents.
observations.managementAction: Management required the Acting Municipal Treasurer to intensify collection of delinquent real property taxes and to send notices to market stall owners and water concessionaires to settle their unpaid rental fees and water bills.
observations.reason:
No evidence that all recommended collection measures were fully implemented; some stall owners and concessionaires remain with unpaid accounts.
Reiterated in Observation No. 22, Pages 86-89 of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated the previous year’s recommendation that Management make a follow-up on the demand letters sent to debtors. In case where the debtors fail to settle their accounts after due notices, apply legal sanctions. This may help to improve the collection efficiency of the LGU wherein the funds collected may be utilized for re-loaning to other beneficiaries.
We also recommended that Management submit the following documents to enable the Audit Team to review and evaluate the nature of the fund transfer from the national government and loan agreement between the Municipality and loan borrower, to wit: 1. Copy of Memorandum of Agreement between the Municipality and the source agency and other supporting documents; and 2. Copy of Loan Agreement between the LGU and the debtors.
Further, we recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to send letter to the source agency to inquire on the terms and conditions of the fund transfer received and to obtain a copy of the written agreement and to submit a copy thereof to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: Demand Letters were sent to the debtors. Management submitted some documents; however, the retired Municipal Treasurer failed to provide the MOA. Only disbursement vouchers were provided to the Audit Team since there was no loan agreement executed between the recipients and the LGU.
observations.reason:
No legal sanctions were undertaken by the LGU to enforce collection. Required documents (MOA and loan agreements) were not submitted to the Audit Team.
Reiterated in Observation No. 24, Pages 90-92 of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to identify the specific gender issues caused by the unequal situation between women and men in the locality in the GAD Plan and Budget and to formulate PPAs that are responsive to these gender issues in order to attain the main intentions in promoting gender-responsive governance, protection and fulfilment of women’s human rights and the promotion of women’s economic empowerment.
We further recommended that Management require the GFPS to prioritize in the succeeding GAD Plan and Budget the recurring/emerging gender issues in the LGU, results of gender assessment of the organization or its PPAs and other GAD PPAs identified in the GAD Code of the LGU that have not been considered in the previous year to achieve a more effective GAD Plan and Budget.
observations.managementAction: Management required the GFPS to comply with the recommendations. The GFPS was required to prioritize recurring gender issues in the succeeding GPB.
observations.reason:
The gender issues to be addressed by some PPAs were not well-defined in the GAD Plan of CY 2021. The GAD database update was planned for CY 2022 to address deficiencies.
Reiterated in Observation No. 25, Pages 92-94 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the designated employee or official in the issuance of driver’s trip ticket to see that the required information are duly indicated before issuance and to oblige the driver of each government vehicle to transcribe or summarize all trip tickets issued during the month in the Monthly Report of Official Travels in chronological order to support the reasonableness and propriety of gasoline expenses.
observations.managementAction: Management required the designated employee in the issuance of DTT to ensure that necessary data are duly provided and required drivers to properly accomplish the portion of the DTT. Management required preparation of the Summary of Requisition and Issue Voucher, Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption.
observations.reason:
DTTs were not duly accomplished and required reports were not prepared and submitted to the Audit Team.
Reiterated in Observation No. 27, Pages 97-99 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management insure all of its insurable assets relevant in the delivery of public service to provide safeguard against substantial losses in the event of damage to, or loss of, properties brought by fortuitous events by requiring the Municipal Treasurer with the assistance of the Municipal Accountant to identify the insurable assets and to submit the inventory report to the GSIS for evaluation; and to instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds needed for the payment of premiums.
observations.managementAction: Management reviewed all the insurable properties of the LGU and applied for insurance with the GSIS for some properties. Management advised the Acting Municipal Treasurer to check all insurable properties and submit them to the GSIS for quotation of insurance premium in order to include it in the annual budget.
observations.reason:
Some properties with book value of ₱36,130,119.36 were not insured with the GSIS. Some listed assets were already transferred to DepEd and some were unserviceable; proper action for these assets will be done.
Reiterated in Observation No. 30, Pages 103-105 of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to conduct a detailed review and analysis of the Due to PhilHealth and Due to Pag-IBIG accounts in prior years and if warranted, make the corresponding adjustments; and to cause the immediate remittance of the premiums/contributions to the government agencies concerned.
We also recommended that Management ensure timely remittance of premiums to GSIS by requiring the Human Resource and Management Office to prepare and transmit promptly the required forms in updating the records of the personnel to the GSIS to update the personal records of employees both in the LGU and GSIS.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant exerted effort to review and analyze the accounts of prior years. The Municipal Treasurer was required to remit the premiums/contributions and to coordinate with the agencies as needed.
observations.reason: Premiums/contributions of ₱252,056.00 remained unremitted/unadjusted. For Due to GSIS, remittance made in Jan 2022 resulted in a negative balance of ₱156,630.46 indicating unresolved reconciliation issues. Reiterated in Observation No. 32, Pages 108-110 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE ensure strict compliance with the provisions of Section 59, Volume I of MNGAS Manual for LGUs and to: a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to payees of the stale checks at least one month before it becomes stale. Consequently, for those unclaimed checks that become stale in her custody, return the original DV and supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books; b. Require the Municipal Accountant, upon receipt of the original DV and supporting documents of the stale checks, to prepare Journal Entry Voucher to record the transaction as Accounts Payable; and c. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to process the replacement of stale checks in the hands of the payee upon submission of the stale check to the Treasury Office.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer sent notices to payees of the stale checks in CY 2020.
observations.reason: Stale checks in the custody of the Municipal Treasurer were not returned to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books. Stale checks were not surrendered to the Acting Municipal Treasurer for recording in the books and some corrective procedures remain uncompleted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE ensure strict compliance with the procurement provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and the fundamental principle on financial transactions prescribed in Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445. We also recommended that Management instruct the BAC, the BAC Secretariat, TWG, Municipal Engineer, Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to submit the required documents prescribed in conformity with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 pursuant to Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the justification on the observations and some documents.
observations.reason:
Some required documents were not submitted. Verification showed that documents for Rehabilitation/Concreting of Core Local Access Road at Poblacion-Awag Road were not submitted.
NS No. 21-001-300 (18-19) [Mun. of Anda-RO1-Pangasinan] was issued by the Audit Team on April 30, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE ensure strict adherence to the guidelines provided under Section 10, Annex E of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in granting time extension. Further, the LCE require the contractor concerned to submit the written consent of bondsmen and a certification from appropriate government agency on the scarcity of cement during the last quarter of 2018; otherwise, impose the collection of applicable liquidated damages.
observations.managementAction: Submitted justification and some required documents.
observations.reason: Management was not able to submit the Certification from appropriate government agency on the scarcity of cement during the last quarter of 2018. NS No. 21-001-300 (18-19) [Mun. of Anda-RO1-Pangasinan] was issued by the Audit Team on April 30, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE ensure strict compliance with the procurement provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and the fundamental principle on financial transactions prescribed in Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445; and to instruct the BAC, BAC Secretariat, TWG, Municipal Engineer, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to submit the required documents prescribed in conformity with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 pursuant to Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the required documents.
observations.reason: Some required documents were not submitted to fully validate compliance with procurement requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the responsible persons to submit the required documents for the donated assets and the liquidation of the BUB Program to facilitate further verification of the project expenditures and avoid the issuance of Notice of Suspension in audit.
observations.managementAction: The MHO submitted the required documents for the donated assets and the liquidation of the BUB Program.
observations.reason: The Approved Project Expenditures or Estimated Expenses indicating the project objective and expected output remained unsubmitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the OIC-City  Accountant  agreed  to  continue  to  exert
effort to obtain the details of the other reconciling items and  effect  the
necessary adjustments in the books to correct the balances of  the  Cash  in
Bank-LCCA and LCSA and other affected accounts.

We recommended that  Management  direct  the  officials  concerned  to  send
demand letters to the market vendors with delinquency,  otherwise  institute
sanctions and penalties pursuant to the ordinance.

We recommended  that  the  Management  direct  the  OIC-City  Accountant  to
closely  coordinate  with  the  City  Treasurer  to  establish  the  correct
balances of the RPT and SET accounts so that appropriate  adjusting  journal
entries can be made to reflect the accurate balances in the books.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the OIC-City Accountant to:


a. Request for write-off of the dormant receivable amounting to ₱324,687.93
   in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005;


b. Closely monitor  and  follow-up  the  barangays  with  long  outstanding
   unliquidated receivables/fund transfers and maintain aging  schedule  of
   the Due from LGU account;


c. Effect the following adjusting entry to correct the  charging  to  wrong
   subsidiary ledger:


       Accounts payable – Barangay Casili    ₱150,000.00
           Accounts payable - AC/DC Aircon Medic        ₱150,000.00


d. Submit an explanation to justify  the  recording  of  the  ₱1,350,000.00
   unreleased funds.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the  OIC-City  Accountant  to
effect the necessary adjusting entry to correct  the  errors  identified  in
Annex  D  and  to  exert  effort  in   identifying   the   causes   of   the
inconsistencies in the Due to and Due  from  Other  Fund  accounts  for  the
prior  period  transactions.  We  further  recommended   that   a   periodic
monitoring be done by the concerned bookkeepers of  each  fund  to  maintain
the reconciliation of the two accounts.

We recommended that Management demand  from  the  contractor  the  immediate
refund of the balance of the advance payment for the unfinished Medium  Rise
Housing Project to prevent disallowance in audit.

We recommended that Management direct the:


a. BAC Secretariat to henceforth consolidate the PPMPs into  only  one  APP
   considering that the BAC is the sole recommendatory committee insofar as
   the mode of procurement is concerned and furnish a copy thereof  to  the
   Supply Office;

b. Supply Office to henceforth initiate the requisitioning of supplies  and
   materials and maintain inventories equivalent to a two-month requirement
   and observe proper warehousing  and  maintenance  of  inventory  control
   records such as the stock cards.


c.  OIC-City  Accountant   to   refrain   from   recording   the   procured
   supplies/inventory items directly as expense account; and


d. Inventory Committee to conduct inventory-taking of supplies to ascertain
   the existence and condition of the reported balances of inventories.

We recommended that Management direct the OIC-City Accountant to  coordinate
with the DGS and  secure  a  complete  list  of  all  City  owned  land  and
buildings which were held to earn rentals or  for  capital  appreciation  to
facilitate the preparation of the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV)  in  order  to
reclassify the affected PPE accounts to the Investment Property account.

We recommended that Management direct the concerned accountable officers  to
submit to  COA  an  application  for  relief  from  property  accountability
together  with  the  required  documents  for  evaluation.  Henceforth,   we
recommended that any request for relief from accountability for any loss  of
government  funds  and  property  must  be  filed  immediately  within   the
reglementary period.

We recommended that Management direct the DGS and the City Legal  Office  to
give priority to the registration of these 142 lots in the name of the  City
Government of Mandaue under the Torrens Title System.  In  the  alternative,
we  recommended  for   the   creation   of   Land   Titling   Committee   to
intensify/expedite the titling efforts of the City.

We recommended that Management direct the City Treasurer  to  formulate  the
necessary procedures/guidelines for  the  implementation  of  collection  on
advance payment including the application/imposition  of  the  corresponding
Tax Discount pursuant to Section 44 of the RPT Tax Code of the City.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the TEAM  Executive  Director
to evaluate the current practice/procedures and adopt measures to  intensify
the collections of towing and storage fees from the owners of the  impounded
motorcycles to maximize income for the City.

Management’s Comment:


During the exit conference,  the  City  Administrator  explained  that  upon
learning of the incidence  of  double  payment  of  payroll,  the  City  had
immediately conducted an investigation which resulted in the termination  of
the contract of the Job Order personnel involved in the incident.   Further,
it was added that the City had already filed a case against the  erring  Job
Order personnel with the Prosecutor’s Office in Mandaue City.

With regard to the refund of the  double  payment,  the  City  Administrator
stated that in case of failure of the Job  Order  personnel  to  settle  his
liability,  the  persons  liable  for  the  disbursement  shall  assume  the
responsibility.

We  recommended  and  Management  agreed  to  direct  the  Bids  and  Awards
Committee and the Engineering Office to  submit  the  lacking  documents  to
avoid disallowance of the transaction in audit.

We recommended and Management agreed to secure from  the  NTC  the  required
Purchaser’s Permit to consider  the  procurement  of  handheld  radios  with
accessories valid and legitimate expenditures  to  prevent  disallowance  in
audit.

We recommended and Management agreed to  submit  the  lacking  documents  to
support the disbursements of PAGCOR Funds for the Audit Team’s  verification
to avoid disallowance of the transactions.  Henceforth, we  recommended  and
Management agreed that all projects and activities  to  be  charged  against
the PAGCOR funds shall be supported  with  an  ordinance/resolution  jointly
approved by the City  Mayor  and  the  Sangguniang  Panlungsod  pursuant  to
PAGCOR guidelines.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

a. Submit an amended  LDRRMFIP  that  includes  the  list  of  projects  and
  activities  charged  to  the  unexpended  LDRRMF  of  previous  years  in
  conformity with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and

b. immediately submit the Report on Sources and Utilization  of  DRRMF  duly
  certified correct by the OIC-City Accountant and henceforth, observe  the
  timely submission of the said report.

Management’s Comment:

There are funds allocated  for  specific  purposes  and  earmarked  but  not
obligated for certain reasons. Under Capital  Outlay,  funds  are  earmarked
for PPEs that have yet to be delivered since it  is  manufactured  overseas.
In addition, the construction of the Phase  1  of  the  proposed  City  DRRM
Office building is still on the planning stage.  Hence,  the  obligated  and
earmarked funds for Capital Outlay amounting to ₱37,249,250.00 or 70.55  per
cent only of the total allocation.  While  for  the  MOOE  and  supplemental
fund, there are funds earmarked for acquisition of food, medicine,  vaccines
and others. The earmarked and obligated funds  amounted  to  ₱125,813,422.54
or 87.55 per cent of the total allocation.  Considering  the earmarked  and
obligated funds, and with the total appropriation of ₱196M the total  LDRRMF
utilization rate is 83.14 per cent, of which ₱37,249,250.00 is from  Capital
Outlay and ₱125,813,422.54 is from MOOE.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:

It should be emphasized that the  term  earmarked  is  not  synonymous  with
obligation. Earmark is to designate fund for a  particular  purpose  and  is
synonymous with  appropriation.   On  the  other  hand,  obligation  is  the
specific amount within the allotment which is committed to be  paid  by  the
LGU for any lawful expenditure made by an accountable  officer  for  and  in
behalf of the LGU concerned, and is synonymous  to  utilization.  Thus,  for
the purpose  of  identifying  the  percentage  of  utilization,  the  amount
obligated is considered in the computation and not the amount earmarked.

We recommended that Management always  ensure  that  priority  projects  are
timely carried out and the fund is  optimally  utilized  with  the  view  of
effecting efficiency,  economy  and  effectiveness  in  the  disposition  of
government resources in compliance with the provisions set  forth  in  DILG-
DBM JMC No. 2017-1 and 2020-1.

We recommended that Management cause the recording of  the  transactions  of
the local college as a special account in the General Fund of  the  City  to
properly  account  the  collections  and  disbursements  intended  for   its
operations so as to ensure the fair presentation  of  the  City’s  financial
statements.

We recommended that the City of Mandaue establish a CPES  Implementing  Unit
that will implement the CPES implementing guidelines to provide  information
on the performance of constructors  in  government  projects  and  reap  the
benefits of timely completion of infrastructure projects  with  the  desired
quality, among others.

We recommend that the agency submit appropriate documents that would show how the amounts for SRA and AHDP were computed and arrived at.  We also recommend that reconciliation of actual entries made in the DTR with those reflected in the payrolls be made.

We recommend the following Proposed Adjusting Entries (PAJE No.1) for the Supply of Labor, Equipment and Materials for the Completion of Anda Cultural Center and Evacuation Center:
Furthermore, we recommend that moving forward, the Municipal Accounting Unit prepare complete and proper Journal Entries so as to show the true nature and effects of every transaction recorded and to produce accurate monthly and quarterly reports.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to send out notices to the various payees whose checks have become stale and facilitate the cancellation or replacement thereof.  We further recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary JEV for the restoration of the amount in the cash account.
We Propose the Adjusting Journal Entries (PAJE No. 3) to correct the records of the agency as follows:

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive assign appropriate committee to undertake proper disposal of unserviceable assets in accordance with COA Circular No. 89-296 and 2020-006 so that these will be derecognized in the books of the agency.

We recommend that the Accounting Department prepare adjusting entries of the affected accounts by debiting Due from LGUs and crediting Prior Period Adjustment. Also, we recommend immediate liquidation of the fund transferred if the purpose for which it has been given has been served.

We recommend that management submit the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) between the LGU and the barangays, the certification from the Accountant that funds previously transferred to the barangays were liquidated as well as other documents necessary to support the validity and propriety of the transfer of funds made from the LGU to the barangays.

We recommend that management submit an explanation and the SB Resolution authorizing the transfer of funds with specific purpose from the previous 13 barangays to the new lead barangay, Sta. Cruz, for a newly identified project.

We recommend that the agency prepare the Monthly Report for Fuel Consumption (MFRC) as prescribed by COA Circular No. 77-61 to effectively manage and assess the reasonableness of fuel, gasoline and oil expenditures and submit such report to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommend that management justify the noted observation. Moreover, we recommend that henceforth, the Bids and Awards Committee does not allow processing of requisitions which make reference to brand names to promote equal opportunity for products to be considered and ensure acquisition of goods at most advantageous terms to the LGU.

We recommend that management justify the charging of expenses against the development fund that are not allowed as listed in DBM, DOF and DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020. Moreover, we recommend that management strictly adhere to the Joint Memorandum Circulars and updates of DBM, DILG and DOF regarding the utilization of 20% Development Fund.

We recommend that management prepare the annual LDRRMFIP to include projects, programs, and activities chargeable from prior year’s unexpended balance of LDRRMF to show judicious and meticulous planning in the procurement activities of the LGU.  Moreover, we recommend that these be incorporated in the revised AIP and be approved by the Local Sanggunian and LCE in accordance with NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1.

We recommend that the Municipal Accounting and the Municipal Assessor’s Office continue their commendable archival system. We also recommend that other departments and offices emulate the same to ensure the safety and protection of public documents and improve work productivity and efficiency.

We recommended that Management:
1.7.1 Require the Municipal Treasurer to perform daily reconciliation of
cash balance on hand with the cashbook and the Municipal
Accountant to regularly reconcile the subsidiary ledger balances of
the Cash-Local Treasury account with the cashbook balances every
end of the month; and
1.7.2 Direct the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher
(JEV) to effect the necessary adjustment in Cash-Local Treasury
account to reflect the correct balance of the account.

The following are the suggested adjusting journal entries:
In the SEF books
Cash Local Treasury 66,481.05
Deferred Special Education Tax 66,481.05
Special Education Tax Receivable 66,481.05
Due to LGUs 33,240.53
Special Education Tax 33,240.52
In the TF books
Cash Local Treasury 8,000.00
Due to Other NGAs 8,000.00

2.7 We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that
Management require the Municipal Accountant to review/analyze the
transactions in prior years pertaining to the discrepancy of ₱3,362,538.20
and take-up the necessary adjustments to reconcile the Due from and Due
to Other Funds accounts to present the accurate balances of the reciprocal
accounts in the financial statements.

3.5 We recommended that Management:
3.5.1 Require the Municipal Accountant to record the purchase of supplies
as inventories using the perpetual inventory method and to maintain
Supplies Ledger Cards;
3.5.2 Instruct the end-users to prepare the RIS for every request in the
issuance of supplies to facilitate the preparation of SSMI by the
Municipal Treasurer; and
3.5.3 Direct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain a stock card, prepare the
SSMI and complete the physical inventory of supplies for the
preparation of the RPCI and to submit the said inventory report to
the Audit Team not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for
the first and second semesters, respectively.

4.6 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to
transfer the cost of Office Building from TF to GF based on available
records consistent with the accounting entries provided in Section 104,
Volume 1 of MNGAS for LGUs and subsequently provide depreciation
cost thereon to present fairly the balance of Office Building account in the
financial statements.

5.9 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to
draw a JEV to correct the recording of the LGU counterpart in the
procurement of 18 rescue vehicles for barangay use in the amount of
₱14,400,000.00 to present fairly the balances of accounts in the financial
statements.

5.10 The suggested adjusting journal entries are as follows:
In the GF books:
Transfers for Project Equity Share/LGU 14,400,000.00
Counterpart
Due to LGUs 14,400,000.00
To correct recording of the transfer of LGU counterpart from GF to
TF books.

5.11 The suggested adjusting entries to transfer the remaining balance of LGU
counterpart from the TF to the GF books:
In the GF books:
Cash in Bank 567,720.00
Transfers from General Fund of LGU 567,720.00
Counterpart/Equity Share
In the TF books:
Due to LGUs 567,720.00
Cash in Bank 567,720.00

6.3 We recommended that Management:
6.3.1 Require the Municipal Accountant to adjust the Original Budget
amounts in the GF in the amount of ₱150,000,000.00 as per
Appropriation Ordinance No. 3, series of 2020 and the Final
Budget to ₱152,197,092.86 to consider the four supplemental
budgets of the LGU;
6.3.2 Direct the Municipal Accountant to analyze the transactions
affecting the balance of Continuing Appropriation and to make the
necessary adjustments on the discrepancy between the ending
balance in CY 2020 and forwarded balance in CY 2021 in the
amount of ₱867,053.06; and
6.3.3 Instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the total actual
receipts in the SCBAA with the total revenue in the SFPer and to
disclose the details of the reconciling items in the NTFS to provide
reliable information to the users of the financial statements and
enable the LGU to identify the major source of difference and
improve its performance in the discharge of its accountability and
obligations.

7.11 We recommended that Management:
7.11.1 Require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain the Property Cards
per category of PPE which are necessary to support the accuracy
and reliability of the balances of PPE account in the financial
statements;
7.11.2 Instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the totals of PPE
Local Road Network Ledger Card with the balance of Road
Network account and to maintain the subsidiary ledger cards for
each group of PPE acquired prior to CY 2019;
7.11.3 Direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical
inventory count and to prepare the RPCPPE pursuant to EO No.
41, series of 2019; and
7.11.4 Instruct the Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant to
reconcile the RPCPPE and the Report on the Physical Count of the
Road Network System with the accounting records and apply the
guidelines and procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006
dated January 31, 2020 for the noted discrepancies.

8.7 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer and
Municipal Accountant to determine the discrepancies noted and effect
necessary adjustments in both records of the Municipal Treasurer and
Municipal Accountant to reconcile the cashbooks with the subsidiary
ledgers to ensure reliability and correctness of balances of said accounts.

8.8 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal
Treasurer regularly reconcile the subsidiary ledger balances of Cash in
Bank- Local Currency Current account with the cashbook balances every
end of the month.

9.6 We recommended that Management:
9.6.1 Require the Municipal Treasurer, with the assistance of the
Municipal Assessor and Municipal Accountant, to cause the
immediate retrieval of all necessary documents of ownership of the
unregistered parcels of land and to facilitate the transfer and
registration of ownership with the LRA to establish absolute
ownership and protect the interest of the LGU;
9.6.2 Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds which
shall cover the expenses on the transfer and registration of parcels
of land;
9.6.3 Direct the Municipal Accountant to record the cost of the two
parcels of land duly supported with certificate of title in the books
of accounts and to gather the necessary documents of the
remaining unrecorded parcels of land as basis in recording its
costs in the books of accounts.

We recommended that Management direct the OIC-City Accountant to observe the proper procedure in recording the receipt of supplies to the appropriate Inventory account instead of directly recording the same as Expense. Also, ensure that a Requisition Issue Slip or an Inventor Custodian Slip is prepared for the issuance of the supplies.
observations.managementAction:

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the BAC, with the assistance of the TWG and BAC Secretariat, to: a. Post the Invitation to Bid, Invitation to Bid, Contract and NTP in the LGU website; b. Ensure that pre-procurement was conducted for procurement of goods costing ₱2,000,000.00 and above; c. Require all bidders to support the submitted SLCC with Contract, Certificate of Completion and Certificate of Acceptance; d. Require the bidders to use the prescribed forms in the bidding documents and emphasize the possibility of non-acceptance of bid for non-compliance; e. Ensure that the NTP was issued to the winning biddings within seven calendar days from the date of approval of the contract; f. Conduct the mandatory review and evaluation of the terms, conditions, and specifications in the bidding documents, in case there was a failure of bidding; and g. Indicate their initial in every page of the original copies of the bid received and opened.

observations.recommendation:

Verify the completeness of the offer of bidder in the conduct of detailed evaluation to ensure that offer address or provide all the required items in the bidding documents; and d. Verify, validate and ascertain the completed projects of the bidder as to incurrence of delays, failure of delivery or unsatisfactory performance of obligations as well as the inspection and testing of the goods/products and indicate the results thereof in the Post-Qualification Report.
