Require the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWB) to coordinate with the Barangay Solid Waste Management Committee and to require therefrom a monitoring report on the operation of the MRF in every barangay; b. Instruct the MSWMB to maintain records on the volume of waste collected and the volume of waste disposed to enable the monitoring of the implementation of the solid waste management program; and c. Require the Municipal Treasurer to collect the garbage fees imposed in Section 49, Article XI of the Municipal Ordinance No. 2.
observations.managementAction: Management required the MSWB to prepare the monitoring report on the operation of the MRF in every barangay. Management instructed the MSWMB to maintain records on the volume of waste collected and disposed. Management required the Acting Municipal Treasurer to collect the garbage fees imposed.
observations.reason: The MSWB submitted Accomplishment Monitoring Reports and records on the operation of MRF and volume of waste collected and disposed. Garbage fees were collected in CY 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive 
(a) create an Inventory Team to:
(i) undertake complete physical inventory of all property, plant and equipment of the municipality; and 

(ii) prepare the corresponding Report of the Physical Count of PPE and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period; 

(b) instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to maintain updated PPE Ledger Cards; 

(c) require the Acting-General Services Officer (GSO) to maintain property cards for PPE to account for the receipt and disposition of properties; and 

(d) require the Acting-GSO and OIC-Municipal Accountant to reconcile their property records after the conduct of physical count to establish correctness of balances of PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by management.
observations.reason:
The creation of Inventory Team who will perform the inventory taking is still not made. 

Reiterated in Observation No. 1.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to 

(a) conduct periodic reconciliation of all Cash in Bank accounts to ascertain the accuracy of the balances of accounts appearing in the Cashbooks and Accounting Financial Records; and 

(b) investigate differences and adjust the corresponding ledgers/cashbooks accordingly.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by management.
observations.reason:
Reconciliation of records between the Cashbooks and Subsidiary Ledgers maintained by the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant is still not made.

Reiterated in Observation No. 2.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the:

(a) HRMO to:
(i) adopt a Contract of Service, instead of the existing appointment documents, in order to include the basic and necessary data required in any contract agreement, including the specific project/program/office which needs the services of a Job Order; 

(ii) ensure that all personnel are accorded rest day as required by Presidential Decree No. 143; and 

(iii) evaluate the necessity of the services of the JOs and avoid year-round appointments as stipulated in Section 77 of R.A. 7160; and 

(b) the immediate supervisors to check correctness of the DTRs submitted by the JOs before signing approval thereof.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by management.
observations.reason: The same practice/ condition still exists.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Acting Property and Supply Officer and the Bids and Awards Committee to procure all common-use supplies directly from the DBM-Procurement Service, and resort to local suppliers only upon unavailability of the supplies needed.
Likewise, the Local Chief Executive require the BAC Committee to submit yearly to the DBM its Annual Procurement Plan for Common-Use Supplies and Equipment to assist the Procurement Service in strengthening its service in delivery and expanding its product list.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by management.
observations.reason: The procurement of office supplies is still not made with the PS-DBM.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
(a) instruct the Collecting Officers/Accountable Officers to prepare and submit the monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) to the Municipal Treasurer; and 

(b) require the Municipal Treasurer to consolidate the individual RAAF and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month for the timely monitoring of accountabilities on various accountable forms in compliance with Sections 50 and 51 of the NGAS Manual for Local Government Units, Volume II.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer is still in the process of reconciling the records for accountable forms.
observations.reason:
The monthly submission of CRAAF was still not made by the Municipal Treasurer.

Reiterated in Observation No. 18

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to observe the PS limitation in compliance with Section 325 of RA No. 7160. Likewise, the Municipal Mayor and OIC-Municipal Budget Officer are advised to ensure that the filling–up of vacant position and  hiring/promotion of personnel shall be suspended until the required 45/55 PS limitation is achieve.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by management.
observations.reason: The Municipality did not prioritize the implementation of the recommend-dations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to exercise due prudence in the disbursement of government funds to avoid audit suspensions/disallowances.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by management.
observations.reason: The balance remained unsettled as of December 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE:

a. Instruct the Inventory Committee to complete the required physical count of all properties at least once a year and to submit the RPCPPE to facilitate the reconciliation of the balance of the PPE with Property Cards and Property Ledger Cards every year thereafter to ensure accuracy of the PPE accounts presented in the financial statements; 



Require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain Property Cards per category of Property, Plant and Equipment as required under MNGAS for proper control and monitoring of the PPE and to facilitate identification thereof; and

Submit to the Audit Team the RPCPPE in compliance with Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs for review and verification.
observations.managementAction: The LGU through the inventory team conducted a physical count of PPE.
observations.reason:
Although the inventory team conducted the physical count of PPE, the RPCPPE was not submitted to the Audit Team and the reconciliation of the balance of the PPE with Property Cards and Property Ledger Cards was not conducted.

The Municipal Treasurer was not able to maintain the Property Cards.

No RPCPPE was prepared and submitted to the Audit Team.

Reiterated in Observation No. 5, Pages 40-42 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to review the double recording of the Mariculture Park and make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to reflect the correct amount in the books of the Municipality.
observations.reason:
The necessary adjustment was not taken up in the books of accounts. 

Reiterated in Observation No. 2, Pages 35-37 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the prior year’s audit recommendation that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to transfer the cost of Office Building from Trust Fund to General Fund and subsequently provide depreciation cost thereon and to ensure that all depreciable properties are provided with depreciation cost in compliance with International Public Sector Accounting Standard (IPSAS) 17.
observations.reason:
The Office Building was not yet transferred to the General Fund books and no provision for depreciation cost was recognized thereon in the books of accounts.

Reiterated in Observation No. 4, Pages 39-40 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE cause the immediate retrieval of all necessary documents of ownership of the unregistered lots occupied by the Municipality to facilitate the transfer and registration of ownership with the Land Registration Authority in accordance with Section 39 of PD No. 1445 and Section 449 of GAAM Volume I to establish absolute ownership and protect the interest of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer was able to register five lots in CY 2020 and first quarter of CY 2021.
observations.reason:
Land with total area of 32,835 square meters with assessed value totaling ₱1,098,290.00 remains unregistered.

Reiterated in Observation No. 15, Pages 75-76 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE ensure compliance with the provisions of Sections 3.2 and 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 by requiring the Municipal Treasurer to return the long outstanding DIT in the absence of proof of deposit and to request from all depository banks to furnish the Municipal Accountant of complete copies of Credit Memo and Debit Memo every month; and to direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately record all valid reconciling items that require adjustment and correction in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the Cash in Bank account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The long outstanding DIT was deposited by the Municipal Treasurer and reflected in the bank statement of the LGU on March 10, 2020 and the unrecorded CM was debited by the bank on December 2020.
observations.reason:
As of year-end, there were four reconciling items that were not recorded in the books in the net amount of ₱41,477.47.

Reiterated in Observation No. 18, Pages 80-81 of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE ensure strict compliance with the provisions of Section 59, Volume I of MNGAS Manual for LGUs and to:

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to payees of the existence of checks at least one month before it becomes stale. Consequently, for those unclaimed checks that become stale in her custody, return the original DV and supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books; 

Require the Municipal Accountant, upon receipt of the original DV and supporting documents of the stale checks, to prepare Journal Entry Voucher to record the transaction as Accounts Payable; and

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to process the replacement of stale checks in the hands of the payee upon submission of the stale check to the Treasury Office.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer sent notices to payees of staled checks in CY 2020.
observations.reason:
Stale Checks in the custody of the Municipal Treasurer were not returned to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books.



Staled checks in the custody of the Municipal Treasurer were not recorded as Accounts Payables.



The staled checks were not surrendered to the Municipal Treasurer for the replacement.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that that the LCE ensure compliance with the aforecited provisions of Chapter 7, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs for the fair presentation of the inventory account in the Financial Statements and provide adequate safeguard of government assets against the risk of misuse or loss and to:

Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to maintain Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively;

Instruct the end-users to prepare the RIS for every request for supplies to facilitate the preparation of SSMI by the Municipal Treasurer; and

Direct the Municipal Treasurer to complete the physical inventory of supplies and to prepare the RPCI for submission to the Audit Team not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.
observations.reason:
No Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards were maintained.

No RIS and SSMI were prepared by the end-user and Municipal Treasurer, respectively.

The RPCI was not prepared due to non-conduct of the physical inventory of supplies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE ensure strict compliance with the procurement provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and the fundamental principle on financial transactions prescribed in Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445. We also recommended that Management instruct the BAC, the BAC Secretariat, TWG, Municipal Engineer, Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to submit the required documents prescribed in conformity with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 pursuant to Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted justification on the observations and some required documents.
observations.reason: Verification showed that the supporting documents for the Rehabilitation/ Concreting of LCAR at Poblacion-Awag Road was not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE ensure strict adherence to the guidelines provided under Section 10, Annex E of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in granting time extension. Further, the LCE require the contractor concerned to submit the written consent of bondsmen and a certification from appropriate government agency on the scarcity of cement during the last quarter of 2018; otherwise, impose the collection of applicable liquidated damages.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted justification on the observations and some required documents.
observations.reason: Verification showed that Management was not able to submit the Certification from appropriate government agency on the scarcity of cement during the last quarter of 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% of the IRA in accordance with the provisions of Section 287 of the Local Government Code and Section 5.0 of the DILG and DBM Joint Circular Memorandum No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 in order to deliver the benefits of desirable social, economic and environmental outcomes to the constituents of the municipality towards the attainment of the constitutional objective of providing desired quality of life for all.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2020, the Management was able to implement 14 out of 17 projects under the 20% Development Fund.
observations.reason: The following developmental projects were not implemented: a) Concreting of Siapar Road, b) Water Way at Poblacion and c) Animal Bite Center and Social Hygiene Clinic was not implemented. However, in consideration of the effects of COVID-19 and implementation of work schedules, the audit observation was not reiterated in the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE ensure strict compliance with the procurement provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and the fundamental principle on financial transactions prescribed in Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445; and to instruct the BAC, the BAC Secretariat, TWG, Municipal Engineer, Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to submit the required documents prescribed in conformity with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 pursuant to Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.reason: The Management did not submit the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the prior year’s audit recommendation that LCE require the Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant to observe due care in charging to and payment of expenditures out of the LDRRMF to ensure that the LDRRMF is utilized solely for approved DRRM-related expenditures.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant observed due care in charging to and paying expenses charged to LDRRMF.
observations.reason:
LDRRMF funds realigned to COVID-19 PPAs were used to implement PPAs which were not supportive of COVID-19 response.

Reiterated in Observation No. 7, Pages 46-52  of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management see to it that GAD Plan of the agency will fully address and adhere to the basic purpose and objectives towards the role of women in becoming a participative partner in nation building and development, with the end view of protecting their lives and rights and to live a modest life in a society.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Plan of the LGU was endorsed to DILG for their approval before it was incorporated in the annual budget.
observations.reason:
The gender issues to be addressed by four PPAs with total allocation of ₱12,687,694.88 were not well-defined in the GAD Plan of CY 2020. 

Reiterated in Observation No. 25, Page 92-94 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU ensure that all of its insurable assets, properties and interests are insured with the GIF administered by the Government Service Insurance System in compliance with RA No. 656, otherwise known as the “Property Insurance Law”, as amended by PD No. 245 dated July 13, 1973 and Administrative Order No. 33 dated August 25, 1987 by identifying all its insurable assets, submitting an inventory report of insurable assets to the System, setting aside in its appropriation the amount needed for premiums and remitting the premiums to the System within the prescribed period to avoid unnecessary losses in the event of damage to, or loss of, assets and properties brought by fortuitous events and guarantee immediate repair or replacement and uninterrupted delivery of public service.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer provided the GSIS a listing of all insurable properties of the LGU and paid the applicable premiums for the insurance of these properties.
observations.reason:
Verification showed that properties with book value of ₱46,732,742.51 were not insured to the GSIS.

Reiterated in Observation No. 27, Pages 96-98 of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE ensure compliance with the requirements prescribed in Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 and cause the submission of copies of contracts to the Audit Team to enable the auditor to conduct timely review and technical evaluation of the contracts. 



Further, take appropriate administrative disciplinary action as provided in Section 4 of the same Circular against the responsible officer concerned for unjustified non-compliance with the Circular.
observations.managementAction: The BAC secretariat submitted the documents of bided projects to the Audit Team the soonest time possible.
observations.reason:
Records show that copies of perfected contracts other than procured through public bidding, were only submitted to the Audit Team during the monthly transmittal of accounts. Further, submissions of contracts for projects implemented through public bidding were delayed by two to 146 days from the execution of contract.

No appropriate action taken against the concerned accountable official/s for non-compliance.

Reiterated in Observation No. 19, Pages 81-82 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the personnel concerned to submit the lacking documents as prescribed in Section 5.13 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 in order to avoid the issuance of Notice of Suspension.
observations.reason: The required supporting documents were not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE ensure adherence to the provisions of Section 52 of RA No. 11260 by requiring the Human Resource Management Officer to review all applications for loan and if possible discourage the officials and employees in availing loans that will make the take home pay below the minimum amount to strengthen their ability to meet their families basic needs without resorting to debts.
observations.managementAction: No loans were approved for those employees with net take home pay below ₱5,000.00.
observations.reason: Verification showed that the net take home pay of some employees in CY 2020 were below ₱5,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE ensure collection of the delinquent accounts by adopting the procedures and remedies provided in Sections 254 and 256 of RA No. 7160, otherwise known as the “Local Government Code of 1991 and employing all legal measures to enable the collection of unpaid Real Property Tax, Special Education Tax, stall rentals, market electricity and water bills to provide additional funds to support the operations and programs of the Municipality geared towards the welfare of the constituents.
observations.reason:
No legal actions were undertaken by the LGU to enforce collection of delinquent accounts. 

Reiterated in Observation No. 23, Pages 89-90 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated the prior year’s audit recommendation that the LCE ensure strict adherence to the existing laws, rules and regulations on remittance of mandatory contributions/premiums to GSIS, PhilHealth and PAG-IBIG by requiring the Municipal Accountant to conduct a detailed review and analysis of the concerned transactions and if warranted, make the corresponding adjustments; and to cause the immediate remittance of the premiums/contributions to the government agencies concerned. This is to ensure that the members will enjoy the services and benefits offered by the said government institutions and also to avoid the interest, penalties and surcharges that may be imposed for non-compliance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has identified the details of the CY 2020 transactions and remitted the applicable premiums/contribution to the government agencies concerned.
observations.reason:
Premiums/contributions of prior years’ and current year totaling ₱596,105.79 remains unremitted/unadjusted.

Reiterated in Observation No. 28, Pages 98-100 of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated the previous year’s recommendation that Management make a follow up on the demand letters sent to debtors. In case where the debtors fail to settle their accounts after due notices, apply legal sanctions. This may help to improve the collection efficiency of the municipality wherein the funds collected may be utilized for re-loaning to other beneficiaries.

We also recommended that Management submit the following documents to enable the Audit Team to review and evaluate the nature of the fund transfer from the national government and loan agreement between the Municipality and loan borrower, to wit:

Copy of Memorandum of Agreement between the Municipality and the source agency and other supporting documents; and

Copy of Loan Agreement.
observations.managementAction: Demand Letters were sent to the debtors.
observations.reason:
No legal sanction was undertaken by the LGU to enforce collection.



Required documents were not submitted to the Audit Team. 

Reiterated in Observation No.24, Pages 90-92 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Office of the Municipal Accountant prepare a reconciliation report of reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure that all reconciling items are resolved and/or adjusted on a timely basis.

We recommend that management instruct the inventory committee to conduct physical inventory on the remaining Breeding stocks and require the maintenance of Ledger Cards and submission of monthly inventory report to the Municipal Accountant so as to validate its existence.

Require the Officer-in-Charge of the Waterworks Operation in coordination with the Municipal Accountant to identify and set up the receivables for the unpaid accounts. Likewise require the Waterworks Supervisor to regularly prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant’s Office summary reports on consumers’ meter readings, billings and payments in order that the corresponding income   and receivable accounts will be correctly/ accurately established and taken up in the books of accounts
observations.managementAction: Prior Years | Management had instructed  the Waterworks Supervisor  to prepare monthly reports on water billings and collectibles

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) create the Inventory Team to (i) undertake complete physical count of all property, plant and equipment and the inventories of the municipality; and (ii) prepare the corresponding Report of the Physical Count of PPE and Inventories and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period; (b) instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain Supplies Ledger Cards and PPE Ledger Cards; (c) direct the General Services Officer to maintain stock cards and property cards for supplies and PPE, respectively; and (d) require the General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their property records after the conduct of physical count to establish correctness of balances of PPE and Inventories accounts.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant to (i) submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements for the months of January-December 2020 for all the banks accounts maintained by the Municipality; (ii) strictly observe the timely submission of monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements; and (iii) analyze the transactions recorded in the Subsidiary Ledgers to determine the erroneous entry made which resulted to a discrepancy in the balances in the controlling accounts, the General Ledgers; and (b) Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to (i) conduct periodic reconciliation of all Cash in Bank accounts to ascertain the accuracy of the balances of accounts appearing in the Cashbooks and Accounting Financial Records; and (ii) investigate the differences and adjust the corresponding ledgers/cashbooks accordingly.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to (a) identify the problems concerning the failure to submit the transaction documents/Disbursement Vouchers and determine and implement the best possible measures for the prompt submission thereof; and (b) submit the required documents/DVs pursuant to Sections 100 and 122 of PD No. 1445.
We also recommended the withholding of salary of persons responsible for the continued failure to submit the transaction documents/Disbursement Vouchers until the submission of all the required accounts/documents for audit.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the monthly trial balances and year-end financial statements for all funds as required under Section 4.1 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010 and Section 70 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I as amended by Section 361 of Government Accounting Manual, Volume I, for LGUs.
Also, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive make a personal re-assessment and evaluation on the performance of the Accounting Unit and accordingly introduce necessary corrective measures pursuant to penalty clause under Section 5 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all subsequent financial reports are submitted within the reglementary period as prescribed in the Manual on NGAS for LGUs.
We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to withhold the salary of persons responsible for failing to submit the financial reports until the submission of all the required reports for audit.

We recommended and the Municipal Development Council headed by the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) Bids and Awards Committee to issue Notice of award (NOA) and ensure posting thereof to GPPB online portal for emergency procurement; (b) the Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from charging expenditures which are not aligned with the fund’s intended purpose; (c) Municipal Development Council to meticulously and judiciously plan the annual investment program and ensure that the PPAs implemented under the 20% Development Fund are geared towards the realization of the Municipality’s development targets and the attainment of the ultimate objective of improved quality of life for its constituents; and (d) Municipal Accountant to comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the receipt and utilization of 20% Development Fund to present fairly the expense and inventory accounts in the Financial Statements pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 and COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) require the Municipal Social Welfare Development Officer (MSWDO) to (i) take necessary action to prove the receipt of five beneficiaries that cannot be located during the conduct of
random confirmation; (ii) facilitate the certification of identification cards submitted by the beneficiaries; and (iii) ensure compliance with the guidelines on emergency program pursuant to DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 04 s. 2020; and  (b) MSWDO, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to (i) cause the submission of photos of the beneficiaries during the actual receipt of the assistance; and (ii) arrange the submitted identification cards in chronological order based on the name of beneficiaries as appearing in the payrolls.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Accountant, Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) and BAC Chairman to submit the required documents immediately to the Office of the Auditor for proper/further evaluation of the transactions pursuant to Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and in compliance with the required reportorial requirements of GPPB Circular 2020-001; (b) Municipal Accountant to (i) comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the receipt and utilization of LDRRMF to present fairly the expense and inventory accounts in the Financial Statements pursuant to COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 and COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015; (ii) ensure that all  the disbursements are supported with complete required documents before making the payment; and (iii) ensure that all  the supporting documents are properly signed and the necessary information in various forms are properly and completely filled-up;  and (c) designated MDRRMO to provide the Audit Team the copy of Projects/Programs/Activities of the allocation amounting to 3,150,000.00 to be used in validating the eligibility of the expenditures made out of the said allocation.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the (a) Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and BAC Chairman to submit the required documents immediately to the Office of the Auditor for proper/further evaluation of the transactions pursuant to Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and comply with the required reportorial requirements of GPPB Circular 2020-001; and (b) Municipal Accountant (i) ensure that all  the supporting documents are properly signed and the necessary information in various forms are properly and completely filled-up; and (ii) explain/justify the discrepancies noted in the supporting documents.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) ensure that the GAD Focal Point System are in place and fully functional; and (b) require the GAD Focal Person to (i) maintain a GAD database that could either be manually operated or developed through software; (ii) propose a training program on the HGDG in its GAD Plan and Budget for the incoming year and seek the assistance of a gender analysis specialist/expert in the use of and/or administration of the HGDG; (iii) deepen their knowledge and build their competencies on GAD-related laws and international commitments, gender mainstreaming, gender analysis, gender-responsive planning and budgeting and GAD-related tools among others; and (iv) ensure that GAD Plan and Budget be fully implemented when the current situation is back to normal.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) refrain from approving/allowing payment of claims against the government funds without complete supporting documents pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445; (b) direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness of the supporting documents before certifying box A of the Disbursement Vouchers; and (c) require the concerned Municipal Officials to submit the lacking supporting documents to properly support the validity and propriety of transactions.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to (a) refrain from charging prior year’s expenditures against the current year appropriations; (b) obligate/recognize the expenditures in the year they are incurred to reflect the correctness of accounts recorded in the books; and (c) adhere to the provisions of Local Government Code of 1991 and Section 119 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 in the use of appropriations to prevent the incurrence of irregular expenditures and to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to require all accountable officers to prepare the Report of Collections and Deposits on a daily basis to facilitate the intact remittance/deposits of collections to its depository bank pursuant to Section 63 of the Government Accounting Manual for Local Government Units, Volume I.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the designated officer responsible for the receipt, distribution and inventory of donations in-kind to submit the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances and supporting documents of the donations in-kind received from all  the donors such as the acknowledgement receipt, proof of distribution and inventory report as required under Section II.1.a of COA Circular 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020.
Management Comment:
The Municipal Administrator informed the Audit Team that donations-in-kind received by the Municipality were distributed to the intended beneficiaries. He also added that they are not aware with the provisions on the proper reporting of the said donations. Nevertheless, they committed to observe with the rules related thereto in the future.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive and the Local School Board agreed to (a) conduct proper review/evaluation on programs/projects/activities to ensure that only eligible expenses shall be charged to Special Education Fund (SEF) in compliance with the Section 272 of RA No. 7160 and DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017; and (b) discontinue charging to SEF the medical assistance granted to DepEd teachers/utility personnel.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to provide legal basis for the payment of OT services to the Assistant Municipal Accountant, otherwise, cause the refund of the amount paid.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman, Human Resources and Management Officer and  General Services Officer/ICO-Municipal Treasurer to: (i) strictly comply with COA Circular Nos. 96-010 and 2009-001 requiring the submission of Purchase/Job Orders/Contracts and its supporting documents within five (5) days after perfection; and (ii) furnish the Office of the Auditor copy of Notices of Deliveries within twenty four (24) hours from acceptance to ensure timely review/evaluation of the contracts and inspection of deliveries, respectively, on a sampling basis.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) instruct the Collecting Officers/Accountable Officers to prepare and submit the monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) to the Municipal Treasurer; and (b) require the Municipal Treasurer to consolidate the individual RAAF and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month for the timely monitoring of accountabilities on various accountable forms in compliance with Section 51 of the Government Accounting Manual for Local Government Units, Volume II.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to (i) submit to Office of the Auditor all the aforesaid cancelled checks for inspection; and (ii) henceforth, ensure that all cancelled checks are attached to the Report of Checks Issued and reported chronologically with the other checks issued with the word Cancelled clearly indicated in the face of the check.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue to remit all the taxes withheld within ten (10) days following the end of the month in compliance with the Revenue Memorandum Circular (RMC) No. 23-2012 dated February 14, 2012.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to remit intact all compulsory contributions/loan repayments withheld to the GSIS and adhere the requirements of Republic Act No. 8291.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to remit promptly all compulsory contributions/amortization withheld to the Pag-IBIG within the reglementary period.
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue to remit promptly all compulsory contributions withheld to the PhilHealth.
We recommended and management agreed to exercise due prudence in the disbursement of government funds to avoid audit suspensions/disallowances.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to further review/analyze the transactions in prior years pertaining to the discrepancy of ₱3,362,538.20 and take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to reconcile the balances of the Due from and Due to Other Funds accounts.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to correct the double entry made in recognizing the cost and the corresponding depreciation for the Mariculture Park amounting to ₱2,066,495.00 and ₱189,773.12, respectively, to present fairly the balances of Other Structures account, Accumulated Depreciation-Other Structures account, Depreciation Expense-Buildings and Other Structures accounts and Government Equity in the financial statements.
The suggested adjusting entry is as follows:
Accumulated Depreciation-Other Structures              189,773.12
Government Equity				       1,955,248.69
Other Structures     				          2,066,495.00
Depreciation Expense-Buildings and Other Structures	               78,526.81

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to record the cost of the completed RHU 2 amounting to ₱10,785,844.00 in the General Fund books to present fairly the balances of Buildings, Depreciation Expense-Buildings and Other Structures, Accumulated Depreciation - Buildings and Government Equity accounts in the financial statements.
The following is the suggested adjusting entry:

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to transfer the cost of Office Building from Trust Fund to General Fund and subsequently provide depreciation cost thereon and to ensure that all depreciable properties are provided with depreciation.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain the Property Cards per category of PPE which are necessary to support the accuracy and reliability of the balances of PPE account in the financial statements.
We also recommended that Management require the Inventory Committee and the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the RPCPPE with the accounting records and apply the guidelines and procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 for the noted discrepancies.

We recommended that Management:
Require the BAC, with the assistance of the BAC Secretariat, to post all NOA, contract, or Purchase Order in the website of the LGU or any conspicuous place in the premises of the PE and GPPB online portal for Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act for information purposes and to require the submission of the mandatory documentary requirements from the suppliers, distributors, manufacturers or contractors concerned;
Require the Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the  Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (MSWDO) and Municipal Health Officer (MHO), to submit the list of affected families and proof of receipt of distribution of relief goods, medicines and vitamins and use of testing kits and other supporting documents; and
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to require the supplier to provide the Sales Invoices for the payments made.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the MSWDO and Municipal Treasurer, to submit the copy of valid ID, Barangay Certification with specimen signature of the beneficiary provided in the schedule and the certificate of eligibility of the 132 beneficiaries in Barangay Sablig for further evaluation of the Audit Team; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant, with the assistance of the MSWDO, to issue demand letters to the five recipients of financial subsidy who were not listed in the Certificate of Eligibility to cause the refund of the financial subsidy amounting to ₱27,500.00 and return the amounts collected to the DSWD.

We recommended that Management require the BAC, with the assistance of the TWG and BAC Secretariat, to strictly comply with the procurement provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and the fundamental principle on financial transactions prescribed in Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445. We also recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the required documents.

We recommended that Management require the BAC, with the assistance of the TWG and BAC Secretariat to strictly comply with the procurement provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and the fundamental principle on financial transactions prescribed in Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445; and to instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents in conformity with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 pursuant to Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that Management require the BAC, with the assistance of the TWG and BAC Secretariat to:
Post the Invitation to Bid, NOA, Contract and NTP in the LGU website, require the bidders to use the prescribed forms in the bidding documents and emphasize the possibility of non-acceptance of bid for non-compliance and indicate their initial in every page of the original copies of the bid received and opened;
Ensure that equipment to be pledged by a bidder on a particular project are not the same equipment listed on another project where the bidder is already declared the bidder with lowest calculated responsive bid especially when the construction period will coincide and that the winning bidder post the performance security prior to the signing of the contract; and
Require the Municipal Engineer to supervise the construction of a project to ensure that the construction will commence only after the issuance and receipt of the NTP by the winning bidder.
We also recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking documents.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Procuring Officer to indicate the complete item name, complete detailed item specification, including accessories i.e., narrative description, size/dimension/volume, model, use of item, color, capacity, horsepower, voltage, watts, gauge, kind of equipment where spare part is to be used whether replacement or original (for spare parts), part number of spare parts, type and classification of material, brand new or second hand and brand name of the items to be procured in the Purchase Order;
Require the Inspection Officer to verify and inspect carefully all purchases made by the LGU to ensure its conformity with specifications provided in the Purchase Order; and
Require the supplier to replace the delivered 10 Wheeler Dump Truck and Mini Dump Truck in conformity with the specifications indicated in the Purchase Order and bid proposal, respectively.

We recommended that Management require Municipal Treasurer, with the assistance of the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Accountant, to cause the immediate retrieval of all necessary documents of ownership of the unregistered parcels of land and to facilitate the transfer and registration of ownership with the Land Registration Authority in order to establish absolute ownership and protect the interest of the local government unit. We further recommended that Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds which shall cover the expenses on the transfer and registration of parcels of land.
We also recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to gather the necessary documents and record in the books of accounts the four parcels of land with total land area of 10,212 square meters.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to determine the discrepancies noted and effect necessary adjustments for the noted errors in both records of Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile the cashbooks with  the subsidiary ledgers to ensure reliability and correctness of balances of said accounts.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to regularly reconcile the subsidiary ledger balances of the Cash in Bank-LCCA accounts with the cashbook balances every end of the month.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to make a follow-up letter to Land Bank of the Philippines to provide the details of the Debit Memo dated September 14, 2020 and Credit Memo dated August 8, 2019 and January 24, 2020 and request all depository banks to furnish the Municipal Accountant of copies of Credit Memo and Debit Memo every month.
Further, we recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to immediately record the amount of Credit Memo and Debit Memo in the books of accounts once the details of the Credit Memo and Debit Memo are obtained to present the correct balances of the Cash in Bank account in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management instruct the BAC Chairman, with the assistance of the BAC Secretariat, to observe the prescribed period of submission of five working days from the execution of a contract in order to gain the benefits of facilitating the review and evaluation process and obtaining suggested corrective measures on the defects and deficiencies noted in the contract and its supporting documents which are relevant in decision making.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to monitor the remittance of collections of all collecting officers by verifying the RCD, checking the statement of accountable forms as to initial balances on hand, receipts, issuance and the ending balances on hand, making physical count of the accountable forms remaining in the custody of the collectors and comparing the same against the ending balance on hand before affixing her signature at the back of the triplicate copy of the last official receipt issued. We also recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to count the money turned over to her before signing the certification and receipt portion in the RCD to provide adequate safeguard of the government funds against possible loss or misuse.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board (MSWB) to coordinate with the Barangay Solid Waste Management Committee and to require therefrom a monitoring report on the operation of the MRF in every barangay;
Instruct the MSWMB to maintain records on the volume of waste collected and disposed to enable the monitoring of the implementation of the solid waste management program; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer to collect the garbage fees imposed in Section 49, Article XI of the Municipal Ordinance No. 2.

We recommended that Management ensure the immediate operation of the Anda RHU 2 by requiring the MHO to secure the Letter of Acceptance from the DOH and to facilitate the immediate preparation of project proposal for the needed equipment of the RHU under the HFEP of the DOH so that the constituents of the LGU may already avail of the basic, essential as well as the specialized health services offered by the Health Facility Enhancement Program.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to ensure collection of the delinquent accounts on real property tax and special education tax by requiring the Municipal Treasurer to adopt effective collection system by sending notice to delinquent taxpayers and request the assistance of barangay officials.
We also recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to send notice to market stall owners to settle their unpaid rental fees and electric bills and to the water concessionaires for the unpaid water bills in order to provide additional funds to support the operations and programs of the LGU geared towards the welfare of the constituents.

We reiterated the previous year’s recommendation that Management make a follow up on the demand letters sent to debtors. In case where the debtors fail to settle their accounts after due notices, apply legal sanctions. This may help to improve the collection efficiency of the LGU wherein the funds collected may be utilized for re-loaning to other beneficiaries.
We also recommended that Management submit the following documents to enable the Audit Team to review and evaluate the nature of the fund transfer from the national government and loan agreement between the Municipality and loan borrower, to wit:
Copy of Memorandum of Agreement between the Municipality and the source agency and other supporting documents; and
Copy of Loan Agreement between the LGU and the debtors.
Further, we recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to send letter to the source agency to inquire on the terms and conditions of the fund transfer received and to obtain a copy of the written agreement and to submit a copy thereof to Audit Team.

We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to identify the specific gender issues caused by the unequal situation between women and men in the locality in the GAD Plan and Budget and to formulate PPAs that are responsive to these gender issues in order to attain the main intentions in promoting gender-responsive governance, protection and fulfilment of women’s human rights and the promotion of women’s economic empowerment.
We further recommended that Management require the GFPS to prioritize in the succeeding GAD Plan and Budget the recurring/emerging gender issues in the LGU, results of gender assessment of the organization or its PPAs and other GAD PPAs identified in the GAD Code of the LGU that have not been considered in the previous year to achieve a more effective GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommended that Management require the designated employee or official in the issuance of driver’s trip ticket to see to it that the required information are duly indicated before issuance and to oblige the driver of each government vehicle to transcribe or summarize all the trip tickets issued during the month in the Monthly Report of Official Travels in chronological order to support the reasonableness and propriety of gasoline expenses.

We recommended that Management insure all of its insurable assets relevant in the delivery of public service to provide safeguard against substantial losses in the event of damage to, or loss of, properties brought by fortuitous events by requiring the Municipal Treasurer with the assistance of the Municipal Accountant to identify the insurable assets and to submit the inventory report to the GSIS for evaluation; and to instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds needed for the payment of premiums.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to conduct a detailed review and analysis of the Due to PhilHealth account and Due to Pag-IBIG account in prior years and if warranted, make the corresponding adjustments; and to cause the immediate remittance of the premiums/contributions to the government agencies concerned.
We also recommended that Management ensure timely remittance of premiums to GSIS by requiring the Human Resource and Management Office to prepare and transmit promptly the required forms in updating the records of the personnel to the GSIS to update the personal records of employees both in the LGU and GSIS.

We recommend that management provide a written explanation to justify the non-posting in the online GPPB portal, appropriate documents relative to the procurement projects undertaken under the Bayanihan to Heal as One Act.

We recommend that management provide justification through written explanation why the Purchase Orders / Contracts do not indicate the required warranty clause or provide additional documentary requirement that would show the same.

We recommend that management require the Bids and Awards Committee to submit justification for the procurement of the aforecited items above the suggested price of the DOH.  Henceforth, procure medical supplies at reasonable prices guided by the Standard Retail Price (SRP) set by appropriate government agencies to ensure procurement that is most advantageous to the government.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to create a special account in the General Fund for the Bayanihan Grant to Cities and Municipalities. Separate financial statements should be prepared and presented as part of the General Fund for easy monitoring of the utilization and to ensure the availability of this fund to support COVID prevention programs and projects.

We recommend that management re-evaluate the payroll in the liquidation of Social Amelioration Program to determine the propriety of the payments made and submit justification as to the noted observation. Furthermore, we recommend that in the forthcoming transactions, documents should bear proper approval by authorized signatories before disbursements or payments are made.

We recommend that management submit a certified list of items contained in the food packs and relief goods distributed to the beneficiaries with corresponding amount to account the delivery and actual distribution of items procured.  Henceforth, ensure payments be properly supported with complete documentation to establish the propriety, validity and completeness of transactions.

We recommend that management require the inventory committee to provide the necessary details in the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to support the validity and reliability of the PPE accounts reported in the financial statements.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer reconcile their books periodically, at least quarterly, so as to ascertain the accuracy and validity of the Cash in Treasury account.  Moreover, we recommend that the collections pertaining to the SEF also be recorded in the fund’s Cash in Local Treasury account.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory Committee guided by the provisions of COA Circular 2020-06 and that the Internal Auditor shall only serve as a witness to the inventory taking.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the 
(a) Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Certified List of Taxpayers and tax due and furnish the OIC-Municipal Accountant; and 

(b) OIC-Municipal Accountant to set-up the RPT/SET Receivables based on the Certified List of Taxpayers and tax due and active taxpayers, to support the general ledger balances and facilitate monitoring and reconciliation of tax collections and delinquencies.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer already prepared the Certified List of Taxpayers and furnished the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with Section 122 of PD 1445 and Section 7.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 on the rendition of accounts to ensure timely review and verification of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted the transactions documents.
observations.reason: Some documents are still not submitted.

observations.managementAction: The LDRRM Officer submitted the required lacking documents and justification and ensure that charging of expenses for 5% Calamity Fund is among the required expenditures set in the guidelines.
observations.reason: The BAC failed to submit the justification on the Resolution Issued.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Development Council headed by the Local Chief Executive to:

(a) specifically describe the projects for inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan, and always secure approval or authorization of the Sanggunian before incurring expenditures and/or entering into contract in the implementation of projects described in generic terms; and 

(b) meticulously and judiciously plan the annual investment program and ensure that the PPAs implemented under the 20% Development Fund are geared towards the realization of the Municipality’s development targets and the attainment of the ultimate objective of improved quality of life for its constituents.
observations.reason: The disbursement still includes items that should not be charged against the 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local School Board headed by the Local Chief Executive and District Supervisor as co-Chairman to:
(a) conduct a thorough review and evaluation of the planning and budgeting practices of the Municipality; and 

(b) prepare the Annual Budget using the prescribed format in compliance with the provisions of Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2017 of the Department of Education, Department of Budget and Management and Department of the Interior and Local Government.
observations.managementAction: Programs/ projects/ activities funded in SEF is aligned with the guidelines.
observations.reason: Some programs  indicated in the plan should not be charged against the SEF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the:
(a) PESO Manager, OIC-Municipal Accountant and BAC Committee to submit the lacking documentary requirements to support the claim; 

(b) PESO Manager submit the approved copy of the Master List in order to verify the rightful beneficiaries; and 

(c) BAC Committee ensure that bidding documents submitted by the bidder bear their proper initials as part of control in reviewing the documents during the conduct of bidding.
observations.managementAction: Instructions were given to the concerned official to comply with the recommenda-tions.
observations.reason: The submission of lacking documents is still not made by the concerned officials.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the:
(a) Market Supervisor to:
(i) ensure that all lessees have valid contracts; 

(ii) limit the contract period to two years; and 

(iii) secure authorization from the Sangguniang Bayan allowing the Municipal Mayor to enter into a contract with the lessee; and 

(iv) initiate extra efforts to enforce collection of unpaid rentals and impose penalties on delayed payments, as the case maybe; 

(b) OIC-Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers to monitor payment of rental of each stallholders; and 

(c) ICO-Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the Official Receipts are properly and completely accomplished to include stall numbers and rental period.
observations.managementAction: The management complied with the recommenda-tions
observations.reason: Lack of manpower who will maintain the subsidiary ledgers. Likewise, imposing of penalties for delayed payment is still not implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
