(b) Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to draft proposed implementing guidelines, policies and procedures in granting financial assistance in the municipality which should be aligned to the prescribed guidelines of the DSWD for submission to and approval of the Sanggunian and the Municipal Mayor; and 

(c) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to use appropriate account corresponding to every government transactions.
observations.managementAction: The management complied with the recommend-dations
observations.reason: Draft guidelines not yet approved by the Sangguniang Bayan.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the: 
(a)OIC-Municipal Accountant to ensure that correct account are used in recording the transactions pertaining to income and conduct proper review to ensure that disbursement are covered by valid appropriations; and 

(b) Municipal Treasurer and Asst. Municipal Treasurer to provide legal basis for the payment of OT services; otherwise, cause the refund of the amount received.
observations.managementAction: Service Fee from PhilHealth are now recognized under General Fund account.
Prudence in the use of government funds is now being observed by the Municipality.
observations.reason: Refund of excess payment and submission of lacking documents are still not done.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials, particularly the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman and the General Services Officer to:
(i) strictly comply with COA Circular Nos. 96-010 and 2009-001 requiring the submission of Purchase Orders and supporting documents within five (5) days after perfection; and 
(ii) furnish the Office of the Auditor copy of Notices of Deliveries within twenty four (24) hours from acceptance to ensure timely review/ evaluation of the contracts and inspection of deliveries, respectively, on a sampling basis.
observations.managementAction: The GSO Officer submitted the Purchase Order.
observations.reason:
The submission of Purchase Order is not religiously made. The documents required under COA Circular No. 96-010 are not submitted within five days. Also, notification of deliveries to the Auditor is not made.

Reiterated in Observation No. 17

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
(a) ensure that the GAD Focal Point System are in place and fully functional; and 

(b) instruct the GAD Focal Person to strictly adhere with the provision of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 regarding submission of Accomplishment Report within five (5) working days from the end of January of the preceding year.
observations.managementAction: The Accomplish-ment Report for CY 2020 is already submitted to DILG.
observations.reason: Re-organization of the GFPS is still not made.

observations.managementAction: Granting of Cash advances only to the employee concerned is now being observed by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the:
(a) Acting Municipal General Services Officer to:
(i) submit a Report of Physical Count for Property, Plant and Equipment for all the assets and properties of the Municipality to the GSIS; and 

(ii) that all insurable assets and properties are insured against any insurable risk with the GIF of the GSIS in compliance with RA No. 656 otherwise known as the Property Insurance Law, as amended; and 

(b) OIC-Municipal Budget Officer appropriate funds in the annual budget the cost of insurance expenses of the properties of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Some motor vehicle were insured to GIF of the GSIS.
observations.reason: There was no appropriations for the insurance of other properties of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
(a) instruct the Municipal Accountant to conduct proper review of the submitted supporting documents before certifying the box A of the Disbursement Voucher; and 

(b) instruct the Human Rersource and Management Officer to:
(i) review the purpose indicated in the Travel Order submitted by employees before approving the same; 

(ii) limit the number of personnel doing the same purpose of travel, especially if the purpose can be done by only one person; and 

(iii) require all employees to clearly specified the purpose of travel to support claims.
observations.managementAction: The management complied with the recommend-dations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to remit all the taxes withheld within ten (10) days following the end of the month in compliance with the Revenue Memorandum Circular (RMC) No. 23-2012 dated February 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The correct withholding and prompt remittance is consistently observed by the concerned officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue to remit promptly all compulsory contributions/loan repayments withheld to the GSIS and adhere the requirements of Republic Act No. 8291.
observations.managementAction: The correct withholding is consistently observed by the concerned officials.
observations.reason: Intact remittance of full amount is not observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to remit promptly all compulsory contributions/amortization withheld to the Pag-IBIG within the reglementary period.
observations.managementAction: The correct withholding is consistently observed by the concerned officials.
observations.reason: Intact remittance of full amount is not observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue to remit promptly all compulsory contributions withheld to the PhilHealth.
observations.managementAction: The correct withholding and prompt remittance is consistently observed by the concerned officials.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE: 

Require the Inventory Committee to conduct an inventory and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Local Road Network at the end of every accounting period; and



Require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to reconcile the list of inventory per local road inventory report and road map with those recorded in the Registry of Public Infrastructure.
observations.managementAction: The Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network and Local Road Network Property Cards were submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason:
The Management submitted the Report on the Physical Count of the Local Road Network to the Audit Team.


The Inventory Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network did not reconcile with the totals of the Local Road Network Property Cards and the balance of the Road Networks account presented in the financial statements. Also, the reconciliation of the road map with those recorded in the Registry of Public Infrastructure could not be validated due to non-submission of the road map and Registry of Public Infrastructure.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendations that the LCE take the following courses of action:

Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to determine the discrepancies noted and effect necessary adjustments for the noted errors in both records of Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to reconcile the subsidiary ledger with cashbook pursuant to Section 10 of MNGAS for LGUs Volume II, Section 111 of PD No. 1445 and Item F, Section 63 of the Local Treasury Operations Manual to ensure reliability and correctness of balances of said accounts;

Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to regularly reconcile the subsidiary ledger balances of the Cash in Bank-LCCA accounts with the cashbook balances every end of the month; and



Take appropriate action against the responsible officers for non-compliance and repeated unjustified failure to comply with the requirements pursuant to Section 127 of PD No.1445.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer has already adjusted the reconciling items identified in prior years in respective books. On-going reconciliation is being done on the remaining discrepancies.



Reconciliation of the subsidiary ledger balances with the cashbook balances was done every end of the month as evidenced by significant decrease in discrepancies by 98.68 percent.
observations.reason:
Verification stilled showed unreconciled difference between Subsidiary Ledger with the Cashbook in the amount of ₱104,473.97.

Reiterated in Observation No. 17, Pages 78-80 of the report.



Verification showed that discrepancy between the cashbook and subsidiary ledger was reduced by 98.68 percent as the result of the reconciliation done by the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant.



No appropriate action taken against the concerned accountable official/s for non-compliance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to further review/analyze prior year’s transactions pertaining to the discrepancy of ₱3,362,538.20 and take up the necessary adjustments to correct the balances of the affected accounts. 



Further, we recommended the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular review and reconciliation of reciprocal accounts and effect adjustments periodically, as identified and substantiated, to prevent existence of long outstanding unadjusted reconciling items.
observations.managementAction:
The Accounting Office reviewed the entries in the GL for CYs 2014 to 2016 and taken-up in the books the necessary adjustments.



The Municipal Accountant conducted regular review and reconciliation of the reciprocal accounts.
observations.reason:
The reciprocal accounts showed a net difference of ₱3,362,538.20 after consolidation of all funds pertaining to prior years.

Reiterated in Observation No. 1, Pages 34-35 of the report.

Verification showed that unreconciled amount did not increase in CY 2020.

observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant religiously makes follow-ups on the settlement of cash advances.

Demand Letters were issued to the accountable officers in C Y 2020.

Management through the Municipal Accountant assured that necessary supporting documents were attached to the DV.

The Municipal Mayor issued an Executive Order on the procurement of airline tickets.

Management through the Municipal Accountant assured that necessary supporting documents were attached to the DV.
observations.reason:
Some cash advances remain unliquidated.



Prior years’ unliquidated cash advances remains unsettled despite issuance of demand letter. 

Post-audit of CY 2020 liquidation reports showed that disbursements for membership fees and term dues were supported with official receipt.

Post-audit of CY 2020 liquidation reports showed that plane tickets were directly obtained from airline companies.

Post-audit of liquidation reports showed that some documentary requirements were not submitted.

Reiterated in Observation No. 16, Pages 76-78 of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer refrain from assigning the disbursement of her cash advance for load allowance, payroll and honorarium to the Assistant Municipal Treasurer in compliance with the provisions of Section 75 of PD No. 1445 and Section 4.1.6 of COA Circular No. 97-002. 

It was further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer discuss her concerns on workloads to the LCE and make a recommendation to designate a new disbursing officer for payroll.
observations.managementAction:
The payrolls for the months of November and December 2020 were already under the name of the Assistant Municipal Treasurer.

The Assistant Municipal Treasurer was appointed as the disbursing officer for payroll.
observations.reason:
Verification showed that the payrolls for November 2020 and December 2020 were under the name of the Assistant Municipal Treasurer.



Verification showed that the payrolls for November 2020 and December 2020 were under the name of the Assistant Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE ensure compliance with Section 3, paragraph 3 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 and Sections 5.1.2 and 5.1.3 dated COA Circular No. 2012-002 by requiring the LDRRMO to include the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years to ensure implementation of projects and activities for optimal utilization of the fund.
observations.managementAction: The unexpended LDRRMF of prior years were included in the LDRRMFIP of the current year.
observations.reason: Verification showed that the unexpended LDRRMF of prior years were included in the LDRRMFIP for CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant to submit the original Disbursement Voucher supported with complete documents as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-001 and the Municipal Treasurer to secure the Sales Invoices for the payments made to the supplier concerned amounting to P299,320.00. We also recommended that Management cause the refund of the amount of P4,600.00 from the supplier concerned due to erroneous computation.
observations.managementAction: Disbursement Voucher No. 100-19-09-22247 and its supporting documents was submitted to the Audit Team on November 21, 2019. The overpayment of ₱4,600.00 was refunded  on September 23, 2020 under OR No. 6903186.
observations.reason: Verification showed that Disbursement Voucher No. 100-19-09-22247 and its supporting documents was submitted to the Audit Team on November 21, 2019. Also, copy of OR No. 6903186 was provided to the Audit Team for the refund of ₱4,600.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE instruct the GFPS to monitor the implementation of GAD PPAs as required under JMC No. 2013-01 and to conduct regular coordination with the implementing offices to ensure that plans are undertaken.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Plan was fully implemented.
observations.reason: Verification showed that all PPAs included in the CY 2020 GAD Plan were implement by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE ensure compliance with Sections 2 and 16 of RA No. 9003 by instructing the employees concerned to expedite completion and approval of the revised 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan in order to have concrete guidelines that will be adopted for an efficient implementation of programs and activities for the segregation, collection, transport, storage, processing, treatment and disposal of solid waste towards ensuring protection of public health and its environment.
observations.managementAction: The 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan for its Ecological Waste Management Program was approved by the NSWMC of the DENR on December 9, 2020 under NSWMC Resolution No. 1409, Series of 2020.
observations.reason: The copy of the approved 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan was submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE ensure compliance with Sections 4(1) and 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 in the utilization of SEF funds by submitting the required LSB Resolution and to:

Require the Accounting Office to ensure that disbursements are supported with complete documents as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-001 and other documents necessary under the circumstances to prove the propriety and validity of the transactions;

Require the recipients schools of the LGU counterpart in Brigada Eskwela 2019 and Municipal, Unit and District Meet to submit the pertinent documents to support the disbursement of funds; and 



Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that expenses charged to SEF budget have specific appropriation, otherwise, seek an authorization from the Local School Board to support the authority of its execution.
observations.managementAction:
Disbursement out of SEF were supported with complete documents.



The required supporting documents were submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason:
Post-audit of disbursement out of SEF showed complete documentation.



Post-audit of the submitted documents for the LGU counterpart in Brigada Eskwela 2019 and Municipal, Unit and District Meet showed complete documentation.

The expenses for Brigada Eskwela 2020 were not supported with Board Resolution authorizing the use of SEF fund despite that Brigada Eskwela is not among the PPAs identified in the SEF Budget for CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE ensure compliance with the provisions of Sections 5.3, 5.5 and 5.6 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-5A dated October 7, 2005 by requiring the officials and employees concerned to submit the Notice of Award to support the claims for honoraria; otherwise, cause the refund of the excess honoraria amounting to P295,574.00 from the payees.
observations.managementAction: Documents needed for the 25 bided projects were submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Post-audit of the documents for CY 2019 BAC honoraria showed that the BAC was able to bid 25 projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE ensure compliance with the bonding of accountable officers whose duties requires the possession, custody or control of funds or properties pursuant to Item F, Section 305 of RA No. 7160 and COA Circular No. 2006-005 and cause the immediate application/renewal of the bond of the accountable officer to ensure that the interest of the government is safeguarded in case of loss or misappropriation of public funds or properties.

It was further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer review the cash accountabilities of the collecting officers from time to time and recommend adjustment of their bond coverage whenever necessary to ensure that all collecting officers are adequately bonded before discharging their functions.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer renewed the bonds of the collecting officers.



The bond coverage of the collecting officers was adjusted and maximum accountability was observed.
observations.reason:
Verification showed that two bonded collecting officers has gap in the effectivity of their bond coverage.






Comparison of the bond coverage and the maximum amount of remitted collection showed that cash accountabilities of collecting officers are within the bond coverage.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer require all collecting officers to remit their collections to the Treasurer’s Office within the prescribed period in compliance with Section 29, Volume 1 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs to safeguard the government funds against possible loss or misuse. 

It is further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure that collecting officers remit their collections daily and avoid delay in the remittance thereof considering the distance of the place of their assignment from the Treasury Office.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer conducted a meeting with the collecting officers reminding them to observe the prescribed period in remitting their collections.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Treasurer submitted a signed letter of the collecting officers committing to remit their collection within the prescribed period.



Post-audit of collections and deposits disclosed two to six days for the collecting officers to remit their collections to the Municipal Treasurer/Liquidating Officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure that all Accountable Forms are issued in numerical sequence in compliance with Section 24 of COA Memorandum No. 2013-004 and Section 30 of the New Government Accounting System Manual.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer directed all collectors to properly check the numerical sequence of accountable forms when issuing the official receipts.
observations.reason: In the post-audit of collections and deposits, the Audit Team observe that issuance of accountable forms were in sequential order.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare and submit the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable forms to the Audit Team within the prescribed period to facilitate the verification of the correctness and propriety of the quantities and inclusive serial numbers of all the accountable forms received, issued or cancelled as well as the monitoring of balances of accountable forms.
observations.managementAction: CRAAF were submitted to the Audit Team.
observations.reason: Verification showed that monthly CRAAF were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to conduct investigation on the subsidiary ledgers comprising the various liability accounts with negative balances and prepare the adjustments where necessary. Moreover, we recommend that appropriate disclosures be indicated in the Notes to Financial Statements as to these abnormal balances noted.
observations.managementAction: Reconcile SLs and schedules

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Bids and Awards Committee to observe strictly Sections 20.1 and 20.2 of the IRR of R.A 9184 to ensure the readiness of all aspects concerning the procurement of the project. Moreover, we recommend that management justify their failure to conduct the pre-procurement conference in this particular project.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted minutes of Preprocurement conference

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Accountant to monitor and control disbursement of the funds by not exceeding fifty (50%) of the uncollected estimated revenue in addition to the actual collections so that no cash overdraft in any local fund shall be incurred at the end of the year.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management make appropriate revision of the GAD Plan and Budget in the succeeding years and ensure that the total cost of the related activities is at least 5% of the total budget appropriations.
observations.managementAction: 5% budget is streamlined through different programs

observations.recommendation: In view of the Supreme Court’s decision pronouncing the unconstitutionality of the PDAF, we recommend that the remaining funds shall no longer be released but shall be returned to the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) so that it can be used for other priority programs of the government.
observations.managementAction: Amount was already returned.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (MRAAF) properly in order to give accurate movements of accountable forms. Moreover, it is recommended that the Municipal Treasurer reconcile his monthly RAAF prepared to the RAAF produced by the system in order to prevent misuse or loss of said forms
observations.managementAction: Management complied

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct the Municipal Accountant to disclose relevant and significant information not presented on the face of the financial statements so as to give valuable information relevant to the accounts thus, give the stakeholders understanding thereof and present the financial statements fairly.
observations.managementAction: Relevant and significant information have already been disclosed in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management explain the hiring of consultants that did not undergo Public Bidding or any other alternative mode of procurement as required in RA 9184 or the Government Procurement Law.

We recommend:
a) Grant the subsequent cash advances for intelligence and confidential fund only after the submission of liquidation vouchers to the COA Chairman or after the issuance of credit notice from the COA Chairman in accordance with COA Circular No. 2003-003.

b)Require the concerned official to immediately follow up the credit notices from COA Central Office on the submitted liquidations for its settlement.
observations.managementAction: Follow-up letters were sent to COA Central Office

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management, in coordination with the Local Sanggunian and the Local Budget Officer, create the other three positions and appoint all these  personnel to conduct Disaster Risk Reduction and Management  (DRRM) activities in compliance with Section 2, Rule 6 of the IRR of RA10121.
observations.managementAction: HRMO and the MBO have already talked and planned. However for year 2020, they focused mainly on COVID response.

observations.managementAction: Continue exercise of taxing authority even amidst COVID-19

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management closely monitor and ensure that the LDRRM planned mitigation/disaster preparedness program/projects/activities are fully implemented so that intended purpose is attained/achieved.
observations.managementAction: Continued utilization of the LDDRM fund and focus programs in COVID Response

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer be required to make representation to the Bureau of Internal Revenue for the computation of taxes due them from the exercise of corporate power in the LGU’s Quinale Beach Bar.
observations.managementAction: Representation has been made by the management. However, there is still no response from the BIR

observations.recommendation: Require the immediate refunds from all concerned officials and employees who receive the Extra Cash Gift of P18, 000.00.

a. Require the Municipal Accountant to send confirmation and/or demand letters to individuals concerned.

b. For receivables from officials and employees arising from overpayment of claims, the Municipal Treasurer shall institute deductions on their subsequent claims and salaries in satisfaction of their indebtedness
observations.managementAction: Awaiting for the settlement of other concerned employees.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management :

a) Exercise prudence and economy in the disbursement and utilization of government funds.  Be guided with  COA Circular No. 85-55A on the   prevention of the incurrence of irregular, unnecessary, excessive or extravagant expenditures and Administrative Order No. 103 on the continued adoption and implementation  of the austerity measures.  

b) Minimize  or   stop  the       practice  of  serving  meals  and  snacks   during  regular monthly meetings or conferences except on seminars and trainings when catering services are part of the activity design and properly funded.

c) Utilize the LGU’s limited resources to projects, plans and programs that would benefit the majority of its constituents.
observations.managementAction: Well taken and noted by the officials concerned

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to closely coordinate on the immediate implementation of the priority development projects, programs and activities funded under the 20% Development Fund during the year the funds are actually appropriated in order to ensure that the intended beneficiaries could avail of the maximum benefits and the desired outcomes from the said programs or projects.
observations.managementAction: Continued utilization of the 20% DF, some programs and projects were realigned for COVID Response

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant, guided by COA Circular 2016-005, initiate the verification, validation, review and analysis of the abovementioned non-moving accounts to effect necessary adjusting/ correcting journal entries for the eventual disposition thereof. Further, the said accounts can be requested for write-off as these affect the fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Accounts were being reviewed and is already in the Sanggunian for review

observations.recommendation:
a) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to require or demand for the issuance of an official receipt of the government by the recipient Leagues concerned in order to support the release of  financial aid to the latter, pursuant to Section 69 of the GAAM, Volume I.

b) Require the Municipal Accountant to monitor the utilization of the funds released to these Leagues by requiring the recipient Leagues concerned to submit liquidation reports. In this manner, the propriety and validity of the expenditures could be properly evaluated.
observations.managementAction: No action taken since they can’t require the different leagues in using official receipt of the Republic of the Philippines. Moreover, receipts used by the different leagues are registered with the BIR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the 

(a) Municipal Treasurer and BPLO Officer to 
(i) require the former BPLO Officer to explain in writing the noted discrepancy in the Mayor’s Permit issued to SJ Pen Enterprises; 

(ii) require the owner of the business to submit proof of payment for CYs 2015-2017 to determine the propriety of the transactions with SJ Pen Enterprises; and 

(b) Municipal Accountant and BAC Officials to ensure that the Municipality procure items only with business establishments who has the authority to engage/conduct business transactions; and 

(c) Municipal Accountant to ensure that payee indicated in the DVs is the name of the business and not of the owner to avoid encashing of checks by another person.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Treasurer ensure that payment of check is under the name of business and not under the name of the owner to avoid encashing of the check by other person.

Management also ensure that procurement will be made only with those who has the authority to engage/ conduct business transactions.
observations.reason: No action taken by management to implement the other recommen-dation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to

(ii) ensure that all payment of government transactions are supported with complete documentation before certifying Box A of the Disbursement Vouchers.

(a) Municipal Treasurer and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to explain and make justification on the inconsistencies in the dates of the supporting documents attached in the Disbursement Voucher for consultancy services in preparing maps and socio-economic profile of the Municipality;

(c) Municipal Accountant to properly scrutinize the transactions before signing the Box A of the Disbursement Voucher to ensure validity, propriety and legality of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by management to comply with the recommenda-tions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Engineer to ensure/monitor the posting of signboards containing relevant information of the PPAs of the Municipality in either of the forms stated in Section 2.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 to secure the right of the people to information on matters of public concern.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by management.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to 

(a) allocate a separate budget for the operational expenses of the DRRM Office; and 

(b) refrain from charging ineligible expenses to the 5% LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by management

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC Chairman to prepare and submit to the GPPB the Procuring Monitoring Report in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester in compliance with Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by management.
observations.reason: There was no report submitted by the concerned officials.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Collecting Officers/Accountable Officers to prepare and submit the monthly RAAF to the Municipal Treasurer. 

Further, upon submission by the Collectors/Accountable Officers of their individual reports, the Municipal Treasurer consolidate the reports in the CRAAF and submit to the Office of the Auditor not later that 5th day of the ensuing month for the timely monitoring of accountabilities on various accountable forms in compliance with Sections 50 and 51 of the NGAS Manual for Local Government Units, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer is still in the process of reconciling the records for accountable forms.
observations.reason: The monthly submission of CRAAF was still not made by the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management exercise due prudence in the disbursement of government funds to avoid audit suspensions/ disallowances.

Further, the said accounts can be requested for write-off as these affect the fair presentation of the financial statements.

observations.reason: Breeding Stocks still had the same balance but no report on the physical count / inventory of the dispersed animals were submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the inventory committee/team to prioritize the conduct of a complete inventory count of all properties and submit the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to the auditor in accordance with Section 124 of Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS), Volume I. Henceforth, require the reconciliation of records maintained by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and effect the necessary adjustments when appropriate.
observations.reason: The Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment was still not submitted to this office.

Likewise require the Waterworks Supervisor to regularly prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant’s Office summary reports on consumers’ meter readings, billings and payments in order that the corresponding income   and receivable accounts will be correctly/ accurately established and taken up in the books of accounts
observations.managementAction: Management had instructed  the Waterworks Supervisor  to prepare monthly reports on water billings and collectibles
observations.reason: Pertinent reports not submitted to this office

observations.recommendation:
a) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to require or demand for the issuance of an official receipt of the government by the recipient Leagues concerned in order to support the release of  financial aid to the latter, pursuant to Section 69 of the GAAM, Volume I.

b) Require the Municipal Accountant to monitor the utilization of the funds released to these Leagues by requiring the recipient Leagues concerned to submit liquidation reports. In this manner, the propriety and validity of the expenditures could be properly evaluated.
observations.managementAction: No action taken since they can’t require the different leagues in using official receipt of the Republic of the Philippines. Moreover, receipts used by the different leagues are registered with the BIR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE):
a. Instruct the Inventory Committee to complete the required physical
count of all properties at least once a year and to submit the Report on
the Physical Count of Property (RPCPPE) to facilitate the reconciliation
of the balance of the PPE with Property Cards and Property Ledger
Cards every year thereafter to ensure accuracy of the PPE accounts
presented in the financial statements;
b. Require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain Property Cards per
category of Property, Plant and Equipment as required under MNGAS
for proper control and monitoring of the PPE and to facilitate
identification thereof; and
c. Submit to the Audit Team the RPCPPE in compliance with Section 124 of
the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs for review and verification.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant to review the double recording of the Mariculture Park and make the
necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to reflect the correct amount in the
books of the Municipality.

We reiterated the prior year’s audit recommendation that the Local Chief
Executive require the Municipal Accountant to transfer the cost of Office Building
from Trust Fund to General Fund and subsequently provide depreciation cost
thereon and to ensure that all depreciable properties are provided with depreciation
cost in compliance with International Public Sector Accounting Standard (IPSAS)
17.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct an inventory and prepare
the Report on the Physical Count of the Local Road Network at the end
of every accounting period; and
b. Require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to reconcile the list of
inventory per local road inventory report and road map with those
recorded in the Registry of Public Infrastructure.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the immediate
retrieval of all necessary documents of ownership of the unregistered lots occupied
by the Municipality to facilitate the transfer and registration of ownership with the
Land Registration Authority in accordance with Section 39 of PD 1445 and Section
449 of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual Volume I to establish absolute
ownership and protect the interest of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure compliance with the
provisions of Sections 3.2 and 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011 by requiring the
Municipal Treasurer to return the long outstanding DIT in the absence of proof of
deposit and to request from all depository banks to furnish the Municipal
Accountant of complete copies of Credit Memo and Debit Memo every month; and
to direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately record all valid reconciling items
that require adjustment and correction in the books of accounts to present the
correct balances of the Cash in Bank account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure strict compliance
with the provisions of Section 59, Volume I of Manual on the New Government
Accounting System (MNGAS) Manual for LGUs and to:
a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to send a written notice to payees of the
existence of checks at least one month before it becomes stale.
Consequently, for those unclaimed checks that become stale in her
custody, return the original DV and supporting documents to the
Municipal Accountant for recording in the books;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant, upon receipt of the original DV and
supporting documents of the stale checks, to prepare Journal Entry
Voucher to record the transaction as Accounts Payable; and
c. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to process the
replacement of stale checks in the hands of the payee upon submission of
the stale check to the Treasury Office.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendations that the Local
Chief Executive take the following courses of action:
a. Require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to
determine the discrepancies noted and effect necessary adjustments for
the noted errors in both records of Municipal Treasurer and Accountant
to reconcile the subsidiary ledger with cashbook pursuant to Section 10 of
Manual on the NGAS for LGUs Volume II, Section 111 of Presidential
Decree No. 1445 and Item F, Section 63 of the Local Treasury Operations
Manual to ensure reliability and correctness of balances of said accounts;
b. Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to regularly
reconcile the subsidiary ledger balances of the Cash in Bank-LCCA
accounts with the cashbook balances every end of the month; and
c. Take appropriate action against the responsible officers for non-
compliance and repeated unjustified failure to comply with the
requirements pursuant to Section 127 of P.D. 1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to further review/analyze prior year’s transactions pertaining to the
discrepancy of ₱3,362,538.20 and take up the necessary adjustments to correct the
balances of the affected accounts. Further, we recommended the Municipal
Accountant to conduct regular review and reconciliation of reciprocal accounts and
effect adjustments periodically, as identified and substantiated, to prevent existence
of long outstanding unadjusted reconciling items.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure strict adherence to
the provisions of Section 89 of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445 and Section 1.2 of
COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 on the grant, utilization and
liquidation of cash advances and to:
a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate
liquidation/settlement of all cash advances;
b. Require the former officials and employees of the LGU to immediately
settle their unliquidated cash advances through the issuance of demand
letters;
c. Instruct the accountable officers to ensure that receipts issued by the
government institutions for the payment of their membership fees and
term dues are in the form of government official receipt (AF No. 51);
d. Advise the municipal officials and employees to obtain plane tickets
directly from airline companies to avoid other charges imposed by travel
agencies. If bookings with these travel agencies are inevitable,
Management should ensure that the most advantageous price is obtained
by requiring the submission of comparison of prices published on airline
websites and travel agency’s quotation with detailed breakdown of cost.
e. Ensure that the submitted documentary requirements for the liquidation
of local travel enumerated in COA Circular No. 2012-001 are the original
copies.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer refrain from assigning the
disbursement of her cash advance for load allowance, payroll and honorarium to
the Assistant Municipal Treasurer in compliance with the provisions of Section 75 of
Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445 and Section 4.1.6 of COA Circular No. 97-002. It
was further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer discuss her concerns on
workloads to the Local Chief Executive and make a recommendation to designate a
new disbursing officer for payroll.

We recommended that that the Local Chief Executive ensure compliance
with the aforecited provisions of Chapter 7, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for
LGUs for the fair presentation of the inventory account in the Financial Statements
and provide adequate safeguard of government assets against the risk of misuse or
loss and to:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to maintain
Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively;
b. Instruct the end-users to prepare the RIS for every request for supplies to
facilitate the preparation of SSMI by the Municipal Treasurer; and
c. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to complete the physical inventory of
supplies and to prepare the RPCI for submission to the Audit Team not
later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second
semesters, respectively.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure strict compliance
with the procurement provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and the
fundamental principle on financial transactions prescribed in Section 4(6) of P.D.
No. 1445.
We also recommended that Management instruct the BAC, the BAC
Secretariat, TWG, Municipal Engineer, Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal
Accountant to submit the required documents prescribed in conformity with COA
Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 pursuant to Section 4 (6) of P.D. No.
1445.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) ensure strict
adherence to the guidelines provided under Section 10, Annex E of the 2016 Revised
IRR of RA No. 9184 in granting time extension. Further, the LCE require the
contractor concerned to submit the written consent of bondsmen and a certification
from appropriate government agency on the scarcity of cement during the last
quarter of 2018; otherwise, impose the collection of applicable liquidated damages.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure the optimal
utilization of the 20% of the IRA in accordance with the provisions of Section 287 of
the Local Government Code and Section 5.0 of the DILG and DBM Joint Circular
Memorandum No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 in order to deliver the benefits of
desirable social, economic and environmental outcomes to the constituents of the
municipality towards the attainment of the constitutional objective of providing
desired quality of life for all.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure strict compliance
with the procurement provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 and the
fundamental principle on financial transactions prescribed in Section 4(6) of P.D.
No. 1445; and to instruct the BAC, the BAC Secretariat, TWG, Municipal Engineer,
Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to submit the required
documents prescribed in conformity with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June
14, 2012 pursuant to Section 4 (6) of P.D. No. 1445.

We reiterated the prior year’s audit recommendation that Local Chief
Executive require the Municipal Budget Officer and Accountant to observe due care
in charging to and payment of expenditures out of the LDRRMF to ensure that the
LDRRMF is utilized solely for approved DRRM-related expenditures.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The expenses for diesel fuel used in the various activity of the LDRRMO and the
installation of the CCTV equipment in the Municipality’s premises were budgetary
requirements for maintenance and other operating expenditures and capital outlay of the
LDRRM Office which shall be sourced from the GF of the LGU as required under Item
4.3 of the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-01.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) ensure compliance
with Section 3, paragraph 3 of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 and
Sections 5.1.2 and 5.1.3 dated COA Circular No. 2012-002 by requiring the
LDRRMO to include the list of projects and activities charged to the unexpended
LDRRMF of previous years to ensure implementation of projects and activities for
optimal utilization of the fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to submit the original Disbursement Voucher supported with complete
documents as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-001 and the Municipal
Treasurer to secure the Sales Invoices for the payments made to the supplier
concerned amounting to P299,320.00. We also recommended that Management
cause the refund of the amount of P4,600.00 from the supplier concerned due to
erroneous computation.

We recommended that Management see to it that Gender and Development
(GAD) Plan of the agency will fully address and adhere to the basic purpose and
objectives towards the role of women in becoming a participative partner in nation
building and development, with the end view of protecting their lives and rights and
to live a modest life in a society.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the GFPS to
monitor the implementation of GAD PPAs as required under JMC No. 2013-01 and
to conduct regular coordination with the implementing offices to ensure that plans
are undertaken.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) ensure compliance
with Sections 2 and 16 of RA No. 9003 by instructing the employees concerned to
expedite completion and approval of the revised 10-year Solid Waste Management
Plan in order to have concrete guidelines that will be adopted for an efficient
implementation of programs and activities for the segregation, collection, transport,
storage, processing, treatment and disposal of solid waste towards ensuring
protection of public health and its environment.

We recommended that the local government unit ensure that all of its
insurable assets, properties and interests are insured with the General Insurance
Fund (GIF) administered by the Government Service Insurance System in
compliance with Republic Act No. 656, otherwise known as the “Property Insurance
Law”, as amended by Presidential Decree No. 245 dated July 13, 1973 and
Administrative Order No. 33 dated August 25, 1987 by identifying all its insurable
assets, submitting an inventory report of insurable assets to the System, setting aside
in its appropriation the amount needed for premiums and remitting the premiums
to the System within the prescribed period to avoid unnecessary losses in the event
of damage to, or loss of, assets and properties brought by fortuitous events and
guarantee immediate repair or replacement and uninterrupted delivery of public
service.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure compliance with
Sections 4(1) and 4(6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-
001 in the utilization of SEF funds by submitting the required LSB Resolution and
to:
a. Require the Accounting Office to ensure that disbursements are supported
with complete documents as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-001 and
other documents necessary under the circumstances to prove the propriety
and validity of the transactions;
b. Require the recipients schools of the LGU counterpart in Brigada Eskwela
2019 and Municipal, Unit and District Meet to submit the pertinent
documents to support the disbursement of funds; and
c. Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that expenses charged to
SEF budget have specific appropriation, otherwise, seek an authorization
from the Local School Board to support the authority of its execution.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) ensure compliance
with the requirements prescribed in Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001
and cause the submission of copies of contracts to the Audit Team to enable the
auditor to conduct timely review and technical evaluation of the contracts.
Further, take appropriate administrative disciplinary action as provided in
Section 4 of the same Circular against the responsible officer concerned for
unjustified non-compliance with the Circular.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure compliance with the
provisions of Sections 5.3, 5.5 and 5.6 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-5A dated
October 7, 2005 by requiring the officials and employees concerned to submit the
Notice of Award to support the claims for honoraria; otherwise, cause the refund of
the excess honoraria amounting to P295,574.00 from the payees.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the
personnel concerned to submit the lacking documents as prescribed in Section 5.13
of COA Circular No. 2012-001 pursuant to Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 in order to
avoid the issuance of Notice of Suspension in audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure adherence to the
provisions of Section 52 of R.A. No. 11260 by requiring the Human Resource
Management Officer to review all applications for loan and if possible discourage
the officials and employees in availing loans that will make the take home pay below
the minimum amount to strengthen their ability to meet their families basic needs
without resorting to debts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure compliance with the
bonding of accountable officers whose duties requires the possession, custody or
control of funds or properties pursuant to Item F, Section 305 of Republic Act No.
7160 and COA Circular No. 2006-005 and cause the immediate application/renewal
of the bond of the accountable officer to ensure that the interest of the government
is safeguarded in case of loss or misappropriation of public funds or properties.
It was further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer review the cash
accountabilities of the collecting officers from time to time and recommend
adjustment of their bond coverage whenever necessary to ensure that all collecting
officers are adequately bonded before discharging their functions.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer require all collecting officers
to remit their collections to the Treasurer’s Office within the prescribed period in
compliance with Section 29, Volume 1 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs to
safeguard the government funds against possible loss or misuse. It is further
recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure that collecting officers remit
their collections daily and avoid delay in the remittance thereof considering the
distance of the place of their assignment from the Treasury Office.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure that all Accountable
Forms are issued in numerical sequence in compliance with Section 24 of COA
Memorandum No. 2013-004 and Section 30 of the New Government Accounting
System Manual.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare and submit the
Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable forms to the Audit Team
within the prescribed period to facilitate the verification of the correctness and
propriety of the quantities and inclusive serial numbers of all the accountable forms
received, issued or cancelled as well as the monitoring of balances of accountable
forms.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure collection of the
delinquent accounts by adopting the procedures and remedies provided in Sections
254 and 256 of Republic Act No. 7160, otherwise known as the “Local Government
Code of 1991 and employing all legal measures to enable the collection of unpaid
Real Property Tax, Special Education Tax, stall rentals, market electricity and water
bills to provide additional funds to support the operations and programs of the
Municipality geared towards the welfare of the constituents.

We reiterated the prior year’s audit recommendation that the Local Chief
Executive ensure strict adherence to the existing laws, rules and regulations on
remittance of mandatory contributions/premiums to GSIS, PhilHealth and PAG-
IBIG by requiring the Municipal Accountant to conduct a detailed review and
analysis of the concerned transactions and if warranted, make the corresponding
adjustments; and to cause the immediate remittance of the premiums/contributions
to the government agencies concerned. This is to ensure that the members will enjoy
the services and benefits offered by the said government institutions and also to
avoid the interest, penalties and surcharges that may be imposed for non-
compliance.

We reiterated the previous year’s recommendation that Management make a
follow up on the demand letters sent to debtors. In case where the debtors fail to
settle their accounts after due notices, apply legal sanctions. This may help to
improve the collection efficiency of the municipality wherein the funds collected
may be utilized for re-loaning to other beneficiaries.
We also recommended that Management submit the following documents to
enable the Audit Team to review and evaluate the nature of the fund transfer from
the national government and loan agreement between the Municipality and loan
borrower, to wit:
1.) Copy of Memorandum of Agreement between the Municipality and the
source agency and other supporting documents; and
2.) Copy of Loan Agreement.

We recommended  that the Local Chief Executive (a) create an Inventory Team to (i) undertake complete physical inventory of all property, plant and equipment of the municipality; and (ii) prepare the corresponding Report of the Physical Count of PPE and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period; (b) instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to maintain updated PPE Ledger Cards; (c) require the Acting-General Services Officer (GSO) to maintain property cards for PPE to account for the receipt and disposition of properties; and (d) require the Acting-GSO and OIC-Municipal Accountant to reconcile their property records after the conduct of physical count to establish correctness of balances of PPE accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer and OIC-Municipal Accountant to (a) conduct periodic reconciliation of all Cash in Bank accounts to ascertain the accuracy of the balances of accounts appearing in the Cashbooks and Accounting Financial Records and (b) investigate differences and adjust the corresponding ledgers/cashbooks accordingly.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Certified List of Taxpayers and tax due and furnish the OIC-Municipal Accountant; and (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant to set-up the RPT/SET Receivables based on the Certified List of Taxpayers and tax due and active taxpayers, to support the general ledger balances and facilitate monitoring and reconciliation of tax collections and delinquencies.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with Section 122 of PD 1445 and Section 7.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 on the rendition of accounts to ensure timely review and verification of the transactions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council to strictly comply with the provisions of the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1; (b) BAC to submit written explanation on the deficiencies noted on the BAC Resolution issued; (c) GSO to identify the recipient of the other three units of cellphone without Property Acknowledgement Receipt and ensure that property number is indicated in therein; and (d) MDRRM Officer to submit written justification on the need to purchase ten 10 units of cellphone charged to DRRM Fund, otherwise cause a refund of the purchase price.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Development Council headed by the Local Chief Executive to (a) specifically describe the projects for inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan, and always secure approval or authorization of the Sanggunian before incurring expenditures and/or entering into contract in the implementation of projects described in generic terms; and (b) meticulously and judiciously plan the annual investment program and ensure that the PPAs implemented under the 20% Development Fund are geared towards the realization of the Municipality’s development targets and the attainment of the ultimate objective of improved quality of life for its constituents.

We recommended that the Local School Board headed by the Local Chief Executive and District Supervisor as co-Chairman to (a) conduct a thorough review and evaluation of the planning and budgeting practices of the Municipality; and (b) prepare the Annual Budget using the prescribed format in compliance with the provisions of Joint Circular No. 1 series of 2017 of the Department of Education, Department of Budget and Management and Department of the Interior and Local Government.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) PESO Manager, OIC-Municipal Accountant and BAC Committee to submit the lacking documentary requirements to support the claim; (b) PESO Manager submit the approved copy of the Masterlist in order to verify the rightful beneficiaries; and (c) BAC Committee ensure that bidding documents submitted by the bidder bear their proper initials as part of control in reviewing the documents during the conduct of bidding.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Market Supervisor to (i) ensure that all lessees have valid contracts; (ii) limit the contract period to two years; and (iii) secure authorization from the Sangguniang Bayan allowing the Municipal Mayor to enter into a contract with the lessee; and (iv) initiate extra efforts to enforce collection of unpaid rentals and impose penalties on delayed payments, as the case maybe; (b) OIC-Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledgers to monitor payment of rental of each stallholders; and (c) ICO-Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the Official Receipts are properly and completely accomplished to include stall numbers and rental period.

We  recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the (a) OIC-Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the required supporting documents before signing Box A of the Disbursement Voucher; (b) Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to draft proposed implementing guidelines, policies and procedures in granting financial assistance in the municipality which should be aligned to the prescribed guidelines of the DSWD for submission to and approval of the Sanggunian and the Municipal Mayor; and (c) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to use appropriate account corresponding to every government transactions.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct (a) the HRMO to (i) adopt a Contract of Service, instead of the existing appointment documents, in order to include the basic and necessary data required in any contract agreement, including the specific project/program/office which needs the services of a Job Order; (ii) ensure that all personnel are accorded rest day as required by Presidential Decree No. 143; and (iii) evaluate the necessity of the services of the JOs and avoid year-round appointments as stipulated in Section 77 of R.A. 7160; and (b) the immediate supervisors to check correctness of the DTRs submitted by the JOs before signing approval thereof.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (a) ensure that all trainings, seminars, workshops or similar activities undertaken/conducted by the Municipality and participated by its officials, be made within their territorial jurisdictions in compliance with DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2011-59 dated April 25, 2011 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 12, 2012; (b) require participants to refund the excessive claim for meal allowance; (c) instruct the Municipal Accountant to avoid granting of cash advance to employees who are not properly bonded and with existing cash advances; and (d) require the GAD Focal Person to submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Acting Property and Supply Officer and the Bids and Awards Committee to procure all common-use supplies directly from the DBM-Procurement Service, and resort to local suppliers only upon unavailability of the supplies needed.
Likewise, the Local Chief Executive require the BAC Committee to submit yearly to the DBM its Annual Procurement Plan for Common-Use Supplies and Equipment to assist the Procurement Service in strengthening its service in delivery and expanding its product list.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (a) ensure that the GAD Focal Point System are in place and fully functional; and (b) instruct the GAD Focal Person to strictly adhere with the provision of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 regarding submission of Accomplishment Report within five (5) working days from the end of January of the preceding year.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (a) discontinue the practice of granting cash advances to an employee in behalf of other employees; and (b) ensure that only bonded officials and employees will be granted cash advances.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the Collecting Officers/Accountable Officers to prepare and submit the monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) to the Municipal Treasurer; and (b) require the Municipal Treasurer to consolidate the individual RAAF and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month for the timely monitoring of accountabilities on various accountable forms in compliance with Sections 50 and 51 of the NGAS Manual for Local Government Units, Volume II.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the (a) Acting Municipal General Services Officer to (i) submit a Report of Physical Count for Property, Plant and Equipment for all the assets and properties of the Municipality to the GSIS; and (ii) that all insurable assets and properties are insured against any insurable risk with the GIF of the GSIS in compliance with RA No. 656 otherwise known as the Property Insurance Law, as amended; and (b) OIC-Municipal Budget Officer appropriate funds in the annual budget the cost of insurance expenses of the properties of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to observe the PS limitation in compliance with Section 325 of RA No. 7160. Likewise, the Municipal Mayor and OIC-Municipal Budget Officer are advised to ensure that the filling–up of vacant position and  hiring/promotion of personnel shall be suspended until the required 45/55 PS limitation is achieved.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the Municipal Accountant to conduct proper review of the submitted supporting documents before certifying the box A of the Disbursement Voucher; and (b) instruct the Human Rersource and Management Officer to (i) review the purpose indicated in the Travel Order submitted by employees before approving the same; (ii) limit the number of personnel doing the same purpose of travel, especially if the purpose can be done by only one person; and (iii) require all employees to clearly specified the purpose of travel to support claims.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to remit all the taxes withheld within ten (10) days following the end of the month in compliance with the Revenue Memorandum Circular (RMC) No. 23-2012 dated February 14, 2012.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue to remit promptly all compulsory contributions/loan repayments withheld to the GSIS and adhere the requirements of Republic Act No. 8291.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to remit promptly all compulsory contributions/amortization withheld to the Pag-IBIG within the reglementary period.

We recommended and management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue to remit promptly all compulsory contributions withheld to the PhilHealth.

We recommended that Management immediately transfer their  deposits  from
   PNB to any of the AGDBs listed under Section  2  of  the  DOF  Memorandum
   Circular, with due consideration of Section 6.2 of the DOF  DC  No.  001-
   2015 providing that the Heads of  LGUs  shall  be  responsible  with  the
   compliance  of  the  Circular  without  prejudice  to   criminal   and/or
   administrative liability in accordance  with  existing  laws,  rules  and
   regulations.

We recommended that Management discontinue granting  cash  advances  to  the
elected officials for purposes other than that stated in Item 4.1.4  of  COA
Circular No. 97-002 dated  February  10,  1997  and  Rules  and  Regulations
Implementing RA  8185  (An  Act  amending  Section  324  (d)  of  the  Local
Government Code.

Management’s Comment:

Management adheres to our recommendation.

We recommended that the City  Accountant  direct  the  accounting  personnel
handling the cash advances and liquidations to strictly observe  the  proper
recording of the transactions to ensure the  accurate  presentation  in  the
financial records.

We recommended that Management file appropriate charges against  Rosan  Ople
for failure to liquidate her cash advance for  confidential/intelligence  fund
of 1M within the prescribed period. Moreover, we recommended that the City
Accountant identify cash  advances  wherein received copies of the  liquidation
from the Intelligence and Confidential Fund Audit  Unit (ICFAU) were already
received and record the liquidation of the cash  advance  in  the  books  of
accounts with the following adjusting entry:


      Prior Year Adjustment                                   ₱16,115,000.00
           Advances        to        Officers         and         Employees
      ₱16,115,000.00
                 #
Further, we recommended that management re-evaluate those  accounts  that
remained unliquidated/unsettled and determine if any  of  the  conditions
stated in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19,  2016 exists, thus,
allowing them to request for authority to write-off the agency books.

We recommended that the  City  Accountant  strictly  comply  with  IPSAS  17
especially in the provision of depreciation in all PPE items and  to  effect
the depreciation of those  undepreciated  PPE  items  over  their  remaining
useful life.

We recommended that the City Accountant reclassify  the  cited  transactions
to its appropriate account to present fairly  the  financial  statements  of
the  City  pursuant  to  IPSAS  1,  recognize   Depreciation   Expense   and
henceforth, strictly observe proper classification of transactions  pursuant
to COA Circular 2015-009.

We recommended that Management direct the Accounting Section to  record  the

We  recommended  that  Management  direct  the  City  Accountant  to   remit
Philhealth contributions regularly and fully, on or before its due  date  to
avoid payment of interest/penalties, preclude delay  in  the  processing  of
members’/employees’  claims  and  present  the  account  correctly  in   the
financial statements.

We recommended that Management direct the Accounting  Section  to  reconcile
the  accounts,  explain  the  incurrence  of  negative  balances  and  remit
whatever balance due to the  GSIS  to  avoid  incurrence  of  penalties  and
surcharges and to enable the member beneficiaries of the City  to  avail  of
benefits from the  GSIS.  Furthermore,  write-off  the  unsupported  balance
under SEF and TF, thus fairly present the account balance in  the  financial
statements of the agency as of a given period.

We also recommended that Management direct  the  Human  Resource  Office  to
update its employee’s information regularly and register new employees  with
the GSIS to avoid non-remittance of their contributions.

We recommended that Management direct the Accounting  Section  to  speed  up
reconciliation  of  accounts  to  establish  the  correct  balance  of   the
unremitted portion and avoid incurrence of interest for  delayed  remittance
and present the account correctly  in  the  financial  statements.  We  also
recommended that the City impose sanctions on  the  officials  or  employees
responsible for the non-remittance of  contributions  or  payments  due  the
Home Development Mutual Fund or the PAG-IBIG Fund.

We  recommended  that  Management  require  the  Accounting  Department   to
determine the validity of the undocumented Due  to  Officers  and  Employees
Account otherwise request authority  from  the  Sangguniang  Bayan  for  the
reversion of the amount  of  ₱1,193,604.69  to  Government  Equity  Account;
analyze the abnormal balance and effect the  necessary  adjusting  entry  as
follows:

Management’s Comment:

Management adhered to our recommendation.

We recommended that Management:

a. Require the different market collectors to prepare, on a periodic basis,
   the  Report  on  Unpaid  Stall  Rentals   and   submit   to   the   City
   Treasurer/Accountant for monitoring/recording  purposes; and
