   stall rental fees is  properly  taken  up  in  the  books  of  the  City
   Government with the following adjusting entry:


      Accounts Receivable                     ₱320,177.00
           Receipt            from             Market             Operation
      ₱320,177.00


c. Require the market administrator to review all contracts  and  recommend
   for the cancellation of the contracts for those who  were  not  able  to
   comply with the terms and specification of the stall contracts

We recommended that the City Accountant reclassify  the  cited  transactions
to its appropriate account to present fairly  the  financial  statements  of
the City pursuant  to  IPSAS  1  and  henceforth,  strictly  observe  proper
classification of transactions pursuant to COA Circular 2015-009.

We recommended that the City Accountant reclassify  the  cited  transactions
to its appropriate account to present fairly  the  financial  statements  of
the City pursuant  to  IPSAS  1  and  henceforth,  strictly  observe  proper
classification of transactions pursuant to COA Circular 2015-009.

We recommended that the City Disaster Risk Reduction and  Management  Office
strengthen its capabilities to  enable  it  to  perform  mandated  functions
under Section 4, Rule 6, IRR of R.A. 10121 and  COA  Circular  No.  2012-002
and that LDRRMO submit explanation / justification on  the  low  utilization
of the LDRRMF specifically those which pertains to the  70%  allocation  for
preparation and mitigation.

We recommended that the statutory/mandatory positions  required  under  the
NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) 2014-1 dated  April  4,
2014 be filled up immediately by the City to properly institutionalize  the
organization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office and
have continuity in service and the implementation of its programs.

We  reiterated  our  prior  year’s  audit  recommendation  that   Management
meticulously  and  judiciously  review  and  evaluate  projects  before  its
inclusion in the Local Development  Fund  and  Annual  Investment  Plan  and
promptly implement programs/projects/activities embodied in the 20%  LDF  in
order to achieve the desirable socio-economic and environmental benefits  as
prescribed under DILG & DBM  Joint  Memorandum  Circular  (JMC)  No.  2017-1
dated February 22, 2017.

Management’s Comment:

The Mandaue City Planning and Development  Office  commented  that   of  the
eleven projects enumerated for  the  20%  LDF,  nine   were  either  already
completed, on-going or temporarily suspended  pending  completion  of  their
initial phase.  The remaining two were not yet implemented  due  to  a  road
right of way issue and the pending implementation  of  the  drainage  master
plan output.

In order to resolve the Road Right of Way issues of the projects,  the  City
planned to advance the surveying activities so that  these  issues  will  be
identified earlier and can be resolved  before  the  start  of  the  project
implementation. It is because of this need  that  the  City  contracted  the
services of a surveying consultant for this year.

We  recommended  that  Management   direct   the   SWMB   to   submit    its
Accomplishment Report to check whether the LGU’s Solid  Waste  are  properly
implemented,  assessed  and   monitored   to   ensure   sustainability   and
achievement of the goals of ecological solid waste management program.

We  recommended  that  Management  submit  a  written  explanation  on   the
foregoing deficiencies and submit a copy  of  the  grantee’s  transcript  of
records for verification.

We recommended that Management  submit  the  required  supporting  documents
like the plan, scope of work, bill of materials, and  sketch  of  the  areas
where the maintenance projects were done.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive adopt the  Plantilla  for  the
creation of the Internal Control  Division  and  facilitate  that  these  be
filled  up  immediately,  to  strengthen  accountability,  ensure   ethical,
economical, efficient and effective operations,   improve  the  quality  and
quantity  of outputs and enable agency to better respond to the  requirement
of the  public they serve.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to conduct investigation on the subsidiary ledgers comprising the various liability accounts with negative balances and prepare the adjustments where necessary. Moreover, we recommend that appropriate disclosures be indicated in the Notes to Financial Statements as to these abnormal balances noted.

We recommend that management require the Bids and Awards Committee to observe strictly Sections 20.1 and 20.2 of the IRR of R.A 9184 to ensure the readiness of all aspects concerning the procurement of the project. Moreover, we recommend that management justify their failure to conduct the pre-procurement conference in this particular project.

We recommend that management, in coordination with the Local Sanggunian and the Local Budget Officer, create the other three positions and appoint all these  personnel to conduct Disaster Risk Reduction and Management  (DRRM) activities in compliance with Section 2, Rule 6 of the IRR of RA10121.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Accountant to monitor and control disbursement of the funds by not exceeding fifty (50%) of the uncollected estimated revenue in addition to the actual collections so that no cash overdraft in any local fund shall be incurred at the end of the year.

We recommend that the municipality exercise fully the revenue collecting power vested on them as provided in Sections 142 to 150 of R.A. 7160 to increase its income to finance projects for the constituents and not be dependent to the Internal Revenue Allotment from the national government.

We recommend that management closely monitor and ensure that the LDRRM planned mitigation/disaster preparedness program/projects/activities are fully implemented so that intended purpose is attained/achieved.

We recommend that management make appropriate revision of the GAD Plan and Budget in the succeeding years and ensure that the total cost of the related activities is at least 5% of the total budget appropriations.

We recommend that the Office of the Municipal Accountant prepare a reconciliation report of reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure that all reconciling items are resolved and/or adjusted on a timely basis.

In view of the Supreme Court’s decision pronouncing the unconstitutionality of the PDAF, we recommend that the remaining funds shall no longer be released but shall be returned to the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) so that it can be used for other priority programs of the government.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer be required to make representation to the Bureau of Internal Revenue for the computation of taxes due them from the exercise of corporate power in the LGU’s Quinale Beach Bar.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to effect the adjusting entries to record the completed infrastructure assets to their appropriate asset accounts and close the Construction in Progress account to reflect the proper presentation of the account in the financial statements.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant provided for the required documents and prepared the identified adjusting entries to record the identified misclassified properties to their appropriate asset accounts under JEV no. 15, 16, 17, 18, 19 and 20 dated January 2019.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
 Instruct the MSWDO to furnish the Audit Team with the copies of the abovementioned documentary requirements, in order to evaluate the supporting documents in the implementation of SFP for Day Care Pupils and to exert extra effort for the timely implementation of the SFP;
 Instruct the BAC TWG to check the arithmetical computation of all submitted bids to ensure that computational errors are corrected in the evaluation of bids; and
 Require the supplier/bidder to refund the amount of P8,100.00 due to erroneous computation of the total amount of item 23 (black pepper).
observations.managementAction: The discrepancy of P8,100.00 was refunded to the Municipality. Further, required supporting documents were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the responsible persons to submit the required documents for the donated assets and the liquidation of the BUB Program to facilitate further verification of the project expenditures and avoid the issuance of Notice of Suspension in audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the GAD Focal Point System to initiate the creation of GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered sex-disaggregated data to provide the bases for gender analysis, planning, programming and policy formulation and to undertake thorough gender analysis based on relevant data for proper identification of existing gender issues to ensure that the approved programs, projects and activities to be incorporated in the GAD Plan and Budget are responsive to address the same.
observations.managementAction: The Management through the initiative of the MSWDO has maintained a system a census of male and female and data on age group in the Anda Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive 

(a) instruct the Municipal Treasurer to 

(i) prepare an updated Certified List of Taxpayers, bearing the names, amount due and collectible during the year, and submit the same to the accounting unit as the basis in setting up of RPT/SET receivables in the succeeding year; 

(ii) ensure that the list of taxpayers bear vital information, such as the addresses, to facilitate, if necessary, the sending of notices and other follow up communications relative to tax campaign activities and other collection strategy that maybe  implemented by Management; and 

(iii) introduce collection strategy that would significantly help increase the tax collection of the municipality; and 

(b) direct the Municipal Accountant to 

(i) observe the provision under Section 20 of NGAS Manual for LGUs; and 

(ii) use the Certified List of Taxpayers and Tax Due prepared by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer as the basis in recording the RPT/SET receivables at the beginning of the year.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer’s Office is in the process of updating the Municipality’s Certified List of Taxpayers.
observations.reason:
Due to backlog, the completion of the preparation of Certified List of Taxpayer will take some time, hence not completed in CY 2019. 

Reiterated in Observation No. 3

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to 

(a) deposit intact the collections, including the collections remitted to her, to the authorized bank daily; and 

(b) prepare the required Report of Collections and Deposits every end of the business day to facilitate the monitoring of her accountability.
observations.managementAction: The preparation of RCD Report is now being done on a daily basis.
observations.reason: There were instances that some collectors did not prepare the Report of Collections and Deposits on a daily basis.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the 

(a) Municipal Treasurer to conduct examination of books, accounts and pertinent records of registered businesses operating within its jurisdiction to ensure that correct amount of business tax is collected; 

(b) Business Permits and Licensing Office (BPLO)  to 

(i) be prudent in assessing the declared gross sales to compute for the correct amount of tax due; and 

(ii) require submission of Certified Income Tax Return and sworn statement of gross receipts/sales in accordance with the Revised Revenue Code of the Municipality; and 

(c) BPLO and Municipal Treasurer to cause recovery of the under collections of ₱4,724.03 arising from undeclared gross receipts/sales of the  sampled business establishment.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer have conducted examination of books of some business establishments and collected the tax deficiency from the supplier amounting to ₱4,724.03.
observations.reason: The concerned official still failed to require the taxpayer to submit the copy of Income Tax Return for recomputation to determine if the tax due is the same as amount paid.

observations.recommendation:
We  recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the 

(a) OIC-Municipal Accountant to ensure that all payments for AICS be adequately and properly supported by documents to establish  the validity and propriety of claims in compliance to Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445; 

(b) Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer 

(i) render a satisfactory explanation on the denial of receipt of the assistance by the reported beneficiaries; and 

(ii) formulate the municipality’s own implementing guidelines, policies and procedures on the management of the AICS subject to approval of the Sanggunian; and 

(c) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to use appropriate account corresponding to every government transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Social Officer have drafted the policy but not endorsed to the Sangguaniang Bayan for approval.
observations.reason: No action taken by management to comply with the other recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive 

(a) stop the practice of regularly using the vehicle intended to be used as rescue vehicle to ensure its availability in case of emergency; and 

(b) instruct the 

(i) General Services Officer to comply immediately with the above provisions by marking the vehicles with “For Official Use Only” and the name of the LGU; and 

(ii)Municipal Administrator ensure that concerned drivers prepare Driver’s Trip Ticket for every travel made.
observations.managementAction: The vehicle is now marked for official use only with the name of the LGU.
observations.reason: There were instances that the vehicle is not used for intended purpose.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the 

(a) Municipal Accountant to ensure that all financial transactions are supported with complete documentation pursuant to Section 4 (6) of PD 1445; and 

(b) Municipal Agriculturist 

(i) submit the duly approved list of beneficiaries of the 400 pieces of livestock (itik) purchased, together with acknowledgment receipt and identification card of recipients or its equivalent; 

(ii) submit copy of guidelines for the implementation of the project; and 

(iii) provide information on when and where the distribution was conducted.
observations.managementAction: No action taken by management to comply with the recommenda-tions.
observations.reason: The requested documents/information were still not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly monitor the effectivity of their Fidelity Bond to ensure that renewal shall be made before its expiry date in compliance with Section 4.2 of Section 4.2 of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer is strictly monitoring the the period of Fidelity Bond of accountable officer to ensure that it will be renewed before its expiry date.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Members of the Local School Board ensure compliance with the limitations provided in Sections 100 and 272 of RA No. 7160; RA No. 5447; DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01, s. 2017 and its implementing regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer advised the Municipal Local School Board to comply with the SEF limitations and ensure that any approved request for assistance by the school district were charged to the General Fund instead of the Special Education Fund.
observations.reason: Ineligible expenditures were still charged against the fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct 

(a) the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to continue to implement more programs and projects that will redound to the benefit and well-being of its constituents; and 

(b) the Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from charging projects to the development fund which are not in conformity with the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor instructed the concerned officials to make sure that programs and projects were aligned with the DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1.
observations.reason: Ineligible expenditures were still charged against the fund.

observations.managementAction: The Municipality conducted clean-up drive in various Barangays. Other programs to improve segration were also being done such as distribution of colored sacks to some Barangays.
observations.reason: The construction of MRF of Brgy. San Mariano is still not made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management continue to uphold the necessary mechanisms and processes in placed as prescribed by the Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM, and NEDA to ensure implementation of gender-responsive programs towards addressing gender responsive issues in the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The LGU continued to incorporate gender mainstreaming in all its programs, projects and activities.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the 

(a) Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer to remit all the taxes withheld within ten (10) days following the end of the month in compliance to Section 6 of Revenue Regulation No. 10-93 to avoid sanctions as stated in Section 272 of Republic Act No. 8424 otherwise known as the Tax Reform Act; and 


(b) Municipal Accountant to cause the preparation of the Disbursement Voucher for taxes withheld but not yet remitted to the BIR as of December 31, 2018.
observations.managementAction: The withholding and remittance was consistently observed by the concerned officials.
observations.reason: There were months wherein the delayed remittance of taxes were incurred.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to 

(a) strictly observe the rules and regulations governing the withholding and remittance of premium contributions with PhilHealth; and 

(b) ensure that contributions withheld for the month were remitted on or before the tenth (10th) calendar day of the month following the applicable month for which the payment is due and applicable as prescribed in Section 20, Rule III of the Republic Act No. 9241.
observations.managementAction: The withholding and remittance was consistently observed by the concerned officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue to remit promptly all compulsory contributions withheld to the GSIS.
observations.managementAction: The withholding and remittance was consistently observed by the concerned officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to continue to remit promptly all compulsory contributions withheld to the Pag-Ibig.
observations.managementAction: The withholding and remittance was consistently observed by the concerned officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the division heads to require all Job Order personnel to submit quantified accomplishment reports to facilitate evaluation of the achievement of the established work program.

observations.reason: Only some job-order personnel have complied with the recommenda-tion.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned officials, particularly the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman and the General Services Officer to 

(i) strictly comply with COA Circular Nos. 96-010 and 2009-001 requiring the submission of Purchase Orders and supporting documents within five (5) days after perfection; and
 (ii) furnish the Office of the Auditor copy of Notices of Deliveries within twenty four (24) hours from acceptance to ensure timely review/evaluation of the contracts and inspection of deliveries, respectively.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Development Officer submitted the required copy of purchase order.
observations.reason: The submission of Purchase Order and Contracts covered only the months of May and June 2019. No other reports were submitted after June 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to submit report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (DRRMF) on or before the 15th day after the end of each month. Also, we would like to highlight to the management to be continually guided by the provisions and laws regarding the allocation and utilization of LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: LDRRMO submits reports every month.
observations.reason: Receipt of the Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund through email.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Accounting Department to prepare adjusting entries of the affected accounts by debiting Due from LGUs and crediting Prior Period Adjustment. Furthermore, we recommend immediate liquidation of the fund transferred right after the purpose for which it has been transferred has been served.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has already effected the adjusting entry. Moreover, Aid to Barangays during Jan-June 2019 were already recorded as Due from LGUs.
observations.reason: Review of interim FS and Check Disbursement Journals.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to record collections in the appropriate Cashbook in order to reflect accurate balances in the General, Special Education Fund and Trust Fund Cashbooks at any given time.  Deposit slips should also be prepared per fund to facilitate the recording thereof and fund transfers be done regularly to the different, appropriate bank accounts.  

We further recommend that the Municipal Treasurer record transactions as they occur with the totals and balances written in ink and that regular reconciliation with the accounting records be done so that deficiencies would be easily detected and corrected.
observations.managementAction: Audit recommendations were well taken and complied by the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.reason: Cash books were verified.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that henceforth, concerned employees indicate all the necessary data in the Purchase Orders to easily determine compliance by the supplier’s/contractors on their responsibilities on the contract, facilitate imposition of applicable fines and penalties and avoid wastage of government funds
observations.managementAction: Audit recommendations were well taken and complied by the management.
observations.reason: Review of Purchase Orders upon submission.

observations.recommendation: Require the persons making requests to list items with complete and specific descriptions. Require the BAC to carefully review the items first and not proceed with the Invitation to Bid/Request for Quotation if the items are not specifically described.
observations.managementAction: Audit recommendations were well taken and complied by the management.
observations.reason: Review of Purchase Orders upon submission.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management require complete documents to support the grant of financial assistance as required under Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012. We further recommend that management set-up internal control measures in order to monitor the releases of financial assistance to the different schools like recording the same as Due from LGUs first and recording under Subsidy to LGUs only upon submission of liquidation report.
observations.managementAction: Audit recommendations were well taken and complied by the management.
observations.reason: Verification and review of related disbursement vouchers and supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management provide a venue to discuss with the municipal employees the role of the LGU in ensuring that its employees are able to receive a minimum of ₱4,000 take home pay per month in pursuance of Section 47 of the 2017 GAA.  

Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant and HRMO monitor payroll deductions and refuse to sign certifications for application of loans should an employee’s net pay fall below ₱4,000.00.
observations.managementAction: Audit recommendations were well taken and complied by the management.
observations.reason: Inquiry with the management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management create a Local Council for the Protection of Children to advocate child rights, plans and recommends interventions and monitors children’s programs and projects in the locality.

We also recommend that one percent (1%) of the Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) be allocated to ensure that all plans for children are provided with funds, pursuant to Section 15 of Republic Act 9344.
observations.managementAction: LCPC already created. Moreover,  1% of the IRA is allocated to ensure that all plans for children are provided with funds.
observations.reason: Inquiry with the management. Review and verification of the annual budget and utilization reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommend management to require the contractor and official’s in-charge in the supervision of the project to submit justification on the delay in the completion of the project. Henceforth, speedy and systematic implementation of the projects be conducted based on the duration of project agreed upon by both the LGU and the contractor.
observations.managementAction: Other projects, programs and activities for implementation during the remaining months of the year

observations.managementAction: Audit recommendations were well taken and complied by the management.
observations.reason: Inquiry with the accountant and verification of DVs.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management appropriate at least 5% from the total estimated income from regular sources for LDRRMF.  Give due regard on the maximum utilization of the 70% thereof for pre disaster preparedness programs/activities in accordance with the budget so that the intended purpose is attained/achieved.
observations.managementAction: Audit recommendations were well taken and complied by the management.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management intensify their knowledge and competence of GAD, its goals, objectives, laws, regulations and the utilization of the GAD Budget.  Likewise, consider as GAD PPAs only those programs and activities which address issues concerning women’s human rights and economic empowerment in accordance with Joint Circular No. 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA.
observations.managementAction: Audit recommendations were well taken and complied by the management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that:
a) The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) consolidate requisitions for one or more items needed at or about the same time by the requisitioner and avoid splitting of government contracts.

b) The BAC ensure that all procurement of supplies, materials, furniture’s and equipment are done  through competitive public bidding, pursuant to Section 10, Article IV of R. A. 9184 in order to give the widest publicity possible to all prospective and qualified suppliers with price offers or quotations most advantageous to the government.

c)The BAC strictly comply with the conditions enumerated under Section 52.1, Rule XVI of the same IRR in the event that the original mode of procurement through competitive public bidding as recommended in the APP cannot be pursued and shopping is resorted to as an alternative mode of procurement by the Bids and Awards Committee.

d) The management strictly adhere with the provisions contained under Section 7.2, Rule II of the same IRR requiring the formulation of the Annual Procurement Plan which shall consolidate the respective Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) of the different programs, projects and activities of the different end-user units the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Audit recommendations were well taken and complied by the management.
observations.reason: Review of submitted POs and bidding documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly comply with the required
physical count of all properties at least once a year and to submit a copy
of the Inventory Report to the Audit Team not later than January 31 and
every year thereafter to ensure accuracy of the PPE account presented in
the financial statements; and
2. Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to maintain
Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, and shall,
henceforth, conduct periodic reconciliation of the balances of both
records.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to provide depreciation costs on all depreciable properties in compliance
with Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standard (PPSAS) 17. We also
recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to
transfer the cost of Office Building from Trust Fund to General Fund and
subsequently provide depreciation thereon.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to effect the adjusting entries to record the completed infrastructure
assets to their appropriate asset accounts and close the Construction in Progress
account to reflect the proper presentation of the account in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to transfer the amount of
P2,744,245.00 completed project and procured properties funded out from Trust
Fund to the General Fund books.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant to effect the necessary adjusting entries to record the purchased IT
equipment to Information and Communication Technology Equipment in the books
of accounts for proper presentation in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
 Require the Inventory Committee to prepare the Report on the Physical
Count of the Local Road Network at the end of every accounting period;
 Instruct the Municipal Accountant to keep and maintain separate
subsidiary ledgers for the road and its components namely: a) road lot, b)
road pavement, c) drainage and slope protection structures, and d) other
miscellaneous structures; and
 Require the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to reconcile the list of
inventory per local road inventory report and road map with those
recorded in the Registry of Public Infrastructure.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to regularly
reconcile the general and subsidiary ledger balances of the Cash in Bank-
LCCA accounts with the cashbook balances at month-end pursuant to
Section 10, Volume II of NGAS Manual for LGUs to ensure reliability and
accuracy of balances of the accounts;
2. Determine the discrepancies noted and effect the necessary adjustments in
both records of Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile
the subsidiary ledger with cashbook; and
3. Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to retrieve the
documents evidencing discrepancies arising from long outstanding deposit in
transit amounting to P59,226.29 and thereafter make a representation with
the bank to credit the amount to the account of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Treasurer to send a written notice to the payees of the existence of the stale checks,
and require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to comply with the provisions
of Section 59 of MNGAS for LGUs for the disposition of the stale checks.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that the Local Chief
Executive require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Intra-agency accounts,
Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds to promptly identify the
discrepancies noted between the two accounts and to effect the necessary
adjustments, if appropriate, to bring the two accounts into agreement.

Likewise, we recommended that the Accounting Office ensure that balances
of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds are reconciled every end of
the month and maintain the general ledger balance tally with the totals of the
subsidiary ledger balances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Require all Accountable Officers concerned to liquidate their cash
advances on time, otherwise, impose administrative sanctions against
them for any unjustifiable reasons due to failure to liquidate cash
advances within the prescribe period, in accordance with Section 89 of
P.D. No. 1445;
2. Consider to obtain plane tickets directly from airline companies to avoid
other charges imposed by travel agencies; however, booking with these
agencies may still be allowed provided that their offers will be scrutinized
through comparison of existing prices on airline websites, and request
itineraries with detailed breakdown of cost and/or have their quotations
submitted in order for the management to choose the most advantageous
price; and
3. Ensure that all the documentary requirements for the liquidation of local
travel enumerated in COA Circular No. 2012-001 are submitted and that
the original copies of the Official Receipts and boarding pass are
submitted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to adhere with the prescribed accounting and
monitoring policies or procedures on the release of supplies by the agency so as to
present the correct inventory balance in the financial statements and to avoid the
risk of possible misappropriation of these assets.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
 Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that all of the Municipality’s
insurable properties are adequately covered by the General Insurance
Fund (GIF) administered by the Government Service Insurance System
(GSIS) in compliance with Republic Act No. 656 or the “Property
Insurance Law”, as amended by Presidential Decree No. 245 dated July
13, 1973, in order to avoid the risk of non-indemnification in case of
damage or loss due to fortuitous event; and
 Submit a complete inventory of insurable properties to GSIS for them to
make appropriate evaluation whether the items included can be covered
or not, and the annual report of which must also be submitted to the
audit team.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to record the identified
properties to their appropriate asset accounts.

We also recommended that the Accounting Office observe the recording
requirements provided under Section 5.5.4 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No.
2013-1 and Section 5.1.9 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 for the procurement of
small items purchased for disaster response and rescue activities.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to observe due care in charging to
and payment of expenditures out of the LDRRMF to ensure that the LDRRMF
is utilized solely for approved DRRM-related expenditures pursuant to Joint
Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-1 of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG and
Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2014-01 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-
CSC.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to
secure the Sales Invoices for the payments made to Costsaver’s Supermarket Inc.
and Dennchell Printing & General Merchandise amounting to P117,750.00 and
87,000.00, respectively and submit the same to the Audit Team.

We also recommended that expenses charged to QRF are duly supported
with Local Sanggunian Resolution and the declaration of state of calamity;
otherwise, refrain from charging expenses against the QRF in the absence of a
declaration of state of calamity and to submit said documents to support the
expenses incurred therein.

We recommended that Management:
1. Instruct the BAC Secretariat to ensure the submission of all the perfected
contracts together with the complete supporting documents within the
period prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12,
2009 to enable the Audit Team to conduct timely review and technical
evaluation of the contracts;
2. Require the BAC Secretariat to submit the required documentary
requirements to the Audit Team for the conduct of auditorial review of
the contract; examine the bidding forms used to ensure that it is in
conformity with GPPB prescribed forms; and ensure that copies of all
executed contracts together with complete supporting documents are
submitted within five working days from signing thereof for review; and
3. Instruct the BAC Secretariat to submit the required documentary
requirements to the Audit Team for the conduct of auditorial review of
the contract and ensure that copies of all executed contracts together with
complete supporting documents are submitted within five working days
from signing thereof; ensure that Performance Security posted by
winning bidders in the form of surety bond issued by a surety or
insurance company is supported by Certification from Insurance
Commission that said Insurance Company is authorized to issue such
security; and Require the Municipal Treasurer to demand from the
Contractor concerned to issue a valid Official Receipts/Sales Invoices for
the 1st and 2nd progress billing to properly substantiate the payments
made; and ensure that subsequent payments are supported with valid
Official Receipts to avoid the issuance of Notice of
Suspension/Disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
 Instruct the MSWDO to furnish the Audit Team with the copies of the
abovementioned documentary requirements, in order to evaluate the
supporting documents in the implementation of SFP for Day Care
Pupils and to exert extra effort for the timely implementation of the
SFP;
 Instruct the BAC TWG to check the arithmetical computation of all
submitted bids to ensure that computational errors are corrected in
the evaluation of bids; and
 Require the supplier/bidder to refund the amount of P8,100.00 due to
erroneous computation of the total amount of item 23 (black pepper).

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the responsible
persons to submit the required documents for the donated assets and the liquidation
of the BUB Program to facilitate further verification of the project expenditures and
avoid the issuance of Notice of Suspension in audit.

We recommended that Management instruct the GAD Focal Point System to
initiate the creation of GAD database with adequate and systematically gathered
sex-disaggregated data to provide the bases for gender analysis, planning,
programming and policy formulation and to undertake thorough gender analysis
based on relevant data for proper identification of existing gender issues to ensure
that the approved programs, projects and activities to be incorporated in the GAD
Plan and Budget are responsive to address the same.

We recommended that Management instruct the GAD Focal Person System
to monitor the implementation of GAD PPAs as required under JMC No. 2013-01
and to conduct regular coordination with the implementing offices to ensure that
plans are undertaken.

We also recommended that Management continue to appropriate the
required minimum of at least five percent of their total budget appropriations for
GAD in its annual budget program and incorporate only GAD related programs
and activities to address gender issues and concerns affecting the welfare and full
development of women on gender based inequalities.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal
Planning and Development Coordinator to fast-track the establishment of the
Municipal sanitary landfill as required in the above provisions of law to improve the
existing dump site in addressing common solid waste management problems and/or
establishing common waste disposal facilities of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal
Accountant to ensure that all claims for honoraria are supported with all the related
BAC documentary requirements particularly the certified true copies of the
contract to prove completed procurement projects, and as supporting documents of
the claims. Also, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the
Municipal Accountant to ensure that withholding taxes are deducted from the
payment of honoraria in accordance with the BIR Revenue Memorandum Order
No. 23-2014.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Human
Resource Management Officer, Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to ensure
compliance with the minimum limit of P5,000.00 for employee’s monthly net take
home pay pursuant to Section 48 of RA No. 10964.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to
ensure prompt and full remittance of the Municipal Government’s contributions to
the GSIS, PhilHealth and PAG-IBIG to avoid the imposition of interest and penalties
and limit in the access to services and benefits of the Municipal Employees.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert more effort in
identifying the unremitted balances noted, review as to its validity, and remit said
balances to the government agencies concerned.

We recommended that Management make a follow-up on the demand letters
sent to debtors. In case where the debtors fail to settle their accounts after due
notices, apply legal sanctions. This may help to improve the collection efficiency of
the Municipality wherein the funds collected may be utilized for re-loaning to other
beneficiaries.

Likewise, we recommended that Management submit the following
documents for review and evaluation the nature of the fund transfer from the
national government and loan agreement between the Municipality and loan
borrower, to wit:
1.) Copy of Memorandum of Agreement between the Municipality and the
source agency and other supporting documents; and
2.) Copy of Loan Agreement

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the Municipal Treasurer to (i) prepare an updated Certified List of Taxpayers, bearing the names, amount due and collectible during the year, and submit the same to the accounting unit as the basis in setting up of RPT/SET receivables in the succeeding year; (ii) ensure that the list of taxpayers bear vital information, such as the addresses, to facilitate, if necessary, the sending of notices and other follow up communications relative to tax campaign activities and other collection strategy that maybe  implemented by Management; and (iii) introduce collection strategy that would significantly help increase the tax collection of the municipality; and (b) direct the Municipal Accountant to (i) observe the provision under Section 20 of NGAS Manual for LGUs; and (ii) use the Certified List of Taxpayers and Tax Due prepared by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer as the basis in recording the RPT/SET receivables at the beginning of the year.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer to (a) deposit intact the collections, including the collections remitted to her, to the authorized bank daily; and (b) prepare the required Report of Collections and Deposits every end of the business day to facilitate the monitoring of her accountability.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the (a) Municipal Treasurer to conduct examination of books, accounts and pertinent records of registered businesses operating within its jurisdiction to ensure that correct amount of business tax is collected; (b) Business Permits and Licensing Office (BPLO)  to (i) be prudent in assessing the declared gross sales to compute for the correct amount of tax due; and (ii) require submission of Certified Income Tax Return and sworn statement of gross receipts/sales in accordance with the Revised Revenue Code of the Municipality; and (c) BPLO and Municipal Treasurer to cause recovery of the under collections of ₱4,724.03 arising from undeclared gross receipts/sales of the  sampled business establishment.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the (a) Municipal Treasurer and BPLO Officer to (i) require the former BPLO Officer to explain in writing the noted discrepancy in the Mayor’s Permit issued to SJ Pen Enterprises; (ii) require the owner of the business to submit proof of payment for CYs 2015-2017 to determine the propriety of the transactions with SJ Pen Enterprises; and (b) Municipal Accountant and BAC Officials to ensure that the Municipality procure items only with business establishments who has the authority to engage/conduct business transactions; and (c) Municipal Accountant to ensure that payee indicated in the DVs is the name of the business and not of the owner to avoid encashing of checks by another person.

We  recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the (a) OIC-Municipal Accountant to ensure that all payments for AICS be adequately and properly supported by documents to establish  the validity and propriety of claims in compliance to Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445; (b) Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer (i) render a satisfactory explanation on the denial of receipt of the assistance by the reported beneficiaries; and (ii) formulate the municipality’s own implementing guidelines, policies and procedures on the management of the AICS subject to approval of the Sanggunian; and (c) Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to use appropriate account corresponding to every government transactions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) stop the practice of regularly using the vehicle intended to be used as rescue vehicle to ensure its availability in case of emergency; and (b) instruct the (i) General Services Officer to comply immediately with the above provisions by marking the vehicles with “For Official Use Only” and the name of the LGU; and (ii) Municipal Administrator ensure that concerned drivers prepare Driver’s Trip Ticket for every travel made.
Management Comment:
The management claimed that they already have plate with a mark “For Official Use Only”, though it was not yet attached to the vehicle. Management also agreed to comply with the preparation of Driver’s Trip Ticket for every travel.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) submit the lacking documents as prescribed in Section 5.8 of DILG Circular No. 2016 dated January 19, 2016; and (ii) ensure that all payment of government transactions are supported with complete documentation before certifying Box A of the Disbursement Vouchers.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the (a) Municipal Treasurer and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to explain and make justification on the inconsistencies in the dates of the supporting documents attached in the Disbursement Voucher for consultancy services in preparing maps and socio-economic profile of the Municipality; (b) BAC to judiciously perform their functions to ensure that documents prepared and submitted by the Bidders are appropriate; and (c) Municipal Accountant to properly scrutinize the transactions before signing the Box A of the Disbursement Voucher to ensure validity, propriety and legality of the transactions.
Management Comments:
The management claimed that they requested price quotations with other companies, however, most of them quoted high prices. They also informed the Audit Team that Sinai Engineering and Consultancy Services was recommended by other LGU and offered the lowest price. The management, however, failed to explain the inconsistencies in the dates of supporting documents.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the (a) Municipal Accountant to ensure that all financial transactions are supported with complete documentation pursuant to Section 4 (6) of PD 1445; and (b) Municipal Agriculturist (i) submit the duly approved list of beneficiaries of the 400 pieces of livestock (itik) purchased, together with acknowledgment receipt and identification card of recipients or its equivalent; (ii) submit copy of guidelines for the implementation of the project; and (iii) provide information on when and where the distribution was conducted.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly monitor the effectivity of their Fidelity Bond to ensure that renewal shall be made before its expiry date in compliance with Section 4.2 of Section 4.2 of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 dated August 6, 2009.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Engineer to ensure/monitor the posting of signboards containing relevant information of the PPAs of the Municipality in either of the forms stated in Section 2.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 to secure the right of the people to information on matters of public concern.
Management Comment:
The management claimed that they were actually compliant with the Circular in posting of signboards, but were only taken by the residents. They also ascertained to adopt signboard templates which are not susceptible to theft.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Members of the Local School Board ensure compliance with the limitations provided in Sections 100 and 272 of RA No. 7160; RA No. 5447; DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01, s. 2017 and its implementing regulations.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct (a) the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to continue to implement more programs and projects that will redound to the benefit and well-being of its constituents; and (b) the Municipal Budget Officer to refrain from charging projects to the development fund which are not in conformity with the DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the MPDC to (a) embark effective projects and programs that support the Municipality’s Ecological Solid Waste Management Program to sustain economic development that is in harmony with the environment; (b) intensify the information drive and awareness campaign on the regulations on solid waste management in coordination with barangay officials; (c) accelerate efforts in the enforcement of “no segregation, no collection” and “no littering” policies; (d) take appropriate actions to expedite necessary restoration of the MRF of Barangay Sinasajan and Barangay Callos; and (e) pursue the establishment of MRFs in Barangays San Mariano and Poblacion III to avoid illegal dumping of assorted garbage along barangay roads, public market, drainage, canals and waterways.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to (a) allocate a separate budget for the operational expenses of the DRRM Office; and (b) refrain from charging ineligible expenses to the 5% LDRRMF.

We recommended and Management agreed to continue to uphold the necessary mechanisms and processes in placed as prescribed by the Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM, and NEDA to ensure implementation of gender-responsive programs towards addressing gender responsive issues in the Municipality.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the (a) Municipal Accountant and ICO-Municipal Treasurer to remit all the taxes withheld within ten (10) days following the end of the month in compliance to Section 6 of Revenue Regulation No. 10-93 to avoid sanctions as stated in Section 272 of Republic Act No. 8424 otherwise known as the Tax Reform Act; and (b) Municipal Accountant to  cause the preparation of the Disbursement Voucher for taxes withheld but not yet remitted to the BIR as of December 31, 2018.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to (a) strictly observe the rules and regulations governing the withholding and remittance of premium contributions with PhilHealth; and (b) ensure that contributions withheld for the month were remitted on or before the tenth (10th) calendar day of the month following the applicable month for which the payment is due and applicable as prescribed in Section 20, Rule III of the Republic Act No. 9241.
We recommended and Management agreed that the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continue to remit promptly all compulsory contributions withheld to the GSIS.
We recommended and Management agreed that the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer continue to remit promptly all compulsory contributions withheld to the Pag-Ibig.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the division heads to require all Job Order personnel to submit quantified accomplishment reports to facilitate evaluation of the achievement of the established work program.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to require the BAC Chairman to prepare and submit to the GPPB the Procuring Monitoring Report in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester in compliance with Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the concerned officials, particularly the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman and the General Services Officer to (i) strictly comply with COA Circular Nos. 96-010 and 2009-001 requiring the submission of Purchase Orders and supporting documents within five (5) days after perfection; and (ii) furnish the Office of the Auditor copy of Notices of Deliveries within twenty four (24) hours from acceptance to ensure timely review/evaluation of the contracts and inspection of deliveries, respectively.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Collecting Officers/Accountable Officers to prepare and submit the monthly RAAF to the Municipal Treasurer. Further, upon submission by the Collectors/Accountable Officers of their individual reports, the Municipal Treasurer consolidate the reports in the CRAAF and submit to the Office of the Auditor not later that 5th day of the ensuing month for the timely monitoring of accountabilities on various accountable forms in compliance with Sections 50 and 51 of the NGAS Manual for Local Government Units, Volume I.
We recommended and management agreed to exercise due prudence in the disbursement of government funds to avoid audit suspensions/disallowances.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City  Treasurer  to  secure
authority from the Sangguniang Panlungsod to support the investment of all-
time deposit without authority.  We also recommended the submission of the
specific sources of the funds  used  for  the  total  investments  in  time
deposits  to  establish  whether  these  were  essentially  idle  funds  in
accordance with Section 21 and 22 of COA Circular No. 92-382.

We recommended that the City Accountant exert extra  effort  to  review  the
long outstanding/stale checks. Require the  City  Treasurer  to  cancel  the
stale checks and furnish  the  accountant  with  the  original  disbursement
vouchers (DV) and its supporting  documents,  for  the  preparation  of  the
Journal Voucher following the procedures as cited in Section 59 of MNGAS.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the persons  responsible  for  the
immediate refund of the amount of  ₱429,138.80  representing  surcharges  on
the delayed payment of taxes.

We recommended that Management direct  the  City  Treasurer  to  submit  the
disbursement vouchers and it’s supporting documents to the  City  Accountant
for proper recording in the books of the agency.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to  conduct  a
thorough verification of the dormant items and request  for  write-off  with
the Commission on Audit for the particular accounts that  qualify  and  meet
the requirements  of  COA  Circular  No.  97-001  dated  February  5,  1997.
Thereafter, effect the necessary adjustments to  clean  the  books  of  this
deficiency.   We  further  recommended  a  regular  review  of  all  account
balances so that dormant accounts can immediately be  identified  and  acted
upon.

We recommended and Management agreed to  direct the City Accountant and  the
General Services Officer to formulate plans to reconcile the  PPE  items  in
the  accounting  records  and  property  ledger  cards  maintained  by   the
Accounting Department and  General  Services  Office,  respectively  and  to
furnish GSO with the necessary supporting documents.

We recommended that the City Mayor:

1) Instruct the City Assessor and the  General  Services  Officer  to  exert
   extra effort in locating the lot documents to establish the cost  of  the
   lots;
2) Require the City Assessor to facilitate the titling of the  250  untitled
   parcels of land; and
3) Require the City Budget Officer to appropriate funds for the  payment  of
   transfer taxes and other  fees  so  that  issuance  of  titles  could  be
   facilitated.

We recommended that Management direct the Accountant to make  the  necessary
reclassification entry to comply with COA Circular No. 2015-00

We recommended that the LSB of the City comply with the  guidelines  in  the
planning and budgeting of SEF funds pursuant to Item 5.0 of  Joint  Circular
No. 1 to wit:

1) LSB three-year program
2) DEPED approved SIP and DEDP
3) CY 2018 allocation of each school
4) CY 2018 SEF Budget using SEF Budget Preparation Form No. 1.

We recommended that Management direct the concerned City officials  and  the
LSB to explain or justify the payment of expenditures  that  is  beyond  the
purposes otherwise, limit the utilization of the SEF  to  the  programs  and
projects defined under RA 7160 and DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circular  No.  0
s 2017 dated January  19, in  order  to  maximize  the  benefits  that  the
students and teachers could avail under this fund.

We recommended that henceforth, the City transfer the unexpended balance  of
the DRRM funds to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund after five
years.

We recommended  that  the  City  Accounting  Office  regularly  prepare  and
provide copies of the Report on Sources and Utilization  of  DRRM  funds  to
the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Office  (LDRRMO)  so  that  the
said  implementing  office  will  be   well   informed   of   the   accurate
balance/amount of  DRRM  funds  available  for  appropriation  and  for  the
concerned offices to closely coordinate with one another  for  an  efficient
and effective implementation  of  the  PPAs  pertaining  to  risk  reduction
preparedness and mitigation.

In addition, we recommended that the LDRRMO incorporate in the LDRRMFIP  the
30% QRF to  present  the  actual  amount  of  DRRM  funds  appropriated  and
available for utilization. Likewise, properly indicate in the  LDRRMFIP  the
funding source of the PPAs proposed to be implemented  for  proper  charging
and recording in the books.

Finally, we recommended that Management  ensure  strict  compliance  on  the
submission of monthly Report on Sources and Utilization  of the  LDRRMF  to
the COA Auditor as required under item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that Management adopt following courses of action:

a. Direct the City Planning Development Office (CPDO) to conduct a detailed
   engineering to establish the technical  viability  of  the  project  and
   conformance to land use and zoning  guidelines  prescribed  by  existing
   laws which includes  the  Preparation  of  Site  or  Right-of-Way  Plans
   including Schedule of Acquisition;
b. Closely monitor and ensure the efficient implementation of all  priority
   development projects and programs under the 20% DF that  are  envisioned
   to help in attainment of social, economic and environmental  development
   of the City;
c.    Observe proper timing in  the  implementation  of  the  infrastructure
   projects, for the implementing office to have ample time in accomplishing
   the projects and guarantee prompt completion  for  the  benefits  of  the
   public; and
d. Ensure that there is maximum utilization and implementation  of  the  20%
   Development Fund during the year to ensure the  attainment  of  desirable
   socio-economic  development   and   environmental   management   outcomes
   essential in the attainment of the constitutional objective of a  desired
   quality life for the constituents.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the GAD  Focal  Person  to  submit
GAD Accomplishment Report to the Auditor within five (5) working  days  from
the end of January  to  facilitate  timely  monitoring  and  evaluation  and
determine whether the activities based on the DILG  approved  GAD  Plan  and
Budget were implemented.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct  the OIC  of  the  CPDO  and  the
City’s GFPS to lead or spearhead the setting up and  maintenance  of  a  GAD
Database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning,  programming  and
policy formulation in compliance with the undated PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC  No.
2013-01.

We recommended that Management stop the practice of  charging  prior  year’s
expenses against the  current  year’s  appropriations.   Unpaid  obligations
shall be recorded as payables to validate their payments in  the  succeeding
years.  For the process to be valid however,  said  payable  shall  be  duly
supported with complete documentations.

We recommended that Management require the City Accountant  to  prepare  the
financial statements of the Mandaue City  College,  an  economic  enterprise
and consolidate these in the General Fund.

We recommended that Procurement Monitoring Report be approved and  submitted
by the Head of the Procuring Entity to the GPPB in  printed  and  electronic
format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester.

We recommended that Management:

1. Stop the practice of paying expenses thru reimbursement basis;
2. Strengthen internal control,  and  make  representation  and  arrangement
   with the BJMP Regional Office 7, on how  the  financial  assistance  that
   will be given to the Mandaue inmates be utilized so that it  will  enable
   BJMP to consolidate all the advances  for  the  immediate  needs  of  the
   inmates; and
3. Consider the direct transfer of the  financial  assistance  to  the  BJMP
   Regional Office.

We recommended  that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  (LCE)  request  for  the
availment of the fund which shall be used in implementing projects that  are
mentioned in the Local Budget Circular No. 116.

We recommended that  Management  strictly  observe  the  requirements  under
Section 22(c) of the Local Government Code (RA No. 7160) and direct the Pre-
Audit Division of the Accounting Department to  explain  why  a  Sangguniang
Resolution  was  not  secured  for  transactions   charged   to   Lump   Sum
Appropriations.

We recommended that Management adopt the following courses of action:

1. Rescind/terminate the contracts for those projects  with  road  right  of
   way problems; and
2. Henceforth, conduct a detailed engineering  to  establish  the  technical
   viability of project and conformance to land use  and  zoning  guidelines
   prescribed by existing laws which includes the  Preparation  of  Site  or
   Right-of-Way Plans including Schedule of Acquisition.

We recommended that Management observe  the  provisions  stipulated  in  COA
Circular No. 2013-004,  for  information  dissemination  to  the  public  on
projects and activities  of  the  city  to  promote  transparency  and  good
governance.

We recommended that Management do the following courses of action:

1. Strictly adhere to the new Policies and Guidelines on  Overtime  Services
   and Overtime Pay  for  Government  Employees  under  CSC  and  DBM  Joint
   Circular No. 1 series of 2015 dated November 25, 2015 and  submit  Report
   on Overtime Services with Pay to DBM’s regional office copy furnished the
   Civil Service Commission (CSC);
2. Formulate a detailed and systematic plan of  activities  per  department.
   The planned activities shall integrate therewith the volume and frequency
   of regular and routine tasks to be  performed  to  ensure  that  overtime
   services will be avoided; and
3. Make sure  that  infrequent  overtime  services  have  proper  and  prior
   authorization before the conduct of the same and  stating  thereupon  the
   detailed overtime work program and ensure that overtime claims  shall  be
   supported by quantified accomplishment report.

We recommend that the  City  Accountant  intensify  collection  efforts  and

demand  full  settlement  from  the  persons  who  failed  to  pay  on   the
installment basis; and

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (MRAAF) properly in order to give accurate movements of accountable forms. Moreover, it is recommended that the Municipal Treasurer reconcile his monthly RAAF prepared to the RAAF produced by the system in order to prevent misuse or loss of said forms.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant, guided by COA Circular 2016-005, initiate the verification, validation, review and analysis of the abovementioned non-moving accounts to effect necessary adjusting/ correcting journal entries for the eventual disposition thereof. Further, the said accounts can be requested for write-off as these affect the fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommend that management instruct the inventory committee to conduct physical inventory on the remaining Breeding stocks and require the maintenance of Ledger Cards and submission of monthly inventory report to the Municipal Accountant so as to validate its existence.

We recommend to the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to submit report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (DRRMF) on or before the 15th day after the end of each month. Also, we would like to highlight to the management to be continually guided by the provisions and laws regarding the allocation and utilization of LDRRMF.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to send out notices to the various payees whose checks have become stale to facilitate the cancellation or replacement thereof. We further recommend that the Municipal Treasurer cancel unclaimed stale checks and furnish the accountant with the disbursement voucher (DV) and its supporting documents for the preparation of journal entry voucher following the procedures prescribed to ensure recording of such cancellation.

We recommend that management explain the hiring of consultants that did not undergo Public Bidding or any other alternative mode of procurement as required in RA 9184 or the Government Procurement Law.
We further recommend that management submit relevant and necessary documents to ascertain the basis of the computation of the rates of the compensation of consultants hired by the LGU.

We recommend that management direct the Municipal Accountant to disclose relevant and significant information not presented on the face of the financial statements so as to give valuable information relevant to the accounts thus, give the stakeholders understanding thereof and present the financial statements fairly.

We recommend that management require the inventory committee/team to prioritize the conduct of a complete inventory count of all properties and submit the corresponding Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment to the auditor in accordance with Section 124 of Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS), Volume I. Henceforth, require the reconciliation of records maintained by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer and effect the necessary adjustments when appropriate.

We recommend that Management require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer to closely coordinate on the immediate implementation of the priority development projects, programs and activities funded under the 20% Development Fund during the year the funds are actually appropriated in order to ensure that the intended beneficiaries could avail of the maximum benefits and the desired outcomes from the said programs or projects.

We recommend that management require the Accounting Department to prepare adjusting entries of the affected accounts by debiting Due from LGUs and crediting Prior Period Adjustment. observations.recommendation: We recommended that management explain the deviations of actual project implementation against the project proposal agreed upon by both parties or provide any documented approval from the DSWD-source agency on the changes noted in the program implementation.
observations.managementAction:
The project implementation was delayed due to the bidding process required to be followed. Though the implementing department considers it as blessing in disguise since Anda was among the Municipalities in Central Luzon that was badly hit by the typhoon Lando in October 2015. The project was a big help in the repair of centers greatly affected by the said typhoon. Out of the 22 Day Care Centers, 18 Centers were repaired by the project, this was agreed upon by the federation of Anda Day Care Workers. The four Centers which are not covered were Awag DCC, Imbo DCC, Sablig DCC and Mal-ong DCC. Reasons are as follows:
●For Awag DCC, the Barangay has a plan to construct new center thus repair for the old one is not practical.
●For Sablig and Imbo DCC, these were just repaired in 2014 being recipients of DSWD project.
●For Mal-ong DCC, it needed no repair since it is well maintained by the Barangay Council. Thus repair fund was utilized in addition to their fund to purchase equipment and supplies needed.
Several Day Care Centers reduced their ECCD materials allocations and added to their center repair needs in order to repair and rehabilitate well their respective centers. That is why the actual cost did not meet the planned expenditures.

observations.recommendation:

A. 9184 in order to give the widest publicity possible to all prospective and qualified suppliers with price offers or quotations most advantageous to the government.

c)The BAC strictly comply with the conditions enumerated under Section 52.1, Rule XVI of the same IRR in the event that the original mode of procurement through competitive public bidding as recommended in the APP cannot be pursued and shopping is resorted to as an alternative mode of procurement by the Bids and Awards Committee.

d) The management strictly adhere with the provisions contained under Section 7.2, Rule II of the same IRR requiring the formulation of the Annual Procurement Plan which shall consolidate the respective Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) of the different programs, projects and activities of the different end-user units the LGU.

observations.reason:
As of December 31, 2017, the disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents totaling P425,387.29 which was subject of NS No. 2016-009 (’16) dated April 15, 2016 remains in the reconciling items labeled as unrecorded check disbursements due to non-submission of the disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents.

The Accounting and Treasurer’s Office should coordinate with each other to correct and settle the issue of “Treasurer’s Office non-submission of the disbursement vouchers and it’s supporting documents to the Accounting Office.”

observations.recommendation: Make the necessary adjustments regarding the checks which remain outstanding after two (2) years from date of its issue as required under Section 97 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The City Accountant will prepare the corresponding journal entry vouchers to adjust the stale checks amounting to P94,789.41.  Other stale checks of P127,873.99 were already adjusted in the books. On the procurement of two (2) units garbage compactor of P10,621,981.42, the City Accountant recorded only P10,000,000.00, since only this amount was appropriated in the Annual Budget at that time.
observations.reason:
Stale checks totaling P127,873.99 was reclassified to A/P regardless of the years the checks remained outstanding.  

Management should consider Section 97 of PD 1445 which provides that checks remained outstanding for than two (2) years from date of issue will no longer be paid by the Treasurer of the Philippines or by any duly authorized depository, hence should also be cancelled.  

Management’s comment on the procurement of two (2) units garbage compactor of P10,621,981.42 is unacceptable. We reiterate the  auditor’s rejoinder in the AAR for CY 2013) that “during the exit conference, it was agreed that a review should be made on the terms of the contract which is essential in the determination as to whether or not Mandaue City is obligated to pay the excess amount of P621,981.42.  Further, the BAC Resolution to make the foreign purchase of the subject garbage dump trucks including all other procurement documents, should also be re-evaluated. These reviews are important to establish the necessity of the said additional costs before a supplemental budget will be requested from the Sangguniang Panlungsod.

observations.reason: 61.76% of the total bank accounts or twenty one (21) bank accounts were updated but submitted late and 38.24% or thirteen (13) were still not submitted.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the officials concerned to prepare and submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment (LDRRMFIP) as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 indicating the 30% allocation of QRF and the allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness showing the projects and activities to be funded, for evaluation and monitoring purposes.

Likewise, require the City Accountant to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the prescribed format in COA Circular No. 2012-002 and require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) to submit such report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the COA Auditor through the LDRRMC and the Local Development Council.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting Office submitted the said report on a quarterly basis contrary to the requirements mandated in COA Circular No. 2012-002 since beginning of the year 2016 and only this year that they have been verbally informed that it should be on a monthly basis. They assumed that the mode of submission of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) and the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (DRRMF)   is same with the DILG, which is quarterly. Nevertheless, they admitted the lapses and have failed to consider the mandated requirement on the said circular. Management committed that this year’s submission of the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF will be in adherence with COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.reason: This is reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: Direct the BAC to explain the basis on the awarding of the contract to CFSI when it did not fully comply with the procurement procedures required under RA 9184. Direct the budget committee to set up realistic  revenue targets to avoid budgetary problems in the implementation of programmed projects and activities.

Instruct all the department/offices concerned to coordinate in the identification of unpaid obligations and unimplemented projects, programs and activities and to reconcile unappropriated balances with the City’s cash position which is necessary in the preparation of the succeeding year’s budget to establish budget savings and avoid budget and cash overdrafts in the future.

Direct the Finance Committee and other officials concerned to review the Schedule of Market Values of the City which was last updated for about 20 years ago.

Further, we recommend that management observe prudence in making deposits with authorized private commercial banks to protect Mandaue City Government funds.

Observe the proper procedures in granting financial assistance to NGOs/POs in consonance with COA Circular 2007-001.

Submit the required documents enumerated above otherwise, the said transactions will be suspended or disallowed in audit.

Direct the City Engineering Office in coordination with the School Superintendent of the City of Mandaue to secure copies of the required program of works and floor plans as required in the MOA to establish validity of the financial counterparts made by the City to construct/ refurbish six school buildings within its component barangays.

Create a team to review the transactions based on the records maintained by DGS and determine the actual utilization of the subject supplies and materials costing P3.1 million to ensure that these inventories were actually used in the operations of the City of Mandaue and not lost due to misapplication or pilferage.  If the items have been actually issued or can no longer be found on hand, then the DGS shall issue the SSMI.   Consequently, the City Accounting Office shall make the necessary adjusting entries to report the correct account balances.
