Remind the BAC to observe the proper procurement procedures pursuant to the pertinent provisions of RA 9184 and other related issuances particularly in negotiated procurements to ensure that the government paid the most advantageous price. 

Conduct judicious examination and review on all procurement contracts to deter approving questionable transactions.

Direct the City Administrator to instruct the BAC Chairman and Secretariat to observe the proper procedures in the preparation of the purchase order or contracts to avoid any legal problem in the future and ensure that the City gets what is expected in design, brand, model of any item that will be procured at the most reasonable price for the government and avoid questions on the documentation thereof.

Direct the City Accountant to furnish monthly the City Mayor a copy of the utilization report of trust funds.

Direct the City Administrator  to look into each unexpended balance of the said grants from various national government agencies  and other LGUs  and for him to coordinate with the different offices concerned on the status  of each programs, projects and activities where there are reported excess funds so that appropriate action can be made as required under COA Circular No. 94-013 for implementing agencies.

Instruct the City Administrator to advise the Accounting Office in writing to prepare the disbursement voucher to effect the said return.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that management:
Strictly comply with the required physical count of all properties at least once a year and to submit a copy of the Inventory Report to the Audit Team not later than January 31 and every year thereafter to ensure accuracy of the PPE accounts presented in the financial statements.
Require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to maintain Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, and shall henceforth conduct periodic reconciliation of the balances of both records.
Provide necessary adjusting entries to correct the noted errors in the recording of various PPE transactions; and
Require the Accounting Department to provide depreciation on all depreciable properties in compliance with Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standard (PPSAS) 17 and shall adjust accordingly the applied depreciation for Local Road Networks without providing residual value in determining the depreciable cost.
We recommended that management provide the necessary adjusting entries to correct the noted errors in the recording of various PPE transactions.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that Management require the Accounting Department to provide depreciation on all depreciable properties in compliance with Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standard (PPSAS) 17. Also, the management should expedite the tracing or securing of records relative to the noted assets existing since the 1997 Financial Statements so as to provide the necessary depreciation and final disposition/treatment of the cited assets in the books of accounts, including the transfer of the building from trust fund to general fund.
Likewise, adjust accordingly the applied depreciation for Local Road Network without providing residual value in determining the depreciable cost.

We recommended that management take the following courses of action:
Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to regularly reconcile the general and subsidiary ledger balances of the Cash in Bank-LCCA accounts with the cashbook balances before closing of the book at month end pursuant to Section 10 of NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume II to ensure reliability and correctness of balances of said accounts.
Provide the necessary adjustments for the noted errors in both records of Municipal Treasurer and Accountant.
Exert more effort in identifying the discrepancies in prior years’ records under PNB and DBP accounts and provide adjustments accordingly.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to cause the immediate adjustment of valid reconciling items to present the correct balances of the Cash in Bank and other affected accounts. Likewise, require the Municipal Treasurer to refund the amount charged against the fund of the agency for the NSF check.

We recommended that management observe the following courses of action:
Require the Municipal Accountant to provide necessary adjusting entries for the findings cited; and
Require the periodic reconciliation of the Cash Local Treasury account between the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to determine discrepancies that need adjustments in the books of accounts in order that the financial statements could be accurately and fairly presented.

We recommended that management send a written notice to the payees of stale checks and require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to comply with the regulations laid out under Section 59 of NGAS Manual for LGUs for the proper disposition of the noted stale checks.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that management require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Intra-agency accounts, Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds to promptly identify any discrepancy in the balances of the two accounts and to be able to effect the necessary adjustments if appropriate, to bring the affected accounts into agreement.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to make a detailed review of the RPT and SET Receivables accounts vis-à-vis the List of Delinquent Taxpayers; and shall henceforth observe the proper rules on setting up the accounts at the beginning of the year.

We recommended that management take the following courses of action:
Require all Accountable Officers to liquidate their cash advances on time, otherwise, impose administrative sanctions against them for any unjustifiable reasons due to failure to liquidate cash advances within the prescribe period, in accordance with Section 89 of PD 1445, COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and Civil Service Commission Memorandum Circular No. 12, series 2012.
Ensure that vehicle rentals will only be employed if it is the most advantageous mode of transportation during a specific official travel. Likewise, ensure to adhere with the procurement law if renting vehicles to attract competitive prices and to transact only with eligible suppliers.
Provide the missing official receipt related to hotel accommodation, otherwise, refund the excess over the allowable amount.
As much as possible, obtain plane tickets directly from airline companies to avoid other charges imposed by travel agencies; however booking with these agencies may still be allowed provided that their offers will be scrutinized through comparison of existing prices on airline websites, request itineraries with detailed breakdown of cost and/or have their quotations submitted in order for the management to choose the most advantageous price.

We recommended that management require the Accounting Office in cooperation with the Treasury Office to ensure the complete submission of accounts within the prescribed period for timely examination and verification of accounts pursuant to Section 7.2.1 of the 2009 of the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts and Section 122 of Presidential Decree 1445.

We recommended that management expedite the negotiation pertaining to the transfer of monthly IRA releases from PNB to LBP to be able to comply with the rules provided under Department of Finance (DOF) Department Circular No. 001-2015 and BLGF Memorandum Circular No. 07-01-2017.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to adhere with the prescribed accounting and monitoring policies or procedures over the supplies being used by the agency so as to present the correct inventory balance in the financial statements and to avoid the risk of possible misappropriation of these assets.

We recommended that Management take the following courses of action:
Ensure that all of its insurable properties are adequately covered by the General Insurance Fund (GIF) administered by the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) in compliance with Republic Act No. 656 or the “Property Insurance Law”, as amended by Presidential Decree No. 245 dated July 13, 1973, in order to avoid the risk of non-indemnification in case of damage or loss due to fortuitous events.
Submit a complete inventory of insurable properties to GSIS for them to make appropriate evaluation whether the items included can be covered or not, annual report of which must also be submitted to the audit team; and
Expedite negotiation with GSIS for the possible coverage with the GIF of the various buildings that were previously insured under PNB.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that management:
Instruct the Municipality’s Inventory or Property and Supply Unit/Team, as well as the Office of the Accountant, to conduct physical inventory of the supplies and equipment procured out of the LDRRMF in coordination with the LDRRMO and provide adjusting entries to set-up asset accounts thereof; and shall henceforth
Ensure observance of the rules set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-002 and COA Circular No. 2014-002 pertaining to the accounting, inventory and monitoring guidelines for procured resources out of the LDRRMF.

We recommended that management, in the conduct of ensuing public bidding(s) for the operation of fishery lots, must ensure that the initial prices for each fishing lot be carefully computed and reviewed, and have it approved by the Sanggunian before disseminating in public to avoid errors or conflicts during the actual bidding process. Likewise, submit to the Office of the Auditor the Memorandum of Agreement or S.B. Resolution granting priority to Barangay councils the award of some fishing zones.

We recommended that management ensure the submission of all the perfected contracts together with the complete supporting documents within the period prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 to enable the auditor to conduct timely review and evaluation of the transactions.

We recommended that management submit the documentary requirements for the conduct of auditorial review of the contracts as mentioned in COA MEMO Circular No. 2005-027 and for payment of claims under COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that management explain the deviations of actual project implementation against the project proposal agreed upon by both parties or provide any documented approval from the DSWD-source agency on the changes noted in the program implementation.

We recommended that management require the responsible persons to submit the required documents for the donated assets and the liquidation of the BUB Program to facilitate further verification of the project expenditures.

We reiterated our recommendation that management:
Require the GAD Focal Person to elaborate/explain the gender issues being addressed by the particular program/project/activity as reflected in the GAD Plan and Budget with consideration to the foregoing rules and the official Gender and Development Code of the Municipality.
Prepare the Annual GAD Accomplishment Report as prescribed under DILG-NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-01 which is also a requirement for the conduct of GAD Audit.

We recommended that management establish their own sanitary landfill as required in the above provisions of law to improve the existing dump site in addressing common solid waste management problems and/or establishing common waste disposal facilities of the Municipality.

We recommended that management exert more efforts in identifying the noted unremitted balance already due for remittance to GSIS, and review as to its validity, otherwise effect the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts if error is determined on the same.

We reiterated our recommendation contained in the previous Annual Audit Reports that management make a follow up on the demand letters sent to debtors. In case where the debtors fail to settle their accounts after due notices, apply legal sanctions. This may help to improve the collection efficiency of the municipality wherein the funds collected may be utilized for re-loaning to other beneficiaries. Likewise, please submit the following documents to enable us to make a decision in audit:
Copy of Memorandum of Agreement between the Municipality and the source agency and other supporting documents; and
Copy of Loan Agreement

We reiterated our previous recommendation and management agreed to (a) create an inventory committee and conduct complete physical inventory of all properties of the municipality to determine their existence and physical conditions in accordance with above mentioned rules and regulations; and (b) exert extra efforts to locate old records of acquisition to provide basis in the preparation of subsidiary records, computation for depreciation or request for the dropping from records all obsolete, destroyed and unserviceable properties.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to (a) conduct periodic reconciliation of all Cash in Bank accounts to ascertain the accuracy of the balances of accounts appearing in the Cashbooks and Accounting Financial Records and (b) investigate differences and adjust the corresponding ledgers/cashbooks accordingly.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make a detail review of the accounts and determine the cause of the unreconciled amounts. Thereafter, make the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the affected accounts. Henceforth, excercise care in recording and classifying the Municipality’s financial transactions to ensure that the balances of reciprocal accounts are equal at all times.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the Municipal Treasurer to (i) prepare an updated Certified List of Taxpayers, bearing the names, amount due and collectible during the year, and submit the same to the accounting unit as the basis in setting up of RPT/SEF tax receivables in the succeeding year; (ii) ensure that the list of taxpayers bear vital information, such as the addresses, to facilitate, if necessary, the sending of notices and other follow up communications relative to tax campaign activities and other collection strategy that maybe  implemented by Management; and (iii) introduce collection strategy that would significantly help increase the tax collection of the municipality; and (b) direct the Municipal Accountant to (i) observe the provision under Section 20 of NGAS Manual for LGUs; and (ii) use the Certified List of Taxpayers prepared by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer as the basis in recording the RPT/SEF tax receivables at the beginning of the year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to (a) discontinue the charging of ineligible expenses to SEF,  which should be rightfully charged against the funds of the DepEd; and (b) strictly comply with the provisions of RA Nos. 5447, 7160 and DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 concerning the proper utilization of the fund.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the officials concerned to ensure that the programmed projects for the 20% Development Fund comply with Section 3.0 of DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 to attain its optimal utilization and to fully achieve the socio economic development and environmental outcomes as expressly prescribed by the circular.

We recommended and management agreed to observe the limit and comply with Section 325 of R.A. No. 7160. Likewise, the Local Chief Executive and Municipal Budget Office are advised to ensure that the filling–up of vacant position and  hiring/promotion of personnel shall be suspended until the required 55/45 PS limitation is achieve.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) submit the lacking documents as prescribed in Section 5.8 of DILG Circular No. 2016 dated January 19, 2016; and (ii) ensure that all payment of government transactions are supported with complete documentation before certifying Box A of the Disbursement Vouchers.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Municipal General Services Officer to (a) submit a Report of Physical Count for Property, Plant and Equipment for all the assets and properties of the Municipality to the GSIS; and (b) ensure that all insurable assets and properties are insured against any insurable risk with the GIF of the GSIS in compliance with RA No. 656 otherwise known as the Property Insurance Law, as amended.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit the monthly trial balances and year-end financial statements for all funds within the deadline as required under Section 4.1 of COA Circular No. 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010 and Section 70 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.
Also, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive make a personal re-assessment and evaluation on the performance of the Accounting Unit and accordingly introduce necessary corrective measures.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Collecting Officers/Accountable Officers to prepare and submit the monthly RAAF to the Municipal Treasurer. Further, upon submission by the Collectors/Accountable Officers of their individual reports, the Municipal Treasurer consolidate the reports in the CRAAF and submit to the Office of the Auditor not later that 5th day of the ensuing month for the timely monitoring of accountabilities on various accountable forms in compliance with Sections 50 and 51 of the NGAS Manual for Local Government Units, Volume I.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to require the Local DRRM Officer and Municipal Accountant to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the format in Annex B on or before the 15th day after the end of each month pursuant to Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012–002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Engineer to (i) submit the monthly monitoring report of the project status; and (ii) inform the Audit Team of the posting of project signboards and/or public notices within the time specified in Sections 3.1 and 3.2 of COA Circular No. 2013-004  dated January 30, 2013 for validation.

We recommended that the Municipality continue to uphold the necessary mechanisms and processes in placed as prescribed by the Joint Memorandum Circular 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM, and NEDA to ensure implementation of gender-responsive programs towards addressing gender responsive issues  in the Municipality.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to (a) strictly observe the rules and regulations governing the withholding and remittances of premium contributions, etc. with the BIR, GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG; and (b) regularly coordinate to facilitate processing of documents necessary to support the completeness of remittance, recording of transactions and timely transmission of disbursement vouchers to the respective Offices concerned

We recommend that Management direct the City Accountant to hasten the preparation of the BRS to establish the correctness of the bank balances appearing the financial statements.
We further recommend that bank reconciliation statements submitted to COA be properly supported by bank statements and paid and cancelled checks to facilitate the verification of the accuracy of the BRS submitted.
To reflect the correct balance of the Cash in Bank, Local Currency, Current Account, we recommend the following to the management:
make representation with the concerned bank for correction of bank error noted in the BRS.
prepare the necessary Journal Entry Voucher for the (1) cancellation of stale checks, (2) unrecorded interest earned net of withholding tax, (3) unrecorded debit memos, credit memos and cash deposits, (4) unrecorded EGPS payment credited in the bank.
direct the City Accountants Office to coordinate with the City Treasurer’s Office on the non-submission of disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents.
make the necessary adjustments regarding the other book reconciling items noted.
explain and provide action plan for the Overstatement of the Cash-in-Bank, Local Currency by ₱621,981.42 which remained unrecorded as to date.

We recommend that the City Mayor direct all accountable officers to fully liquidate their cash advances immediately for the long-outstanding accounts and henceforth, enforce strict liquidation at year-end pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997. Otherwise, we recommend that the Accountant cause the withholding of the AO’s salary in accordance with paragraph 5.9 of the mentioned circular.
We request for regular update on actions taken by the City Legal division. As to deceased employees with unliquidated cash advances, we further recommend that the City Accountant make a request for write-off of the account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We reiterate our prior year’s recommendation that Management direct the City Treasurer to furnish the Chief Accountant with the duly certified list of all taxpayers at the beginning of the year showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as basis in setting-up the RPT/SET Receivables in the books in order to present reliable and accurate balances thereof in the financial statements.

We recommend that the City Mayor direct the City Engineering and the City Accounting to submit the information/ data together with the status of these projects where there are outstanding advances to contractors.
We further enjoin the City government to investigate why the advance payments were not deducted from the progress billings and the non-forfeiture of the surety bonds, if any, to initiate legal action, if warranted, to recoup the advance payments made to contractors, and to determine and hold liable the persons responsible for the non-recoupment.

We reiterate our prior years’ recommendation that the City Mayor,
Direct the Accounting Department to continue updating its property listing for to account for unidentified property items with a total cost of ₱4.2 billion.
Direct the DGS who is supervising the Inventory Committee-
to observe the same group classification of property items with that of the Accounting Department to facilitate reconciliation of the Accounting and DGS property records; and
to reconcile the unserviceable items per Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment and Inventory and Inspection Report so that these can already be dropped from the books
Instruct the Accounting Department and DGS to coordinate for the purpose of identifying items which were not found during the conduct of physical inventory amounting to ₱4.2 billion and make appropriate actions to minimize substantially if not eliminate the numerous unaccounted property items.

We recommend that the City Accountant effect the necessary accounting entries to revert prior years’ accounts payable where no actual claims, whether administrative or judicial, have been filed. Henceforth, we recommend that no obligation should be certified to accounts payable unless it is funded on a valid claim that is properly supported by sufficient evidence and unless there is proper authority for its incurrence pursuant to Section 4(s) Volume 1 of NGAS Manual and Section 157 Volume 1 of the GAAM.
We further recommend that Management reassess and enhance the internal controls set for the process flow and recording of payables. We also recommend that Management observe strictly the pertinent provisions of the NGAS Manual with regards to recognition, monitoring and control of payable accounts.

We recommend that Management make the necessary adjustments regarding checks which remain outstanding after two (2) years from date of its issue which was reclassified to accounts payable as required under Section 97 of PD 1445.
We recommend that the City Mayor require the responsible officials/employees to remit immediately the withheld funds to BIR, GSIS, Pag-ibig and Philhealth.

We recommend that Management submit the required documents to validate the payment of consulting services.
We also recommend that Management undertake future procurements of consulting services in accordance with the provisions of Republic Act No. 9184 so that only those who possess expertise in their field of assignment and whose services are very necessary will be hired and to refrain from hiring consultants whose functions are performed by existing offices of the LGU as set forth in laws, rules and regulations or in their position description form to avoid the unnecessary duplication of work.

We recommend that the BAC explain and justify the basis on the awarding of the contracts even if it did not fully comply with the procurement procedures required under RA 9184 and submit the required lacking documents.
Henceforth, the BAC members are enjoined to comply strictly with the proper procurement procedures pursuant to the provisions of R.A. 9184 and its IRR and to perform their tasks as required for an efficient and effective procurement process and remind them of their accountabilities.
Likewise, on the SLRF Project (Asphalt overlay/ pavement resurfacing along UN Avenue St., Mandaue City) amounting ₱3,371,688.00 we recommend that Management direct the persons liable to settle the disallowance immediately thru refund or by filing an appeal with the Commission on Audit.
We recommend that Management direct the persons liable to settle the disallowance immediately and henceforth, refrain from purchasing unnecessary items.

We recommend that Management instruct the Local School Board to submit the required documents enumerated to validate the payment of the honoraria for the kitchen staffs.  Henceforth, we recommend that Management ensure that all disbursements are supported with complete documentation.

We recommend that Management:
direct the officials concerned to prepare and submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment (LDRRMFIP) as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 indicating the 30% allocation of QRF and the allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness showing the   projects and activities to be funded, for our evaluation and monitoring purposes.
require the City Accountant to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on time using the prescribed format in COA Circular No. 2012-002 and require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) to submit such report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to the COA Auditor through the LDRRMC and the Local Development Council.
implement the implemented its LDRRMP as to the thematic areas of disaster preparedness, prevention and mitigation, response, rehabilitation and recovery.
direct the City Accountant to properly fill up the LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements the amount of the Available and Utilized Current Year Appropriations for the 70% Mitigating Fund and 30% Quick Response Fund.

We recommend that Management explain the non-provision of funds for the LCPC.
Further, we recommend that Management strictly follow the budgetary requirement as well as the programs, projects and activities (PPAs) for LCPC under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 dated July 4, 2012 and require the City LCPC to submit the details of the PPAs, the council ordinances and policies in consistent with the appropriated amount for the purpose of the budget year.

We recommend that the concerned City officials look into the contractor’s failure to complete the project within the contract period and re-compute the actual work-days, with considerations of suspensions and extensions, in order to determine whether there was breach of contract committed by the contractor and to demand for possible liquidated damages.

We recommend that the City Mayor direct the Collection Team of the hospital to intensify its collection efforts by sending demand letters on their unpaid bills with the hospital. Moreover, we also recommend that the Hospital send separate letters to the Punong Barangay requesting for their assistance in the collection of the unpaid bills. We also recommend that the City Legal Officer of the City Government of Mandaue initiate legal action against those patients who have the capacity to pay.
We further recommend that the City Accountant trace back the unrecorded receivable from prior periods, reconcile it with the collections reported and record the unpaid bills as part of the Receivables account of the City.

We recommend that Management submit a comprehensive explanation on the reason why the procurement of drugs and medicines was made without observing the guidelines set forth in EO 49.
Likewise we recommend that henceforth, procurement of medicines comply with the procedures as mandated under EO 49 and be made from the retail pharmacy of the Vicente Sotto Memorial Medical Center or at any DOH retail drugstores, if available, in order to generate savings and sustain the health programs/projects of the City.

We recommend that the City Government direct the City Accountant to:
exert efforts to enforce the settlement of the disallowances in compliance with the COE and NFD issued and to properly identify the other persons liable so that collections of the disallowed claims can be made upon them.
require the withholding of salaries or other compensation due the persons liable in satisfaction of the disallowances or charges as mandated under COA Circular 2009-006.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to record
collections in  the  appropriate  Cashbook  in  order  to  reflect  accurate
balances in the General, Special Education Fund and Trust Fund Cashbooks  at
any given  time.   Deposit  slips  should  also  be  prepared  per  fund  to
facilitate the recording thereof and fund transfers  be  done  regularly  to
the different, appropriate bank accounts.



We further recommend that the Municipal  Treasurer  record  transactions  as
they occur with the totals and balances written  in  ink  and  that  regular
reconciliation with the accounting records  be  done  so  that  deficiencies
would be easily detected and corrected.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that Management require the immediate liquidation of  the
outstanding cash advance balances granted as provided for in Section 5.1  of
COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 89  of  Presidential  Decree  No.  1445.
Moreover, send individual letter of demand to every  officer  and  employee,
enjoining immediate liquidation of their long outstanding cash advances.

Management Comment

      The audit exception was well taken by Management.  They  admitted  the
deficiencies and assured compliance to our audit recommendations.

Recommendation:

We recommend that Management:

   1. Require the persons making requests to list items  with  complete  and
      specific descriptions.

   2. Require the BAC to carefully review the items first  and  not  proceed
      with the Invitation to Bid/Request for Quotation if the items are  not
      specifically described.

Management Comment

      The  officials  concerned  explained  that  the  financial  assistance
granted to schools were treated as cash advance by the school  head  and  is
subject to liquidation.  However,  they assured us  for  compliance  of  the
recommendation in the ensuing transactions.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that Management require complete documents to support the
grant of financial  assistance  as  required  under  Section  3.1.1  of  COA
Circular No. 2012-001  dated  June  14,  2012.  We  further  recommend  that
management  set-up  internal  control  measures  in  order  to  monitor  the
releases of financial assistance to the  different  schools  like  recording
the same as Due from LGUs first and recording under  Subsidy  to  LGUs  only
upon submission of liquidation report.

Management Comment


      Concerned officials admitted the  deficiencies  noted.   They  however
assured the compliance of our audit recommendations in the ensuing year.


Recommendation:

      We recommend that management provide  a  venue  to  discuss  with  the
municipal employees the role of the LGU in ensuring that its  employees  are
able to receive a minimum of ₱4,000 take home pay per month in pursuance  of
Section 47 of the 2017 GAA.


      Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant and HRMO  monitor
payroll deductions and refuse to  sign  certifications  for  application  of
loans should an employee’s net pay fall below ₱4,000.00.

Management Comment

       The  audit  recommendation  was  well  taken   by   Management.   The
municipality will create the Local Council for Protection  of  Children  for
CY 2018.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that management create a Local Council for the Protection
of Children to advocate child rights,  plans  and  recommends  interventions
and monitors children’s programs and projects in the locality.

      We also recommend that  one  percent  (1%)  of  the  Internal  Revenue
Allotment (IRA) be allocated to ensure  that  all  plans  for  children  are
provided with funds, pursuant to Section 15 of Republic Act 9344.

We have recommended the following:

Strictly comply with the required physical count of all properties at least once a year and to submit a copy of the Inventory Report to the Audit Team not later than January 31 and every year thereafter to ensure accuracy of the PPE accounts presented in the financial statements.

Require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to maintain Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, and shall henceforth conduct periodic reconciliation of the balances of both records.

Require the Inventory Committee to conduct an inventory of local roads under their jurisdiction and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Local Road Network at the end of every accounting period.

Pursue complete reconciliation of the RPCLRN and the updated Registry of Public Infrastructures in coordination with the General Services Officer/Local Treasurer and the Accountant through detailed review of prior year’s transactions in order to identify unrecorded road networks.

Direct the Treasurer and the Accountant to maintain Local Road Network Ledger Card, and complete Local Road Inventory records with Road Map and Local Road Network Property Card, respectively.

Require the Office of the Accountant to adhere with the prescribed accounting rules relative to the asset recognition under Property, Plant and Equipment, including maintenance of Construction in Progress account in the Trust Fund books, and the subsequent transfer of completed projects and equipment from Trust Fund to General Fund; and

Provide necessary adjusting entries to correct the noted errors in the recording of various PPE transactions.
observations.managementAction:
Management already created Inventory and Disposal Teams to commence the inventory process.

Adjustments were provided for the errors noted in the audit.
observations.reason:
The Inventory Team instituted by the LGU still haven’t completed the required physical count of all properties of the municipality, hence the non-submission of the report on physical count. Although the management has submitted report pertaining to Local Road Networks, existence of which couldn’t be verified since the audit team was not invited to witness the physical count. Hence, generally, the total PPE account was not corroborated as to existence, completeness and accuracy. This deficiency was aggravated further due to the absence of various records that must be maintained by management, hence reiterated in Part II of the report.

Adjustments were already provided for misclassified accounts and the errors noted in depreciation particularly with the incorrect residual value. However, there were still items not adjusted and new errors noted for the year 2017.

With the foregoing, reiteration was made Part II under finding no. 1.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that both the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer regularly reconcile the general and subsidiary ledger balances of the Cash in Bank-LCCA accounts with the cashbook balances before closing of the book at month end pursuant to Section 10 of NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume II to ensure reliability and correctness of balances of said accounts.
observations.managementAction: The management had already provided adjustments for some items which caused the unreconciled balance and is on the process of reviewing further previous years’ transactions to make both records the same as to the cash in bank balances.
observations.reason: The two bank accounts noted in the audit were reconciled as of December 31, 2017, however, this is only due to the fact that the Municipal Treasurer had used the 2014 and 2015 ledger balances of Municipal Accountant so as to have a reconciled balances in succeeding periods, hence prior years’ differences are yet to be identified. Furthermore, other bank accounts showed differences between the two records for the year 2017, thus  reiterated in Part II under finding no. 2.1

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our previous recommendation that management require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Intra-agency accounts, Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds to promptly identify any discrepancy in the balances of the two accounts and to be able to effect the necessary adjustments if appropriate to bring the affected accounts into agreement.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant started identifying the discrepancies in the balances of the reciprocal accounts, Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, and to effect the necessary adjustments to bring the affected accounts into agreement.
observations.reason:
Adjusting entries were provided by the Accountant for the errors in the years 2014, 2015 and 2016; however, it would really take time for the agency to fully reconcile the said accounts since the discrepancy has been existing prior 2014. 

This was reiterated in Part II under finding no. 5

observations.recommendation:
We have reiterated that Management:

Require all Accountable Officers to liquidate their cash advances on time, otherwise, impose administrative sanctions against them for any unjustifiable reasons due to failure to liquidate cash advances within the prescribe period, in accordance with Section 89 of PD 1445, COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and Civil Service Commission No. 0676 dated June 17, 2004.

Strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

Cause or order the withholding of any money claims against the accountable officers concerned until they have fully settled their unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Management did continuously exert all efforts to require all Accountable Officers to liquidate their cash advances by issuing demand letters. Also, withholding of salaries against some accountable officers has been made until they have fully settled their unliquidated cash advances.
observations.reason: There’s still remaining balance of unliquidated cash advances pertaining to prior years; and some of these accountable officers were still allowed to draw cash advances in the current year, thus reiterated in Part II of the report under finding no. 7

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Accounting Office in cooperation with the Treasury Office ensure the complete submission of accounts within the prescribed period for timely examination and verification of accounts pursuant to Section 7.2.1 of the 2009 of the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts,   Section 39 (1) and (3) of P.D 1445, thus to avoid disciplinary actions and/or penalties  provided for under Section 23 (1) and (3) of The Administrative Code of 1987 and Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.
observations.managementAction: The Treasury and Accounting Office have done their best to comply. The monthly accounts, trial balances, statement of bank reconciliation and disbursement vouchers were submitted on a monthly basis but still did not meet the required date of submission.
observations.reason: Regular monitoring of the submission of accounts revealed radical improvements in the timely submission of accounts, but was still beyond the strict rules/period provided by pertinent laws hence reiterated in Part II of the report under finding no. 8

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management expedite the transfer of Cash in Bank account balance maintained with Philippine National Bank to the new account of the Municipality with the Land Bank of the Philippines pursuant to the aforementioned provisions.
observations.managementAction: The management had requested for change of depository bank and transfer of internal revenue allotment and all other funds authorized for LGUs from PNB to Landbank and they had been continuously transferring cash balances from PNB to the new LBP account.
observations.reason: Final approval by the Bureau of Local Government Finance (BLGF) is yet to be received by the management thus reiterated in Part II of the report under finding no. 9

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our recommendation that Management explain and justify the payments of guarantee fees and provide legal bases for the payments thereof.
observations.managementAction: The management provided explanation on the findings and had already ceased paying the guarantee fees to PNB upon receiving the approval of the latter for the termination of guarantee agreement.
observations.reason:
The transaction was already suspended in audit through the issuance of NS No. 17-001-100-(13-16) dated February 09, 2017.

Final disposition/ audit action for this matter is still pending with a request for legal opinion sent to Regional Office/ Central Office dated June 07, 2017 for legal interpretation on the justifications provided by the management.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the Management the following courses of action:

Ensure that all of its insurable properties are adequately covered by the General Insurance Fund (GIF) administered by the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) in compliance with Republic Act No. 656 or the “Property Insurance Law”, as amended by Presidential Decree No. 245 dated July 13, 1973, in order to avoid the risk of non-indemnification in case of damage or loss due to fortuitous events.

Submit a complete inventory of insurable properties to GSIS for them to make appropriate evaluation whether the items included can be covered or not, annual report of which must also be submitted to the audit team; and

Provide explanation or basis on the insurance coverage applied with the PNB General Insurers Co., Inc; otherwise, do not cause the insurance renewal for Old Market and Municipal Building, and coordinate thereof to GSIS for possible coverage with the GIF.
observations.managementAction: Treasury Office did not cause insurance renewal for Old Market and Municipal Building to PNB, instead these assets were included in the list coordinated to GSIS for coverage.
observations.reason: There were still some properties noted as not being covered with necessary insurance, hence reiterated in Part II of the report under finding no. 11

observations.managementAction: The unexpended balances of the LDRRMF-QRF and MOOE for CY 2016 have been transferred to Trust Liability-DRRM account as of March 1, 2017. The management has also required the LDRRMO to ensure lump-sum presentation of the 30% QRF in the LDRRMFIP and that all unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF for CO shall be reflected under a separate caption in the LDRRMFIP.
observations.reason: The unexpended balances of LDRRMF – QRF and MOOE for CY 2016 were already transferred to TF through the issuance of Check No. 676961 dated March 01, 2017. Furthermore, the LDRRMFIP for year 2017 has also shown the correct presentation of QRF as lump sum appropriation and those programs to be charged from Special Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We have reiterated our recommendation that the management:

Instruct the municipality’s Inventory or Property and Supply Unit/Team, as well as the Office of the Accountant, to conduct physical inventory of the supplies and equipment procured out of the LDRRMF and provide adjusting entries to set-up asset accounts thereof; and shall henceforth

Ensure observance of the rules set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-002 and COA Circular No. 2014-002 pertaining to the accounting, inventory and monitoring guidelines for procured resources out of the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction:
The Accounting Office provided the necessary adjusting entries to set up asset accounts as recommended, except for the rescue rope which was already used during the calamity.

The management also instructed the LDRRMO, Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to observe the rules pertaining to the management of LDRRM supplies and other inventory.
observations.reason:
Various adjusting entries were provided by the management to correct the noted errors in the recording of PPE purchased out of LDDRMF. However, for the year 2017, there were still some noted transactions that were erroneously recorded.

Also, since the proper management of supplies under LDRRM is still not being implemented, the observation is reiterated in Part II of the report under finding no. 12

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the management, in the conduct of ensuing public bidding(s) for the operation of fishery lots, must ensure that the set of rules and regulations is comprehensive to cover all agreed terms and that the implementers shall abide thereto to avoid misunderstanding and conflicts between the Committee on Auction and the participants. Also, since the Government of the Philippines is promoting/enhancing the type of participatory governance, it is recommended that some aspects of any activities the management may undertake of public concern be regulated with opinions from the main community or group of people who might be affected by the agency’s decisions.

Documented copy of the ruling on the under-bid fishing lots for the validation of awarded bid prices.
List of participants with unsettled accounts from previous bidding. Also, require them to settle first their liabilities before awarding the new lots.
observations.managementAction: A comprehensive rules and regulations for the conduct of public bidding for the operation of fishery lots have been made.
observations.reason: The management had already ensured that rules are properly disseminated to avoid conflict with the participants. Although, another deficiency was noted during 2017 bidding, this has different nature and recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the management the following courses of action:

Instruct all concerned employees and officials to strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001, COA Circular No. 2005-003, COA Circular No. 92-61A and COA Circular No. 96-004 relative to expenses on travel, seminars and conferences to correct the foregoing findings.

Oblige all signatories of the documents, particularly on Itinerary of Travel, Liquidation Reports and Disbursement Vouchers to certify only travel-related expenses which are observant of the said provisions.

Obtain plane tickets directly from airline companies to avoid other charges imposed by travel agencies; however booking with these agencies may still be allowed provided that their offers will be scrutinized through comparison of existing prices on airline websites, request itineraries with detailed breakdown of cost and/or have their quotations submitted in order for the management to choose the most advantageous price.
observations.managementAction: The management instructed all the concerned employees and officials to strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001, COA Circular No. 2005-003, COA Circular No. 92-61Aand COA Circular No. 96-004 relative to expenses on travel and conferences.
observations.reason: Audit of 2017 liquidations of cash advances and other disbursements relative to traveling and seminar/ training expenses still revealed some deficiencies; hence reiterated in Part II of the report under finding no. 7

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the management provide justification on the necessity of conducting the ELA Formulation and Budget Call in Ilocos Norte and shall henceforth observe the proper procurement procedure of training/conference/seminar venues with consideration to prudence, economy, suitability, accessibility and proper procurement procedures.
observations.managementAction: Management provided justification as to the venue of the agency’s affair and committed to observe the proper procurement procedure of training/ conference/ seminar venues for the succeeding events the LGU will hold.
observations.reason: The audit team, after thorough evaluation of the prevailing circumstances accepted the justifications of the management. It is likewise noted that there’s no transaction of the same nature and deficiency was incurred by the management in the year 2017.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management submit the documentary requirements for the conduct of auditorial review of the contracts as mentioned in COA MEMO Circular No. 2005-027 and for payment of claims under COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: Some of the documentary requirements have been submitted. The management also instructed the responsible persons to submit the remaining documentary requirements to comply with COA Circular No. 2005-027 and payment of claims under COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.reason:
Some of the documents noted to be missing were still not submitted to the audit team. Moreover, other documentary requirements for different projects were noted in the current year audit were likewise not included in the submitted bid documents and disbursement vouchers.

Reiterated in Part II of the report under finding no. 15

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the management require the person responsible to properly fill up all information in the Purchase Requests (PR), Purchase Orders (PO) and Inspection Reports for proper referencing and easier determination of the reasonableness of the transaction and compliance with the requirements of R.A. 9184.
observations.managementAction: The management required the persons responsible to properly fill up all information in the PR, PO, and IAR.
observations.reason: The management ensured that in 2017, the said documents are being properly filled up in compliance with RA 9184 and other pertinent rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the management refrain from using brand names in conducting agency’s procurements and that all transfers of accountability for particular asset shall be documented properly.

Copy of DOH-RO I and HLGP guidelines and specifications
Sangguniang Bayan Resolution authorizing Hon. Mayor Aldrin C. Cerdan to enter and sign for and in behalf of the Municipality a Memorandum of Agreement with the DOH-RO I
Approved Work and Financial Plan incorporated in the Municipal Health Action Plan of the LGU
Approved Project Expenditures or Estimated Expenses indicating the project objective and expected output
Copy of the OR upon receipt of fund transferred
Copy of OR issued by the Source Agency evidencing refund of unexpended balance of fund transfer
Proof of Donation
Proof of Acceptance of Donation
observations.managementAction:
The BAC ensures that no brand names will be indicated in procurements. Same was advised to all officers and employees of LGU-Anda. 

For the documents, management has advised the implementing unit to provide the need documents and submit the same to the audit group.
observations.reason:
Based on procurements made in 2017, no brand names were referred to during the process.

However, the documentary deficiencies noted were not submitted by the management hence reiterated in Part II of the report under finding no. 17

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the management the following courses of action:

Submit the complete documentary requirements for the conduct of auditorial review of contract and the payment of claims in compliance with COA Memo Circular No. 2005-027, COA Circular No. 2012-001 and other pertinent provisions of the revised IRR of RA 9184.
Ensure that 10% retention money is being deducted from the progress payment to the contractor to serve as warranty for hidden defects during the construction period and shall only be released upon final acceptance of the project.
Strictly observe the requirement of R.A. 9184 on the payment of mobilization fees which should be based solely on the 15% of the total contract price regardless whether the contract includes detailed engineering design fee.
observations.managementAction:
All the documentary requirements have been submitted and that 10% retention money is being deducted from the progress payment to the contractor to serve as warranty for hidden defects during the construction period. 
Further, management strictly observe the requirement of R.A 9184 on the payment of mobilization fees which should be based solely on the 15% of the total contract price.
observations.reason:
The documentary requirements for the said project were already furnished to the audit team.

Likewise, in the year 2017, for the retention money and mobilization fees, the management was noted to be compliant with the rules pertaining thereof.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management stop granting advance payment for goods procured unless authorized based on the Section 4 of “Annex D” of the revised IRR of RA 9184. Likewise, ensure that progress payments shall all be charged first against the advance payment until fully exhausted.
observations.managementAction: The management instructed the responsible officials to observe strictly the rules on advance payments and mobilization fees.
observations.reason: Based on the audit of 2017 procurements, the management was compliant with the said rules on advance payment and mobilization fees.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management explain the use of Repeat Order mode of procurement for the second batch of beneficiaries from the same supplier with original contract awarded through Negotiated Procurement contrary to Section 51 of the revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction:
The management advised those persons responsible strictly comply with the provisions of RA 9184 pertaining to Repeat Order mode of procurement.

Management also provided explanation on the use of Repeat Order.
observations.reason: The management explained that due to the consideration of time to execute the procurement, they made an honest mistake to overlook one of the criteria for the use of Repeat Order mode of procurement, hence forgiven.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the management maintain registry of eligible suppliers, contractors and consultants in compliance with Section 53.1.2.2 of the revised IRR of RA 9184. Furthermore, submit the foregoing eligibility requirements of Traction Four Wheel Drive Performance Shop to ascertain the validity of the transaction.

Invitation or request for submission of price quotation was not posted in the PhilGEPS website contrary to Section 54.2 of the revised IRR of RA 9184. Also, observers were not invited in all stages of negotiation which is not in conformity with Section 53.1.6 of the same.

We have recommended that management strictly observe the compliance of all the provisions of the revised IRR of RA 9184 during Negotiated Procurement particularly with the posting of invitation or request for submission of price quotation in PhilGEPS (Section 54.2) and the invitation of observers in all stages of negotiation (53.1.6).
observations.managementAction:
The management required the BAC to maintain registry of eligible suppliers, contractors and consultants in compliance with Section 53.1.2.2 of the revised IRR of RA 9184.

Also, some of the documentary deficiencies noted were submitted to the auditor.
observations.reason:
The BAC as required by the management maintains registry of eligible suppliers, contractors and consultants in compliance with Section 53.1.2.2 of the revised IRR of RA 9184.

For the documentary deficiencies to establish the eligibility of the supplier, the management failed to provide such hence, Notice of Disallowance (ND) No. 18-001-300 was issued dated January 19, 2018.


The management admitted that they were unaware of the detailed process of implementing negotiated procurement, thus the management, upon getting well-equipped of the rules, had strictly implemented such in their succeeding procurements.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Management to accomplish a Statement of Itemized Peace and Order Program presenting the specific activities to ensure effective and efficient control of the Confidential Fund, and to further corroborate the propriety of the allocated amount for the said fund.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the required Itemized Peace and Order Program to comply with the provisions of Section 3.23 and 6.1.8.3 (b) of the Joint Circular No. 2015-01 of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG and DND dated January 8, 2015.
observations.reason: The management had submitted the required Statement of Itemized POP to support the computation of allowable Confidential Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the management the following course of actions:

Provide necessary adjusting entries for the noted erroneous recording relative to the Due to GSIS account.
Explain the differences noted between the remitted employee payments and agency contributions, and the withheld amounts from salary releases to employees. If the remittances made are valid, require the concerned employees to refund the relative amount; otherwise, coordinate with GSIS on the erroneous remittance.

Explain the differences noted between the remitted employee payments and agency contributions, and the withheld amounts from salary releases to employees. If the remittances made are valid, require the concerned employees to refund the relative amount; otherwise, coordinate with GSIS on the erroneous remittance.
observations.managementAction: The management will require the office of the municipal accountant to review the said account and provide the necessary adjusting entries for the errors noted and to cause immediate remittance of amount due for remittance.
observations.reason:
Although some adjusting entries had been provided, the ledger balance of Due to GSIS Account as of December 31, 2017 still shows unremitted balance/ possible errors attributable to previous years’ transactions. 

Reiterated in Part II of the report under finding no. 20

observations.recommendation:
We have reiterated our recommendation contained in the previous Annual Audit Reports that management adopt or design a better policy in granting loans to ensure its collection. The loan agreement must be revised, wherein the date of maturity must be indicated, payment must not be on a voluntary basis and imposition of interest and penalty must be strict. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should make a follow up on the demand letters sent to debtors. In case where the debtors fail to settle their accounts after due notices, apply legal sanctions. This may help to improve the collection efficiency of the Municipality wherein the funds collected may be utilized for re-loaning to other beneficiaries.

Likewise, Management was requested to submit the following documents to enable the Audit Team to make a decision in audit:

 Copy of Memorandum of Agreement between the Municipality and the source agency and other supporting documents;
 Copy of Loan Agreement; and
 Copy of Demand Letters sent to the borrowers.
observations.managementAction: Collection letters were already made and delivered to the debtors. However, only ₱300.00 was collected during 2017. The management was informed that some of the beneficiaries already died and we are in the process of requesting copies of their death certificates to support such claims.
observations.reason:
Despite the issuance of demand letters, as of December 31, 2017, the Treasurer’s Office has only received P300.00 representing payment of one recipient. Furthermore, the management still failed to secure MOA and Loan Agreement to determine the real terms of the transaction.

Reiterated in Part II of the report under finding no. 21

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the management should ensure that the disbursements charged to SEF are supported with complete documentation with proper receipting of transferred funds if the LGU will not directly implement the PPAs. Likewise, require the recipient of funds to submit proof of expenditures to substantiate the implementation of local school board activities for the year 2016.

Further we reiterate our previous recommendation that the officials concerned refrain from incurring expenditures not chargeable to Special Education Fund or which are not the priority programs under pertinent rules; and make further assessment on more vital issues concerning the quality of education and not merely focusing on athletic and other DepEd-related competitions
observations.managementAction: The management in coordination with the Local School Board agreed to require liquidations of the amount received for the SEF disbursements.
observations.reason: For the year 2017, all fund releases to schools were liquidated by submitting documentary evidences to support the spending made by the school personnel in line with the main purpose of Special Education Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the management the following course of actions:

Ensure that the responsible officials and employees, as withholding agents, should see to it that taxes withheld are accordingly remitted on or before their due dates to avoid the possible sanctions imposed by BIR Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2012 and the National Internal Revenue Code for the non-remittance of taxes withheld within the due dates.
Effect adjustments in the affected subsidiary ledgers noted with errors in the recording of withholding tax on compensation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made the necessary analysis of the Subsidiary Ledgers of the said account and effected the necessary adjustments. After determining the valid amount payable to BIR, immediate payment was made to settle all prior years’ obligations.
observations.reason: Verification of pertinent records disclosed that as of December 31, 2017, the balance of the due to BIR account is ₱1,178,778.30 which is due for remittance on January 2018.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management require the suppliers/contractors on the issuance of Sales Invoice and/or Official Receipts on various procurement of goods made in compliance with Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 2-2014 dated January 13, 2014 and likewise, strictly observed said regulations on future disbursements made.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting and Treasury Office required all suppliers and contractors to adhere with the provisions of Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 2-2014 dated January 13, 2014 for all succeeding transactions of the Municipality.
observations.reason: In the audit of 2017 transactions, the responsible persons were already compliant with the said rules by requiring their contractors / suppliers to issue BIR official receipts/ sales invoices.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the management require the responsible officials to strictly observe BIR Revenue Memorandum Order No. 23-2014 and other rules and regulations relative to the collection of taxes to avoid penalties arising from non-compliance thereto.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office applied the correct tax percentage on all transactions.
observations.reason: Audit of 2017 payments of the Municipality to various contractors revealed that the responsible persons have already complied with Section II.A of BIR Revenue Memo Order No. 23-2014.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous recommendation that management

 (a) create an inventory committee and conduct complete physical inventory of all properties of the municipality to determine their existence and physical conditions in accordance with above mentioned rules and regulations; and 

(b) exert extra efforts to locate old records of acquisition to provide basis in the preparation of subsidiary records, computation for depreciation or request for the dropping from records all obsolete, destroyed and unserviceable properties.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Unit sent a summary of PAR per Department and requested to account for the properties stated therein.
observations.reason:
No Report of Physical Count for PPE was submitted to the Audit Team because the conduct of the physical count of PPE was not yet completed.

Reiterated in Observation No. 1

observations.recommendation:
We recommended  the following courses of action:
Enforce strict compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002 in the granting of cash advances and the subsequent liquidations thereof;  
Monitor liquidation of outstanding cash advances, refrain from  granting new/additional cash advances unless the previous cash advance has been liquidated or a proper accounting thereof has been made; and
Send demand letters to those officers and employees and/or if warranted effect the withholding of salary for those who failed to settle immediately their accounts as required by the regulations and initiate appropriate legal action against the former Municipal Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office strictly monitored the granting and liquidation of all cash advances.
observations.reason: The location of the former Municipal Treasurer, on AWOL status since CY 2013, cannot be located.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the 

(i) Municipal Accountant
(a) ensure that all payments for AICS be adequately and properly supported by the required documents enumerated under Section 4.2 of DSWD MC No. 02 s. 2014 to establish the validity and propriety of claims; and

(b) ensure that the proper account is used in recording the payment of AICS; and

(ii) MSWDO is advised to prepare and maintain a file of each client/beneficiary’s case evaluation to facilitate validation of the propriety of said transaction.
observations.managementAction: The documents for AICS submitted by MSWDO to Accounting  Office for review were now supported by the necessary supporting documents.
observations.reason: The Annual Approved Budget for AICS for CYs 2017-2018 were still under OMOE account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management 

(a) procure all common-use supplies directly from the PS-DBM;  and 

(b) cause the yearly submission of its Annual Procurement Plan for Common-Use Supplies and Equipment to assist the Procurement Service in strengthening its operations and improve its delivery of service.
observations.managementAction: The budget for procurement of supplies and materials of the Municipality were now centralized under the Municipal General Services Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of charging payment of membership dues of appointed officials in their respective association/league against the funds of the Municipality in compliance with the Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) Opinion No. 99 dated July 3, 2002.
observations.managementAction:
The payment of membership dues of the league/associa-
tion of appointive officials was stopped.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended tha the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to comply with the provision on the minimum monthly net take home pay of Three Thousand Five Hundred  Pesos (₱3,500.00) for all the regular employees of the municipality.  

The Municipal Accountant is likewise advised to observe the order of preference of the authorized deductions of loans and contributions from salaries and other benefits of employees concerned as mandated by Section 52 of Republic Act No. 10717, otherwise known as the General Appropriations Act for FY 2016.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor ensure that the provision of the minimum take home pay of  ₱3,500.00 was fully observed by the employees.
observations.reason: There were still employees of the Municipality whose salary are below the minimum take home pay.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the activities or duties performed by the officials of NEUST in addition to, or over and above their regular functions be enumerated to determine the basis of the grant of the honoraria. 

We also recommended that the said honoraria be remitted to NEUST who shall cause the payment of the same to the concerned officials.
observations.managementAction: The payments of honoraria of NEUST Officials are now paid by check payable to NEUST-Main Campus.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management stop its dealings with business establishments wherein the officials and or employees of the municipality have directly or indirectly have financial or material  interest.
We also advised management to ensure that compliance with  Sections 3 (1) and 7 (a) of the Code of Conduct and Ethical Standards for Public Officials and Employees (Republic Act No. 6713).
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had already stopped the practice of dealings with business establishments with conflict of interest with the management.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adjust the affected accounts involved by debiting the Cash in Bank to reflect its actual balances and crediting the Accounts Payable to restore the liability to concerned creditors in the amount of ₱10,480.00.  

Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer is advised to send written notices to payees of checks which has long been outstanding a month before said checks become stale.  

Strict adherence to existing rules and regulations regarding the disposition of stale checks should be observed by concerned officials of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant have already effected the necessary adjusting entries for Cash in Bank and Accounts Payable accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management to remit all contributions deducted from the employees promptly to the GSIS.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality observed the intact withholding and remittance of GSIS contributions.
observations.reason: The full remittance of the amount of premiums deducted for a particular month was not fully observed due to delayed submission of transactions to the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to continue performing the function of a withholding agent of the National Government and adhere with the timely and intact remittance of taxes withheld as required under Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007 dated March 20, 2007.

We also advised the management to exert efforts to locate pertinent documents pertaining to the previous years’ withheld taxes from suppliers and dealers in order to effect remittance of their respective taxes.  

Likewise, identify the employees with over withheld taxes so that refund could be made.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality observed the intact withholding and remittance of taxes withheld.
observations.reason: The full remittance of the amount of taxes withheld for a particular month was not fully observed due to delayed submission of transactions to the Accounting Office.

Direct the officials and employees concerned to refund the unauthorized excess payment of the incentives given to them and stop the practice of granting the same in excess of the allowed P10,000.00 per employee to avoid disallowance in the future.  


Further, strictly comply with DBM Budget Circular 2010-3 and other issuances on additional personnel benefits.

Thus, only JO personnel who were paid thereto are required to refund which until now has not been made. Notices of Disallowance were already sent but management had formally sent an appeal to the COA Regional Office on this matter.

observations.recommendation: Follow-up the request for reconsideration of the travel authority, otherwise, refund of the traveling expenses paid by  the city government  amounting to P191,307.60 be sought pursuant to DILG Memorandum No. 2001-52  dated May 8,2001.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Accounting Personnel to record the purchases of supplies and materials for stock like drugs and medicines, medical, dental and laboratory supplies to the appropriate inventory accounts in order to account its receipt. Likewise, assign personnel to consolidate weekly the RIS for supplies and materials issued using the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI). The SSMI together with the original copy of the RIS shall be submitted to the Accountant who will compute the supplies issued using the moving average method including the ending inventory pursuant to Section 121 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

Instruct the OIC, General Services Officer/ Designated Property Officers of the Hospital/Rural Health unit to maintain the stock cards for supplies to account for the receipt and disposition of the same. He shall see to it that the balance per stock card should always reconcile with the ledger cards of the accounting unit.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting office together with the City Hospital, City Health & DGS already adopted the perpetual inventory method of recording drugs and medicines and medical, dental and laboratory supplies. Documents were sent to COA-Mandaue City office as proof of implementation of inventory recording as well as the recognition of issuance of inventory as per Manual on the NGAS, Volume I for LGUs.
observations.reason: As of December 2017, the accounting section has already booked up drugs and medicine as inventory.

observations.recommendation: Secure an amended BAC resolution to specify the correct item that was purchased pursuant to Section 48.3 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.  Further, direct all signing officials to review all the data contained in the documents to avoid any error.
observations.managementAction: Management explained that the error committed, if any is clerical and inadvertent
observations.reason: The previous BAC committee was already replaced therefore compliance of the board resolution can no longer be obtained. The transaction has already been overtaken by subsequent events.

observations.recommendation:
Submit an explanation on the circumstances on the hiring of consultants without conducting the required procurement process for consulting services pursuant to the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 or the Government Procurement Reform Act.  Submit the basis on the payment of honoraria in addition to the consultancy fee of the consultants; otherwise the honoraria shall be disallowed in audit.

Further, submit the appropriate DTRs and accomplishment reports to support the claims.
observations.managementAction:
In line with the criteria for the hiring of consultants, DepEd School Div. Superintendent explained that “the hiring of consultants was made in good faith without vested interest of enriching there-from and was anchored on the precept of the curriculum and in observance to the criteria prepared for the desired field. Inadvertently overlook down on some provisions of the said law particularly in the aspect of procurement, the impressive performance of the consultants has been the barometer of the local School board for the formers’ yearly renewal of service.“

The Visual Arts & Rondalla Consultants explained that the discrepancies on their DTRs were not intentionally done and committed to be vigilant in the future to prevent the occurrence of the lapses.
observations.reason: This finding is already undertaken by subsequent events as the consultants for the Mandaue School of Arts are no longer in service.

observations.recommendation:
Require the supplier to deliver 955 CDs to comply with the issued purchase order or refund the commensurate amount to the City.  Otherwise, determine the reasonable amount that shall be determined as the cost of the 955 CDs and require the supplier to refund said amount.

Further, refrain from indicating the titles/brand names of items to be purchased in the purchase request and canvass forms pursuant to Section 18 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of R.A. 9184 to prevent its disallowance in post-audit.
	
Direct the CSWS to submit an evaluation report on the choice of the purchased books relative to its technical specifications and as compared with other book
observations.managementAction: Management, thru Ms. Maricel Yu, ECCD action officer explained DEPED has a list of instructional materials and specification of the titles of the textbooks and workbooks are in accordance with DEPED approved Textbook.
observations.reason: The cost of the 955 CDS that were not delivered cannot be determined thus issuance of subsequent Notice of Disallowance cannot be made.  This is already undertaken by subsequent events.

observations.recommendation:
Evaluate the amounts given and refund the amount of P1,592,590.00 or a portion thereof that is not within a specific activity or project contemplated under Local Budget Circular No. 62.

Henceforth, avoid paying honoraria to salaried officials and employees who are designated to act as members of the different committees/ councils/ boards or ad hoc bodies created by the City to tackle issues and concerns related to the improvement of its services, operations, fiscal resources and the proper implementation of its programs and projects to avoid audit suspensions or disallowances.
observations.managementAction: Honorarium is no longer granted to members of these committees anymore since we received the audit observation memorandum.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the City Accountant to make the appropriate adjusting entry to record the cost of 1,000 copies of coffee table books amounting to P1,600,000.00 and reconcile its records with the General Services Officer (DGS) or Property Custodian to effectively monitor the issuance thereof and determine the proper accountability of the accountable officer concerned.
