Require the offices releasing copies to official visitors to maintain a logbook to account the number of books received from the DGS to account every item received by the distributing office.
  
Direct the City Accountant to record the purchase of supplies and materials for stock, regardless of whether or not they are consumed within the accounting period, as inventory following the perpetual inventory method pursuant to Section 114 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.

Take concrete steps in correcting deficiencies noted by maintaining the required property records, submitting an accurate, complete and reliable inventory report on time and reconciling the results of the physical inventory and property records, and continuous coordination between property and finance departments on PPE transactions to ensure immediate reconciliation of records.

observations.recommendation: Direct the City Treasurer and the Accounting Offices to coordinate and effect the remittance of all unremitted withheld taxes which had accumulated through the years. Henceforth, the City Treasurer to instruct the personnel concerned in his office to submit the RCI and supporting DVs to the Accounting Office weekly so that by the 5th day of the succeeding month of all RCIs and DVs are already submitted to the Accounting Office to give ample time for the latter to prepare the complete remittance returns; and the two offices to coordinate and initiate measures to avoid recurrences of this severe lapse in operation which will possible result in BIR surcharges and penalties due to deficient remittance returns.

Partial remittances have been made.

A memorandum dated Feb. 5, 2018 was sent to the City accountant to provide its comments and/ or document to facilitate the implementation of COA’s recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
Utilization reports amounting to P7.4 million submitted by two NGOs in July 2013 were not yet recorded as of December 31, 2013 or a delay of almost six (6) months after receipt pending review by the agency’s pre-audit section of the Accounting Department which is in violation of Rule VI Section 1 of RA 6713 Rules Implementing the Code of Conduct and Ethical Standards for Public Officials and Employees and overstating the subject account in the City’s financial statements

Further, subsidiary ledgers maintained did not provide sufficient information about the accounts which is necessary in monitoring settlement thereof and to ensure that the financial assistance given to said NGOs/POs were utilized in accordance with the purpose these were granted.  Moreover, reports prepared did not show dates when the financial assistance were granted nor were aging schedules prepared which would have guided the agency in monitoring the utilization reports thereof.

Direct the City Legal Officer, in coordination with the City Accountant, to immediately send demand letters to NGOs/ POs that have not submitted their Utilization Reports as some of these accounts had been in the books since CY 2010 and earlier yet and also for the submission of the documents found lacking in their Utilization Reports as the event and activities for which these FAs were granted had long been done.

Require the City Accountant to explain the delay in the recording of the subject Utilization Reports of RAFI and MCCI.  Further, we recommend that the Accounting Department look into the root cause of this problem so that appropriate action can be made to address it.
Instruct the City Accountant to monitor closely the personnel handling the subsidiary ledgers to ensure that these are updated and these contain the necessary information to facilitate the gathering of data for each financial assistance granted as well as the monitoring of the required Utilization Reports.

Review the programs, projects and activities for which those financial assistance were granted so that appropriate actions can be made to-

maximize benefits to its constituents, if the agreements/contracts supporting the grant allows the continued use of the funds; or
Secure approval from the source of agency for the use of the funds for some other related program, project or activity if there are limitations in the grant of the financial assistance; or return immediately the unutilized funds as required under Circular 94-013.
observations.managementAction:
Management has exerted efforts to demand liquidation resulting to a reduction in the balance of the account.

Recorded already.

As of December 31, 2017 balances amounted to 
P 6,999,730.45.

The Finance Committee had already conducted a meeting on the processes to be observed in using those financial assistance which had been received in the previous years.
observations.reason:
Validation disclosed that Subsidiary ledgers (SLs) are already maintained.

As of December 31, 2017, the financial assistance remained unliquidated.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Dept. of General Services and the City Engineering Office to submit all the supporting documents like the Purchase Request to Inspection and Acceptance documents to the Accounting Office for the preparation of the disbursement voucher (DV) for the payment of  the procurement transactions.  Also, personal follow up should be made with the appropriate offices concerned where the DV is submitted for review and signature to ensure that the claim is processed at the expected pace until payment or release of check has been facilitated. 

Instruct the personnel assigned thereto to maintain records of receipt and releases where the DV is submitted to identify offices which are acting late on the transaction for no apparent reason so that proper measures are undertaken by management to address the issue. 

Issue guidelines directing concerned officials who are signatories to the DV and other supporting documents to act on every claim with dispatch and indicate thereto the dates of signing to establish accountability and eliminate the snail pace process of payment and bureaucratic red tape to improve the image of the City and encourage the participation of more suppliers or contractors in order to widen bidding competition and obtain a well-meaning procurement process to result in the most advantageous price for goods, services and infrastructure projects of the City.
observations.managementAction: These issues are addressed in the Performance Governance System adopted by the City in finding ways to improve their payment system
observations.reason: Validation disclosed that payments to suppliers and creditors were still delayed from 60 days to one (I) year.

observations.managementAction: The City Administrator indorsed to the City Legal Office the list of functionaries who failed to liquidate their respective cash advances to initiate legal action, as per memorandum dated February 21, 2018.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the City Engineer to disapprove requisitions for construction supplies and materials if no pre-inspection report and approved program of work are submitted by the requisitioning party to support a particular repair and maintenance activity or for other purposes to be undertaken, to avoid improper thereof.

Instruct the City Engineer to assign a competent personnel to oversee the proper application of CSM withdrawn who will render a report thereto determine the propriety and extent of usage with the instruction that anu unutilized item found after work completion be turned over to the supply officer by the requesting party.

Direct the supply officer to issue proper receipt for unutilized CSM turned over by the requisitioners and record the same in the stock card for updating and reconciliation with the inventory on hand and submit a corresponding report thereof to the Accounting Office for appropriate adjustment of their records.

observations.recommendation:

Moreover, we also recommend that the Hospital send separate letters to the Barangay Captain requesting for their assistance in the collection of the unpaid bills.  We also recommend that the City Legal Officer of the City Government of Mandaue initiate legal action against those patients who have the capacity to pay.    

Prepare a follow-up letter requesting payment of unsettled hospital bills from Philhealth, PCSO, PSWD and PDAF.

However, the documents on the receivables per patient beginning 2010 to 2015 is still lacking.

The City Accountant admitted that they were not practicing the setting up of receivable regarding the hospital bills instead book income every time there are collections made. Although the Mandaue City Hospital has the complete list of receivables, the same is not being submitted to them for recording.

As an initial compliance, they already sent a communication to the Mandaue City Hospital for the immediate resolution of this issue.
observations.reason:
AOM No. 2018-018 (’17) dated February 15, 2018 was issued on this.

This is reiterated in Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management intensify their knowledge and competence of GAD, its goals, objectives, laws, regulations and the utilization of the GAD Budget.  Likewise, consider as GAD PPAs only those programs and activities which address issues concerning women’s human rights and economic empowerment in accordance with Joint Circular No. 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that: The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) consolidate requisitions for one or more items needed at or about the same time by the requisitioner and avoid splitting of government contracts. The BAC ensure that all procurement of supplies, materials, furnitures and equipment are done through competitive public bidding, pursuant to Section 10, Article IV of R. A. 9184 in order to give the widest publicity possible to all prospective and qualified suppliers with price offers or quotations most advantageous to the government. The BAC strictly comply with the conditions enumerated under Section 52.1, Rule XVI of the same IRR in the event that the original mode of procurement through competitive public bidding as recommended in the APP cannot be pursued and shopping is resorted to as an alternative mode of procurement by the Bids and Awards Committee. The management strictly adhere with the provisions contained under Section 7.2, Rule II of the same IRR requiring the formulation of the Annual Procurement Plan which shall consolidate the respective Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) of the different programs, projects and activities of the different end-user units the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Management stated that office supplies are now procured by bulk except for some instance specially in the RHU and Waterworks Department, but the Municipal Mayor signified that these will not happen again.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management : a) Exercise prudence and economy in the disbursement and utilization of government funds. Be guided with COA Circular No. 85-55A on the prevention of the incurrence of irregular, unnecessary, excessive or extravagant expenditures and Administrative Order No. 103 on the continued adoption and implementation  of the austerity measures. b)Minimize  or   stop  the practice  of  serving  meals  and  snacks   during  regular monthly meetings or conferences except on seminars and trainings when catering services are part of the activity design and properly funded. c)Utilize the LGU’s limited  resources to projects, plans and programs that would benefit the majority of its constituents.

direct the concerned project engineers
and other concerned City officials
involved in the projects, to address and
resolve the issues for the suspension of
these projects and implement fully
projects and activities funded out of the
20% Development Fund to attain its
goals and objectives beneficial to the
constituents.

 Direct the budget committee to set up
realistic revenue targets to avoid
budgetary problems in the
implementation of programmed
projects and activities;
 Instruct all the department/offices
concerned to coordinate in the
identification of unpaid obligations and
unimplemented projects, programs and
activities and to reconcile unappropriated
balances with the City’s cash position
which is necessary in the preparation of
the succeeding year’s budget to establish
budget savings and avoid budget and
cash overdrafts in the future and
 Direct the Finance Committee and other
officials concerned to review the
Schedule of Market Values of the City
which was last updated for about 20
years ago.

Advise the PVB to secure the DOF
approval on maintaining deposits of
government offices pursuant to DOF
Department Circular Letter dated March
14, 2006. Otherwise, it was recommended
that the City Mayor direct the City
Treasurer to transfer said deposits to an
authorized government depository banks
as early as possible. Further, we recommend
that management observe prudence in
making deposits with authorized private
commercial banks to protect Mandaue City
Government funds.

We recommended that Management
direct the ICO-City Treasurer’s Office to
explain the changes he made in the ADA
which authorized PVB to deduct from the
City’s bank account P2,028,170.39 which
included surcharges and other BIR
charges amounting to P429,138.80
which could not be charged to the
agency, hence disallowable in audit.
We also recommended that penalties
imposed by private companies and
government institutions due to delays
in payments and remittances of
withholding taxes and GSIS loans and
premiums be paid by the personnel
responsible for the delays.

We recommended that Management
establish the necessity and reasonableness
of the bank LC opening charges for the
procurement of those heavy equipment and
coordinate with all offices concerned to
validate this disbursement transaction,
otherwise this amount shall be disallowed
in audit.
observations.managementAction: This is still a reconciling item in the bank statement.
observations.reason: No appropriation was provided in the Budget.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management
instruct the City Accountant to verify all
those reconciling items and prepare the
necessary adjusting/ correcting entries to
ensure correctness of the City’s cash in
bank accounts.
We recommended further that
Management require the Chief
Accountant to explain the delays in the
recording of those reconciling items
since 2010 to the present as it is not
proper for the Accounting Division not to
make adjusting/correcting entries which
are necessary to ensure that the correct
cash in bank balances are reflected in
the books.
observations.managementAction: 12 debit memos were already taken up in our books but checks were still unrecorded due to untransmitted check disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents. The City Treasurer submitted to COA copies of transmittal letters of some disbursement vouchers duly received by accounting.

observations.recommendation:

refund the unauthorized excess payment
of the incentives given to them and stop
the practice of granting the same in excess
of the allowed P10,000.00 per employee to
avoid disallowance in the future.
Further, strictly comply with DBM Budget
Circular 2010-3 and other issuances on
additional personnel benefits.
observations.managementAction: Management sent COA a letter in connection therewith. DBM RO7 opined that PEI maybe granted to LGU employees in excess of P10,000.00 provided it has sufficient funds. Thus JO personnel who were paid are required to refund which until now has not been made. Notices of Disallowance were already sent but management had formally sent an appeal to the COA Regional Office on this matter.

observations.recommendation:

required procurement process for consulting
services pursuant to the Revised Implementing
Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 or the
Government Procurement Reform Act. Submit
the basis on the payment of honoraria in
addition to the consultancy fee of the
consultants, otherwise the honoraria shall be
disallowed in audit.
Further, we recommended for the submission
of the appropriate DTRs and accomplishment
reports to support the claims.
observations.managementAction: Management explained that hiring of the consultants was made in good faith and anchored on the curriculum. The consultants committed to submit DTRs and accomplish reports.
observations.reason: Basis as to the grant of honoraria was not satisfactorily explained.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require
the immediate liquidation of all cash advances
on intelligence fund that were unliquidated as
of December 31, 2009. Further require all
accountable officers for intelligence fund to
liquidate within one (1) month from the date
the purpose of the cash advance was
accomplished, pursuant to COA Circular No.
2003-003 dated July 30, 2003. Further, grant
subsequent cash advances for intelligence
fund only after the issuance of credit advice
from the Chairman or the submission of
liquidation vouchers/ reports for the previous
cash advance to the COA Chairman.
observations.managementAction: Some liquidation documents for cash advances on intelligence funds were already submitted to COA Central Office. However, some liquidation reports totaling P3,950,000.00 were not submitted.
observations.reason: Reports due to the lack of supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management collect
from the beneficiaries of the program their
due accounts and strictly enforce its
collection so that other beneficiaries of the
program can avail of its benefits. Moreover,
strictly scrutinize the granting of loans to the
associations so that only qualified associations
can avail of the community mortgage program
of the city.
observations.managementAction: Exerted measures to collect from recipients the amount due.
observations.reason: Collections have yet to be realized. However, there were collections from some accounts.

observations.recommendation:

the accounting personnel to reconcile their
records with the Treasurer’s Office and trace
any discrepancy between the two records.
Correcting entries debiting the RPT/SET
Receivables and crediting the Deferred
RPT/SET Income accounts should be
prepared to present the correct account
balances. Moreover, trace the difference
between the RPT Receivable and Deferred
RPT Income accounts amounting to
P1,025,190.89.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of records was made to correct errors noted.
observations.reason: Full reconciliation has not been achieved.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor:
a) Direct the Accounting Department to continue updating its property listing to account for unidentified property items with a total cost of P1.2 billion.
b) Direct the DGS who is supervising the Inventory Committee-  to observe the same group classification of property items with that of the Accounting Department to facilitate reconciliation of the Accounting and DGS property records; and  to reconcile the unserviceable items per Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment and Inventory and Inspection Report so that these can already be dropped from the books
c) Instruct the Accounting Department and DGS to coordinate for the purpose of identifying items which were not found during the conduct of physical inventory amounting to P1.2 billion and make appropriate actions to minimize substantially if not eliminate the numerous unaccounted property items.
observations.managementAction: Initializing actions/approach with DGS by providing them schedule of reports (as of December 2015) and list of classification of accounts for their guidance in their preparation of physical inventory.
observations.reason: Still unreconciled ledger.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly comply with DOF Circular No. 001-2015, as amended by Circular No. 002-2015 by transferring all funds deposited at PVB to its regular depository, the Land Bank of the Philippines.

We further recommended that Management intensify its tax collection drive for an increased real property/special education tax collection and avail of all legal remedies either by administrative action through levy on real property or judicial actions as authorized under Sections 256, 257 and 258 of Republic Act 7160.
observations.managementAction: No management action

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the head of the HRMO closely monitor the implementation of the “rules and regulations in the use of bundy clock/biometrics, logbook, pass slips” as mandated under CSC Memorandum Circular No. 04, series of 1991. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the responsible officials/employees remit immediately the withheld funds to BIR, GSIS, Pag-ibig, and Philhealth funds; reconcile the accounts with abnormal balances; make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the account balance in the balance sheet and make a detailed explanation of the Due to GSIS account under the Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction: The accounting department is preparing monthly remittances before due date.

observations.recommendation:

We recommended further that Management henceforth avoid giving benefits or assistance which were not among those benefits authorized under the Standardization Law as prescribed under Section 12 of RA 6758, Section 2 of Administrative Order No. 37 dated November 21, 1998 so that City funds are spent in accordance with laws, rules and regulations and the implementation of the City’s priority projects, programs and activities can be carried out immediately for an efficient and effective delivery of government services.
observations.managementAction: On appeal with COA RO VII

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the city government strictly comply with the rules and regulations relative to the procurement of consulting services specifically on the necessity of hiring and setting a limit to the number of consultants to be hired to ensure that there is no duplication of duties and functions with the regular employees in order to avoid unnecessary expenditures of government funds.

Government (DILG) Joint Circular No. 01 on

unreconciled balances.

Direct City Treasurer to explain ADA changes authorizing PVB to deduct the amounts and that penalties imposed due to delays be charged to responsible personnel.
Establish necessity/reasonableness of bank LC opening charges and coordinate with offices to validate; otherwise disallow in audit.

observations.managementAction: Responses from Visual Arts & Rondalla consultants that discrepancies on DTRs were inadvertent and committed to be vigilant; required documents not yet submitted.
