observations.managementAction: Management explained that price at time of purchase was fair and reasonable; further evaluation pending before issuance of appropriate audit action.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that manage-ment: Direct the Municipal Treasurer to require or demand for the issuance of an official receipt of the government by the recipient Leagues concerned in order to support the release of financial aid to the latter, pursuant to Section 69 of the GAAM, Volume I. Require the Municipal Accountant to monitor the utilization of the funds released to these Leagues by requiring the recipient Leagues concerned to submit liquidation reports. In this manner, the propriety and validity of the expenditures could be properly evaluated.
observations.managementAction: No action taken
observations.reason: Still the different leagues issued their own ORs. No circularized memo-randum received requiring the use of official receipt Cannot compel the leagues to submit liquidation because funds released to leagues were treated as donation or financial aids

observations.recommendation: Require the Officer-in-Charge of the Waterworks Operation in coordination with the Municipal Accountant to identify and set up the receivables for the unpaid accounts. Likewise require the Waterworks Supervisor to regularly prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant’s Office summary reports on consumers’ meter readings, billings and payments in order that the corresponding income and receivable accounts will be correctly/ accurately established and taken up in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management had instructed the Waterworks Supervisor to prepare monthly reports on water billings and collectibles
observations.reason: The Assistant Mun. Treasurer being the new waterworks manager promised to implement the recommendation in the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation:
We have reiterated our recommendations that management:
Strictly comply with the required physical count of all properties at least once a year and to submit a copy of the Inventory Report to the Audit Team not later than January 31 and every year thereafter to ensure accuracy of the PPE accounts presented in the financial statements.
Require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to maintain Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, and shall henceforth conduct periodic reconciliation of the balances of both records.

We have recommended that the management:
Require the Inventory Committee to conduct an inventory of local roads under their jurisdiction and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of the Local Road Network at the end of every accounting period.
Pursue complete reconciliation of the RPCLRN and the updated Registry of Public Infrastructures in coordination with the General Services Officer/Local Treasurer and the Accountant through detailed review of prior year’s transactions in order to identify unrecorded road networks.
Direct the Treasurer and the Accountant to maintain Local Road Network Ledger Card, and complete Local Road Inventory records with Road Map and Local Road Network Property Card, respectively.
Maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for the road and its components namely a) road lot, h) road pavement, c) drainage and slope protection structures and d) other miscellaneous structures

We have recommended that management:
Require the Office of the Accountant to adhere with the prescribed accounting rules relative to the asset recognition under Property, Plant and Equipment, including maintenance of Construction in Progress account in the Trust Fund books, and the subsequent transfer of completed projects and equipment from Trust Fund to General Fund; and
Provide necessary adjusting entries to correct the noted errors in the recording of various PPE transactions in the books of accounts of the Municipality in order that the PPE account would be fairly presented in the financial statements.

We have recommended that Management require the Accounting Department to provide depreciation on all depreciable properties in compliance with Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standard (PPSAS) 17 and shall henceforth adopt the 5% residual value in the computation of annual depreciation. Also, the management should expedite the tracing or securing of records relative to the noted assets existing since the 1997 Financial Statements so as to provide the necessary depreciation and final disposition/treatment of the cited assets in the books of accounts, including the transfer of the building from trust fund to general fund.

We have recommended that both the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer regularly reconcile the general and subsidiary ledger balances of the Cash in Bank-LCCA accounts with the cashbook balances before closing of the book at month end pursuant to Section 10 of NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume II to ensure reliability and correctness of balances of said accounts.

We have recommended that management send a written notice to the payees of stale checks and require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to comply with the provisions laid out under Section 59 of NGAS Manual for LGUs for the disposition of the noted stale checks.

We have reiterated our previous recommendation that management require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Intra-agency accounts, Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds to promptly identify any discrepancy in the balances of the two accounts and to be able to effect the necessary adjustments to bring the affected accounts into unison.

We have reiterated that Management:
Require all Accountable Officers to liquidate their cash advances on time, otherwise, impose administrative sanctions against them for any unjustifiable reasons due to failure to liquidate cash advances within the prescribe period, in accordance with Section 89 of PD 1445, COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and Civil Service Commission No. 0676 dated June 17, 2004.
Strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
Cause or order the withholding of any money claims against the accountable officers concerned until they have fully settled their unliquidated cash advances.

We have recommended that the Accounting Office in cooperation with the Treasury Office ensure the complete submission of accounts within the prescribed period for timely examination and verification of accounts pursuant to Section 7.2.1 of the 2009 of the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts,   Section 39 (1) and (3) of P.D 1445, thus to avoid disciplinary actions and/or penalties  provided for under Section 23 (1) and (3) of The Administrative Code of 1987 and Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.

We have recommended that management expedite the transfer of Cash in Bank account balance maintained with Philippine National Bank to the new account of the Municipality with the Land Bank of the Philippines pursuant to the aforementioned provisions.

We have recommended that management direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to adhere with the prescribed accounting and monitoring policies or procedures over the supplies being used by the agency so as to present the correct inventory balance in the financial statements and to avoid the risk of possible misappropriation of these assets.

We have reiterated our recommendation that Management explain and justify the payments of guarantee fees and provide legal bases for the payments thereof. Refrain from paying a guarantee fee when availing a regular loan from any authorized government financial lending institution to avoid disallowance in audit.

We have recommended the Management the following courses of action:
Ensure that all of its insurable properties are adequately covered by the General Insurance Fund (GIF) administered by the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) in compliance with Republic Act No. 656 or the “Property Insurance Law”, as amended by Presidential Decree No. 245 dated July 13, 1973, in order to avoid the risk of non-indemnification in case of damage or loss due to fortuitous events.
Submit a complete inventory of insurable properties to GSIS for them to make appropriate evaluation whether the items included can be covered or not, annual report of which must also be submitted to the audit team; and
Provide explanation or basis on the insurance coverage applied with the PNB General Insurers Co., Inc; otherwise, do not cause the insurance renewal for Old Market and Municipal Building, and coordinate thereof to GSIS for possible coverage with the GIF.

We have reiterated our recommendation that the management:
Instruct the municipality’s Inventory or Property and Supply Unit/Team, as well as the Office of the Accountant, to conduct physical inventory of the supplies and equipment procured out of the LDRRMF and provide adjusting entries to set-up asset accounts thereof; and shall henceforth
Ensure observance of the rules set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-002 and COA Circular No. 2014-002 pertaining to the accounting, inventory and monitoring guidelines for procured resources out of the LDRRMF.

We have recommended that the management, in the conduct of ensuing public bidding(s) for the operation of fishery lots, must ensure that the set of rules and regulations is comprehensive to cover all agreed terms and that the implementers shall abide thereto to avoid misunderstanding and conflicts between the Committee on Auction and the participants. Also, since the Government of the Philippines is promoting/enhancing the type of participatory governance, it is recommended that some aspects of any activities the management may undertake of public concern be regulated with opinions from the main community or group of people who might be affected by the agency’s decisions.

Likewise, submit to the Office of the Auditor the following documents:
Documented copy of the ruling on the under-bid fishing lots for the validation of awarded bid prices.
List of participants with unsettled accounts from previous bidding. Also, require them to settle first their liabilities before awarding the new lots.

We have recommended the management the following courses of action:
Instruct all concerned employees and officials to strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001, COA Circular No. 2005-003, COA Circular No. 92-61A and COA Circular No. 96-004 relative to expenses on travel, seminars and conferences to correct the foregoing findings.
Oblige all signatories of the documents, particularly on Itinerary of Travel, Liquidation Reports and Disbursement Vouchers to certify only travel-related expenses which are observant of the said provisions.
Obtain plane tickets directly from airline companies to avoid other charges imposed by travel agencies; however booking with these agencies may still be allowed provided that their offers will be scrutinized through comparison of existing prices on airline websites, request itineraries with detailed breakdown of cost and/or have their quotations submitted in order for the management to choose the most advantageous price.

We have recommended that the management provide justification on the necessity of conducting the ELA Formulation and Budget Call in Ilocos Norte and shall henceforth observe the proper procurement procedure of training/conference/seminar venues with consideration to prudence, economy, suitability, accessibility and proper procurement procedures.

We have recommended that management ensure the submission of all the perfected contracts together with the complete supporting documents within the period prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 to enable the auditor to conduct timely review and evaluation of the transactions.

We have recommended that management submit the documentary requirements for the conduct of auditorial review of the contracts as mentioned in COA MEMO Circular No. 2005-027 and for payment of claims under COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We have recommended that the management require the person responsible to properly fill up all information in the Purchase Requests (PR), Purchase Orders (PO) and Inspection Reports for proper referencing and easier determination of the reasonableness of the transaction and compliance with the requirements of R.A. 9184.

We have recommended that the management refrain from using brand names in conducting agency’s procurements and that all transfers of accountability for particular asset shall be documented properly.

Likewise, please submit the following documents to enable us to further verify the transactions incurred relative to subject matter herein, to wit:
Copy of DOH-RO I and HLGP guidelines and specifications
Sangguniang Bayan Resolution authorizing Hon. Mayor Aldrin C. Cerdan to enter and sign for and in behalf of the Municipality a Memorandum of Agreement with the DOH-RO I
Approved Work and Financial Plan incorporated in the Municipal Health Action Plan of the LGU
Approved Project Expenditures or Estimated Expenses indicating the project objective and expected output
Copy of the OR upon receipt of fund transferred
Copy of OR issued by the Source Agency evidencing refund of unexpended balance of fund transfer
Proof of Donation
Proof of Acceptance of Donation

We have recommended the management the following courses of action:
Submit the complete documentary requirements for the conduct of auditorial review of contract and the payment of claims in compliance with COA Memo Circular No. 2005-027, COA Circular No. 2012-001 and other pertinent provisions of the revised IRR of RA 9184.
Ensure that 10% retention money is being deducted from the progress payment to the contractor to serve as warranty for hidden defects during the construction period and shall only be released upon final acceptance of the project.
Strictly observe the requirement of R.A. 9184 on the payment of mobilization fees which should be based solely on the 15% of the total contract price regardless whether the contract includes detailed engineering design fee.

We have recommended that management explain the use of Repeat Order mode of procurement for the second batch of beneficiaries from the same supplier with original contract awarded through Negotiated Procurement contrary to Section 51 of the revised IRR of RA 9184.
We have recommended that the management explain the deviations of actual project implementation against the project proposal agreed upon by both parties.

We have recommended that the management submit appropriate documentary evidences to support the appropriateness of the selection of Makabata Para sa Bayan, Inc. as the provider of the aforementioned services.

We have recommended that management explain the initial payment made to the contractor for the materials required in the repair works despite having awarded the contract as an infrastructure project which only allows 15% of Advance Payment or Mobilization Fee based on the above-cited provision, and that payments for the contract should be in accordance with submitted and validated percentage of work accomplished.

We have recommended that the management maintain registry of eligible suppliers, contractors and consultants in compliance with Section 53.1.2.2 of the revised IRR of RA 9184. Furthermore, submit the foregoing eligibility requirements of Traction Four Wheel Drive Performance Shop to ascertain the validity of the transaction.

We have recommended that management strictly observe the compliance of all the provisions of the revised IRR of RA 9184 during Negotiated Procurement particularly with the posting of invitation or request for submission of price quotation in PhilGEPS (Section 54.2) and the invitation of observers in all stages of negotiation (53.1.6).

We have recommended that Management prepare a Statement of Itemized Peace and Order Program presenting the specific activities to ensure effective and efficient control of the Confidential Fund, and to further corroborate the propriety of the allocated amount for the said fund.

We have reiterated our recommendation that management:
Require the GAD Focal Person to elaborate/explain the gender issues being addressed by the particular program/project/activity as reflected in the GAD Plan and Budget with consideration to the foregoing rules and the official Gender and Development Code of the Municipality.
Prepare the Annual GAD Accomplishment Report as prescribed under DILG-NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-01 which is also a requirement for the conduct of GAD Audit.

We have recommended the management the following course of actions:
Provide necessary adjusting entries for the noted erroneous recording relative to the Due to GSIS account.
Explain the differences noted between the remitted employee payments and agency contributions, and the withheld amounts from salary releases to employees. If the remittances made are valid, require the concerned employees to refund the relative amount; otherwise, coordinate with GSIS on the erroneous remittance.
Exert more effort in identifying the noted unremitted balance already due for remittance to GSIS, and cause the review thereof as to its validity, otherwise effect necessary adjustment if error is determined for such transactions.

We have recommended that the management ensure that the disbursements charged to SEF are supported with complete documentation with proper receipting of transferred funds if the LGU will not directly implement the PPAs. Likewise, require the recipient of funds to submit proof of expenditures to substantiate the implementation of local school board activities for the year 2016.
Further, we reiterated our previous recommendation that the officials concerned refrain from incurring expenditures not chargeable to Special Education Fund or which are not the priority programs under pertinent rules; and make further assessment on more vital issues concerning the quality of education and not merely focusing on athletic and other DepEd-related competitions.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Assessor to make a detailed review of the RPT and SET Receivables accounts vis-à-vis the List of Delinquent Taxpayers; and shall henceforth observe the proper rules on setting up the accounts at the beginning of the year.

We have reiterated our recommendation contained in the previous Annual Audit Reports that management adopt or design a better policy in granting loans to ensure its collection. The loan agreement must be revised, wherein the date of maturity must be indicated, payment must not be on a voluntary basis and imposition of interest and penalty must be strict. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should make a follow up on the demand letters sent to debtors. In case where the debtors fail to settle their accounts after due notices, apply legal sanctions. This may help to improve the collection efficiency of the Municipality wherein the funds collected may be utilized for re-loaning to other beneficiaries.

Likewise, Management was requested to submit the following documents to enable the Audit Team to make a decision in audit:
Copy of Memorandum of Agreement between the Municipality and the source agency and other supporting documents;
Copy of Loan Agreement; and
Copy of Demand Letters sent to the borrowers.

We have recommended that management take the following courses of action:
Ensure that the responsible officials and employees, as withholding agents, should see to it that taxes withheld are accordingly remitted on or before their due dates to avoid the possible sanctions imposed by BIR Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2012 and the National Internal Revenue Code for the non-remittance of taxes withheld within the due dates.
Effect adjustments in the affected subsidiary ledgers noted with errors in the recording of withholding tax on compensation.
Provide breakdown on the amounts remitted to BIR on the months cited with inconsistencies in the withheld and remitted 3% withholding taxes to facilitate further validation.

We have recommended that management require the suppliers/contractors on the issuance of Sales Invoice and/or Official Receipts on various procurement of goods made in compliance with Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 2-2014 dated January 13, 2014 and likewise, strictly observed said regulations on future disbursements made.

We have recommended that the management require the responsible officials to strictly observe BIR Revenue Memorandum Order No. 23-2014 and other rules and regulations relative to the collection of taxes to avoid penalties arising from non-compliance thereto.

1.6 We reiterated our previous recommendation that management (a) create an inventory committee and conduct complete physical inventory of all properties of the municipality to determine their existence and physical conditions in accordance with above mentioned rules and regulations; and (b) exert extra efforts to locate old records of acquisition to provide basis in the preparation of subsidiary records, computation for depreciation or request for the dropping from records all obsolete, destroyed and unserviceable properties.

2.6  We recommended  the following courses of action:
Enforce strict compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002 in the granting of cash advances and the subsequent liquidations thereof;
Monitor liquidation of outstanding cash advances, refrain from  granting new/additional cash advances unless the previous cash advance has been liquidated or a proper accounting thereof has been made; and
Send demand letters to those officers and employees and/or if warranted effect the withholding of salary for those who failed to settle immediately their accounts as required by the regulations and initiate appropriate legal action against the former Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that the (i) Municipal Accountant (a) ensure that all payments for AICS be adequately and properly supported by the required documents enumerated under Section 4.2 of DSWD MC No. 02 s. 2014 to establish the validity and propriety of claims; and (b) ensure that the proper account is used in recording the payment of AICS; and  (ii) MSWDO is advised to prepare and maintain a file of each client/beneficiary’s case evaluation to facilitate validation of the propriety of said transaction.

4.7  We recommended that management (a) procure all common-use supplies directly from the PS-DBM;  and (b) cause the yearly submission of its Annual Procurement Plan for Common-Use Supplies and Equipment to assist the Procurement Service in strengthening its operations and improve its delivery of service.

5.5  We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of charging payment of membership dues of appointed officials in their respective association/league against the funds of the Municipality in compliance with the Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) Opinion No. 99 dated July 3, 2002.

6.5 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to  instruct the Municipal Accountant to comply with the provision on the minimum monthly net take home pay of Three Thousand Five Hundred  Pesos (₱3,500.00) for all the regular employees of the municipality.  The Municipal Accountant is likewise advised to observe the order of preference of the authorized deductions of loans and contributions from salaries and other benefits of employees concerned as mandated by Section 52 of Republic Act No. 10717, otherwise known as the General Appropriations Act for FY 2016.

We recommended that the activities or duties performed by the officials of NEUST in addition to, or over and above their regular functions be enumerated to determine the basis of the grant of the honoraria.
We also recommended that the said honoraria be remitted to NEUST who shall cause the payment of the same to the concerned officials.

8.7  We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to prepare and submit to the GPPB the Procurement Monitoring Report in printed and electronic format within fourteen (14) calendar days after the end of each semester in compliance with Section 12.2 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.

9.5 We recommended and management agreed to stop its dealings with business establishments wherein the officials and or employees of the municipality have directly or indirectly have financial or material  interest.
9.6 We also advised management to ensure that compliance with  Sections 3 (1) and 7 (a) of the Code of Conduct and Ethical Standards for Public Officials and Employees (Republic Act No. 6713).

10.5 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adjust the affected accounts involved by debiting the Cash in Bank to reflect its actual balances and crediting the Accounts Payable to restore the liability to concerned creditors in the amount of ₱10,480.00.  Likewise, the Municipal Treasurer is advised to send written notices to payees of checks which has long been outstanding a month before said checks become stale.  Strict adherence to existing rules and regulations regarding the disposition of stale checks should be observed by concerned officials of the municipality.

11.6 We recommended that management furnish the Audit Team with the copies of Memorandum of Agreement (MOA)/Trust Agreement on all funds transferred by National Government Agencies (NGAs) as provided for in Item 3.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to facilitate evaluation and audit of disbursements made out of the said fund transfers.
11.7 We also advised that management explain the change in the project  undertaken by the municipality out of the funds received from DTI amounting to ₱1,000,000.00.  Likewise, we requested that management furnish the audit team with the approval of the source agency (DTI) on the change of project to be implemented.

12.4 We recommended that management observe the  PS limitation  and comply with Section 325 of RA No. 7160. Likewise, the Local Chief Executive and Municipal Budget Officer were advised to ensure that there will be no filling – up of vacant position and no hiring/promotion until the required 45/55 PS limitation is achieved.
14.5  We recommended and management agreed to remit all contributions deducted from the employees promptly to the GSIS.

15.4  We recommended and management agreed to continue performing the function of a withholding agent of the National Government and adhere with the timely and intact remittance of taxes withheld as required under Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 23-2007 dated March 20, 2007.
15.5  We also advised the management to exert efforts to locate pertinent documents pertaining to the previous years’ withheld taxes from suppliers and dealers in order to effect remittance of their respective taxes.  Likewise, identify the employees with over withheld taxes so that refund could be made.

We recommend that Management:
a) Adhere to existing policies on property management in order to determine
proper accountability and conduct of physical inventory taking on all properties
of the City pursuant to COA Circular No. 80-124 dated January 18, 1980;
b) Take concrete steps in correcting deficiencies noted by maintaining the required
property records, submitting an accurate, complete and reliable inventory
report on time and reconciling the results of the physical inventory and property
records, and continuous coordination between property and finance
departments on PPE transactions to ensure immediate reconciliation of records.

We recommend that Management direct the City Accountant to hasten the
preparation of the bank reconciliation statements in order to ascertain the
correctness of the bank balances appearing in the financial statements.
We further recommend that bank reconciliation statements submitted to COA be
properly supported by bank statements and paid and cancelled checks to facilitate
the verification of the accuracy of the BRS submitted.

We recommend that Management make the necessary adjustments regarding the
checks which remain outstanding after two (2) years from date of its issue as
required under Section 97 of PD 1445.

We recommend that Management direct the City Treasurer to furnish the Chief
Accountant with the duly certified list of all taxpayers at the beginning of the year
showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year as
basis in setting-up the RPT/SET Receivables in the books in order to present
reliable and accurate balances thereof in the financial statements.

We recommend that Management direct officials concerned to submit the required
documents.
Likewise, we further recommend that Management enforce strict compliance with
Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445, COA Circular No. 2012-002 and the
Revised IRR of R.A. 9184 which require complete documentation of the
transactions.

We recommend that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to conduct a
thorough verification of the dormant items and request for write-off of the accounts
with the Commission on Audit pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated
December 19,2016 . We further recommend that the City Accounting Office
conduct a regular review of all account balances so that dormant accounts can be
immediately identified and acted upon.

We recommend that the City Mayor direct all accountable officers to fully liquidate
their cash advances immediately for the long-outstanding accounts and henceforth,
enforce strict liquidation at year-end pursuant to COA Circular 97-002 dated
February 10, 1997. Otherwise, we recommend that the Accountant cause the
withholding of the AO's salary” in accordance with paragraph 5.9 of the mentioned
circular.

We recommend that Management:
a. discontinue the practice of paying individual claims through a cash advance by
the Accountable Officer and pay expenses through check only. A petty cash fund
shall be maintained for transactions which can be paid practically in cash.
b. direct the accounting section to strictly observe accounting rules and regulations
in the granting and liquidation of cash advances to ensure that these are valid
expenses.

We recommend that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to conduct
reconciliation of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts to
identify the cause/s of the discrepancy in the balances of the two accounts and make
the necessary adjustments, if warranted.

We recommend that the BAC explain the basis on the awarding of the contracts to
various consultants when it did not fully comply with the procurement procedures
required under RA 9184 and submit the required documents.
Henceforth, we recommend that the BAC members comply strictly with the proper
procurement procedures pursuant to the provisions of R.A. 9184 and it’s IRR and
to perform their tasks as required for an efficient and effective procurement process
and remind them of their accountabilities.

We recommend that the City Mayor direct the Head of the Department of General
Services and/or designated Supply/Property Officer to submit copies of purchase
orders and contracts within five days from the issuance thereof pursuant to the
above-cited circular for the timely review by the Auditor and implementation of
appropriate corrective action, if necessary.

We recommend that Management direct the officials concerned to use the perpetual
inventory method in recording drugs and medicines and medical, dental and
laboratory supplies as provided in the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I for LGUs,
and make the adjustments in the books, if necessary.

We recommend that Management submit a written explanation on the foregoing
deficiencies and submit the required disbursement vouchers and supporting
documents.

We recommend that Management submit the authority to claim and certification
that the use was for official purpose.

We recommend that Management submit a written explanation on the foregoing
deficiencies and submit the required supporting documents to facilitate
determination of the propriety and the cost reasonableness of the cleaning services
of portalets. Henceforth, we recommend that Management direct the accounting
section to stop the practice of processing claims unless supported with complete
documentation.

We recommend that Management submit an explanation why the subject
procurement appeared to have been split and (the required documents.
We recommend that Management direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to
comply strictly with RA 9184 on their procurement process. If the conditions
warrant, then the BAC shall determine and recommend the appropriate alternative
method of procurement based on the prevailing conditions to the head of procuring
committee, as cited under the IRR of RA 9184.

We recommend that Management refund the disallowed amount or appeal this with
the COA Regional Office No. VII.

We recommend that Management comply with the requirements indicated in the
Notice of Suspension and henceforth ensure that all payments are supported with
complete documentary requirements.

We recommend that the BAC submit the required documents and explanation on
the basis of the award of the contract to Boston Home, Inc. even when it did not
fully comply with the procurement procedures required under RA 9184.
Henceforth, we recommend that the BAC members comply strictly with the proper
procurement procedures pursuant to the provisions of R.A. 9184 and it’s IRR and
to perform their tasks as required for an efficient and effective procurement process
and remind them of their accountabilities.

We recommend that the City Mayor direct the officials concerned to prepare and
submit the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment
(LDRRMFIP) as required under COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12,
2012 indicating the 30% allocation of QRF and the allocation for disaster
mitigation, prevention and preparedness showing the projects and activities to be
funded, for evaluation and monitoring purposes.
Likewise, we recommend that Management require the City Accountant to prepare
the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the prescribed format in
COA Circular No. 2012-002 and require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management Officer (LDRRMO) to submit such report on or before the 15th day
after the end of each month to the COA Auditor through the LDRRMC and the
Local Development Council.

We recommend that the City’s officials look into the contractor’s failure to complete
the project within the contract period and re-compute the actual work-days, with
considerations of suspensions and extensions, in order to determine whether there
was breach of contract committed by the contractor and to demand for possible
liquidated damages.

We recommend that management send demand  letters  for  the  immediate
liquidation of the cash advances.  In  case  of  failure  by  the  concerned
officials and employees to liquidate, withhold their salaries in  accordance
with  Section  9.3.2  of  COA  Circular  97-002.  Henceforth,  require   the
liquidation of cash advances promptly as  soon  as  the  purpose  for  which
these were granted had been served or within  the  reglementary  period  and
the accountant to abide by COA Circular No.  97-002  as  to  the  manner  of
granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer  to  send
out notices to the  various  payees  whose  checks  have  become  stale  and
facilitate the cancellation or replacement thereof.   We  further  recommend
that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary JEV for the  restoration
of the amount in the cash account.

We recommend that Management require  complete  documents  to  support
the grant of  financial  assistance  to  the  barangays  as  required  under
Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.  We  further
recommend that management set-up  internal  control  measures  in  order  to
monitor the releases of financial  assistance  to  the  different  barangays
like recording the same as Due from LGUs first and recording  under  Subsidy
to LGUs only upon submission of liquidation report.

We recommend that Management follow-up and monitor the  reconciliation
of the account balances  by  the  Municipal  Accountant  and  the  Municipal
Treasurer in order to reflect reliable data  in  the  reports.   We  further
recommend that  the  personnel  in-charge  of  the  inventory-taking  should
review the entries  presented  in  the  Report  of  the  Physical  Count  of
Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and make  the  necessary  corrections
and additional entries like the property number, status  of  the  items  and
its whereabouts.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer issue  an  official  receipt
for all funds received by the LGU as required in Section 68 of PD 1445.

RECOMMENDATION

    We recommend that management appropriate at least  5%  from  the  total
estimated income from regular sources for LDRRMF.  Give due  regard  on  the
maximum utilization  of  the  70%  thereof  for  pre  disaster  preparedness
programs/activities in accordance with  the  budget  so  that  the  intended
purpose is attained/achieved.

We recommend that management revisit its procurement process with  the
end view of ensuring transparency and competitiveness in  every  procurement
activity as provided in Section 3 of RA 9184.

      We further recommend that the Bids and Awards Committee practice  wide
dissemination  of  bid  opportunities,  send   invitation   to   independent
observers,  extend  equal  opportunity  and  issue  bid  documents  to   all
qualified parties to dispel presumptions  of  rigged  biddings  and  promote
transparency and competitiveness in the procurement process.

We recommend that check payments be made strictly payable  to  Quinale
Beach Bar or to the official title or designation of the official  concerned
and not to any personal names to  prevent  unauthorized  encashment  of  the
checks.

We have recommended the following:

Strictly comply with the required physical count of all properties at least once a year and to submit a copy of the Inventory Report to the Audit Team not later than January 31 and every year thereafter to ensure accuracy of the PPE accounts presented in the financial statements.

Require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to maintain Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, and shall henceforth conduct periodic reconciliation of the balances of both records.

Require the Office of the Accountant to adhere to the prescribed accounting rules relative to the asset recognition under Property, Plant and Equipment, including maintenance of Construction in Progress account, and the transfer of completed projects and equipment under Trust Fund to General Fund; 

Require the Municipal Accountant to provide necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the misclassified PPE accounts and those completed infrastructure projects.

Instruct the Accounting, Treasury and Engineering departments to identify and complete the records in the Registry of Public Infrastructures to aid in the full compliance with PPSAS.

Require the Accounting Department to provide depreciation on all depreciable properties in compliance with Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standard (PPSAS) 17. Also, the management should expedite the tracing or securing of records relative to the noted assets existing since the 1997 Financial Statements so as to provide the necessary depreciation and final disposition/treatment of the cited assets in the books of accounts, including the transfer of the building from trust fund to general fund.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2015 | Management already created Inventory and Disposal Teams to commence the inventory process.

Adjustments were already provided for most of the errors noted in the audit.
observations.reason:
The Inventory Team instituted by the LGU still haven’t completed the required physical count of all properties of the municipality, hence the non-submission of the report on physical count.

There were still unadjusted transactions of which the same natures of errors were again noted in 2016 financial records.

(Reiterated under finding no. 1)

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that both the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer regularly reconcile the general and subsidiary ledger balances of the Cash in Bank-LCCA accounts with the cashbook balances before closing of the book at month end pursuant to Section 10 of NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume II to ensure reliability and correctness of balances of said accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | The management had already provided adjustments for some items which caused the unreconciled balance and is on the process of further review of previous years’ transactions to make both records the same as to the cash in bank balances.
observations.reason: There were still unreconciled balances of cash in bank, hence reiterated under finding no. 2

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our previous recommendation that management require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the Intra-agency accounts, Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds to promptly identify any discrepancy in the balances of the two accounts and to be able to effect the necessary adjustments if appropriate to bring the affected accounts into agreement.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | Tracing of erroneous recording has been done by the Accounting Department to some extent which resulted to some adjustments.
observations.reason:
Some discrepancies had already been adjusted however, errors relative to these two accounts already existed since time immemorial; hence further tracing shall still be conducted to fully reconcile the accounts.

 (Reiterated under finding no. 4)

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our previous recommendation that management require the Accountable Officer to deposit her collections promptly and intact as prescribed under COA Memorandum No. 2013-004 for the efficient discharge of her duties and avoid the risk of possible loss or misuse of funds.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | The Treasurer made sure that the regularity of cash deposits was sufficient enough to avoid huge amount of cash on hand.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management:

Require the Office of the Accountant to provide necessary adjusting entries for the findings cited; and

Mandate periodic reconciliation of Cash Local Treasury account between the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to identify necessary adjustments prior to the preparation of financial statements.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | Reconciliation was done between the Accounting and Treasury to bring their cash balances per respective record books into agreement.
observations.reason: Discrepancies noted were already adjusted and for CY 2016, the records are reconciled.

observations.recommendation:
We have reiterated that Management:

Require all Accountable Officers to liquidate their cash advances on time, otherwise, impose administrative sanctions against them for any unjustifiable reasons due to failure to liquidate cash advances within the prescribe period, in accordance with Section 89 of PD 1445, COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and Civil Service Commission No. 0676 dated June 17, 2004.

Strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

Cause or order the withholding of any money claims against the accountable officers concerned until they have fully settled their unliquidated cash advances.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | Some of the outstanding cash advances from prior years were liquidated and/or deducted from salary or benefit claims, hence the decrease in the 2016 balance by ₱272,120.00 or 45%.
observations.reason:
Partially Implemented.

There’s still remaining balance of unliquidated cash advances pertaining to prior years; and some of these accountable officers were still allowed to draw cash advances in the current year.

(Reiterated under finding no. 5)

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Accounting Office in cooperation with the Treasury Office ensure the complete submission of accounts within the prescribed period for timely examination and verification of accounts pursuant to Section 7.2.1 of the 2009 of the Rules and Regulations on the Settlement of Accounts,   Section 39 (1) and (3) of P.D 1445, thus to avoid disciplinary actions and/or penalties  provided for under Section 23 (1) and (3) of The Administrative Code of 1987 and Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 | Regular monitoring of the submission of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management expedite the transfer of Cash in Bank account balance maintained with Philippine National Bank to the new account of the Municipality with the Land Bank of the Philippines pursuant to the aforementioned provisions.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | The management had been continuously transferring cash balances from PNB to the new LBP account.
observations.reason: The account is still existing although the management, as necessary, has been transferring cash balances from the said PNB account to the new LBP account.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management collect from the contractor the corresponding liquidated damages pursuant to the provisions of Section 68 of RA 9184 and Annex “E”(8) of its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | The full amount was subjected to disallowance which was already paid by the liable persons.
observations.reason: The amount of liquidated damages has already been fully refunded.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management stop granting the maximum allowable amount of Subsistence and Laundry Allowances to Municipal Health Workers without considering the provisions set forth under DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2012 and require the officers to refund the unauthorized amounts paid to them.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 | Management discontinued the granting of the full amount of SALA to RHU personnel and that the basis of the amount being given to them were already in line with the rules given in the audit observation and recommendation,
observations.reason: The amount provided in the report was still not refunded by the liable persons, thus Notice of Disallowance was issued.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instill to all LGU officials and employees the necessity of submitting complete and proper documentation for all claims pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and require the Office of the Accountant to strictly check details of the vouchers and other pertinent documents, and to submit the same within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | Management informed and required all employees and officials to observe the documentary requirements specific to the observations noted.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management:

Instruct all concerned employees and officials to strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-004, Executive Order No. 298 and COA Circular No. 2012-003 relative to expenses on travel, seminars and conferences.

Oblige all signatories of the documents, particularly on Itinerary of Travel, Liquidation Reports and Disbursement Vouchers to certify only travel-related expenses which are observant of the said provisions.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | The management ensured that the rates allowed for the travels and seminars of the employees and officials of the LGU are within the limit set forth under the said regulations.
observations.reason: The specific deficiencies noted last year were already corrected.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure compliance with DBM Compensation Policy Guidelines No. 98-1, limiting the grant of additional allowances to those specifically enumerated therein. Likewise, require the COMELEC Election Officer and BIR Collection Officers to refund the total allowances received for the period January 2014 to May 2015, otherwise, such payments shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | Management stopped the granting of allowances to BIR and Election Officers pursuant to DBM Compensation Policy Guidelines No. 98-1.
observations.reason: The Election Officer is yet to refund his liability for receiving the unauthorized allowances.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management submit concrete legal basis in the granting of honoraria to the resource speakers beyond what was allowed under DMB Budget Circular No. 2007-1 dated April 23, 2007. Otherwise, the excess honoraria paid will be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | Management provided further analysis on the computation of maximum honoraria for a resource speaker based on DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-1 dated April 23, 2007.
observations.reason: Further review and analysis of the cited circular revealed that the management had actually met the threshold or maximum honorarium rate.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management explain the non-compliance with the provisions of RA 9184 in the procurement of goods and services otherwise the transactions shall be suspended in audit.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | Management informed the audit team that negotiated procurement was employed by the LGU since the list of expenses provided were in different nature thus, individually, will fall under the allowed threshold for negotiated procurement.
observations.reason: Current year transactions/procurement were executed based on the procurement law.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management submit the documentary requirements for the conduct of auditorial review of the contracts as mentioned in COA MEMO Circular No. 2005-027 and for payment of claims under COA Circular No. 2012-001, and shall henceforth observe the prescribed period for the submission of contract documents.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | Management had already instructed the BAC, Accounting and Engineering Departments to furnish the audit team the missing documents as listed in the issued AOM. Most of the documentary requirements were already submitted.
observations.reason:
Some of the documents noted to be missing were still not submitted to the audit team. Moreover, other documentary requirements for different projects were noted in the current year audit were likewise not included in the submitted bid documents and disbursement vouchers.

(Reiterated in finding no. 16)

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Bids and Awards Committee to stringently observe the decreed process in the conduct of procurement stated under the revised IRR of R.A. 9184 to establish higher degree of reliability on every project of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | For the year 2016, Pre-bid conferences were conducted on procurements of various projects with Approved Budget for the Contract of ₱1,000,000.
observations.reason: Review of current year procurements with ABC of ₱1,000,000.00 revealed that the LGU is already compliant with the cited provision.

observations.recommendation: We have reiterated our previous recommendation that management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly reports within the prescribed period to the COA Local Auditor thru the LDRRMO for timely verification of the receipts and charges of the LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | The Local Chief Executive had instructed the LDRRMO and Accounting Office to furnish the audit team Report on the Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: Monthly Reports on the Sources and Utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) for CY 2016 were submitted regularly.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the management exert effort in identifying the noted unremitted balance already due for remittance to GSIS, and review thereof as to its validity, otherwise effect necessary adjustment if error is determined for such transactions.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | The management instructed the Accounting Office to trace the transactions which had caused the balance in the Due to GSIS Account.
observations.reason: The balance identified in the audit recommendations are deemed to have originated from prior years’ transactions which will necessitate further review.

observations.recommendation:
We have reiterated our recommendation contained in the previous Annual Audit Reports that the management adopt or design a better policy in granting loans to ensure its collection. The loan agreement must be revised, wherein the date of maturity must be indicated, payment must not be on a voluntary basis and imposition of interest and penalty must be strict. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant should make a follow up on the demand letters sent to debtors. In case where the debtors fail to settle their accounts after due notices, apply legal sanctions. This may help to improve the collection efficiency of the municipality wherein the funds collected may be utilized for re-loaning to other beneficiaries.

Likewise, Management was requested to submit the following documents to enable the Audit Team to make a decision in audit:

Copy of Memorandum of Agreement between the Municipality and the source agency and other supporting documents;
Copy of Loan Agreement; and
Copy of Demand Letters sent to the borrowers.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 | Demand Letters have already been issued to the recipients of the said loan.
observations.reason:
Despite the issuance of demand letters, as of December 31, 2016, the Treasurer’s Office hasn’t received any payment yet for the loan referred herein. 

(Reiterated under finding no. 26)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the former Liquidating Officer to immediately depository account of the Municipality his undeposited collections for long period of time; and (b) initiate action to enable compliance on the settlement of obligation by the mentioned accountable officer.
observations.managementAction: The concerned accountable officer made a partial payment of ₱150,000.00.  The balance of P195,993.36 was recognized and recorded in the books as Due from Officers and Employees on December 31, 2016.
observations.reason: Management failed to follow up the compliance of the concerned personnel regarding full settlement of his accountability.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendations that the Local Chief Executive: (a) require on the immediate liquidation of the cash advances amounting to ₱989,700.38.  See to it that the liquidation reports are submitted to the Office of the COA Auditor for review and supported by complete documents such as the official receipts, certificate of seminar/trainings completed, signed payrolls, daily time records/accomplishment reports, etc.  Henceforth, introduce controls that would assure that cash advances are liquidated or reported as soon as the purpose of the cash advance is served; (b) endeavour to strengthen internal control practices thru assignment of competent or trusted personnel who will review transactions on an on-going basis for proper monitoring of adherence to prescribed policies and procedures, laws, rules and regulations; (c) direct the Municipal Accountant to monitor timely submission of liquidation reports and completeness of supporting documents in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Some unliquidated accounts were already settled and liquidation reports submitted.
observations.reason:
Reiterated In Finding No. 2.

Some of the persons liable are no longer connected with the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters and/or notifications to officials/employees/and to those who are no longer in the active service, for the settlement of their respective obligations with the agency.  After observing the prescribed due process, if warranted, initiate filing of administrative cases and measures to compel the persons responsible/liable to settle their long overdue accounts.  Strengthen the LGU’s internal control on cash to avoid similar problems that could expose government resources to loss or misuse.
observations.managementAction: Management has partially effected settlement through payroll deductions and adjustment of accounts.
observations.reason: Management was not able to locate the whereabout of the former Municipal Treasurer who is on Absent Without Official Leave (AWOL) since calendar year 2013. Her accountability comprise 90% of the balance of the said account.

observations.recommendation: We therefore reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief Executive: (a) instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a complete Certified List of Taxpayers, bearing the names, amount due and collectible during the year, and submit the same to the accounting unit as the basis in setting up of RPT/SEF tax receivables in the succeeding year; (b) ensure that the list of taxpayers bear vital information, such as the addresses, to facilitate, if necessary, the sending of notices and other follow up communications relative to tax campaign activities and other collection strategy that may be implemented by Management;     (c)  introduce collection strategy that would significantly help increase the tax collection of the municipality; and (d) require the Municipal Accountant to (i) observe the provision under Section 20 of the New Government Accounting (NGAS) Manual for LGUs; and (ii) use the Certified List of Taxpayers prepared by the Office of the Municipal Treasurer as the basis in recording the RPT/SEF tax receivables at the beginning of the year.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Unit has started reviewing the balance of the subject accounts.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower complement in the Municipal Treasurer Office.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our earlier recommendation that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer, Accountant and the Budget Officer to observe and be guided by the provisions of Sections 271 and 272 of the RA No. 7160 of the Local Government Code of the Philippines and Section 15 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.  Cause the timely remittance of trust liabilities due to concerned government agency.
observations.managementAction: Management remitted a total of P1,726,135.51 of the 1015 unremitted RPT/SET in the 1st Quarter of 2016.  SET shares  up to November, 2016 were already remitted.  However, remittance for RPT shares for the period  July to December, 2016 were not yet remitted.
observations.reason: The volume of work in the Accounting Unit hindered the preparation of the schedule of remittances of RPT and SET  shares of the Provincial Government.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal School Board agreed to limit the utilization of the SEF to the programs and projects defined under RA No. 7160 and DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 dated April 14, 1998 in order to maximize the benefits that the students and teachers could avail under this fund.
observations.reason: Management gave in to some of the requests of the school officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the NEUST Peňaranda Academic Extension Campus Administrator to enforce collection of the uncollected fees from students and formulate collection policies to prevent its accumulation.
observations.managementAction: Management had sent a letter and schedule of uncollected fees to the NEUST PAEC School Board.  Likewise, demand letters were sent to students with outstanding obligations.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation and the management agreed to strictly observe the procurement procedures prescribed under RA No. 9184.  Use the facilities of the PhilGEPS to post all procurement opportunities as this will provide advantage in term of wider dissemination of information in favour of the municipality.  Accordingly, the required posting of the same in the bulletin board and other conspicuous places of the municipality should be observed.  This will facilitate  more competitive bidding and transparency in the procurement activities in favour of the government.
observations.managementAction: Partially implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the MSWMB to prioritize the finalization  of the ten-year Solid Waste Management Plan and submit the same to the National Solid Waste Management Commission for approval; (b) exert effort to expedite the establishment and completion of the Sanitary Landfill; (c) strictly enforce implementation of the ordinances pertaining to solid waste management other relevant policies and intensify information campaign on the “no segregation, no collection” policy and “no-littering” policy; and (d) allocate sufficient funds to provide the necessary equipment/facilities and manpower for sound waste management.
observations.managementAction: Fully implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct all concerned officials to (a) formulate and prepare the GAD Plan to determine gender issues and submit to the Budget Officer for budgetary allocation; and (b) prepare and submit the annual GAD Report of Accomplishments of the targeted GAD activities with corresponding amount spent thereon.
observations.managementAction: Fully implemented.

balance of PDAF funds to the Bureau

Court Decision which has become

2013.

deposit intact all collections to the

daily or not later than the next banking

NGAS Manual Volume 1 and avoid
Section 69 (1), PD 1445 to avoid

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned Accountable Officer liquidate cash advance as soon as its purpose has been served pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-001, PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002. Henceforth, we enjoined the City Accountant to closely monitor the liquidations of cash advances as soon as settlement thereof falls due and/or withhold payment of any money due him in case of failure to settle the cash advance despite demand, as provided by law.

We recommended that Management

to continue updating its property

 To prepare the Inventory and
Inspection Report to properly

items so that these can already be

c. Instruct the Accounting Department
and DGS to coordinate for the
purpose of identifying the items
which were not found during the

amounting to P 527.6 million and

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor
direct-
 The City Treasurer and Accounting
Offices to coordinate and effect the
remittance of all unremitted
withheld taxes which had
accumulated through the years.
 Henceforth, the City Treasurer to
instruct the personnel concerned
in his office to submit the RCI and
supporting DVs to the Accounting
Office weekly so that by the 5th day
of the succeeding month of all RCIs

complete remittance returns; and
the two offices to coordinate and
initiate measures to avoid
recurrences of this severe lapse in
operation which will possible result
in BIR surcharges and penalties due to
deficient remittance returns.
observations.managementAction: Partial remittances were made. City Treasurer and Accounting Offices coordinated to effect remittances.
observations.reason: There are still unremitted withheld taxes.

these contain the necessary

of data for each financial assistance
granted as well as the monitoring of

Services Officer to conduct a

Auditor an updated inventory report

Manual, Volume I.
b) Assign permanent personnel in
the Department of General Services
and accounting office to give priority
in the reconciliation of the two records
pursuant to Section 114 of the NGAS
Manual, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: 2012 assigning an employee to start with the reconciliation of PPE accounts and verify the differences per physical count against accounting records involving current and prior year’s transactions
