observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a) Direct the Dept. of General
Services and the City Engineering
Office to submit all the supporting
documents like the Purchase Request to Inspection and Acceptance documents to the Accounting Office for the preparation of the disbursement voucher (DV) for the payment of the procurement transactions. Also, personal follow up should be made with the appropriate offices concerned where the DV is submitted for review and signature to ensure that the claim is processed at the expected pace until payment or release of check has been facilitated.
b) Instruct personnel assigned thereto to maintain records of receipt and releases where the DV is submitted to identify offices which are acting late on the transaction for no apparent reason so that proper measures are undertaken by management to address the issue.
c) Issue guidelines directing concerned officials who are signatories to the DV and other supporting documents to act on every claim with dispatch and indicate thereto the dates of signing to establish accountability and eliminate the snail pace process of payment and bureaucratic red tape to improve the image of the City and encourage the participation of more suppliers or contractors in order to widen bidding competition and obtain a well-meaning procurement process to result in the most advantageous price for goods, services and infrastructure projects of the City.
observations.managementAction: These issues are addressed in the Performance Governance System adopted by the City in many other functions finding ways to improve their payment system
observations.reason: Management find this problem difficult to solve because of the many other functions of the signing officers concerned

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management :
a) Demand from all officers and
employees concerned for the
immediate settlement of these cash
advances and for the Accountant to
henceforth strictly enforce laws, rules
and regulations relave to cash
advances. Also, the Internal Control
Unit should establish reasonable
timelines not only in the pre-audit of
liquidation reports but also in the
return of the same reports by the
accountable officers if found lacking
in supporting documents;
b) Direct the Accounting Division to
exert efforts to find those lost
liquidation reports for the settlement
of cash advances of the accountable
officers concerned;
c) Impose the sanctions, if
warranted, upon the Accountable
Officers for any violation committed
specifically in the delay of the
liquidation of their cash advances
pursuant to Sec. 5, COA Circular No.
97-002.
d) Direct the officials concerned to
submit all the supporting documents
as required above by the
Intelligence/ Confidential Fund Audit
Unit of COA-Central Office and to
designate credible officials as Special
Disbursing Officer/s for Intelligence/
Confidential Funds in order not to
render questionable the grant of cash
advance for this purpose.
observations.managementAction: The City Legal Officer already sent demand letters particularly those long overdue accounts. Collections of certain long overdue accounts were done, substantial amounts of which were collected from DEPED officials who were granted cash advances from the Special Education Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a) Stop utilizing the 20% DF other than
its intended purpose so that the
objectives of the government are fully
attained; and
b) Refrain from paying expenditures
which are not related to development
projects, programs and activities
mentioned under DBM-DILG JMC No.
2011-1.
observations.managementAction: Management is already utilizing DF in accordance to the purposes stated in the aforecited DILG and DBM guide-lines.
observations.reason: The audit team was unable to audit transactions relative to charges against the 20% DF for the current year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management to require the consultants to submit the documents necessary to establish the technical competence and expertise in their respective field of assignment to ensure that highly technical services are delivered and that consultants are hired only when necessary.
observations.managementAction: The City Administrator will require the submission of the documents to support the validity of hiring certain consultants and will henceforth be requiring those documents to be submitted also by the Bids and Awards Committee who will process and prepare the recommendation for hiring the needed consultants of the City.
observations.reason: Some consultants still did not submit supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a) Conduct a careful study on the
performance evaluation of consultants
in each field of assignment and such
shall be properly documented as this
will be made basis in the preparation
of their respective accomplishments
reports;
b) Require that Accomplishment
Reports of Consultants are properly
documented and supporting documents
of deliverables should be clearly
defined/ identified to facilitate
evaluation of the reported
accomplishments; and
c) Be cautious in the hiring of
consultants to ensure that there is no
duplication of duties and functions with
the regular employees and/ or other
consultants in order to avoid
unnecessary expenditures of
government funds.
observations.managementAction: Consultants are now required to submit required accomplishment reports with supporting documents
observations.reason: Some consultants have yet to submit their accomplishment reports

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a) Direct the Bids and Awards
Committee to scrutinize the minimum
specifications of the item to be
purchased per purchase request (PR)
before any procurement procedure is
undertaken; and see to it that all
submitted bids/quotations have
undergone proper evaluation and
contain the desired specifications of the
item before proceeding with
recommendation for the award;
b) Issue only purchase orders with
complete specifications and in
accordance with the submitted bid
quotations of the winning bidder
pursuant to COA Circular 90-010 to
protect the interest of the government.
observations.managementAction: Division heads were already instructed by the City Administrator to give complete specifications of the items to be procured
observations.reason: Some PRs were still found to be deficient on the technical specifications of the items to be procured

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a) Direct the City Engineer to
disapprove requisitions for
construction supplies and materials if
no pre-inspection report and approved
program of work are submitted by the
requisitioning party to support a
particular repair and maintenance
activity or for other purposes to be
undertaken, to avoid improper
withdrawal thereof;
b) Instruct the City Engineer to assign a
competent personnel to oversee the
proper application of CSM withdrawn
who will render a report thereto to
determine the propriety and extent of
usage with the instruction that any
unutilized item found after work
completion be turned over to the supply
officer by the requesting party; and
c) Direct the supply officer to issue proper
receipt for unutilized CSM turned over
by the requisitioners and record the same
in the stock card for updating and
reconciliation with the inventory on hand
and submit a corresponding report
thereof to the Accounting Office for
appropriate adjustment of their records.
observations.managementAction: The City Engineer had instructed the construction supplies officer to submit a project design or program of work for every construction supplies or construction materials (CSM) requested.
observations.reason: There were items served which did not have the supporting documents to justify the need of those construction supplies/materials

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s audit
recommendation that the City Mayor
direct the City Administrator to look into
these unutilized funds and advise the City
Accountant to prepare the disbursement
voucher to effect the return of these funds
to the source agencies as required under
COA Circular 94-013, if the projects will no
longer be implemented.
observations.managementAction: The Finance Committee had already conducted a meeting on the processes to be observed in using those financial assistance which had been received in the previous years
observations.reason: Has to be thoroughly reviewed and determine the factors why the funds were not utilized.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor
direct the Accountant to make the
necessary adjusting entries, reclassifying
completed projects still included in the
CIP account to the Public Infrastructures
account.
We also recommended that henceforth,
the City Accountant observe accuracy and
timeliness in the accounting and recording
of construction in progress and related
accounts to ensure that only on-going and
uncompleted projects are reflected under
the CIP account through the maintenance
of construction in progress ledger cards.
observations.managementAction: The total amount overstated is due to non-transfer of completed projects to registry of public infrastructures. Necessary JEV was made already to reclassify CIP to the Public Infrastructure account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management
direct the Accounting and Property
sections to adopt the perpetual inventory
method in recording the acquisitions and
issuances of supplies. Regular
reconciliation should be done by the two
sections and adopt the following
procedures in order to reflect the correct
balances of the accounts:
a) The Accounting Section shall maintain
Supplies Ledger cards and reconcile with
the Stock cards kept by the custodian.
b) The Supply/ Property section must
prepare and submit the RSMI regularly to
the Accounting Unit as basis in preparing
the JEVs to record the issuance of
supplies and materials issued.
observations.managementAction: Accounting to coordinate with DGS to come up a supplies system of maintaining the supplies ledger cards and stock cards respectively. The accounting office have also started using the supplies ledger cards.
observations.reason: Verification revealed that the City adopted the perpetual inventory method, however, not for all items of inventory.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor
direct the City Accountant to conduct a
thorough verification of the dormant
items and request for write-off of the
accounts with the Commission on Audit
pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-001.
Thereafter, effect the necessary
adjustments to clean the books of this
deficiency. We further recommend a
regular review of all account balances so
that dormant accounts can immediately be
identified and acted upon.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting have an on-going review of the dormant accounts. They have started sending requests to the concerned department regarding the status of the various assets and request for write-off and adjustment as needed.
observations.reason: Verification of the financial statements for 2016 showed that there are still dormant accounts and thus reiterated in AAR 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Accounting department reassess and enhance the internal controls set for the process flow and recording of payables. We also recommend that the City observe strictly the pertinent provisions of the NGAS Manual with regards to recognition, monitoring and control of payable accounts.
We further recommended that the City Accountant effect the necessary accounting entries to revert prior years’ accounts payable where no actual claims, whether administrative or judicial, have been filed. No obligation should be certified to accounts payable unless it is funded on a valid claim that is properly supported by sufficient evidence and unless there is proper authority for its incurrence pursuant to Section 4(s) Volume 1 of NGAS Manual.
observations.managementAction: Dormant items under Trust Fund have been carried in the books for five (5) or more years because there are certain processes to follow before we can close accounts on funds held in Trust. We will verify and make the necessary adjusting entries.
observations.reason: Per verification they still have this problem, they still have not initiated the process. Hence, the accounting records did not show any adjustments made.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City
Accountant and the heads of concerned
Department/Office enjoins all officials
and employees to observe COA Circular
No. 2012-001 and Section 89 of PD 1445,
and to enforce the appropriate
administrative and/or legal sanctions
provided for under COA Circular 2012-
004.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting is promptly sending notice to the City Treasurer’s office to withhold the salary of accountable officers and employees with unliquidated cash advances.
observations.reason: Collections/deductions from salaries and other benefits have been applied

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to hasten the preparation of the bank reconciliation statements to ensure the correctness of the bank balances appearing in the financial statements.
We further recommended that bank reconciliation statements submitted to COA be properly supported with bank statements and paid and cancelled checks to facilitate the verification of the accuracy of the BRS submitted.
observations.managementAction: A letter request dated May 12, 2015 addressed to Atty. Regal Oliva, the City Treasurer, to provide us the check disbursement vouchers which remained unrecorded in our books. In our letter, we enclosed the bank reconciliation statement as of February, 2015 which shows the list of payees still unrecorded in our books.
observations.reason: The City Treasurer submitted to COA copies of transmittal letters of some disbursement vouchers duly received by accounting.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Head of the Department of General Services and/or designated Supply/Property Officer to submit copies of purchase orders and contracts within five days from its issuance or execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-General Services Officer of the Department of General Services in her reply asked for other consideration for the submission. However she committed that these purchase orders together with its supporting documents will be endorsed not later than five (5) days from issuance thereof starting CY 2015 and onward.
observations.reason: Transmittal letters of submitted purchase orders (POs) and contracts indicate delays in its delivery.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Accountant to submit all required reports within the deadline pursuant to Section 107, 122 of PD 1445, and Section 70 and 73 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: For the month of December,2014 alone, the Accounting Office has recorded almost 4,000 financial transactions of the city, but notwithstanding with that we have recorded the following dates: 2/10/15 for Trust Fund 2/11/15 for Gen. Fund 2/13/15 for SEF 2/18 and 24/15 for General Fund schedules 2/25/15 for consolidated FS (general, trust and SEF)
observations.reason: Transmittal letters of submission indicate delays.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit a written explanation on the foregoing deficiencies and submit the required supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: With the additional personnel in the Accounting Department, reports will now be submitted on time to the Commission On Audit. The City Treasurer has already submitted these documents to the Accounting office except for few which remained to be resolved.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City utilize all
the funds in the projects contemplated in
the guidelines by the PAGCOR and comply
with the guidelines set for the proper
handling/disposition thereof in order to
attain the purpose in granting this
assistance.
observations.managementAction: The amount of P5,000,000 have been already transferred to TRUST FUND for collections. Recorded in General Fund from August 2015-May 2016, collections will be recorded in Trust Fund account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management submit the following required documents:
1. A copy of the approved AWP and MWP reviewed by DILG
2. Report of Annual Inventory of Local Roads and its condition as of December 31, 2014 submitted to DILG and DPWH
3. Copy of Report of Status of Accomplishment submitted to DPWH
4. Original copy of MOA between DPWH and LGU Mandaue City for the SLRF Implementation, stating date of execution by the contracting parties
5. Joint Certificate of Completion issued and signed by the DPWH and LGU Mandaue City
6. Inspection Report conducted by the DPWH and LGU Mandaue City
7. Notice of Completion issued by Socor Construction Corporation
8. Certificate of Acceptance by LGU Mandaue City
9. Written explanation of the occurrence of the following:
a) Performance Security Bond was executed after the contract was signed
b) Notice of Award was posted in the PhilGEPS beyond the prescribed period and receipt of Socor Construction Corp, was 2 months after the Notice of Award
10. Notice to Proceed
11. Copy of Eligibility Checklist Report containing documents submitted, information if passed or failed and duly signed by the TWG.
12. A copy of the invitation letter to two (2) observers
13. Certification by Head of BAC Secretariat of posting the Invitation to Bid at a conspicuous place
14. Minutes of Pre-Bid Conference
15. DTI Registration and Mayor’s Permit valid until December 31, 2015
observations.managementAction: CPDO, DGS and BUB Focal Person have been directed to comply with the recommendation of COA.
observations.reason: Transmitted documents were not sufficient to fully validate the project utilization.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management submit the following additional documents to establish regularity and validity of the fund utilization:
a) Authorization letter issued by the recipient/beneficiary to his representative for receiving the assistance
b) Master List of Indigent Senior Citizens per Barangay, submitted and signed by the concerned Chapter President, certified correct by the Punong Barangay, and duly validated by the authorized officials of CSWS
c) Refund the unclaimed financial assistance of Estelita Prado under Barangay Looc amounting to P2,000.00
observations.managementAction: The CSWS Officer was directed through a Memorandum dated August 16, 2016, to implement the recommendation of COA; with the appointment of OSCA Head, list of senior citizens for each barangay has been updated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the documentary requirements under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2013-066, and the Supplier’s (Centrade Hardware Corp.) Delivery Receipt to facilitate our review of the reasonableness of the cost of project utilization.
observations.managementAction: CPDO, DGS and BUB Focal Person have been directed to comply with the recommendation of COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City’s officials look into the contractor’s failure to complete the project within the contract period and re-compute the actual work-days, with considerations of suspensions and extensions, in order to determine whether there was breach of contract committed by the contractor and to demand for possible liquidated damages.
observations.managementAction: The City Engineer has been directed to closely monitor and supervise the infrastructure projects and to immediately refer to the City Legal Office any impediment in the prosecution of the projects.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the City Mayor direct the City Administrator to look into these unutilized funds and advise the City Accountant to prepare the disbursement voucher to effect the return of these funds to the source agencies as required under COA Circular 94-013, if the projects will no longer be implemented.
observations.managementAction: To review the unutilized funds for their proper disposition.
observations.reason: Has to be thoroughly reviewed and determine the factors why the funds were not utilized.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries, reclassifying completed projects still included in the CIP account to the Public Infrastructures account.
We also recommended that henceforth, the City Accountant observe accuracy and timeliness in the accounting and recording of construction in progress and related accounts to ensure that only on-going and uncompleted projects are reflected under the CIP account through the maintenance of construction in progress ledger cards.
observations.managementAction: The total amount overstated is due to non-transfer of completed projects to registry of public infrastructures. Necessary JEV was made already to reclassify CIP to the Public Infrastructure account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Accounting and Property sections to adopt the perpetual inventory method in recording the acquisitions and issuances of supplies. Regular reconciliation should be done by the two sections and adopt the following procedures in order to reflect the correct balances of the accounts:
a) The Accounting Section shall maintain Supplies Ledger cards and reconcile with the Stock cards kept by the custodian.
b) The Supply/ Property section must prepare and submit the RSMI regularly to the Accounting Unit as basis in preparing the JEVs to record the issuance of supplies and materials issued.
observations.managementAction: Accounting to coordinate with DGS to come up a supplies system of maintaining the supplies ledger cards and stock cards respectively. The accounting office have also started using the supplies ledger cards.
observations.reason: Verification revealed that the City adopted the perpetual inventory method, however, not for all items of inventory.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to conduct a thorough verification of the dormant items and request for write-off of the accounts with the Commission on Audit pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-001. Thereafter, effect the necessary adjustments to clean the books of this deficiency. We further recommend a regular review of all account balances so that dormant accounts can immediately be identified and acted upon.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting have an on-going review of the dormant accounts. They have started sending requests to the concerned department regarding the status of the various assets and request for write-off and adjustment as needed.
observations.reason: Verification of the financial statements for 2016 showed that there are still dormant accounts and thus reiterated in AAR 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Accounting department reassess and enhance the internal controls set for the process flow and recording of payables. We also recommend that the City observe strictly the pertinent provisions of the NGAS Manual with regards to recognition, monitoring and control of payable accounts.
We further recommended that the City Accountant effect the necessary accounting entries to revert prior years’ accounts payable where no actual claims, whether administrative or judicial, have been filed. No obligation should be certified to accounts payable unless it is funded on a valid claim that is properly supported by sufficient evidence and unless there is proper authority for its incurrence pursuant to Section 4(s) Volume 1 of NGAS Manual.
observations.managementAction: Dormant items under Trust Fund have been carried in the books for five (5) or more years because there are certain processes to follow before we can close accounts on funds held in Trust. We will verify and make the necessary adjusting entries.
observations.reason: Per verification they still have this problem, they still have not initiated the process. Hence, the accounting records did not show any adjustments made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant and the heads of concerned Department/Office enjoins all officials and employees to observe COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Section 89 of PD 1445, and to enforce the appropriate administrative and/or legal sanctions provided for under COA Circular 2012-004.
observations.managementAction: The City Accounting is promptly sending notice to the City Treasurer’s office to withhold the salary of accountable officers and employees with unliquidated cash advances.
observations.reason: Collections/deductions from salaries and other benefits have been applied.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to hasten the preparation of the bank reconciliation statements to ensure the correctness of the bank balances appearing in the financial statements.
We further recommended that bank reconciliation statements submitted to COA be properly supported with bank statements and paid and cancelled checks to facilitate the verification of the accuracy of the BRS submitted.
observations.managementAction: A letter request dated May 12,2015 addressed to Atty. Regal Oliva, the City Treasurer, to provide the check disbursement vouchers which remained unrecorded in our books. In our letter, we enclosed the bank reconciliation statement as of February, 2015 which shows the list of payees still unrecorded in our books.
observations.reason: The City Treasurer submitted to COA copies of transmittal letters of some disbursement vouchers duly received by accounting.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the Head of the Department of General Services and/or designated Supply/Property Officer to submit copies of purchase orders and contracts within five days from its issuance or execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-General Services Officer committed that purchase orders and supporting documents will be endorsed not later than five (5) days from issuance starting CY 2015 and onward.
observations.reason: Transmittal letters of submitted purchase orders (POs) and contracts indicate delays in its delivery.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the accountant to submit all required reports within the deadline pursuant to Section 107, 122 of PD 1445, and Section 70 and 73 of the NGAS Manual.
observations.managementAction: Accounting Office submitted year end reports on various dates (listed)
observations.reason: Submission was delayed though eventually made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the officials concerned to prepare and submit the LDRRMFIP and the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using prescribed format and submit on or before the 15th day after the end of each month to COA Auditor.
observations.managementAction: Not submitted initially; Management admitted lapses and committed to monthly submission in adherence with COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.reason: Initially submitted quarterly instead of monthly; committed to comply.

observations.recommendation: Instruct City Accountant to verify reconciling items and prepare necessary adjusting/correcting entries; explain delays in recording reconciling items since 2010.
observations.managementAction: 12 debit memos were already taken up in our books but checks were still unrecorded due to untransmitted DVs and supporting documents. The City Treasurer submitted copies of transmittal letters of some disbursement vouchers to accounting.

observations.recommendation: Immediate settlement of cash advances and implement effective monitoring of cash advances and liquidation; impose sanctions if warranted pursuant to COA Circular 97-002.
observations.managementAction: Management has exerted efforts to demand the liquidation resulting to a reduction in the balance of the account.

observations.recommendation: Observe proper procedures in granting financial assistance to NGOs/POs in consonance with COA Circular 2007-001. Submit required documents otherwise transactions will be suspended or disallowed.
observations.managementAction: Management has exerted efforts to demand liquidation resulting to a reduction in the balance of the account. Recorded already. Subsidiary ledgers are still to be properly maintained.
observations.reason: No liquidation papers submitted.

observations.recommendation: Direct City Engineering Office in coordination with School Superintendent to secure copies of required program of works and floor plans to establish validity of counterparts.
observations.managementAction: Directed City Engineer to coordinate with School Superintendent to secure required documents.
observations.reason: No liquidation papers submitted.

observations.recommendation: Create a team to review transactions and determine actual utilization; issue SSMI if items issued or not found; Accounting Office to adjust records.
observations.managementAction: These accounts were still carried in the books
observations.reason: Verification ongoing; some items require write-off requests.

observations.recommendation: Direct City Accountant to make adjusting entry to record cost as inventory; instruct DGS to submit Monthly Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) with supporting issue slips; require offices receiving copies to maintain logbook; record purchases as inventory using perpetual method.
observations.managementAction: These are already adjusted in the books of accounts, taken up as inventory for the inventory account.
observations.reason: Verification for CY 2016 did not show complete details of the inventory listing.

observations.recommendation: Remind BAC to observe procurement procedures; conduct judicious examination and review of procurement contracts; instruct City Administrator to ensure BAC Chairman and Secretariat observe procedures and adequate specifications.
observations.managementAction: Documents were reviewed; BAC instructed to follow procurement rules; adjustments pending.

observations.recommendation: Direct Accounting Department to review composition of accounts and effect transfers/adjustments; review composition and effect corrections.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Division is working on many other deficiencies and will try their best to identify causes of negative entries in order to effect appropriate corrections.
observations.reason: Still with unreconciled balances and adjustments ongoing.

observations.recommendation: Require supplier to refund for use of ordinary BI pipes installed instead of stainless and refund P291,900 for 105 days delay; impose penalties for late deliveries; implement RA 9184 on alternative procurement.
observations.managementAction: Accounting Office is reviewing transaction; penalties are being imposed to suppliers for late deliveries.
observations.reason: None of the recommendations were fully implemented; amended BAC resolution not submitted.

observations.recommendation: Submit legal basis and appropriate fund source for grant of bags; submit OR for bid security; if not, BAC liable for difference; explain deficiencies on supporting document dates.
observations.managementAction: Management transmitted explanation and additional documents; some deficiencies remain unresolved.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to trace the transactions recorded in the Due From Other Funds and Due To Other Funds accounts and make the necessary corrections/adjustments. Moreover, require the Municipal Treasurer to settle these accounts to allow the creditor-fund to utilize the amounts for their intended purposes, if any.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is still reviewing the accounts.
observations.reason: This will be effected as soon as these accounts will be verified.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management speed up the implementation of development projects to attain the desired benefits of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes as intended by DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 2011-1. Henceforth, the Municipal Planning and Development Office and the Municipal Engineering Office meticulously plan the scheduling and prioritization of development projects and closely monitor their implementation to ensure timely completion. We further recommend that management advise the Municipal Accountant to impose liquidated damages on the above-mentioned projects when necessary.
observations.managementAction: Acted

observations.recommendation: Recommendation :             Require the Municipal Accountant to send confirmation and/or demand letters to individuals concerned.  For receivables from officials and employees arising from overpayment of claims, the Municipal Treasurer shall institute deductions on their subsequent claims and salaries in satisfaction of their indebtedness.
observations.managementAction: Demand Letters were send to officials concerned.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management intensify their knowledge and competence of GAD, its goals, objectives, laws, regulations and the utilization of the GAD Budget.  Likewise consider as GAD PPAs only those programs and activities which address issues concerning women’s human rights and economic empowerment in accordance with Joint Circular No. 2013-01 of the PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA.
observations.managementAction: Acted

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management strictly adhere to the provisions prescribed in the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 pertaining to warranty security, in the procurement of goods, in order to protect the interest of the government and those of the third parties involved in the execution of government projects.  Henceforth, future procurements should be deducted with the 10% retention money or a special bank guarantee which is equivalent to 10% of the total contract price should be required, otherwise, the burden of expenses where there will be discovered defects of the completed project or goods delivered be charged to concerned officials approving the payments of these goods or completed projects.
observations.managementAction: Acted

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to stamp “PAID” and to sign and date all disbursement vouchers and payrolls and its supporting documents immediately after payment thereof as a control measure to prevent the possible double payment of claims through the recycling of the same documents.
observations.managementAction: Acted

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management:                             Require the  Municipal Accountant  to immediately conduct investigation of the subsidiary ledgers comprising the Cash in Bank (111) and prepare the necessary adjustment in the books of those affected accounts and submit the corresponding copy/copies to the Commission on Audit for verification.      Require the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular monitoring (quarterly) and analysis of accounts with emphasis on accounts with negative balances and effect immediate preparation of Journal Entry Voucher to effect correcting entries.
observations.managementAction: Updated amount

observations.recommendation: We recommend that:           In view of the Supreme Court’s decision pronouncing the unconstitutionality of the PDAF, we recommend that the remaining funds shall no longer be released but shall be returned to the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) so that it can be used for other priority programs of the government.
observations.managementAction: Acted
observations.reason: Unutilized amount was returned to the Bureau of Treasury

observations.recommendation: We recommend that:           The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) consolidate requisitions for one or more items needed at or about the same time by the requisitioner and avoid splitting of government contracts. The BAC ensure that all procurement of supplies, materials, furnitures and equipment are done through competitive public bidding, pursuant to Section 10, Article IV of R. A. 9184 in order to give the widest publicity possible to all prospective and qualified suppliers with price offers or quotations most advantageous to the government.      The BAC strictly comply with the conditions enumerated under Section 52.1, Rule XVI of the same IRR in the event that the original mode of procurement through competitive public bidding as recommended in the APP cannot be pursued and shopping is resorted to as an alternative mode of procurement by the Bids and Awards Committee.      The management strictly adhere with the provisions contained under Section 7.2, Rule II of the same IRR requiring the formulation of the Annual Procurement Plan which shall consolidate the respective Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) of the different programs, projects and activities of the different end-user units the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Management stated that office supplies are now procured by bulk except for some instance specially in the RHU and Waterworks Department, but the Municipal Mayor signified that these will not happen again.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management : a) Exercise prudence and economy in the disbursement and utilization of government funds. Be guided with  COA Circular No. 85-55A on the prevention of the incurrence of irregular, unnecessary, excessive or extravagant expenditures and Administrative Order No. 103 on the continued adoption and implementation  of the austerity measures. b)Minimize  or   stop  the  practice  of  serving  meals  and  snacks   during  regular monthly meetings or conferences except on seminars and trainings when catering services are part of the activity design and properly funded. c)Utilize the LGU’s limited resources to projects, plans and programs that would benefit the majority of its constituents.
observations.managementAction: Management justified that because the LGU is promoting tourism minimal amount of snacks were served and agreed to effect this in the ensuing year.
observations.reason: To be fully implemented in the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that: a.)The corresponding equip-ment/property ledger cards should also be maintained by the accounting section to serve as their subsidiary records of the Inventories and PPE accounts. b)Reconcile the physical inventory of the PPE with the equipment ledger cards and the control accounts before submission of the inventory report. c)All discrepancies between physical and book inventories must be investigated and cleared immediately. If necessary, written explanations shall be required from persons liable.
observations.managementAction: Noted by the Municipal Accountant. The officials reconciled the report with the books.
observations.reason: Due to lack of personnel in the Accounting Section, the Municipal Accountant finds difficulty in maintaining the equipment ledger cards. She has no basis  since these were not maintained in the previous years.  The only records are the general ledger, schedules of PPE and Memorandum Receipts as their basis in the reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: Grant the subsequent cash advances for intelligence and confidential fund only after the submission of liquidation vouchers to the COA Chairman or after the issuance of credit notice from the COA Chairman in accordance with COA Circular No. 2003-003. Require the concerned official to immediately follow up the credit notices from COA Central Office on the submitted liquidations for its settlement.
observations.managementAction: Follow-up letters were sent to COA Central Office
observations.reason: Still waiting for the credit notice from COA Central Office

observations.recommendation: Require the immediate refunds from all concerned officials and employees who receive the Extra Cash Gift of P18,000.00.
observations.managementAction: Noted
observations.reason: Executive Order was issued to all regular employees to pay the excess amount of P8,000.00. Last December 2016, a payroll deduction amounting to P1,000.00 was initially deducted from payroll until such amount will be fully paid.
